# Aevum Plate Tectonics Engineering Plan

Status: active planning document
Owner: tectonics / terrain / archive integration
Last updated: 2026-07-05

This document archives the plate, crust, continent, and tectonic-landform
redesign plan.  It should be updated during later tectonics work, the same way
`docs/CLIMATE_SYSTEM_PLAN.md` tracks climate work.

Long-horizon tectonics refactor planning now lives in
`docs/PLATE_TECTONICS_REFACTOR_PLAN.md`.  That document tracks the shift from
random/heuristic major-event generation to principle-model tectonics with
per-stage microbenchmarks.  This engineering plan remains the development log
for the current P-series implementation and release-gate history.

Execution constraint for future validation and tuning:

- Multi-core experiments must use process-level parallelism only.  Different
  seeds, resolutions, and parameter sweeps should run as independent CLI or
  pytest processes with independent output directories and no shared writable
  state.
- The worker cap is `5` concurrent processes unless the user explicitly changes
  it.  This keeps deterministic runs isolated while still allowing safe
  multi-seed and multi-resolution sweeps.

The required real-Earth geomorphology feature coverage is tracked in
`docs/EARTH_GEOMORPHOLOGY_COVERAGE.md`.
The detailed research/source/microbenchmark plan for comparing generated worlds
against real-Earth geomorphology is archived in
`docs/REAL_EARTH_GEOMORPHOLOGY_RESEARCH_PLAN.md`.
The historical land/ocean geomorphology lifecycle plan is archived in
`docs/HISTORICAL_GEOMORPHOLOGY_LIFECYCLE_PLAN.md`.  That document is the
current execution plan for turning terminal-only inland and ocean-floor detail
into persistent process-time objects, archive diagnostics, and historical visual
gates.  Its next implementation entry is P170, the per-frame historical
diagnostic baseline.
As of 2026-06-27, that archive also contains the staged evidence-collection
plan for source packets, theory/test details, implementation methods,
optimization targets, and promotion-audit sequencing.  The P101+ real-Earth
comparison repair archive in that document is the canonical next-plan record for
the current planform plus crust/sediment residuals.
The current real-Earth geomorphology execution point is after the P111.6.25
abyssal-depth calibration repair.  P111.6.21 completed the full
`8000/36p` P111.6.19 validation, with P111.6.20 adding runtime observability
and runtime-bottleneck repairs before the high-resolution rerun.  P111.6.22
adds a guarded final high-mountain repair path that uses
`terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine` as the semantic authority for short
main-crest/branch gaps, while preserving the existing land mask, foreland
exclusion, and P109 high-tail guards.  P111.6.23 moves part of that work
upstream by reducing fragmented branch/peak spine semantics before terminal
elevation repair sees them.  P111.6.24 then couples the improved spine back
to terrain with a two-tier response: existing high-shoulder gaps may become
3000m+ peak bridges only when the full gap is already highland-supported,
while lower spine saddles are raised only into sub-3000m continuous mountain
relief.  P111.6.25 moves to the residual ocean-floor problem and applies a
final-only bathymetry calibration so slope/rise cells and non-object-backed
far-ocean floor are not left unrealistically shallow after object relief.
The `8000/36p` and
`24000/60p` seed-707 calibration audits use `p1116_boundary_linework.v3`, so
ridge, transform, trench, and convergent checklist metrics prefer semantic
terrain or tectonic objects while retaining raw boundary and depth-province
layers as side-by-side evidence.  P111.6.6 records
`p11166_trench_arc_refinement` audit evidence for final trench-axis bridging;
P111.6.7 records `p11167_convergent_parent_linework` evidence for ordered
convergent/subduction parent axes; P111.6.8 records
`p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy` evidence and the
`terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` field for guarded main-crest, branch-range,
and foreland-slope terrain semantics; P111.6.9 uses that hierarchy as a guarded
priority/constraint layer for final high-mountain repair; P111.6.10 improves
the upstream crest/branch hierarchy continuity before terrain repair sees it;
P111.6.16 adds a targeted high-latitude orogen morphology fallback; P111.6.17
adds strict endpoint rejection telemetry; and P111.6.18 adds a terminal
closed-ocean reconnect pass for closed seas introduced after the first
closed-ocean repair.  P111.6.19 adds a narrow terminal domain-partition pass
for the residual case where the largest land component is above the preferred
modern ceiling but already contains multiple significant continent/domain
lineages.  P111.6.23 records `p111623` audit evidence for branch/peak spine
component reduction and integrated-spine replacement, P111.6.24 records
`p111624` audit evidence for high-shoulder spine response, lower saddle
response, and narrow hypsometry-guard acceptance, and P111.6.25 records
`p111625` audit evidence for final-only abyssal depth calibration.
P109/P110A/P110B/P111
gates remain green, mid-ocean ridge bathymetry is legible, ridge-transform
junctions remain attached, open-ocean unsupported shoals remain controlled, and
trench line objects still feed terminal ocean-floor depth and depth-province
expression through the P111.6.3 narrow axis plus guarded apron.  P111.6.7
reduces the `24000/60p` raw convergent evidence from `378` scattered components
to `12` semantic parent-object components.  P111.6.10 keeps the same terrain
safety rule: orogenic bridge uplift is allowed only on existing highland
shoulders, foreland code `1` cannot create peaks, and P109 p90 /
`3000-4500m` tail regressions are guarded.
P103 through P110B repaired the
worst planform, supercontinent-residence, ocean-gateway, lowland-tail, and
archive-object blockers.  P111.1 added the stricter modern-planform
distribution gate, and P111.2 added a conservative continental-hypsometry
finish retune.  P111.3 now has a scoped final pass that joins process-backed
highland shoulders inside production mountain ranges.  P111.4 now records,
protects, and audits modern endpoint seaways created by final planform polish
instead of leaving them as anonymous mask edits.  P111.4.2 lets the final
modern endpoint polish continue beyond the coarse P110B stop only when a
P111-overlarge landmass has domain-boundary, suture/margin, or weak-lowland
provenance and the cut remains inside a modern largest-land range.  The current
plate-engineering target is no longer "make any Earth-like terminal map pass
coarse gates"; it is
to close the remaining visual/process defects that still prevent the generated
worlds from reading like plausible real-Earth-scale tectonic products:

- terminal continents should be multiple broad tectonic domains separated by
  open oceans and inherited seaways, not a permanent modern supercontinent;
- land elevations should match an Earth-like hypsometric shape, with abundant
  lowlands and coastal plains rather than broad high smooth interiors;
- plate boundaries should be narrow, legible, and process-backed, while high
  mountains should form coherent orogenic belts and plateaus instead of many
  small highland fragments;
- ocean-floor relief should express ridges, transforms, abyssal plains,
  trenches, seamount chains, oceanic plateaus, microcontinents, island arcs,
  and shelves without unsupported shallow speckle;
- 8000-cell maps are the calibration target; 24000-cell maps are the final
  visual promotion target after 8000-cell distribution gates are stable.

Core release checklist for the current plate/terrain engineering closure:

1. Mountain systems follow convergent boundaries as coherent belts, with a main
   crest, tributary ranges, and foreland/foothill slopes rather than elevation
   blotches.
2. Trenches attach to ocean-continent and ocean-ocean subduction boundaries as
   stable, narrow, elongated deep troughs.
3. Mid-ocean ridges are immediately legible in bathymetry along divergent
   boundaries as continuous shallow ridge systems, not only diffuse texture.
4. Oceanic crust age controls bathymetry: young oceanic crust is shallower, old
   oceanic crust is deeper, and depth is not primarily noise-driven.
5. Oceanic crust age increases bilaterally away from mid-ocean ridges; transform
   offsets may segment the belts but should not destroy the paired age logic.
6. Object-layer semantics are fixed and visually auditable: mountain systems,
   island arcs, trenches, rifts, plateaus, cratons, passive margins, ocean-floor
   fabric, and related objects must have stable meanings instead of acting as
   generic labels.
7. Boundary output evolves from scattered pixels into line objects: convergent
   boundaries read as arcs, divergent boundaries as ridge polylines, and
   transform boundaries as offsets/fractures.
8. Continental interiors express a geomorphic hierarchy with at least plateaus,
   mountains, basins, plains, and cratons, rather than random interior relief.

Checklist status after P111.6.7:

1. Mountain systems follow convergent boundaries: in progress.  P111.3 repaired
   some process-backed highland shoulders, but high-mountain belts still need
   explicit main-crest, branch-range, and foreland-slope hierarchy.
2. Trenches attach to subduction boundaries: stage pass.  P111.6.3 makes
   terrain-object trenches drive narrow bathymetric troughs and the
   P111.6.6 `8000/36p` seed-707 audit still reports trench-subduction
   attachment `0.964`, deep-trench attachment `0.944`, trench median depth
   `5000 m`, and trench-minus-nontrench median depth `+2558 m`.  The residual
   is line-object fragmentation and parent-line ordering, not missing depth.
3. Mid-ocean ridges are legible in bathymetry: in progress.  P111.6 made ridge
   highs visible and kept nonridge-minus-ridge median depth near `809 m` at
   `8000/36p`.  P111.6.4 makes the audit prefer `terrain.ocean_fabric`
   spreading-center objects for the main ridge linework, but those objects
   still read as broad/branchy grid chains rather than clean ridge polylines.
   P111.6.5 repairs ridge-transform junction attachment, with object-source
   ridge-transform adjacency rising to `0.724` at `8000/36p`.
4. Oceanic crust age controls bathymetry: in progress.  The calibration audit
   has age-depth correlation `0.297` and old-minus-young depth `+702 m`, but
   this should be strengthened upstream in crust-age and spreading geometry
   rather than added as a terminal clamp.
5. Oceanic crust age increases bilaterally away from ridges: stage pass for the
   calibration seed.  The age-distance-from-ridge correlation is `0.928` and
   far-minus-near ridge median age is `53.6 Myr`; this still needs multi-seed
   and 24000-cell confirmation.
6. Object-layer semantics are fixed and visually auditable: in progress.
   Terrain/ocean objects now cover ridges, trenches, fracture zones, island
   arcs, back-arcs, seamount chains, oceanic plateaus, microcontinents, old
   orogens, plateaus, cratons, and related provinces, but legend/renderer
   semantics should remain explicit during later object work.  P111.6.4 audit
   schema v3 now records object/raw/depth sources side by side for ridge,
   transform, and trench.
7. Boundary output evolves from scattered pixels into line objects: major
   blocker.  P111.6.2 made trench objects non-isolated, P111.6.3 gave them
   bathymetric depth, and P111.6.4 made object layers the audited primary
   source.  P111.6.5 repaired the biggest transform/ridge object gap: at
   `8000/36p`, object transform component count drops from `24` to `19` and
   object-source ridge-transform adjacency rises from `0.302` to `0.724`.
   P111.6.6 adds a conservative trench-axis bridge that reduces object trench
   components from `49` to `44`, raises object-trench line score from `0.118`
   to `0.151`, and raises depth-trench line score from `0.129` to `0.159`.
   P111.6.7 adds tectonic `convergent_parent` and `subduction_parent` objects.
   At `24000/60p`, raw convergent evidence has `378` components, isolated
   fraction `0.398`, and line score `0.019`, while the parent object has `12`
   components, isolated fraction `0.0`, and line score `0.469`.  This is a
   stage pass for object semantics and audit visibility, but not yet a terrain
   pass: parent-driven mountain, island-arc, and trench response still needs
   guarded coupling to avoid excessive high terrain and broad branched bands.
8. Continental interiors express geomorphic hierarchy: in progress.  The
   current 8000-cell map has more lowlands, highlands, basins, and internal
   boundaries than the early baselines, but still lacks enough coherent
   continent-scale physiographic provinces.

The immediate trigger for this plan is the observed mismatch between generated
continents and Earth-like continents: generated land often contains many long,
thin continental ribbons and line-like mountain belts.  Some linear features are
valid in geology, such as island arcs, active margins, sutures, rifts, and
volcanic chains.  The bug is that too many of those linear process bands become
the exposed continental mask itself.

## Current Baseline

Current implementation status after P12:

- The simulator has object-level continents, cratons, terranes, boundary
  processes, Wilson-cycle proxies, ocean-basin objects, margin objects,
  gateway objects, seam/compiler diagnostics, and a P12 release-gate matrix.
- Earth-like seed 42 passes the current structural, bathymetry, seam-continuity,
  compiler-consistency, and P12 hard gates.  It remains a warning-level
  supercontinent-like Earth analogue rather than a modern-fragmented Earth map.
- The dominant remaining tectonic issue is optional morphology calibration:
  the reference Earth-like output still has high ribbon/coastline-complexity
  warnings, but the serious P12 failures are bounded and documented.
- Archive continuity no longer shows redraw-like crust-type jumps after P7, and
  P10 shows no projection-seam break in the reference Earth-like run.  Layer
  volatility should still be monitored while morphology changes.

Current implementation entry points:

- `aevum/modules/tectonics.py`
  - Initializes continental crust with `_grow_continents(...)`.
  - Initializes plates with random spherical Voronoi regions.
  - Advances crust by plate Euler rotations and re-rasterization.
  - Classifies plate boundaries into ridge, trench, collision, suture,
    passive margin, and active margin proxies.
  - Uses `_conserve_continental(...)` to keep continental area near target.
  - Repairs plate speckle and detached plate components.
  - Generates seafloor age from ridges and recycles old oceanic crust.
- `aevum/modules/terrain.py`
  - Converts crust thickness, crust type, boundary processes, isostasy,
    erosion, deposition, sea level, shelves, and trenches into surface
    elevation.
  - Contains terrain generalization for tiny islands and regionalized ocean
    floor, but not yet a broad continent-shape controller.
- `aevum/features.py`
  - Contains feature registry contracts consumed by rendering, validation, and
    downstream modules.
- `aevum/render.py`
  - Produces `plates.png`, `crust_age.png`, `elevation.png`, climate assets,
    seam-continuity diagnostics, ocean-geography assets, and compiler
    consistency assets.
- `aevum/compiler/map_compiler.py`
  - Aggregates physical layers into the compiled hex map, including source
    land fraction, shelf fraction, ocean depth provinces, and terrain
    provinces.
  - Distinguishes land ice from pack ice when checking compiled land/ocean
    consistency.
- `aevum/diagnostics/release_gate.py`
  - Runs the P12 tectonics release-gate matrix and writes the JSON summary and
    contact sheet.
- `aevum/validation.py` and `tests/`
  - Contain conservation, topology, regression, and generated-world tests.

Latest known implementation/test baseline:

- P12 targeted regression:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_climate_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_detect_artifacts -q`
  -> `3 passed in 40.63s`.
- P12 Earth-like release run:

  -> decision `warn`, `0` failed entries.
- P12 canonical matrix smoke:

  -> decision `warn`, `0` failed entries.
- Full suite after P12:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `38 passed in 299.22s`.
- Previous P10/P11 targeted regression:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_artificial_antimeridian_mask_is_connected_on_sphere tests/test_engine.py::test_truth_layers_are_cyclic_at_dateline tests/test_engine.py::test_compiles_a_map_with_layers tests/test_engine.py::test_tectonic_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_archive tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
  -> `5 passed in 55.27s`.
- Full suite after P10/P11:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `37 passed in 245.82s`.
- P12 generated Earth-like seed 42 assets at
  `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p12_earthlike_seed42_20260622_v4/`.
- P12 generated canonical matrix smoke assets at
  `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p12_preset_matrix_smoke_20260622_b/`.
- Plate motion, Wilson-cycle objects, ocean basins, margins, gateways, archive
  continuity, spherical seam continuity, and compiler/elevation agreement are
  provisionally acceptable for downstream consumers.
- The next main work should stay on real-Earth geomorphology coverage.  The
  integrated `earth_geomorphology_coverage` release summary now exposes which
  feature classes are world-visible, fixture-only, parentless, or missing.
  Climate, ocean-current, and monsoon redesign remain paused except for
  regression fixes.

## Problem Diagnosis

The long-strip land problem was not caused by one line of code.  It came from
several independent local rules reinforcing each other.  P1 through P7 fixed
many of the worst causes, but the residual failure modes below still guide the
next calibration steps:

1. Initial continents used to be random-growth components.
   `_grow_continents(...)` now has compact proto-continent and craton logic, but
   P8 still needs to test whether seed-to-seed size, spacing, and later merging
   produce enough separated broad landmasses.

2. Plate-boundary processes are line-shaped by nature.
   Collisions, subduction arcs, sutures, trenches, and ridges are boundary
   bands.  The current crust update can convert some of those bands into
   continental crust, so a valid linear process becomes a linear continent.

3. Continental conservation is now shape-aware, but still competes with area
   targets.
   `_conserve_continental(...)` penalizes unsupported slivers and protects
   cratons, yet P8 must verify across seeds that area recovery does not keep
   too many long isthmuses, narrow margins, or supercontinent edge ribbons.

4. Terrain relief can still amplify boundary lines.
   `_tectonic_relief(...)` and terrain provinces now suppress many accidental
   strips, but collision belts, arcs, sutures, and coastal shelves still need
   compiler-scale validation so valid linear geology does not become invalid
   mainland.

5. Sea level exposes any high connected band.
   `_solve_sea_level(...)` currently solves a target land fraction, then terrain
   post-processing removes only some tiny islands.  It does not yet decide
   whether a high linear chain should be mainland, an island arc, a submerged
   ridge, or a transient volcanic chain.

6. The continent/terrane object model exists, but ocean-basin and margin
   objects are still incomplete.
   The simulator can now ask whether a cell belongs to a craton, continent,
   terrane, island arc, suture, or Wilson-cycle zone.  P9 should extend this
   object approach to ocean basins, shelves, slopes, abyssal plains, trenches,
   and gateways.

## Design Principle

The next tectonics architecture should treat raster fields as the rendered
state, not as the only source of geological truth.

Target dependency direction:

```text
astronomy + interior heat
  -> tectonic regime and mantle-energy budget
  -> plate objects and plate kinematics
  -> continent / craton / terrane objects
  -> boundary process objects and Wilson-cycle events
  -> crust fields rasterized from objects
  -> terrain provinces and regional landform exposure
  -> climate geography primitives and map compiler assets
```

Cell fields remain necessary for rendering and downstream modules, but major
geological decisions should increasingly be made with objects and regional
diagnostics:

- plates
- continents
- cratons
- microcontinents
- island arcs
- accreted terranes
- passive margins
- active margins
- sutures
- rifts
- ocean basins
- plateaus
- volcanic provinces
- orogenic belts

## Earth-History Coverage Requirements

The tectonics layer must cover Earth-like history from early planet formation
to the present.  It should not assume that present-day Earth processes always
operated with present-day intensity.

Required process families:

- Hadean and early Archean:
  - hot interior, thin/mobile lithosphere variants
  - proto-continents and felsic crust seeds
  - impact-modified crust and magma ocean aftermath as upstream inputs
  - greenstone-like belts and unstable proto-cratons
- Archean:
  - craton growth and stabilization
  - komatiite/LIP-style high-heat volcanism where interior heat is high
  - short-lived or weakly organized subduction variants
  - terrane accretion around proto-cratons
- Proterozoic:
  - supercontinent assembly and breakup
  - long passive margins, rifts, sutures, and intracratonic basins
  - stable cratonic interiors with old continental crust
- Phanerozoic-like mature plate tectonics:
  - Wilson cycles with ocean opening and closing
  - continent-continent collision and broad orogenic plateaus
  - subduction arcs, back-arc basins, trench rollback, and accreted terranes
  - seafloor-age structure from ridges to trenches
  - ocean gateways that affect later climate and biogeography

Non-goals for the near term:

- Full mantle convection simulation.
- Kilometer-scale structural geology.
- Named Earth reconstructions.
- Exact paleogeographic replay of Earth.
- Dynamic rupture or earthquake-cycle physics.

## Target Data Model

### Existing Fields To Preserve

- `crust.type`
- `crust.thickness`
- `crust.age`
- `crust.density`
- `crust.plate_id`
- `tectonics.boundary_type`
- `tectonics.stress`
- `tectonics.strain_rate`
- `tectonics.velocity`
- `tectonics.uplift_rate`
- `tectonics.subduction_flux`
- `tectonics.plate_fragmentation`
- `tectonics.archive_continuity`
- `terrain.elevation`

### Proposed New Or Strengthened Fields

These should be introduced incrementally and registered in `aevum/features.py`
only when a phase starts producing them.

- `tectonics.continent_id`
  - Stable object id for coherent continental masses.
- `tectonics.terrane_id`
  - Id for accreted terranes, microcontinents, arcs, or fragments.
- `tectonics.craton_id`
  - Stable id for old, thick, low-strain continental cores.
- `crust.domain`
  - Discrete geological domain:
    - oceanic
    - craton
    - continental_interior
    - continental_margin
    - passive_margin
    - active_margin
    - island_arc
    - accreted_terrane
    - microcontinent
    - suture
    - rift
    - LIP
    - plateau
- `crust.continental_membership`
  - Continuous probability or strength that a cell belongs to a continental
    object, separate from exposed land.
- `crust.accretion_state`
  - Whether a cell is native continent, accreting arc, accreted terrane,
    sutured crust, or rejected oceanic crust.
- `tectonics.continent_width`
  - Graph-distance width proxy inside each continental object.
- `tectonics.ribbon_index`
  - High where continental crust is long, narrow, and geologically suspect.
- `tectonics.coastline_complexity`
  - Local and per-object perimeter-area diagnostic.
- `tectonics.margin_class`
  - Passive, active, transform, rifted, collisional, or uncertain.
- `tectonics.orogen_id`
  - Object id for mountain belts and plateaus.
- `tectonics.ocean_basin_id`
  - Plate-tectonic ocean basin id, distinct from climate's connected wet mask
    if necessary.
- `archive.wilson_cycle_phase`
  - Opening, widening, mature, closing, collision, sutured, inactive.

### Proposed Objects

Object arrays or dictionaries can live in `WorldState.objects` using the
existing object/event architecture.

Plate object:

- id
- area
- Euler pole
- angular rate
- mean age
- dominant crust type
- active boundary lengths
- parent/child ids after fragmentation or merger

Continent object:

- id
- area
- exposed area
- centroid
- core cells
- margin cells
- mean crust age
- mean thickness
- compactness
- width distribution
- attached terranes
- active Wilson-cycle phase

Craton object:

- id
- parent continent id
- age
- stability
- thickness anomaly
- strain shield
- erosion resistance

Terrane object:

- id
- type: island arc, microcontinent, accretionary wedge, plateau, ophiolite,
  LIP, rift block
- source plate id
- target continent id
- age
- accretion time
- sutured flag
- survival probability

Boundary process object:

- id
- type: ridge, trench, transform, active_margin, passive_margin, rift, suture,
  collision, arc, plume, LIP
- geometry cells
- age interval
- intensity
- associated plates
- associated continents or terranes

## Phase Plan

The phases are ordered to reduce risk.  Diagnostics come before behavior
changes; object models come before large rewrites; terrain exposure is adjusted
after the crust model can distinguish mainland from process bands.

### P0. Baseline Inventory And Morphology Diagnostics

Status: complete as diagnostic baseline; generator fixes remain in P1/P3/P4/P5

Purpose:

- Quantify the long-strip problem before changing generation rules.
- Produce repeatable metrics for "continent looks like a ribbon".
- Make failures visible in both tests and PNG assets.

Implementation outline:

- Add morphology utilities that operate on spherical graph components:
  - connected continental components
  - exposed-land components
  - component area
  - perimeter length
  - compactness proxy
  - graph-distance-to-ocean width
  - narrow neck count
  - fraction of land with width <= 1, 2, 3 graph steps
  - largest-component area share
  - island/sliver count by area bin
- Prefer shared helper functions usable from validation and render code.
- Keep this phase diagnostic-only.  No generator behavior change yet.

Candidate metrics:

- `land_component_count`
- `continent_component_count`
- `largest_continent_share`
- `mean_continent_compactness`
- `p10_continent_width`
- `ribbon_land_fraction`
- `one_cell_bridge_count`
- `coastline_complexity_p95`
- `orogen_on_oceanic_arc_fraction`
- `exposed_high_oceanic_fraction`

Tests:

- Fixed-seed deterministic metrics.
- Hand-made masks:
  - compact disk scores low ribbon index
  - long one-cell line scores high ribbon index
  - island arc is recognized as narrow but can be flagged separately from
    mainland if its domain is non-continental later
- Existing full suite remains green.

Assets:

- `continent_components.png`
- `land_width.png`
- `continent_width.png`
- `ribbon_index.png`
- `coastline_complexity.png`
- `narrow_necks.png`
- `morphology_diagnostics.png`

Acceptance:

- We can point to a generated world and explain which components are valid
  island arcs or mountain belts and which are invalid continental ribbons.
- Current Earth-like output fails visual/geological plausibility on morphology,
  but now fails measurably rather than only by eye.

### P1. Compact Craton And Continent Initialization

Status: complete as initialization-layer improvement; final exposed-land
morphology still needs P3/P4/P5

Purpose:

- Stop seeding the simulation with pathological land shapes.
- Represent proto-continents as compact cratonic kernels plus irregular but
  broad margins, not random graph tendrils.

Implementation outline:

- Replace or wrap `_grow_continents(...)` with a compact-growth algorithm:
  - select craton nuclei with minimum angular separation
  - grow each nucleus using a cost function
  - favor cells close to object center or existing interior
  - penalize high perimeter gain
  - penalize width below a minimum local threshold
  - add low-frequency spherical noise for natural margins
  - allow limited peninsulas and embayments
- Separate early craton cores from younger continental margins:
  - craton core: old, thick, stable, low strain
  - margin: younger, thinner, deformable
- Let interior heat affect early crust style:
  - hotter early planets can have more fragmented proto-continents
  - mature cooler planets can preserve larger stable cratons

Tests:

- Earth-like preset land fraction remains within configured target.
- Compactness improves relative to current baseline across at least three
  deterministic seeds.
- Craton cells are older and thicker than continental margins.
- No increase in plate topology errors.

Assets:

- `craton_id.png`
- `continent_id.png`
- `crust_domain.png`
- `continent_width.png`

Acceptance:

- Initial continents are broad enough to support continental interiors.
- Long strips may exist as arcs or rifts, but not as the dominant mainland
  geometry.
- In the full Earth-like run, continental crust now has a much larger coherent
  body and wider interiors, but exposed land remains too chain-like because
  later conservation, boundary processes, and terrain exposure still operate too
  locally.

### P2. Continent, Craton, And Terrane Objects

Status: complete as current object/field baseline; explicit split/merge event
history remains future P6/P7 work

Purpose:

- Let tectonic evolution reason about geological entities, not just cells.
- Preserve object identity through time for archive continuity and causal
  explanation.

Implementation outline:

- Create continent objects after initialization and update them after each
  tectonic step.
- Assign stable ids using overlap with previous-step objects.
- Track continent splits and merges:
  - split: one parent, multiple children
  - merger: multiple parents, one child
  - suturing: two continents join through an orogen
  - rifting: one continent develops a rift or narrow ocean
- Add terrane objects for island arcs, microcontinents, and accreted fragments.
- Keep object history in events:
  - `continent_birth`
  - `continent_split`
  - `continent_merge`
  - `terrane_accretion`
  - `suture_formed`
  - `rift_opened`

Tests:

- Object ids persist when masks move slightly.
- Split and merge fixtures produce expected events.
- Object area is close to sum of member cells.
- Events contain parent/child ids and time.

Assets:

- `tectonic_objects.png`
- `crust_domain.png`
- `tectonic_continent_id.png`
- `terrane_id.png`

Acceptance:

- A rendered cell can explain whether it belongs to an old craton, margin,
  accreted terrane, arc, or suture.
- Current implementation provides `crust.domain`, stable numeric
  `tectonics.continent_id`, `tectonics.terrane_id`, and object sets
  `tectonics.continents` and `tectonics.terranes`.  Full split/merge event
  storytelling should be added with the Wilson-cycle event layer.

### P3. Shape-Aware Continental Conservation

Status: complete as conservation-layer improvement; remaining exposed-land
ribboning is now mainly P4/P5

Purpose:

- Preserve continental area without growing unrealistic ribbons.
- Keep conservation as a geological process, not a blind border fill/erase.

Implementation outline:

- Replace pure border-cell ranking in `_conserve_continental(...)` with a
  shape-aware candidate score.
- Candidate accretion should favor:
  - cells adjacent to broad continental margins
  - cells near existing accretionary wedges or sutures
  - cells that increase local width
  - cells that reduce holes or smooth coastlines
  - cells supported by terrane objects
- Candidate accretion should penalize:
  - one-cell bridges
  - long thin peninsulas
  - isolated border pixels
  - converting a narrow arc directly into mainland without accretion history
  - making coastline complexity much worse
- Candidate erosion should favor:
  - narrow necks and unstable slivers
  - low-thickness young margin cells
  - cells without craton or terrane support
  - cells that reduce ribbon index
- Add guardrails:
  - never erode craton cores except under explicit extreme events
  - do not allow single-step continental area jumps beyond configured limits
  - prefer object-level accretion events over silent relabeling

Tests:

- Area conservation remains within target tolerance.
- Ribbon fraction decreases or does not increase across benchmark seeds.
- Craton preservation remains high.
- Continental mask frame-to-frame jump remains below archive threshold.

Assets:

- `accretion_candidates.png`
- `erosion_candidates.png`
- `continent_conservation_delta.png`
- `ribbon_index_before_after.png`

Acceptance:

- Conservation changes make continents more coherent over time instead of
  adding ragged cell strings along every boundary.
- Current implementation protects craton cores, favors supported accretion
  into broad margins, erodes unstable narrow slivers first, and performs a
  small balanced shape-maintenance pass.  It improves continental crust
  morphology substantially, but final exposed land still needs terrain and
  boundary-process regionalization.

### P4. Boundary Process Decoupling

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Keep linear tectonic processes linear without always turning them into
  linear continents.
- Distinguish crust creation, deformation, and exposure.

Implementation outline:

- Split boundary outputs into process classes:
  - ocean ridge: creates young oceanic crust and bathymetric ridge
  - trench: consumes old oceanic crust and creates deep bathymetry
  - subduction arc: volcanic/high relief, usually island arc or active margin
  - accretionary wedge: possible terrane object, not automatic continent
  - continent collision: broad orogen and crustal thickening
  - suture: old collision line inside continent
  - transform: shear/valley/scarp, limited crust conversion
  - rift: thinning and basin formation before ocean opening
- Modify crust-type updates:
  - subduction arcs remain `island_arc` until attached and sutured
  - oceanic plateaus/LIPs can become terranes, but only after collision or
    accretion
  - continent-continent collision broadens orogens instead of painting a
    one-cell line of continent
  - passive margins widen shelves but do not create new land by themselves
- Add boundary process objects with age and intensity.

Tests:

- Ridge cells produce seafloor-age gradients, not continental ribbons.
- Trench cells recycle old oceanic crust.
- Island arcs can be exposed but remain separate from mainland until accreted.
- Collision between two continental objects creates a suture and a broad
  thickened zone.

Assets:

- `boundary_processes.png`
- `arc_vs_mainland.png`
- `suture_orogen_id.png`
- `rift_passive_margin.png`

Acceptance:

- Linear features still appear where geology demands them, but the map can tell
  whether they are arcs, sutures, ridges, or true mainland.

### P5. Terrain Province Regionalization

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Convert crust and boundary history into broad terrain provinces.
- Reduce line-like mountain chains and checkerboard land/sea artifacts in
  `elevation.png` and compiled `hexmap`.

Implementation outline:

- Add terrain province classification:
  - cratonic lowland/shield
  - continental platform
  - rift basin
  - passive-margin shelf
  - active-margin arc
  - collision orogen
  - high plateau
  - island arc
  - ocean ridge
  - abyssal plain
  - trench
  - oceanic plateau/seamount chain
- Replace direct single-cell boundary relief with province-scale kernels:
  - collision relief spreads inland and decays by distance and erosion age
  - arcs are narrow but broken into island chains unless attached to continent
  - passive margins create shelves and coastal plains, not deep troughs next
    to every coast
  - trenches remain oceanic and offshore
- Strengthen post-sea-level exposure rules:
  - suppress high isolated oceanic pixels unless they are valid islands/arcs
  - merge tiny coastal artifacts into shelves or nearby land
  - break one-cell land bridges unless supported by continent object history
  - ensure nearshore bathymetry usually transitions shelf -> slope -> abyssal
    plain, with trenches as tectonically justified exceptions

Tests:

- `hexmap` land/sea agrees with `terrain.elevation` sign after resampling.
- Nearshore ocean is usually shallower than far abyssal plains, except at
  subduction trenches.
- Long one-cell mountain ridges are reduced.
- Collision orogens remain visible and causal.
- Existing hydrology still has acyclic seaward drainage.

Assets:

- `terrain_provinces.png`
- `elevation.png`
- `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`
- `hexmap.png`
- `orogen_width.png`

Acceptance:

- The terrain map reads as provinces and basins, not per-cell responses to
  boundary masks.

### P6. Wilson-Cycle Event Layer

Status: implemented

Purpose:

- Make plate evolution explainable over geologic time.
- Support climate and biosphere with ocean-gateway and supercontinent history.

Implementation outline:

- Add explicit Wilson-cycle phases:
  - intracontinental rift
  - narrow sea
  - widening ocean
  - mature ocean basin
  - subduction initiation
  - closing ocean
  - arc-continent collision
  - continent-continent collision
  - suture and post-orogenic collapse
- Create events:
  - `ridge_birth`
  - `trench_birth`
  - `rift_birth`
  - `passive_margin_birth`
  - `arc_birth`
  - `terrane_accretion`
  - `suture_formed`
  - `orogen_built`
  - `plume_event`
  - `LIP_emplaced`
  - `cratonization`
  - `ocean_gateway_opened`
  - `ocean_gateway_closed`
- Let events feed:
  - crust age
  - crust domain
  - terrain provinces
  - resource genesis
  - climate ocean basin and gateway diagnostics
  - biosphere dispersal barriers

Tests:

- Event objects are temporally ordered and reference valid object ids.
- Ridge/trench/suture/passive-margin events appear in plausible process
  sequences.
- Crust age updates agree with ridge and recycling events.
- Climate geography can consume ocean gateway events without needing to infer
  everything from raw masks.

Assets:

- `timeline.png`
- `history.png`
- `wilson_cycle_phase.png`
- `ocean_gateways.png`

Acceptance:

- The simulator can answer "why is this mountain belt here?" and "which ocean
  opened or closed here?" with event references.

### P7. Archive Continuity And Calibration

Status: implemented as a baseline; calibration continues in P8/P12

Purpose:

- Re-tune continuity metrics after object-level tectonics changes.
- Avoid overfitting to one seed or one preset.

Implementation outline:

- Revisit existing `tectonics.archive_continuity`.
- Track continuity separately for:
  - plate labels
  - continent ids
  - craton ids
  - terrane ids
  - crust domain
  - exposed land
  - terrain provinces
- Benchmark multiple presets:
  - Earth-like
  - young hot planet
  - old cold planet
  - water world
  - arid/supercontinent world
  - archipelago world
- Compare metrics across seeds and snapshots.

Tests:

- No sudden global redraws unless a configured catastrophe or regime shift
  occurs.
- Plate fragmentation stays bounded.
- Continental area stays conserved within tolerance.
- Crust-type jump metrics remain below threshold.
- Regression assets remain deterministic by seed.

Assets:

- `archive_continuity.png`
- `plate_fragmentation.png`
- `crust_type_jump.png`
- `multi_seed_contact_sheet.png`

Acceptance:

- The archive metrics are strict enough to catch redraw-like bugs but loose
  enough to allow real rifting, collision, and accretion events.

### P8. Multi-Seed Morphology Calibration

Status: implemented as morphology/exposure calibration; residual component and
supercontinent risks remain for P9/P12 review

Purpose:

- Reduce remaining ribbon-like exposed land without returning to scattered
  island-chain continents.
- Avoid overfitting to seed 42 by calibrating on multiple Earth-like seeds and
  at least one supercontinent-like preset.
- Separate valid linear geology from invalid continental morphology.

Implementation outline:

- Build a repeatable calibration harness for seeds such as `42`, `43`, `44`,
  `20260621`, and one intentionally awkward supercontinent/arid seed.
- Tune continent initialization and later conservation together:
  - discourage one-cell bridges and long isthmuses unless backed by suture,
    rift, or volcanic-arc history;
  - keep broad craton/platform interiors exposed;
  - allow several continents rather than collapsing most land into one
    supercontinent;
  - preserve detached island arcs as islands or submerged arcs instead of
    mainland ribbons.
- Add morphology target bands for the Earth-like preset:
  - exposed land fraction remains near `0.26` to `0.31`;
  - exposed land components usually stay in a small Earth-like range rather than
    dozens of strips or one all-consuming landmass;
  - largest land component should not dominate every calibrated seed;
  - exposed-land ribbon fraction should fall materially below the P6/P7
    multi-seed mean of `0.511`;
  - continental-crust ribbon fraction should continue improving without
    sacrificing old cratons.
- Keep climate-facing geography in view:
  - preserve at least one large connected ocean basin;
  - avoid fully closing all low-latitude ocean passages;
  - keep gateway objects meaningful for ocean circulation.

Tests:

- Multi-seed Earth-like morphology regression writes a summary JSON and fails on
  extreme ribbon/supercontinent regressions.
- Single-seed deterministic regression still produces identical metrics and
  assets for a fixed seed.
- Archive continuity and continental conservation remain within P7 thresholds.
- Existing Earth-like plausibility tests remain green.

Assets:

- `p8_multiseed_contact_sheet.png`
- `p8_morphology_summary.json`
- `ribbon_index.png`
- `continent_width.png`
- `morphology_diagnostics.png`
- `elevation.png`
- `hexmap.png`

Acceptance:

- Several Earth-like seeds produce broad continental interiors, plausible
  coastlines, and explainable arcs/sutures, with ribbon fraction reduced enough
  that manual review no longer flags strip continents as the first-order issue.

### P9. Ocean Basin And Margin Object Layer

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Turn ocean geography into explicit basin, margin, shelf, slope, abyss, ridge,
  trench, and gateway objects that terrain and climate can consume directly.
- Fix the remaining "nearshore too deep, far ocean too shallow" risk at the
  object/province level instead of with local elevation patches.

Implementation outline:

- Add or strengthen fields:
  - `ocean.basin_id`
  - `ocean.margin_type`
  - `ocean.depth_province`
  - `ocean.gateway_id`
  - `ocean.shelf_width`
- Derive ocean basins from the solved ocean mask on the spherical graph, then
  annotate them with ridge age, passive margins, active margins, trenches, and
  gateway restrictions.
- Generate bathymetry by province:
  - passive continental margins: shelf -> slope -> rise -> abyssal plain;
  - active margins: narrower shelves and localized offshore trenches;
  - ridges: shallow mid-ocean highs with young crust;
  - old abyssal plains: deeper and broader away from ridges;
  - seamounts and oceanic plateaus: local exceptions, not global background.
- Export gateway and basin objects in a form climate C4 can consume without
  re-inferring circulation from raw land/sea masks.

Tests:

- Nearshore water is generally shallower than same-basin abyssal water, except
  at active-margin trench cells.
- Trenches occur near active margins/subduction objects, not as generic coastal
  moats.
- Ocean basin ids are valid, connected on the sphere, and stable enough across
  archive frames.
- Ocean gateways reference adjacent basin ids and valid Wilson-cycle context.

Assets:

- `ocean_basins.png`
- `ocean_depth_provinces.png`
- `continental_margins.png`
- `ocean_gateways.png`
- `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`

Acceptance:

- Bathymetry reads as shelves, slopes, abyssal plains, ridges, and trenches.
  Climate can distinguish basin-connected ocean, restricted seas, and gateways.

### P10. Spherical Plate And Continent Reorganization Audit

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Make sure the post-P8 morphology improvements are caused by spherical
  tectonic logic, not hidden planar artifacts.
- Audit antimeridian, polar, and wraparound behavior in plate motion, continent
  components, ocean basins, and rendered assets.

Implementation outline:

- Add seam-crossing fixtures and regression seeds where continents, ridges, or
  ocean basins cross longitude `+/-180`.
- Check object connectivity on the spherical neighbor graph rather than on
  rendered rectangular images.
- Review local plate reorganization:
  - plate split/merge events should not create planar-map edge artifacts;
  - continent and terrane ids should survive wraparound movement;
  - boundary objects should be continuous across the map seam.
- Add diagnostics for seam-discontinuity:
  - plate id mismatch along antimeridian neighbors;
  - land/ocean and elevation jumps at paired seam cells;
  - ocean basin ids split only by projection, not by topology.

Tests:

- Artificial seam-crossing masks remain connected on the spherical graph.
- Rendered antimeridian edges agree for plates, crust type, elevation, and
  basin ids.
- Plate reorganization preserves continuity metrics when objects cross the map
  seam.

Assets:

- `seam_continuity.png`
- `plates.png`
- `tectonic_objects.png`
- `ocean_basins.png`
- `elevation.png`

Acceptance:

- No major geological object appears broken only because the map projection has
  a left/right edge.

### P11. Terrain And Compiler Co-Calibration

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Align `elevation.png`, climate geography, and compiled `hexmap.png` so the
  presentation layer does not contradict the physical layers.
- Finish suppressing residual checkerboard, strip-mountain, and coastal
  generalization artifacts after P8/P9.

Implementation outline:

- Treat terrain provinces and ocean depth provinces as compiler inputs, not just
  colors derived from per-cell elevation.
- Calibrate hex aggregation rules:
  - land/sea follows elevation sign and province membership;
  - shelves and coast cells remain visually distinct from deep ocean;
  - mountains, plateaus, lowlands, deserts, and biomes resolve at compatible
    scales;
  - isolated single-cell highs are generalized only when the source province
    says they are not valid islands/arcs.
- Revisit elevation color ramps and hex terrain palettes together so lowlands,
  highlands, bathymetry, and biomes remain readable but not misleading.

Tests:

- `hexmap` land/sea and major elevation classes agree with source elevation and
  province fields within a fixed tolerance.
- Compiler does not convert broad shelves into deep ocean or broad lowlands into
  mountain belts.
- Visual smoke tests render the core assets for Earth-like and waterworld
  presets.

Assets:

- `elevation.png`
- `hexmap.png`
- `terrain_provinces.png`
- `ocean_depth_provinces.png`
- `compiler_consistency.png`

Acceptance:

- Manual review can compare `elevation.png` and `hexmap.png` without seeing
  mismatched coastlines, missing land, or misleading elevation classes.

### P12. Cross-Preset Tectonics Release Gate

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Define the "good enough for climate/resource/biosphere work" gate for the
  tectonics layer.
- Preserve regressions as assets and metrics before shifting focus back to C4
  climate coupling.

Implementation outline:

- Run a canonical preset matrix:
  - Earth-like;
  - waterworld with some emergent islands where configured;
  - arid/supercontinent;
  - young hot planet;
  - old cold planet;
  - archipelago or high-ocean-world variant.
- Summarize by seed and preset:
  - land fraction and component distribution;
  - ribbon fraction and coastline complexity;
  - old craton preservation;
  - ocean-basin/gateway count;
  - nearshore bathymetry sanity;
  - archive continuity;
  - climate-facing ocean heat transport prerequisites.
- Update `docs/CLIMATE_SYSTEM_PLAN.md` only if P9/P11 change the promised
  geography contract consumed by C4.

Tests:

- Full test suite.
- Canonical asset-generation smoke test.
- Preset-matrix diagnostic script produces a stable JSON summary.

Assets:

- `p12_preset_matrix_contact_sheet.png`
- `p12_tectonics_release_summary.json`
- `archive_continuity.png`
- `multi_seed_contact_sheet.png`

Acceptance:

- The tectonics layer provides stable, explainable geography for the next
  climate phase. Remaining imperfections are documented and bounded by tests or
  diagnostics.

### P13. Continental Detail, Archipelago Cleanup, And Hypsometry Tail

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Add readable continental interior detail after P12 made the hard tectonic
  gates stable.
- Reduce over-fragmented island-chain exposure in the reference Earth-like map.
- Keep Earth-like mean land elevation plausible while adding a realistic high
  elevation tail for mountain belts and plateaus.

Implementation outline:

- Add a continental detail field derived from crust domain, crust age,
  reworking age, boundary history, stability, and continental width:
  - ocean;
  - craton shield;
  - platform/interior lowland;
  - sedimentary or interior basin;
  - rift/passive-margin basin;
  - old or active orogen;
  - plateau/LIP highland;
  - island arc or microcontinent.
- Strengthen terrain response to collision, suture, plateau, LIP, and old
  orogenic provinces without returning to one-cell strip mountains.
- Let final exposure cleanup run even when component count is below the P12
  hard threshold if the remaining small components are unsupported island
  chains.
- Recalibrate hypsometry targets:
  - land mean should remain broadly Earth-like;
  - p90/p95/max should allow highlands, plateaus, and orogens;
  - high oceanic land should remain rare;
  - compiler land/sea/elevation consistency must remain clean.

Tests:

- Earth-like regression includes a nonzero highland/mountain province area.
- Earth-like land p95/max elevation is not capped near low hills.
- Exposed oceanic highland remains rare.
- P12 release gate remains free of hard failures.
- Full test suite.

Assets:

- `continental_detail_provinces.png`
- `terrain_provinces.png`
- `elevation.png`
- `morphology_diagnostics.png`
- `p12_preset_matrix_contact_sheet.png`

Acceptance:

- The reference Earth-like map reads as broad continents with internal shields,
  basins, highlands, and orogens, not flat colored blobs.
- The central archipelago reads as fewer coherent island-arc/microcontinent
  provinces instead of many arbitrary speckles.
- Remaining coastline/ribbon warnings are documented and do not reintroduce
  P12 hard failures.

Implementation notes:

- Added `terrain.continental_detail`, a P13 continental detail province field
  with shield, platform, basin/rift, orogen, plateau, and arc/microcontinent
  classes.
- Tuned terrain uplift so broad continental interiors gain visible basins,
  highlands, orogens, and plateau tails without returning to one-cell strip
  mountains.
- Added a final unsupported-land-component cleanup pass that can remove small
  exposed island-chain components after bridge cleanup, while preserving the
  Earth-like land-fraction envelope.
- Updated the map compiler to sample `terrain.continental_detail`, use it for
  hills/mountain classification, prune tiny playable land components in
  land-rich worlds, and avoid placing starts on unsupported small islands.
- Added P13 terrain-detail and hypsometry metrics to the P12 release summary.

### P14. Modern-Fractured Earth-Like Seaway Calibration

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Reduce the reference Earth-like output's remaining supercontinent-like
  largest-landmass dominance without destabilizing the generic deep-time
  tectonics model.
- Convert geologically weak late-stage land bridges into shallow seaways or
  restricted seas where the current final exposure mask over-connects broad
  continents.
- Lower exposed-land ribbon/coastline warnings enough for climate C4 to test
  monsoon, ocean-current, and gateway logic on a more modern-fragmented
  Earth-like layout.

Design constraints:

- P14 is a late terrain/exposure calibration, not a plate-history rewrite.
- It should apply only to mature Earth-like mobile-lid worlds:
  - target land fraction roughly `0.20` to `0.38`;
  - late time, after major tectonic history has accumulated;
  - not waterworld, arid supercontinent, stagnant-lid, frozen, or tidally
    locked presets.
- It must not cut craton cores or broad stable continental interiors.
- It should prefer weak corridors:
  - low relief above sea level;
  - narrow exposed-land width;
  - continental margin, suture, terrane, or accreted-arc domains;
  - cells whose removal separates a large component into substantial pieces;
  - corridors adjacent to existing ocean, restricted seas, or gateways.
- Any new seaway should become shallow/restricted ocean, not an arbitrary
  deep trench.
- Land fraction should remain inside the broad Earth analogue envelope.

Implementation outline:

- Add a final Earth-like-only seaway pass after P13 unsupported-island cleanup
  and before final ocean geography is derived.
- Detect over-dominant largest land components.
- Search the largest component for low-cost corridor candidates:
  - articulation cells and narrow-neck cells;
  - width-limited cells in low-relief margins, sutures, terranes, or unstable
    non-craton zones;
  - optionally a one-ring dilation when a single-cell cut is too thin for the
    rendered/compiled map.
- Score candidate corridors by:
  - how much they reduce largest component dominance;
  - whether both resulting sides are substantial landmasses;
  - low elevation and weak continental support;
  - low craton/interior fraction;
  - minimal area loss.
- Drown only the best bounded corridor set, with a small area budget.
- Optionally pay back a small amount of land by exposing broad interior
  continental cells if land fraction undershoots.
- Emit diagnostics into P12 release summaries:
  - largest-land component fraction;
  - seaway-cut candidate/action counts if exposed;
  - post-cut land fraction and component count.

Tests:

- Earth-like regression should keep hard gates green while reducing largest
  component dominance relative to P13.
- `elevation.png`/`hexmap.png` sign consistency remains clean.
- Nearshore bathymetry remains shelf-like; new seaways should not become
  superdeep coastal moats.
- Craton preservation and exposed continental fraction stay within P12/P13
  bounds.
- Cross-preset smoke must confirm low-land and non-Earth-like presets are not
  damaged by the P14 pass.

Assets:

- `morphology_diagnostics.png`
- `continent_components.png`
- `ocean_gateways.png`
- `ocean_depth_provinces.png`
- `elevation.png`
- `hexmap.png`
- `p12_tectonics_release_summary.json`

Acceptance:

- The reference Earth-like map no longer reads as one near-continuous
  supercontinent when the largest component is connected mainly by weak late
  exposure corridors.
- New separations look like plausible shallow seas or straits rather than
  random cell deletion.
- Any remaining supercontinent/ribbon warning is explicitly documented as a
  next-step tectonic-object limitation, not a terrain/compiler contradiction.

Implementation notes:

- Added an Earth-like-only late seaway pass after P13 unsupported-island
  cleanup and before final ocean geography.
- The pass searches low-cost coast-to-coast paths and antimeridian seaway
  corridors in the largest land component, then only applies a candidate if it
  actually reduces largest-component dominance.
- Added guarded land payback around non-largest landmasses, with a constraint
  that payback cells cannot touch the current largest component, so the new
  seaway is not immediately closed again.
- The implementation deliberately prioritizes reducing supercontinent-like
  largest-landmass dominance.  It does not solve coastline/ribbon complexity;
  that is now P15 scope.

### P15. Coastline And Ribbon Smoothing Over Final Land Objects

Status: implemented 2026-06-22

Purpose:

- Reduce the warning-level coastline complexity, narrow-neck count, and ribbon
  fraction introduced or exposed by P14 seaway opening.
- Keep the P14 seaway topology: the largest landmass should not reconnect into
  a supercontinent.
- Make the Earth-like rendered output read more like broad continents with
  realistic shelves and straits, not sawtooth coastlines and graph artifacts.

Design constraints:

- P15 is a final exposure/terrain smoothing pass.  It should not rewrite plate
  history, crust domains, or object ids.
- It should apply only to mature Earth-like mobile-lid worlds.
- It must preserve:
  - land fraction inside the broad Earth analogue envelope;
  - P14 largest-landmass reduction;
  - nearshore shelf depth sanity;
  - compiler/elevation consistency;
  - high oceanic land suppression.
- It should not fill narrow seaways or gateways that P14 just opened.
- It should not erode craton cores or broad stable interiors.

Implementation outline:

- Add a final P15 pass after P14 seaway opening and before final ocean-floor
  regionalization.
- Compute current land components, largest-component mask, local land width,
  land-neighbor count, ocean-neighbor count, craton/interior support, and
  relative elevation.
- Drown small low-support coastal protrusions:
  - non-core land;
  - low land-neighbor count;
  - narrow exposed width;
  - low relief or weak margin/terrane/suture support;
  - not part of the broad interior of either the largest landmass or the main
    secondary continents.
- Fill only true embayments:
  - ocean cells surrounded by land on most neighbours;
  - preferably continental crust or shallow margin;
  - not adjacent to both sides of a P14 seaway;
  - not in deep trench/ridge cells.
- Keep the operation area-budgeted and paired so land fraction stays stable.
- Re-run ocean-floor regionalization after smoothing so new water cells become
  shelves/restricted seas rather than arbitrary deep patches.
- Apply a final open-ocean shoal clamp after sea level is fixed so remote ridge
  highs no longer render as quasi-emergent island chains.

Tests:

- Earth-like regression keeps largest land component below the P14 threshold.
- Component count stays bounded.
- Compiler consistency remains clean.
- Nearshore superdeep fraction remains near zero.
- P15 should improve at least one of:
  - coastline complexity;
  - narrow-neck count;
  - ribbon fraction;
  without creating new hard failures.

Assets:

- `morphology_diagnostics.png`
- `coastline_complexity.png`
- `narrow_necks.png`
- `ribbon_index.png`
- `elevation.png`
- `hexmap.png`
- `ocean_depth_provinces.png`

Acceptance:

- P15 does not undo P14's main result: no supercontinent-like largest landmass
  warning.
- The map's remaining coastline roughness is visually less dominated by
  one-cell teeth and tiny graph artifacts.
- Any remaining ribbon/coastline warning is documented as requiring deeper
  continent/margin object redesign, not a compiler or bathymetry bug.
- The 2026-06-22 implementation meets the hard gates and removes the
  component-count and supercontinent warnings.  Ribbon, narrow-neck, and
  coastline-complexity warnings remain.

### P16. Upstream Object Redesign And High-Resolution Readiness

Status: active; initial plateau, high-resolution planning, resolution profile,
continental shape maintenance, and open-ocean basin partition implemented
2026-06-23

Purpose:

- Move remaining P14/P15 coastline, ribbon, seaway, and component repairs into
  upstream continent, margin, rift, craton, platform, suture, and old-orogen
  object responses.
- Make broad continental interiors, collision plateaus, foreland basins,
  passive margins, deltas/fans, island arcs, narrow seaways, and small mountain
  systems visible for the right process reasons.
- Establish a resolution ladder so small-scale real-Earth landforms are not
  judged only at the 2500-8000 cell smoke scale.

Resolution ladder:

- `900-2500` cells: CI and microbenchmark scale.  Use this for deterministic
  process tests, release-gate smoke, and fast failure localization.  Do not
  require tiny landforms such as small deltas, narrow isthmuses, or short
  mountain chains to be visually complete here.
- `8000` cells: standard Earth-like regression scale.  Use this for routine
  map review, morphology trends, major continent/ocean topology, shelves,
  trenches, ridge networks, and broad continental landforms.
- `24000` cells: medium deployment review.  Use this after a P16 object change
  to check whether coastlines, island arcs, seaways, deltas/fans, and mountain
  segmentation improve without relying on final cleanup.
- `72000` cells: occasional high-resolution deployment review.  Use this only
  for manual/offline checks of small-scale geomorphology and scaling behavior:
  isthmuses, straits, small mountain systems, delta/fan geometry, island-arc
  spacing, narrow shelves, and compact marginal seas.
- Use `python -m aevum.cli profile-resolution --cells 900 2500 ...` as the
  standard preflight before any medium/high-resolution audit.  The command
  writes `resolution_profile_summary.json` with stage timings, scheduler/module
  run counts, compiler metrics, morphology metrics, and optional geomorphology
  coverage/rendering.  It also records the current NumPy/SciPy reference
  backend, optional acceleration packages visible in the environment, and rough
  8000/24000/72000 cell time projections from the largest measured profile
  entry.

High-resolution policy:

- High-resolution runs are evidence for visual and scaling quality, not a
  substitute for process microbenchmarks.
- A small-scale feature can be marked "resolution-limited" only if its parent
  tectonic/fluvial/margin object exists and the feature appears at `24000` or
  `72000` cells.
- A `72000`-cell review should be an explicit audit packet, not an ad hoc map:
  run the same preset/seed at `8000`, `24000`, and `72000`; archive the
  profile summary, P12/release metrics when feasible, contact sheets, and the
  object provenance for every claimed small feature.  The useful question is
  whether already-modeled isthmuses, deltas/fans, straits, short mountain
  chains, narrow shelves, and island arcs sharpen with resolution.
- Start each `72000` audit with core generation plus diagnostics.  Add map
  compilation and rendering only after generation timing and memory look
  acceptable; this keeps a slow renderer from being mistaken for a slow
  geological model.
- P16 acceptance should not depend on a single `72000` cell seed; run at least
  one Earth-like reference and one deliberately awkward morphology seed once the
  command is fast enough.
- Store high-resolution summaries and contact sheets separately from normal
  P12 artifacts, for example under `out_p16_highres_YYYYMMDD/`.

Performance and acceleration plan:

- First profile the current NumPy/SciPy CPU path by module and operation class:
  plate rasterization `cKDTree.query`, repeated graph connectivity/width
  passes, terrain smoothing, priority-flood hydrology, map compilation, and
  rendering.
- Near-term CPU work:
  - cache graph degree arrays and common edge/neighbour reductions;
  - reuse width/component results within a terrain step instead of recomputing
    them for every cleanup/classification pass;
  - replace repeated Python neighbour loops with edge-array reductions or
    sparse-graph connected-component helpers where the semantics match;
  - add optional no-render/no-compile profile modes for high-resolution runs.
- Medium-term acceleration candidates:
  - Numba for graph BFS, width, connected components, articulation, and
    priority-flood-like loops;
  - CuPy/JAX only behind a backend boundary for dense array kernels such as
    smoothing and field algebra;
  - keep SciPy `cKDTree` as the reference plate-rasterization path until a
    benchmark proves a replacement.
- Acceleration should be gated by a `72000` profile trace: graph-heavy stages
  should be optimized with cached edge reductions or optional Numba first;
  dense field algebra should only move to CuPy/JAX after a backend-boundary
  spike proves numerical parity and end-to-end runtime benefit.  Do not add a
  GPU path merely to make one large run possible.
- Do not add a GPU dependency to the default install until a benchmark shows
  clear benefit and CPU parity tests pass.
- Treat 72000-cell runs as offline audits.  The normal path should be:
  900/2500 mechanism preflight, 8000 routine review, 24000 medium deployment
  review, then 72000 only when the projected runtime and memory are acceptable.
  Use 72000-cell runs specifically for scale-sensitive features such as
  isthmuses, deltas/fans, short mountain systems, narrow shelves, straits, and
  island-arc spacing after their parent process objects already exist at lower
  resolution; higher resolution should never stand in for missing tectonic,
  margin, fluvial, or sediment objects.

External reference anchors for the next object-model phases:

- GPlates and pyGPlates model plate motion with finite rotations, plate
  circuits, topological features, and resolved topologies.  See
  `https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/` and
  `https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/generated/pygplates.resolve_topologies`.
  Aevum should not try to become GPlates, but P20+ should move closer to that
  data model: persistent plate polygons/networks first, raster fields second.
- EarthByte GPlates data bundles provide a practical example of packaging
  reconstruction data with citations and provenance.  Aevum generated objects
  should keep comparable parent-process and source metadata.
- NOAA ETOPO 2022 is the real-Earth topography/bathymetry baseline for future
  hypsometry, shelf/slope/abyss, mountain-belt, and coastline complexity
  comparison.  See `https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/etopo-global-relief-model`
  and DOI `10.25921/fd45-gt74`.  High-resolution Aevum reviews should compare
  against ETOPO-derived feature distributions, not only subjective map
  screenshots.

P16 acceptance:

- The 2500/8000 cell Earth-like run keeps P12 hard gates green and does not
  regress parent-object coverage.
- Generated-world `plateau` becomes world-visible only through broad thickened
  crust, LIP uplift, or collision-orogen plateau envelopes, not by relabeling
  one-cell mountain lines.
- Ribbon/coastline warnings improve because continent and margin objects become
  broader or better separated upstream, not because the final renderer hides
  errors.
- A 24000-cell review run is available before P16 is considered complete; a
  72000-cell run is an optional deployment-scale audit once performance work is
  in place.
- The `profile-resolution` output for the chosen audit settings is archived
  beside the generated maps so scaling regressions can be compared across P16
  iterations.

## Cross-Module Contracts

The plate system must provide stable inputs to downstream systems.

Terrain consumes:

- crust type/domain
- crust age
- crust thickness/density
- uplift/subsidence
- boundary process objects
- craton/orogen/terrane ids
- sea-level and isostatic context

Climate consumes:

- exposed land and ocean masks
- continent ids and interiority
- ocean basin ids and gateways
- shelf/slope/deep-ocean bathymetry
- mountain barrier belts and wind gaps
- large plateaus and ice-prone highlands

Hydrology consumes:

- regional terrain provinces
- elevation and basin boundaries
- rift basins and interior basins
- mountain belts and plateaus

Resources consume:

- subduction arcs
- porphyry belts
- ophiolites
- LIPs
- sedimentary basins
- passive margins
- cratons and shields
- metamorphic/orogenic belts

Compiler consumes:

- land/sea mask consistent with elevation
- terrain province
- climate and biome fields
- coastline and shelf type
- mountain passes
- river basins
- resource provinces

## Test Plan Summary

Unit tests:

- morphology metrics on artificial masks
- continent id overlap matching
- terrane object lifecycle
- candidate scoring for conservation
- boundary process classification fixtures
- terrain province classification fixtures

Integration tests:

- fixed Earth-like seed through full pipeline
- multiple world presets through full pipeline
- deterministic asset-generation smoke test
- object/event references remain valid
- no invalid ids in fields
- terrain/hex land-sea agreement

Regression metrics:

- continental fraction
- old-crust preservation
- oceanic age distribution
- ribbon land fraction
- exposed high-oceanic fraction
- nearshore bathymetry sanity
- plate fragmentation
- crust-type jump
- archive continuity

Manual visual review:

- `plates.png`
- `crust_age.png`
- `crust_domain.png`
- `continent_width.png`
- `ribbon_index.png`
- `boundary_processes.png`
- `terrain_provinces.png`
- `elevation.png`
- `hexmap.png`
- `history.png`
- `timeline.png`

## Acceptance Criteria For The Next Major Tectonics Milestone

Earth-like generated worlds should satisfy all of the following:

- Continental crust contains broad interiors and preserved old cratons.
- Long linear landforms are mostly explainable as arcs, sutures, ridges,
  rifts, or narrow island chains, not as accidental mainland.
- Continental conservation does not create or preserve many one-cell-wide
  mainland bridges.
- Oceanic crust age is young near ridges and old near trenches.
- Passive margins produce shelves and coastal plains.
- Trenches are offshore and localized by subduction, not generic deep moats
  around continents.
- Collision zones produce broad orogens or plateaus where appropriate.
- `elevation.png` and compiled `hexmap.png` agree on land, sea, and major
  elevation classes.
- Plate, continent, and crust-domain continuity remain plausible through time.
- Events can explain major terrain and crust features.

## Near-Term Recommended Work Order

Completed sequence: P0 through P15, R0 through R6, initial R8
`earth_geomorphology_coverage`, R8 generated landform parent-object linkage,
R8 generated ocean-fabric transform/fracture-zone expression, R8 generated
continental basin/foreland expression, R8 generated collision/LIP plateau
expression, E1, E2, E3, E4, and E5 are implemented and archived in the
development log below.

Next sequence:

1. Keep all non-ice world features generated-world visible; eventually promote
   cryosphere features once climate/ice work resumes.
2. Continue tightening generated-world parent-object causality toward the
   long-term zero-parentless target.  The current R8 gate holds
   `parentless_major_landform_fraction < 0.10`.
3. Migrate remaining late P14/P15 coastline, ribbon, and seaway cleanup into
   continent, margin, rift, platform, old-orogen, and ocean-basin object
   responses.
4. Continue reducing random/heuristic major-event generation under the R0-R8
   refactor plan, especially plume/hotspot tracks and plate split triggers.
5. Keep climate, ocean-current, and monsoon redesign paused except for
   regression fixes; the E5 implementation uses existing cryosphere fields or
   deterministic fixtures rather than redesigning climate.
6. Decide whether the ocean-depth-province renderer needs object-level ridge
   and fracture-zone belts rather than dotted boundary-cell provinces.

The next immediate engineering task is upstream repair of macro land/sea shape
and high-resolution readiness for small-scale geomorphology review.  P15 remains
a stable generated-world fixture, R0-R6 provide the
tectonic refactor harness, initial R8 writes integrated geomorphology coverage
to release summaries, generated landform parent-object causality is now below
the R8 hard threshold, and E1/E2/E3/E4/E5 provide fixture-level Earth
geomorphology coverage for continental interiors, margins, ocean-basin fabric,
arc/plume feature classes, and cryosphere/surface-process classes.  The current
Earth-like coverage gate has no missing required feature and keeps generated
parentless major landform area at zero, but still warns about fixture-only
generated-world cryosphere landforms plus ribbon/coastline shape.  The previous
non-ice fixture-only classes, `interior_basin`, `old_subdued_orogen`, and
`transform_fault`, are now world-visible in the 2500-cell Earth-like gate, and
the previous continental-detail overpaint warning has been removed by
process-belt detail classification.
Modern-scale ancient continental crust calibration remains a longer-run target,
but the 2500 Myr R8 smoke gate no longer applies an impossible `>2500 Myr`
ancient-crust warning.

  continental area but failed the ribbon/coastline gate, so the next phase must
  add shape-aware continent-object constraints before increasing craton or
  continental preservation.
- `[~]` P37 shape-aware restoration guard: optional parent-component width and
  narrowness guard is benchmarked.  It accepts broad parent-margin restoration
  and rejects restoration around ribbon fragments.  It is not production-
  enabled by default; the next step is coupling this guard to continent-object
  lineage and then testing a guarded old-platform recovery run.

  candidate patch to attach to a valid broad `tectonics.continents` parent

  generated-world audit has no regression, but still no safe old-platform

## Development Log

Template:

```text
YYYY-MM-DD - Short title

- Changed:
- Tests:
- Assets:
- Metrics:
- Open questions:
```

2026-06-23 - P21 supercontinent-breakup seaway objects, sediment contrast,
and mature-collage relaxation

- Changed:
  - Added `tectonics.breakup_seaways`, a deterministic tectonic object set for
    supercontinent-breakup and multi-rift seaway corridors.
  - The object generator uses continent components, rift potential, inherited
    weak crust domains, plate topology, and rift-system context to resolve
    corridors through weak lithosphere; stable craton cores are high-cost and
    should not be cut.
  - Terrain now responds to `tectonics.breakup_seaways` at `>=3000` cells by
    drowning only object-backed corridors that measurably reduce largest
    exposed-land dominance.  Low-resolution `2500` generated-world runs keep
    the previous stable fixture behavior.
  - Added a post-seaway component consolidation step: when severe fragmentation
    creates too many tiny components, it can drown small unsupported pieces and
    pay land area back around existing larger landmasses rather than simply
    lowering global land fraction.
  - Added object-seeded breakup path extension in terrain: accepted tectonic
    breakup corridors can now extend to the largest-landmass boundary through
    low-cost weak cells, then still must prove that they split the landmass
    into substantial components before being opened.
  - Added process-gated continental sediment regionalization so exposed
    continental sediment concentrates in rift/foreland/margin depocenters
    rather than staying as a uniform former-shelf blanket on platforms and
    highlands.
  - Added mature accretionary/collisional collage highland relaxation.  Broad,
    non-crest suture/accretionary collages are subdued toward old-orogen or
    platform elevations while narrow active belts and high local peaks remain.
  - Moved the mature Earth-like coastline smoothing budget to the P21
    breakup horizon (`>=2400 Myr`) so object-opened seaways can be followed by
    limited protrusion removal and same-component embayment fill.
  - Added P21 microbenchmarks for object generation, object-driven terrain
    response, and continental sediment depocenter contrast.
- Tests and runs:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> latest `2 passed in 56.26s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p20_resolved_plate_topology_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r5_continental_margin_evolution_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_resolution_profile.py -q`
    -> latest `5 passed in 7.60s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P21 --out
    out_bench_p21_breakup_20260623` -> `pass`, original `2/2`
    microbenchmarks.  The current in-test P21 suite has `3/3`
    microbenchmarks including sediment depocenter contrast.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p12 --presets earthlike --cells 8000 --t-end
    2500 --frames 4 --hex-w 96 --hex-h 48 --starts 6 --out
    out_p12_earthlike_8000_p21_path_smooth_budget2_sediment_collage_20260623`
    -> `warn`, `0` failed.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_p21_breakup_20260623/tectonics_bench_summary.json`.
  - `out_bench_p21_breakup_20260623/p21_microbenchmarks.csv`.
  - `out_p12_earthlike_8000_p21_breakup_cap_payback_20260623/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json`.
