  - `out_p12_earthlike_8000_p21_path_smooth_budget2_sediment_collage_20260623/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json`.
  - `out_p12_earthlike_8000_p21_path_smooth_budget2_sediment_collage_20260623/p12_preset_matrix_contact_sheet.png`.
- Metrics:
  - 8000-cell P20 baseline was `fail`: largest land component `0.900`,
    component count `15`, ribbon fraction `0.485`, narrow necks `8.51` per
    1000 land cells, exposed oceanic land `0.098`.
  - P21 cap/payback run is `warn`, `0` failed: largest land component `0.670`,
    component count `14`, ribbon fraction `0.532`, narrow necks `7.91` per
    1000 land cells, exposed oceanic land `0.089`.
  - P21 sediment microbenchmark now resolves basin/platform/highland contrast:
    basin sediment mean `3431 m`, platform `1311 m`, highland `869 m`, craton
    `886 m`, recorded depocenter-platform/highland contrast `2220 m`.
  - P21 mature-collage relaxation fixes the 2500-cell Earth-like mean-land
    regression: mean land elevation falls from the failing `2219 m` case to
    about `1627 m`, while p95 remains about `3519 m` and maximum relief remains
    above `4500 m`.
  - The latest 8000-cell P21 routine review is `warn`, `0` failed: land
    fraction `0.235`, exposed-land components `13`, ribbon fraction `0.549`,
    ocean basins `6`, and mean land elevation about `1400 m`.  This removes
    the single-supercontinent hard failure and keeps ribbon just below the
    hard-fail threshold, but ribbon/coastline complexity remain warning-level.
- Open questions:
  - P21 medium audit is now complete and failed at 24000 cells: the exposed
    land mask still behaves as a single supercontinent at medium resolution.
    This is no longer an 8000-cell sampling artifact and should define P22.
  - 8000 cells remain the routine visual/release-review scale, but they are
    too coarse to judge some small landforms such as narrow isthmuses, deltas,
    short mountain systems, straits, and shelf details.  Use 72000 cells only
    as an occasional offline deployment audit after the 24000 audit and a
    fresh `profile-resolution` preflight.
  - Hardware acceleration should be staged behind profiling evidence.  The
    first acceleration target should be graph-heavy CPU loops such as repeated
    width/component/articulation passes, seaway path search, hydrology routing,
    and map compilation; consider Numba before GPU backends, and keep CuPy/JAX
    optional behind explicit backend boundaries.
  - The next implementation target should migrate the remaining P14/P15/P21
    ribbon/coastline cleanup into upstream continent, margin, rift, platform,
    craton, old-orogen, and ocean-basin object geometry.

2026-06-23 - P21 medium-resolution audit and P22 handoff

- Changed:
  - Ran a fresh resolution-profile preflight after the P21 sediment/collage
    changes.
  - Ran the planned `24000`-cell Earth-like medium morphology audit before any
    optional `72000`-cell deployment run.
  - Investigated a direct object-guided terrain completion candidate for the
    medium-resolution supercontinent failure.  The attempted terrain-layer
    completion did not change the 24000-cell failure and was not retained as a
    production fix.
- Tests and runs:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli profile-resolution --cells 900 2500 --t-end
    2500 --frames 4 --hex-w 64 --hex-h 32 --starts 4 --out
    out_profile_900_2500_p21_medium_preflight_20260623`
    -> runtime `38.6s`; projected `8000` about `1.1 min`, `24000` about
    `2.6 min`, `72000` about `6.1 min`.

    --t-end 2500 --frames 4 --hex-w 128 --hex-h 64 --starts 8 --out
    out_p12_earthlike_24000_p21_medium_audit_20260623`
    -> `fail`, `1` failed entry.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> latest `2 passed in 278.66s`.
- Assets:
  - `out_profile_900_2500_p21_medium_preflight_20260623/resolution_profile_summary.json`.
  - `out_p12_earthlike_24000_p21_medium_audit_20260623/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json`.
  - `out_p12_earthlike_24000_p21_medium_audit_20260623/p12_preset_matrix_contact_sheet.png`.
- Metrics:
  - 24000-cell audit: land fraction `0.238`, exposed-land components `11`,
    largest land component `0.944`, ribbon fraction `0.482`, narrow necks
    `4.03` per 1000 land cells, ocean basins `26`.
  - Compared with the latest 8000-cell P21 routine review, ribbon improves
    (`0.549 -> 0.482`) and basins increase (`6 -> 26`), but largest component
    regresses from `0.406` to `0.944`.
  - Earth-reference screening at 24000 also flags mean land elevation
    `2102 m`, p95 land elevation `5586 m`, high-land fraction above `2500 m`
    `0.277`, and abyss fraction `0.685`.
  - Diagnostic rerun showed `tectonics.breakup_seaways=2` and terrain opened
    two seaways with area fraction `0.0071`, but those cuts only detached
    peripheral pieces and did not partition the main landmass.
- Interpretation:
  - P21 is sufficient for the 8000-cell routine gate but not for medium
    resolution.  Higher resolution reduces ribbon artifacts but reveals that
    the underlying continent/rift/seaway object geometry still represents one
    dominant connected continent.
  - The next phase should not be another final-terrain shave.  P22 should add a
    fixture/microbenchmark for this failure and then improve upstream
    supercontinent breakup object generation: multiple inherited-weakness
    branches, rift arms that intersect present exposed land, and object
    lifecycle rules that distinguish peripheral embayments from continent-
    splitting seaways.
  - `72000`-cell deployment tests remain blocked until a 24000-cell P22 audit
    no longer has the single-supercontinent hard failure.

2026-06-23 - P22 medium-supercontinent gap fixture

- Changed:
  - Added a P22 benchmark suite to `aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench`.
  - The fixture is a low-cost analogue of the 24000-cell failure: a broad
    supercontinent with peripheral weak necks plus a lower-amplitude interior
    rift hint.  The initial P21 logic opened the peripheral weak necks but left
    the interior rift unopened and the main continent insufficiently
    partitioned.
  - Added an initial regression test that expected this P22 suite to remain a
    red implementation target while verifying that the gap was deterministically
    reproduced and written to CSV.
- Tests and runs:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P22 --out
    out_bench_p22_medium_gap_20260623_b`
    -> initial suite `fail`, `0/1` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic
    `True`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_gap_is_detected -q`
    -> `2 passed in 2.17s` with the initial red-test name.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_p22_medium_gap_20260623_b/tectonics_bench_summary.json`.
  - `out_bench_p22_medium_gap_20260623_b/p22_microbenchmarks.csv`.
- Metrics:
  - The fixture creates `2` breakup objects and opens `2` seaways, so this is
    not an object-absence failure.
  - Largest exposed-land fraction improves from `1.000` to only `0.785`;
    split balance is `0.158`, below the P22 target `0.22`.
  - Interior rift opening fraction remains `0.000`, proving current object
    selection favors peripheral weak necks over the continent-splitting
    interior rift hint.
- Interpretation:
  - At this checkpoint P22 had a fast red benchmark that captured the
    24000-cell failure mode.  The following P22 implementation checkpoint makes
    this suite pass before another 24000-cell audit.
  - The likely mechanism change is upstream: rank or generate breakup objects
    by expected partition topology and interior rift connectivity, not just
    local weak-path quality.  This should be implemented in the tectonic object
    layer, then terrain should remain a verifier/renderer of accepted objects.

2026-06-23 - P22 topology-scored interior rift breakup

- Changed:
  - Updated `TectonicsModule._breakup_seaway_objects` so candidate breakup
    corridors carry partition topology metrics: expected largest remaining
    continental component, split balance, partition count, and topology score.
  - Added interior rift-axis candidates for large continents.  These candidates
    are accepted only when they are non-core, not too wide, and can plausibly
    partition the parent continent; broad weak blankets continue to use narrow
    shortest-path objects instead of becoming oversized terrain cuts.
  - Converted P22 from a red gap detector into a positive regression benchmark
    with schema `aevum.tectonics_bench.p22.v1`.

    out_bench_p22_medium_partition_20260623`
    -> suite `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.

    -> `2 passed in 1.76s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p20_resolved_plate_topology_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e1_real_earth_continental_landform_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e2_real_earth_margin_landform_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_resolution_profile.py -q`
    -> `7 passed in 2.54s`.

- Metrics:
  - The first P22 breakup object is now the interior rift corridor:
    `topology_score=1.029`, object split balance `0.811`, largest partition
    after candidate cut `0.539`, and interior-rift cell coverage `0.603`.
  - Terrain opens `1` object-backed seaway; exposed largest-land fraction falls
    from `1.000` to `0.537`, split balance rises to `0.815`, and interior rift
    opening fraction reaches `1.000`.
- Interpretation:
  - P22 fixes the focused medium-supercontinent failure at the object-selection

2026-06-23 - P22 24000-cell audit and P23 handoff

- Changed:
  - Ran the required `24000`-cell Earth-like medium morphology audit after the
    topology-scored P22 object change.
  - Added `tectonic_object_telemetry` to the P12 release summary so future
    audits directly report rift-system count, breakup-seaway count, breakup
    area, topology/split metrics, terrain seaway openings, and any largest-
    landmass shave area.  This telemetry closes the attribution gap exposed by

    -> `2 passed in 9.64s`.

- Metrics:
  - P22 improved but did not solve the medium world: land fraction `0.237`,
    exposed-land components `10`, largest exposed-land component `0.856`,
    ribbon fraction `0.487`, ocean basins `25`, mean land elevation `2131 m`,
    p95 land elevation `5585 m`, and high-land fraction above `2500 m`
    `0.279`.
  - Compared with the P21 24000 audit, largest component improved
    `0.944 -> 0.856`, while ribbon remained effectively unchanged
    (`0.482 -> 0.487`) and the hard release failure persisted.
- Interpretation:
  - P22's focused mechanism is valid but too local for the generated 24000-cell
    world.  The residual failure is now P23: the world still has a dominant
    connected landmass, likely maintained by high-latitude or marginal land
    bridges and an insufficient multi-branch breakup/seaway network.
  - Do not proceed to a 72000-cell deployment audit yet.  The next code work
    should use the new release-gate object telemetry plus a focused P23 fixture
    to distinguish missing breakup objects from terrain non-opening, then
    improve upstream continent/rift/seaway object generation.

2026-06-23 - P23 medium components and divergent rift lifecycle

- Changed:
  - Added P23 microbenchmarks for three related residual-supercontinent
    mechanisms:
    medium rifted continental components, continental divergent-boundary
    lifecycle, and medium components whose only strong evidence is a divergent
    boundary rather than a high scalar rift-potential field.
  - Relaxed breakup-component eligibility so medium components can generate
    breakup seaway candidates when they either have strong rift/stability
    evidence or a coherent divergent/passive/transform boundary hit.
  - Added `divergent` boundary objects and mapped continental divergent
    boundaries to Wilson-cycle `rift_system` objects without creating oceanic
    spreading centers.  Divergent boundary objects subtract ridge cells so
    ocean ridges are not double-counted as continental rifts.
- Tests and runs:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P23 --out
    out_bench_p23_boundary_seeded_medium_components_20260623`
    -> suite `pass`, `3/3` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r4_ocean_basin_lifecycle_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p23_medium_rifted_component_breakup_microbenchmark_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed in 1.38s`.
  - `out_p23_24000_object_telemetry_after_boundary_seeded_20260623/p23_24000_object_telemetry.json`
    -> generated-world object telemetry after the P23 changes.
- Metrics:
  - Focused P23 suite now verifies that three medium components below the old
    `0.115` global-area threshold each produce topology-capable breakup
    objects.
  - The 24000 generated world now has `12` rift-system objects instead of `0`,
    confirming the divergent-boundary lifecycle repair.  However those rift
    systems are tiny (`3-4` cells each), breakup-seaway object count remains
    `1`, breakup-seaway area remains about `0.0037`, terrain opens `1` seaway,
    and largest exposed-land component only improves to `0.852`.
- Interpretation:
  - P23a/P23b fix two real missing mechanisms, but they are not enough for the
    generated 24000 world.  The remaining P23c problem is rift-corridor extent:
    divergent lifecycle objects exist, but are too skeletal to seed additional
    breakup corridors through the residual dominant landmass.
  - The next code work should add component-eligibility telemetry and/or expand
    continental rift-system influence from thin boundary skeletons into weak
    rift corridors before rerunning a full 24000 P12 audit.  A 72000-cell audit
    remains blocked.

2026-06-23 - P23c component telemetry and multi-corridor rift fixture

- Changed:
  - Added component-level breakup telemetry to
    `tectonics.breakup_component_telemetry` and P12
    `tectonic_object_telemetry`, including evaluated/eligible component counts,
    candidate/accepted totals, skip reasons, and top-component summaries.
  - Added a P23 multi-corridor component fixture that forces one large
    continental component with three separated rift belts to produce multiple
    breakup-seaway objects.
  - Guarded rift-corridor apron expansion so weak stable lithosphere can widen
    a corridor only when it is tied to rift potential or a boundary zone.  This
    preserved the P22 interior-rift behavior while allowing P23 corridor
    expansion.
  - Raised the global-area cap for rift-axis breakup candidates from `0.060`
    to `0.075`; the previous cap incorrectly rejected the valid P22
    continent-splitting rift at medium resolution.
- Tests and runs:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P22 --out
    out_debug_p22_after_apron_area_fix_20260623`
    -> suite `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark passed, deterministic `True`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P23 --out
    out_debug_p23_after_apron_area_fix_20260623`
    -> suite `pass`, `4/4` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r4_ocean_basin_lifecycle_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p23_medium_rifted_component_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `5 passed in 8.00s`.
  - `out_p23_24000_object_telemetry_after_multicorridor_20260623/p23_24000_object_telemetry.json`
    -> generated-world object telemetry after P23c.
- Metrics:
  - The P23 suite now includes `4` microbenchmarks and verifies that a single
    large component with multiple rift corridors can produce at least three
    accepted breakup objects in isolation.
  - The 24000 generated world remains a residual failure: land fraction
    `0.237`, component count `11`, largest exposed-land component `0.852`,
    rift-system object count `12`, breakup-seaway object count `1`, terrain
    breakup-seaway area `0.0036`, and terrain breakup openings `1`.
  - The dominant continental component has high weak-area coverage (`0.909`)
    and `10` candidate corridors, but only `1` accepted object.  The accepted
    object has strong crust-component topology (`topology_score=1.241`,
    `split_balance=0.982`), yet the exposed-land macro-topology is unchanged.
- Interpretation:
  - P23c proves the local object model can represent multiple corridors, but
    the generated world is now failing downstream of candidate creation.  The
    next step should be P24: evaluate breakup objects against actual
    exposed-land connectivity after terrain opening, widen or branch only
    object-backed corridors that fail to sever exposed land, and explicitly
    detect non-continental/terrane land bridges that reconnect both sides.
  - A 72000-cell run is useful later for small-scale landforms such as
    isthmuses, deltas/fans, short mountain systems, straits, narrow shelves,
    and island-arc spacing.  It should stay an offline deployment/performance
    audit until the 24000 macro-topology gate is no longer failing.

2026-06-23 - P24a submerged-axis source reuse for object-backed seaways

- Changed:
  - Updated `terrain._open_breakup_seaway_objects` so a
    `tectonics.breakup_seaways` object whose original cells are already ocean
    can still seed propagation into adjacent weak lithosphere on the current
    largest landmass.
  - Restricted this propagation to the existing process axis and its nearby
    weak apron; it does not create arbitrary modern seaway cuts.
  - Added `terrain.last_breakup_seaway_source_reuse` to terrain globals and
    P12 `tectonic_object_telemetry`.
  - Added P24 microbenchmark coverage for a partially opened rift/seaway axis
    whose middle is already ocean but whose terminal weak land bridges still
    keep the continent connected.

  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P24 --out
    out_bench_p24_source_reuse_20260623`
    -> suite `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark passed, deterministic `True`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p23_medium_rifted_component_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `4 passed in 8.63s`.

    -> generated-world object telemetry after P24a.
- Metrics:
  - P24 fixture: object land fraction before opening `0.000`, largest exposed
    land component `1.000 -> 0.500`, split balance `0.999`, source reuse
    count `1`, propagated rift-axis ocean fraction `0.921`.
  - 24000 generated world: land fraction `0.238`, component count `11`,
    largest exposed-land component `0.851964`, rift-system object count `12`,
    breakup-seaway object count `1`, terrain breakup-seaway area `0.0036`,
    source reuse count `0`.
- Interpretation:
  - P24a fixes a real object-to-terrain conversion failure mode and keeps the
    mechanism object-backed.  It does not solve the current generated 24000
    seed because that seed does not reach the submerged-axis reuse path.
  - P24b should add per-breakup-object terrain attempt telemetry: source land
    fraction, reused-source flag, generated candidate counts, best reduction,
    final applied/rejected reason, and post-opening largest-landmass share.
    The repair should then target the measured failure: either too few accepted
    breakup objects from the `10` tectonic candidates, or a terrain candidate
    that is topology-capable in crust space but too small/narrow in exposed
    land space.
  - The 72000-cell audit remains deferred until P24b or a later mechanism
    moves the 24000 macro-topology gate.

2026-06-23 - P24b terrain-stage telemetry and seaway regionalization guard

- Changed:
  - Added per-breakup-object terrain attempt telemetry: source land fraction,
    source reuse flag, candidate/viable counts, best reduction, cut area,
    applied status, and rejection reasons.
  - Added `terrain.land_component_stage_telemetry` so 24000 audits can show
    how land fraction, component count, and largest-landmass share change
    across the final terrain pipeline.
  - Recorded actual `terrain.breakup_seaway_opened_corridors` and exposed
    opened-corridor count/area in P12 `tectonic_object_telemetry`.
  - Fixed `_regionalize_ocean_floor` so object-backed breakup seaway corridors
    and one ocean-neighbour apron are not raised/smoothed back into land
    bridges during shelf/slope bathymetry regionalization.
  - Added a second P24 microbenchmark proving an opened object-backed breakup
    seaway survives ocean-floor regionalization.
- Tests and runs:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P24 --out
    out_bench_p24_wide_regionalization_guard_20260623`
    -> suite `pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p23_medium_rifted_component_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `4 passed in 7.99s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p12 --presets earthlike --cells 24000 --t-end 2500 --frames 4 --hex-w 96 --hex-h 48 --starts 6 --out out_p24_earthlike_24000_release_audit_20260623`
    -> decision `warn`, `0` failed, `1` warned, runtime `221.3s`.
  - `out_p24_24000_after_wide_regionalization_guard_20260623/p24_24000_after_wide_regionalization_guard.json`
    -> stage telemetry after the final regionalization guard.
- Metrics:
  - Before the guard, stage telemetry showed `_regionalize_ocean_floor` was the
    reconnecting step: largest exposed-land component jumped from `0.483` to
    `0.852` after bathymetry regionalization.
  - After the guard, the same 24000 seed stays split through the final terrain
    stages: before object seaway `0.945`, after first breakup seaway `0.476`,
    after modern smoothing `0.485`, after ocean-floor regionalization `0.486`,
    and final P12 largest exposed-land component `0.486`.
  - Latest 24000 P12 audit: land fraction `0.237`, component count `12`,
    ribbon `0.481`, ocean basins `21`, breakup seaway openings `1`, opened
    corridor area `0.0036`.
- Interpretation:
  - The residual single-supercontinent hard failure is fixed at 24000 cells
    without adding a random split or renderer-only cut.  The accepted tectonic
    breakup object creates a valid seaway, and the ocean-floor pass now
    preserves that process result.
  - The run remains `warn`, not release-clean.  The next work should be P25:
    reduce ribbon/coastline complexity and calibrate excessive land elevations
    and suture/LIP highland extent against Earth-reference distributions.
  - 72000-cell deployment review is still premature for quality claims until
    P25 improves the remaining Earth-reference morphology warnings, though the
    resolution ladder can use it later for small-landform visibility.

2026-06-24 - P25a hypsometry and highland-province calibration

- Changed:
  - Added the `P25` microbenchmark suite with two focused checks:
    `P25.broad_mature_highland_relaxation` and
    `P25.highland_province_requires_active_or_thick_core`.
  - Extended mature accretionary/collisional highland relaxation so broad
    mature suture, mature LIP, and stable-craton highlands are capped unless
    supported by a young active belt, recent volcanism, or a narrow active
    orogenic spine.
  - Reworked `terrain.province` so suture/LIP land is only classified as
    highland when active-orogenic, recently volcanic, thick-cored, or genuinely
    high relief; mature suture/LIP land can degrade to platform/margin instead.
  - Lowered the object-backed breakup-seaway terrain gate from `3000` to
    `2000` cells so the 2500-cell Earth-like regression uses upstream tectonic
    breakup objects instead of relying on late modern-seaway cleanup.
  - Restored small active-orogen detail provinces and added a guarded
    post-orogenic sag-basin fallback so P25a's highland caps do not erase
    required orogen/plateau and interior-basin landform objects.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P25 --out out_bench_p25_highland_calibration_20260624_m`
    -> `2/2` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p25_earth_reference_highland_calibration_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `65 passed in 557.66s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p12 --presets earthlike --cells 24000 --t-end 2500 --frames 4 --hex-w 96 --hex-h 48 --starts 6 --out out_p25_earthlike_24000_highland_calibration_final_20260624`
    -> `warn`, `0` failed, `1` warned.
- Metrics:
  - Against the P24 24000 audit, mean land elevation improves from about
    `2131 m` to `1248 m`, p95 from about `5589 m` to `3116 m`, high land above
    `2500 m` from about `27.9%` to `9.8%`, and suture/LIP/highland terrain
    province share from about `67%` to `9.3%`.
  - The 24000 topology remains in the P24 repaired range: land fraction
    `0.237`, component count `13`, largest land component `0.483`, ribbon
    `0.490`, and no single-supercontinent hard failure.
  - The compiled map no longer turns almost every land hex into hills or
    mountains: compiled hills-or-mountains fraction of land is about `59.5%`
    versus roughly `97%` before P25a.
- Interpretation:
  - P25a fixes the most severe generated-world highland/hypsometry problem
    without adding a random split or renderer-only color change.  The remaining
    Earth-reference warnings are now mainly planform and distribution issues:
    land fraction undershoot, ribbon/coastline complexity, a small mean
    elevation excess, and slightly high abyss fraction.
  - P25b should target continent/margin/rift geometry and land-budget payback,
    not another broad highland cap.  It must preserve the P25a reductions in
    suture/LIP highland overpaint and the P24 object-backed seaway split.

2026-06-24 - P25b planform land-budget payback and seaway-safe coastline cleanup

- Changed:
  - Added `P25.modern_coastline_payback_preserves_breakup_seaways` to the
    `P25` microbenchmark suite.  The fixture builds two landmasses separated by
    an object-backed breakup seaway, then verifies that modern coastline
    smoothing recovers land budget into same-component shallow shelf cells
    without refilling the protected seaway.
  - Added `terrain.breakup_seaway_opened_corridors` protection to modern
    coastline fill/payback and factored it through
    `_breakup_seaway_protection_mask`.
  - Added `_pay_back_modern_coastline_land`, including guarded shallow
    attached-terrane and suture shelf candidates.  Candidates must be oceanic
    cells over continental crust, adjacent to a single existing land component,
    outside protected breakup seaways, and shallow enough to behave as
    coastline/shelf recovery rather than arbitrary land creation.
  - Added cleanup payback after unsupported island-chain removal, both inside
    modern coastline smoothing and after the outer final unsupported-component
    pass.  This preserves the removal of unsupported archipelagos but pays the
    area back into remaining shelf/margin cells.
  - Added release telemetry for modern coastline drowned/fill/payback
    candidate/recovery/payback area and internal smoothing stages, so future
    P12 audits can identify whether land-budget problems are caused by budget,
    candidate scarcity, or later cleanup.
  - Lowered the low-resolution active-orogen continental-detail relief floor
    from `1450 m` to `1250 m` at `<=3000` cells so the expanded lowland shelf
    area does not erase visible active orogen/plateau detail in the 2500-cell
    regression.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P25 --out out_bench_p25_planform_payback_20260624_g`
    -> `3/3` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P24 --out out_bench_p24_after_p25b_e_20260624`
    -> `2/2` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p25_earth_reference_highland_calibration_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p12 --presets earthlike --cells 24000 --t-end 2500 --frames 4 --hex-w 96 --hex-h 48 --starts 6 --out out_p25b_earthlike_24000_planform_payback_final2_20260624`
    -> `warn`, `0` failed, `1` warned.
- Metrics:
  - Against the P25a 24000 audit, land fraction improves from `0.237` to
    `0.253`, entering the current broad Earth-reference `0.25-0.33` envelope.
  - Exposed land component count improves from `13` to `4`, largest land
    component remains modern-fragmented at `0.486`, and exposed oceanic land
    falls to about `9.7%` of land.
  - Ribbon fraction improves from about `0.490` after P25a and `0.483` in the
    first P25b audit to `0.410`.  This remains outside the desired `<=0.35`
    screening envelope, but the direction is correct without reconnecting the
    breakup seaway.
  - Largest-landmass coastline complexity improves from about `24.5` after
    P25a to `21.46`, still above the desired `<=8.0` screening envelope.
  - Mean land elevation improves from `1248 m` after P25a to `1134 m`, while
    land p95 remains plausible at `2958 m`; high false highland overpaint stays
    suppressed with suture/LIP/highland province share about `8.6%`.
  - Modern coastline telemetry for the final 24000 audit records drowned area
    `0.0140`, payback candidate area `0.0449`, recovery need `0.0500`,
    payback area `0.0340`, and `816` payback cells.  Internal smoothing
    recovers from `0.2337` after drowning to `0.2515` after cleanup payback.
- Interpretation:
  - P25b fixes the land-budget undershoot and excessive exposed component
    count without weakening P24 object-backed seaway protection or P25a
    highland calibration.
  - Remaining Earth-reference misses are now narrower: land ribbon fraction,
    largest-landmass coastline complexity, and abyss fraction.  These should
    move to P26 as upstream continent/margin-width and ocean-province geometry
    work, not as another broad final pass that simply flips cells by area.

2026-06-24 - P26 rejected upstream-margin widening spike

- Attempted:
  - Tested a narrow P26 implementation that widened passive-margin
    progradation and area-neutral continental shape maintenance for high
    `tectonics.last_continent_shape_pressure`.
  - Added temporary isolated checks for underwide-margin widening and rift
    protection.  The isolated fixtures passed, but they were not sufficient
    integration evidence.
- Integration result:
  - The first 8000-cell audit failed with `land 25.9%`, `components 3`,
    `ribbon 0.535`, and an antimeridian ocean-basin seam failure.
  - A constrained second attempt removed the seam hard failure at 8000 cells
    but still left `ribbon 0.535`.
  - The 24000-cell integration audit failed hard:
    `out_p26_earthlike_24000_upstream_margin_geometry_20260624` reported
    `land 0.216`, `components 16`, `ribbon 0.565`, largest-landmass coastline
    complexity `34.53`, and `40` ocean basins.
- Resolution:
  - The P26 behaviour change and temporary P26 microbench/test were removed.
  - The useful release telemetry was kept: P12 summaries now expose
    `tectonics_continent_shape_pressure`,
    `tectonics_background_continent_shape_maintenance`,
    `tectonics_passive_margin_progradation_cells`,
    continent gain/loss cells, and unforced gain/loss blocked flags.
  - A restore audit,
    `out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624`, confirms
    the current tree is back to the accepted P25b state: `warn`, `0` failed,
    land `0.253`, components `4`, ribbon `0.410`, largest-landmass coastline
    complexity `21.46`, and basins `14`.
- Interpretation:
  - P26 cannot be validated by isolated progradation fixtures alone.  The next
    P26 design must include an integration gate before acceptance: at minimum
    8000-cell and 24000-cell Earth-like audits must not regress land fraction,
    exposed component count, ribbon fraction, ocean-basin count, or seam
    continuity relative to the accepted P25b baseline.
  - The likely next design path is not simply a wider progradation zone.  It
    needs component-aware continent/margin object geometry, explicit protection
    for ocean-basin partition/seam continuity, and a parameter ledger tied to
    the P12 Earth-reference metrics.

2026-06-24 - P26 integration regression gate

- Changed:
  - Added `aevum.diagnostics.p26_regression_gate`, a lightweight comparator
    for P12 release summaries.  It compares a candidate Earth-like run against
    the accepted P25b baseline and fails if world-level metrics regress.
  - The gate checks release/validation failures, land fraction, exposed-land
    component count, exposed-land ribbon fraction, continental ribbon fraction,
    largest-landmass coastline complexity, ocean-basin count, and ocean-basin
    seam continuity.
  - Added unit tests with synthetic P12 summaries covering a clean candidate,
    the rejected P26 spike shape, and a basin seam-continuity regression.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p26_regression_gate.py -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - Baseline self-check:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.p26_regression_gate --baseline out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json --candidate out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json --out out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624/p26_regression_report_selfcheck.json`
    -> `pass`.
  - Rejected-spike check:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.p26_regression_gate --baseline out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json --candidate out_p26_earthlike_24000_upstream_margin_geometry_20260624/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json --out out_p26_earthlike_24000_upstream_margin_geometry_20260624/p26_regression_report.json`
    -> `fail`, as expected, with failures for release/validation, land
    fraction, component count, land ribbon, continental ribbon, coastline
    complexity, and basin count.
- Required P26 workflow:
  1. Run an 8000-cell P12 candidate audit and compare it to the current
     accepted 8000 baseline if available; otherwise treat warnings manually and
     do not claim acceptance from 8000 alone.
  2. Run the 24000-cell P12 candidate audit.
  3. Run `p26_regression_gate` against
     `out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624/p12_tectonics_release_summary.json`.
  4. Only if the gate passes should the candidate be documented as a P26
     improvement; otherwise revert or redesign before proceeding.

2026-06-24 - P26 ribbon-driver attribution

- Changed:
  - Added `aevum.diagnostics.p26_ribbon_drivers`, a read-only component-level
    diagnostic for the P26 morphology problem.  It uses the existing spherical
    morphology component labels and reports the top exposed-land,
    continental-crust, and exposed-continental components that contribute
    ribbon-like area.
  - P12 release summaries now include `p26_ribbon_drivers` alongside
    `morphology` and `tectonic_object_telemetry`.
  - Each top component records world/parent-mask area, ribbon share, width,
    coastline complexity, crust-domain shares, crust-origin shares,
    continental-detail shares, stability, crust age, and a compact
    `driver_hint` such as young accretionary-margin ribbon, over-complex stable
    coastline, or exposed oceanic/arc-island chain.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p26_ribbon_drivers.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p26_regression_gate.py -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Required P26 workflow update:
  - Before changing continent/margin geometry again, inspect the
    `p26_ribbon_drivers` section of the current accepted baseline and of any
    candidate summary.  The next code change should target the dominant driver
    class rather than widening all margins or adding another final terrain
    cleanup pass.

2026-06-24 - P26 ribbon-driver evidence and rejected mechanism probes

- Baseline attribution:
  - A fresh 8000-cell Earth-like audit with `p26_ribbon_drivers` was written to
    `out_p26_driver_audit_earthlike_8000_20260624`.
  - It remained `warn`, with land `0.259`, exposed components `3`, ribbon
    `0.424`, largest-landmass coastline complexity `13.16`, and ocean basins
    `3`.
  - The dominant driver was not exposed oceanic crust or final hex rendering:
    all top exposed-land components were classified as young unstable
    accretionary-margin ribbons.  The continental crust was one connected body
    but was dominated by suture/accreted-terrane domain shares
    (`~67%` suture and `~25%` accreted terrane), with only small craton/interior
    shares.
- Rejected probe A:
  - A narrow change that allowed width-3 quiet suture/accretionary collage to
    mature into primordial/interior crust passed an isolated fixture but
    worsened the 8000-cell world audit:
    `out_p26_quiet_suture_collage_earthlike_8000_20260624` stayed `warn` but
    moved ribbon from `0.424` to `0.465` and coastline complexity from `13.16`
    to `14.33`.
- Rejected probe B:
  - Adding simple boundary-corridor localization for collision/subduction
    origin rewrites made the integration failure worse:
    `out_p26_boundary_localized_earthlike_8000_20260624` failed hard with land
    `0.230`, components `9`, ribbon `0.575`, and basins `9`.
- Resolution:
  - Both behavior changes were removed.  The current production generator is
    restored to the accepted P25b behavior plus read-only diagnostics.
  - The durable P26 assets are the regression gate and `p26_ribbon_drivers`;
    the rejected probes show that P26 cannot be solved by a local threshold
    tweak to maturity or boundary width alone.
- Added follow-up diagnostic:
  - `p26_ribbon_drivers` now records per-component time-since-rework,
    recent-reworked share, recent-orogeny share, recent-volcanism share,
    active-rework share, and quiet inherited arc/suture share.  This separates
    current active collision/arc reworking from inherited quiet
    suture/accreted provenance before another mechanism change is attempted.
  - It also reports summary-level temporal driver fields for exposed-land and
    continental-crust ribbon area, including
    `primary_temporal_driver_hint`.
- Latest time-attribution audit:
  - `out_p26_time_attribution_earthlike_8000_20260624` reran the current
    accepted P25b behavior with the time-attribution fields enabled.
  - The world-level result matched the earlier 8000 baseline: `warn`, land
    `0.259`, exposed components `3`, ribbon `0.424`, and basins `3`.
  - The temporal cause is now clear: top exposed-land components have
    recent-reworked shares of about `0.94`, `0.95`, and `0.40`; their
    active-rework shares are about `0.98`, `0.96`, and `0.60`.  The full
    continental-crust ribbon body has active-rework share about `0.91` and
    quiet inherited arc/suture share about `0.00`.
  - Therefore the next accepted P26 mechanism should target the spatial and
    temporal footprint of current collision/subduction reworking.  The
    rejected quiet-collage maturation route is not the dominant issue in the
    current Earth-like run.
- Next design constraint:
  - The next microbenchmark should reproduce an over-broad recent
    collision/subduction rework swath while preserving legitimate active
    orogen/arc belts.  Then a world-level 8000 audit must improve or at least
    not regress the `out_p26_driver_audit` baseline before any 24000 run.

2026-06-24 - P26 recent-rework footprint diagnostic microbenchmarks

- Added:
  - `aevum.diagnostics.p26_rework_footprint`, a read-only diagnostic that
    measures whether recent suture/accretionary rework is concentrated inside
    an explicit boundary corridor or spread through broad continental
    interiors.
  - P12 release summaries now include `p26_rework_footprint` alongside
    `p26_ribbon_drivers`.
  - `P26` benchmark suite is now diagnostic-only and initially contains two
    recent-rework footprint fixtures:
    one intentionally over-broad recent collision/suture swath and one
    localized active boundary belt.  The diagnostic must flag the former and
    accept the latter.
- Reference design note:
  - This follows the same broad separation used by pyGPlates/GPlates topology
    tools: `resolve_topologies` resolves boundary and network features at a
    geological time, resolved topological sections provide unique shared
    boundary sub-segments, and resolved topological networks represent
    deforming regions with strain/strain-rate queries.  See
    <https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/generated/pygplates.resolve_topologies.html>,
    <https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/pygplates_reference.html>, and
    <https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/pygplates_primer.html>.
  - The Aevum analogue is to measure a boundary-corridor footprint before
    changing crust provenance, instead of treating every rasterized collision
    swath cell as equally active.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p26_recent_rework_footprint_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P26 --out out_bench_p26_recent_rework_footprint_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Interpretation:
  - This is not yet a production fix.  It gives P26 a reproducible acceptance
    target for the next mechanism change: reduce over-broad recent rework
    outside boundary corridors while preserving localized active belts.

2026-06-24 - P26 active-rework core production probes rejected

- Attempted:
  - Added a production helper that restricted `ORIGIN_SUTURE`/`ORIGIN_ARC`
    provenance, recent rework age, orogeny age, and volcanism age to a
    boundary-corridor core derived from resolved plate contact.
  - The helper passed a fixture-level mask-localization test, but integration
    evidence was mixed.
- Integration results:
  - `out_p26_active_rework_core_earthlike_8000_20260624` improved visible
    exposed-land morphology: land `0.260`, exposed components `2`, exposed
    ribbon `0.371`, and largest coastline complexity `11.43`.
  - The same run regressed upstream continental state: continental components
    increased to `9`, continental ribbon rose to `0.425`, exposed oceanic land
    rose to `0.051`, and stable/ancient craton fractions jumped to about
    `0.48`/`0.46`.
  - A more conservative variant that kept broad deformation rework age while
    limiting only origin/arcs failed hard:
    `out_p26_active_rework_core_reworked_broad_earthlike_8000_20260624`
    reported land `0.259`, components `4`, ribbon `0.634`, and a hard
    ribbon-dominated-land failure.
- Resolution:
  - Both production behavior changes were removed.  Current production
    tectonics is back to the accepted P25b behavior plus read-only P26
    diagnostics.
  - The P26 suite remains diagnostic-only; after the deforming-network state
    additions below it has `4/4` microbenchmarks, not a claim that a production
    rework-core mechanism has been accepted.
- Next design constraint:
  - The visible improvement from the first rejected probe is useful evidence,
    but an accepted fix must also preserve or improve continental-crust
    component count, continental ribbon, exposed-oceanic-land fraction, and
    stable/ancient crust fractions.  A future production mechanism likely needs
    an explicit deforming-network state, not only a narrower provenance write
    mask.

2026-06-24 - P26 explicit deforming-network state

- Added:
  - `tectonics.deformation_intensity`: scalar active deformation intensity
    field, separate from `crust.origin` and `crust.reworked_age_myr`.
  - `tectonics.deformation_style`: categorical field with collision core,
    collision shoulder, subduction core, subduction shoulder, rift, and
    transform styles.
  - `tectonics.deforming_networks`: component objects for active
    deforming-network cores and shoulders.
- Design:
  - The field separates high-confidence shared-boundary cores from lower
    intensity deforming shoulders.  It is deliberately read-only for terrain
    and crust provenance at this stage, so adding it does not change generated
    land/sea or crust-origin behavior.
  - This is the missing state layer implied by the rejected active-rework-core
    probes: future P26 changes can consume deformation intensity/style without
    directly overloading `crust.origin` as both provenance and active-strain
    marker.
- Verification:
  - `P26.deforming_network_state_separates_core_and_shoulder` checks that a
    broad collision swath produces both core and shoulder, preserves crust
    origin unchanged, and emits deforming-network component objects.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P26 --out out_bench_p26_deforming_network_state_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `3/3` microbenchmarks at initial state-layer introduction.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_core.py::test_registry_resolves_dependencies tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p26_recent_rework_footprint_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_crust_origin_stability_and_explanations_are_available -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Next use:
  - The next production-facing P26 step should make terrain province/detail
    response read `tectonics.deformation_intensity/style` first, then only
    later decide whether elevation or crust provenance needs a narrower update.
    This keeps active strain from fragmenting continental-crust topology.

2026-06-24 - P26 deforming-network release telemetry

- Added:
  - `aevum.diagnostics.p26_deforming_networks`, a read-only summary of the new
    deformation state.
  - P12 release summaries now include `p26_deforming_networks` alongside
    `p26_ribbon_drivers` and `p26_rework_footprint`.
- Metrics:
  - active deformation area fractions for world, continental crust, and land;
  - core / shoulder area split and core-to-shoulder ratio;
  - overlap between active deformation and exposed-land / continental ribbon
    masks;
  - per-style area, intensity, land/continental fraction, and ribbon overlap;
  - top deforming-network objects and diagnostic hints.
- Purpose:
  - P26 production terrain changes can now be evaluated against explicit
    deformation metrics before changing crust provenance.  If a terrain change
    reduces visible ribbon but the deformation summary shows broad shoulder
    overprint or excessive ribbon coupling, the candidate should be rejected or
    retuned before a 24000-cell audit.
- Follow-up calibration:
  - The first small Earth-like telemetry smoke exposed that raw rift /
    separating swaths could make the active deformation layer too broad before
    terrain had even consumed it.  `_deforming_network_state` now treats
    plate-contact cells as the active axis for collision, subduction, rift, and
    transform styles; broad collision/subduction process cells outside that
    axis remain lower-intensity shoulders.
  - `P26.deforming_network_state_localizes_rift_axes` keeps this behavior in
    the microbenchmark suite so future terrain work does not amplify raw
    separating noise into world-broad rift relief.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P26 --out out_bench_p26_thinned_deforming_axes_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `4/4` microbenchmarks.
  - 900-cell Earth-like telemetry smoke after axis thinning: active deformation
    area fraction of world `0.254`, rift area fraction of world `0.033`,
    collision/subduction core fraction of active `0.231`, shoulder fraction of
    active `0.624`, and both land/continental ribbon coupling hints are false.
    This smoke is low-resolution evidence only; production acceptance still
    requires 8000/24000 P12 audits against the P25b baseline.

2026-06-24 - P26 terrain semantic deformation response

- Changed:
  - `terrain.province`, `terrain.continental_detail`, and terrain landform
    object parent lookup now consume `tectonics.deformation_intensity/style`.
  - The change is deliberately semantic only: direct elevation and regionalized
    surface generation remain on the P25b path.  A direct elevation response
    probe was rejected because it lowered exposed land and fragmented ocean
    basins at 24000 cells.
- Microbenchmark:
  - Added `P26.terrain_response_uses_deforming_network_not_broad_recent_swath`.
    The fixture gives a broad recent-orogen timestamp to a synthetic suture
    swath but supplies deformation core/shoulder only near the active axis.
    The accepted behavior is that active highland province/detail follows the
    deformation network, not the whole recent timestamp swath.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P26 --out out_bench_p26_terrain_semantic_deformation_response_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `5/5` microbenchmarks.
- World audit:
  - A 24000-cell run with default `t_end=4500` was useful as a future deep-time
    sample but is not comparable to the accepted P25b baseline, which uses
    `t_end=2500`, `frames=4`, and `96x48` hex compilation.
  - The same-configuration candidate
    `out_p26_terrain_semantic_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624` matches the
    accepted P25b macro metrics: land `0.253`, components `4`, ribbon `0.410`,
    ocean basins `14`, and `0` hard failures.
  - `aevum.diagnostics.p26_regression_gate` passes against
    `out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624`.
- Next use:
  - The next P26 elevation/topography change must be a new, separately gated
    increment.  It should consume deformation state only through a constrained
    province-to-relief response and must pass both the synthetic P26 fixture and
    same-configuration 8000/24000 regression gates before being kept.

2026-06-24 - P26 constrained deformation relief

- Changed:
  - Added `_constrained_deformation_relief(...)` in `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The correction only raises cells that are already exposed land,
    continental, non-cratonic, broad enough to avoid narrow-ribbon islands, and
    inside explicit deformation orogen core/shoulder masks.  It does not expose
    submerged cells, solve a new sea level, or rewrite crust provenance.
  - P12 `tectonic_object_telemetry` now records
    `terrain_deformation_relief_area_fraction` and
    `terrain_deformation_relief_mean_m`.
- Microbenchmark:
  - `P26.terrain_response_uses_deforming_network_not_broad_recent_swath` now
    checks both semantic response and constrained relief.  In the fixture,
    deformation core receives stronger relief than shoulder, remote recent
    non-deforming swath receives no relief, and the land mask is unchanged.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P26 --out out_bench_p26_constrained_deformation_relief_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `5/5` microbenchmarks.
- World audit:
  - Same-configuration 24000-cell audit:
    `out_p26_constrained_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`.
  - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
  - Macro metrics remain within the P26 gate against the P25b restore baseline:
    land `0.253`, components `4`, ribbon `0.417`, continental ribbon `0.397`,
    coastline complexity `22.27`, ocean basins `20`, seam basin mismatch `0`.
  - `aevum.diagnostics.p26_regression_gate` status: `pass`.
  - Applied deformation relief area fraction `0.040` of world, mean applied
    correction `46 m`; stage telemetry confirms the land mask is unchanged by
    the relief correction.
- Caveat:
  - This is intentionally small.  It gives active deformation a topographic
    signal without accepting the earlier broad-elevation response that failed
    24000-cell planform gates.  Larger relief changes must be introduced with
    tighter province/locality gates and a new 8000/24000 audit.

2026-06-24 - P26 interior-width relief gate

- Changed:
  - Tightened `_constrained_deformation_relief(...)` so active deformation
    relief only applies to already exposed, elevated (`>=900 m`), non-cratonic,
    broad continental interiors with both continental width and exposed-land
    width support.
  - Shoulder uplift now also requires a wider exposed-land body than core
    uplift.  This keeps broad active-deformation semantics from raising coastal
    lows, narrow island arcs, or one-cell land bridges into new highlands.
- Microbenchmark:
  - `P26.terrain_response_uses_deforming_network_not_broad_recent_swath` now
    includes two negative controls: an active but narrow land cell and an active
    but low-elevation continental cell.  Both must receive `0 m` relief while
    the interior deformation core/shoulder still receives relief and the land
    mask remains unchanged.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P26 --out out_bench_p26_interior_width_relief_regression_guard_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `5/5` microbenchmarks.
- World audit:
  - Same-configuration 24000-cell audit:
    `out_p26_interior_width_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`.
  - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
  - P26 regression gate against
    `out_p25b_restore_earthlike_24000_after_p26_revert_20260624`: `pass`.
  - Macro metrics: land `0.252`, components `4`, ribbon `0.410`,
    continental ribbon `0.397`, coastline complexity `21.98`, ocean basins
    `18`, seam basin mismatch `0`.
  - Applied deformation relief area fraction fell from the first constrained
    relief audit's `0.040` to `0.033` of world, and mean applied correction
    fell from `46 m` to `41 m`, while preserving the intended interior active
    deformation signal.
- Status:
  - Keep this as the accepted P26 terrain-side relief increment.  The next
    terrain change should not broaden relief from active deformation until a
    process-level province/object model explains where interior mountain belts,
    high plateaus, foreland basins, and passive-margin lowlands should occur.

2026-06-24 - P27 orogenic response state

- Design reference:
  - Keep using ETOPO / GEBCO-style global relief products as the target data
    family for real topography/bathymetry comparison.
  - Use GPlates-style plate-reconstruction object lineage as the reference
    pattern for attaching terrain provinces to tectonic parents through deep
    time.
  - Foreland basins are treated as flexural/accommodation responses adjacent to
    active or inherited orogenic loads, not as random lowland labels.
- Changed:
  - Added process fields from `TerrainModule`:
    `terrain.orogenic_load` and `terrain.foreland_accommodation`.
  - Added `_orogenic_response_state(...)`, which derives load from active
    collision/subduction deformation, suture/contact processes, and already
    expressed topographic orogens.  It derives foreland accommodation on broad,
    low, non-cratonic continental cells adjacent to that load.
  - `terrain.continental_detail` can now mark foreland basin detail from this
    shared response state.
  - `terrain.continental_landforms` now uses the same response state for
    `foreland_basin` object selection and keeps parent tectonic ids through
    sutures/deforming-network objects.
- Rejected during tuning:
  - Directly feeding the new response into cross-frame continental sediment
    redistribution failed the 24000-cell same-configuration gate twice by
    lowering final Earthlike land fraction below `0.25`.  That feedback path is
    therefore deferred until P28 adds a sediment mass / land-budget gate.
- Microbenchmark:
  - Added `P27.orogenic_foreland_response_state`.
  - The fixture contains a collision highland, adjacent foreland lowland,
    distal platform, and stable craton.  The benchmark requires load to peak in
    the orogen, accommodation to peak in the adjacent foreland, foreland detail
    to become basin-like, sediment to concentrate in the foreland lowland, and
    generated `foreland_basin` objects to retain parent tectonic ids.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P27 --out out_bench_p27_orogenic_foreland_response_topoload_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmarks.
- World audit:
  - 8000-cell smoke:
    `out_p27_state_only_earthlike_8000_t2500_20260624` -> P12 `warn`, `0`
    hard failures, land `0.260`, components `3`, basins `7`.
  - Same-configuration 24000-cell audit:
    `out_p27_state_only_earthlike_24000_t2500_rerun_20260624`.
  - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
  - P26 regression gate against
    `out_p26_interior_width_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`: `pass`.
  - Macro metrics: land `0.253`, components `4`, ribbon `0.417`,
    continental ribbon `0.397`, coastline complexity `22.87`, ocean basins
    `22`, seam basin mismatch `0`.
- Status:
  - Accept P27 as the state/detail/object foundation for object-level terrain
    provinces.  Do not re-enable sediment/elevation feedback from
    `foreland_accommodation` until a separate P28 gate proves land budget,
    sediment contrast, and basin-count stability together.

2026-06-24 - P28 sediment coupling budget gate

- Scope:
  - Diagnostic / acceptance gate only.  This does not re-enable production
    sediment or elevation feedback from `terrain.foreland_accommodation`.
  - The purpose is to define the red lines a future production coupling must
    satisfy before it can affect generated worlds.
- Design reference:
  - Foreland sediment response is modeled as mass transfer from bypass/source
    highlands and stable interiors into flexural accommodation adjacent to
    orogenic load.
  - Passive-margin response is modeled as a localized margin/shelf sediment
    wedge fed by interior source areas, with a water-depth headroom cap so a
    diagnostic sediment step does not accidentally expose submerged shelves.
- Changed:
  - Added diagnostic candidate `_p28_sediment_coupling_candidate(...)` in
    `aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench`.  It is intentionally not called by
    production `TerrainModule`.
  - Added `P28.foreland_sediment_budget_gate`.
  - Added `P28.passive_margin_sediment_budget_gate`.
  - Added `run_p28_bench(...)`, CLI/CSV support, and pytest coverage.
- Gate requirements:
  - Total continental sediment volume must be conserved within `0.2%`.
  - A conservative projected-surface check must keep land-mask change below the
    benchmark limits; the accepted fixtures currently have `0.0` projected land
    change.
  - Foreland deposition must exceed distal/craton response and draw sediment
    down from the orogenic/source side.
  - Passive-margin deposition must build a localized margin/shelf wedge, draw
    from interior/craton source areas, and respect submerged-shelf headroom.
- Microbenchmark:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P28 --out out_bench_p28_sediment_coupling_gate_20260624_e`
    -> `pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks.
  - Foreland fixture: mean foreland sediment delta `259 m`, orogen/source delta
    `-454 m`, volume change `0.0`, projected land change `0.0`.
  - Passive-margin fixture: mean margin sediment delta `1373 m`, shelf delta
    `875 m`, interior/source delta `-482 m`, volume change `0.0`, projected
    land change `0.0`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p28_sediment_coupling_budget_gates_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p27_orogenic_foreland_response_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p26_recent_rework_footprint_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e1_real_earth_continental_landform_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Status:
  - P28 provides the gate needed before production sediment coupling.  The next
    step should implement a small production-side coupling that passes P28 and
    then re-run the same-configuration 8000/24000 P12 audits against the P27
    accepted baseline.

2026-06-24 - P29 inland geomorphology complexity gate

- Problem:
  - The current generated continents still read as too flat and visually
    monotone inland.  Broad low-elevation interiors are Earth-like, but real
    continental interiors are not a single smooth platform: they combine
    shields, platforms, sedimentary basins, residual old orogens, rift valleys,
    plateau margins, volcanic provinces, and erosional escarpments.
- Real-Earth reference:
  - NOAA ETOPO and GEBCO global terrain products should be used as the
    comparison data family for hypsometry and relief-band targets.
  - The Earth hypsographic curve supports a broad continental mode only several
    hundred meters above sea level; therefore P29 should not simply add random
    mountains everywhere.  It should add structured inland variance around that
    lowland/platform mode.
- Microbenchmark:
  - Added `P29.inland_mechanism_diversity_fixture`.
    - The fixture is a broad continent with a stable craton/shield, platform,
      sedimentary interior basin, old subdued orogen, inland rift basin, and
      LIP/plateau highland.
    - It requires a broad lowland/platform mode plus a high-elevation tail,
      multiple continental detail classes, and parented landform objects.  The
      metric intentionally uses `p95-p05` relief rather than `IQR`, because
      Earth-like hypsometry can have a large lowland mode while still retaining
      highlands and basins.
  - Added `P29.flat_interior_detector`.
    - A synthetic one-elevation continental interior must be flagged as
      underexpressed: low relief, low detail diversity, few object kinds, and
      almost all inland cells in the flat-lowland band.
  - Tightened the diversity fixture so shield, old-subdued-orogen, rift-basin,
    and plateau objects must retain parent tectonic object ids.
  - Added `P29.unparented_speckle_detector`.
    - A deterministic high-relief checkerboard/speckle interior must be flagged
      when it lacks tectonic parentage or province-scale mechanism support.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P29 --out out_bench_p29_production_relief_gate_20260624_c`
    -> `pass`, `4/4` microbenchmarks.
  - Mechanism fixture: `p95-p05` relief `2420 m`, `p90-p10` relief `960 m`,
    detail diversity `6`, object kinds `6`, and parented shield,
    old-subdued-orogen, rift-basin, and plateau objects all present.
  - Speckle fixture: `speckle_detected = true`, `p90-p10` relief `1280 m`,
    parented object kinds `0`, high-component median size `1` cell.
  - Production relief fixture: land mask unchanged, shield/platform mean absolute
    delta `0 m`, old-orogen and plateau mean uplift `240 m`, interior-basin mean
    delta `-184 m`, rift-basin mean delta `-232 m`, applied-area fraction
    `0.110`, and mean absolute applied relief `223 m`.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p29_inland_geomorphology_complexity_gates_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p28_sediment_coupling_budget_gates_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p27_orogenic_foreland_response_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p26_recent_rework_footprint_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e1_real_earth_continental_landform_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `7 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
- Status:
  - P29 now has a first constrained production terrain increment:
    `TerrainModule._constrained_inland_geomorphology_relief`.
  - The increment applies only on already exposed broad continental interiors,
    uses crust stability/age/thickness, old suture/orogen age, rift potential,
    LIP/plateau domains, and continental-detail state, and clamps depressions
    above sea level.  It must not change land/ocean topology.
- Generated-world summary:
  - Added `aevum.diagnostics.p29_inland_geomorphology.inland_geomorphology_summary`.
  - P12 release entries now include `p29_inland_geomorphology`, with inland
    area, broad-interior hypsometry (`p95-p05`, `p90-p10`, IQR), flat-lowland
    fraction, highland-tail fraction, continental-detail diversity, inland
    landform object counts, and flags for monotone flat interiors / low object
    diversity.
  - Smoke output:
    `out_p29_inland_summary_earthlike_3000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - P29 inland metrics: inland area fraction of land `0.300`,
      `p95-p05` relief `1399 m`, flat-lowland fraction `0.504`, highland-tail
      fraction `0.049`, detail diversity `4`, inland object kinds `7`, inland
      landform objects `56`.
    - Diagnostic hints: `monotone_flat_inland = false`,
      `low_inland_object_diversity = false`,
      `broad_lowland_mode_present = true`, `highland_tail_present = true`.
  - Production smoke:
    `out_p29_inland_relief_earthlike_8000_t2500_rerun_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.258`, land components `3`, ribbon fraction `0.493`,
      basins `5`; warnings remain dominated by ribbon land, coastline
      complexity, and missing orogen/plateau provinces.
  - Follow-up from the inland-flatness review:
    `out_p29_inland_relief_platform_quantile_earthlike_8000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures; land fraction `0.258`, land
      components `3`, ribbon fraction `0.493`, basins `5`.
    - The constrained production relief applies to only `0.0039` of world area
      in this seed, with mean absolute applied relief `113 m`.
    - Inland broad-interior metrics still show an over-strong single platform
      mode: `p90-p10` relief `277 m`, IQR `36 m`, p25/p50/p75
      `1087/1090/1123 m`.  This matches the visual complaint that large inland
      areas remain too uniformly light green.
    - A wider old-orogen alignment attempt
      (`out_p29_inland_relief_old_orogen_aligned_earthlike_8000_t2500_20260624`)
      was rejected for this step: it did not improve IQR and regressed ribbon
      fraction to `0.503` / ocean basins to `8`.
    - Conclusion: the conservative P29 production pass is useful and safe, but
      it is not a full fix for monotone continental interiors.  The next real
      fix should move upstream into continental-surface regionalization and
      persistent inland province/state fields: craton shields, platform swells,
      intracratonic sag basins, old-orogen roots, rift shoulders, erosional
      escarpments, and dynamic-topography support.  Do not solve the remaining
      flatness by adding random texture or by broadening final-map postprocesses.
    - P29 inland metrics: inland `p95-p05` relief `1599 m`, `p90-p10` relief
      `277 m`, flat-lowland fraction `0.132`, highland fraction `0.074`,
      detail diversity `4`, object kinds `7`.
  - Production medium audit:
    `out_p29_inland_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.253`, land components `4`, ribbon fraction `0.424`,
      continental ribbon fraction `0.397`, largest-landmass coastline
      complexity `23.276`, basins `25`.
    - P29 inland metrics: inland `p95-p05` relief `2324 m`, `p90-p10` relief
      `216 m`, flat-lowland fraction `0.049`, highland fraction `0.081`,
      detail diversity `4`, object kinds `7`.
    - Last-frame production relief telemetry: applied-area fraction `0.00025`,
      uplift-area fraction `0.00025`, depression-area fraction `0.0`, mean
      absolute applied relief `174 m`.
  - Same-config P27 comparison:
    - Baseline:
      `out_p27_state_only_earthlike_24000_t2500_rerun_20260624`.
    - Candidate:
      `out_p29_inland_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`.
    - `aevum.diagnostics.p26_regression_gate.compare_p12_summaries` -> `pass`;
      no failed checks.  Release/validation status remained `warn`, land
      fraction stayed above the allowed floor, land components stayed `4`,
      exposed-land ribbon stayed within the no-regression limit, continental
      ribbon did not increase, coastline complexity stayed within limit, ocean
      basin count remained within range, and seam-ocean mismatch stayed `0`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p29_inland_geomorphology.py tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p29_inland_geomorphology_complexity_gates_pass -q`
    -> superseded by the parentage/speckle P29 gate above; the generated-world
    summary tests remain part of the P29 diagnostic layer.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
- Remaining production direction:
  - P29 did not solve the larger planform problems: generated Earthlike land
    still has too much ribbon area and an over-complex largest coastline.
  - Plateau expression remains weak in the generated-world P29 detail fractions,
    even though the controlled fixture covers plateau response.
  - P28 sediment coupling is still diagnostic-only; production still lacks a
    sediment mass-balance terrain response.
  - Do not add random or checkerboard visual texture.  Further relief or
    terrain-detail changes must keep the P29 parentage/speckle gates and the
    P26/P27 8000/24000 land-fraction, ribbon, coastline, and basin gates active.

2026-06-24 - P30/P31 inland-state repair and planform-reference gate

- Context:
  - P29 made a conservative production inland-relief pass safe, but the release
    summaries still warn on the larger land-planform problem: too much
    ribbon-like land and over-complex largest-landmass coastline.
  - The next tuning work needs a repeatable gate that separates planform errors
    from inland-relief or renderer issues.
- P30 repair:
  - Restored `P30.inland_state_drives_regional_surface`.
  - The fixture's `platform_swell` was over-thickened and high enough to be
    classified as a plateau margin under the current mechanism rules.  It now
    represents a moderate platform swell (`CONT_THICK + 5200 m`, initial
    elevation `820 m`) instead of a plateau.
  - Slightly raised old-orogen regional support from `90 m` to `105 m` so the
    E1 old-subdued-orogen fixture keeps visible residual relief instead of
    falling below the `70 m` median uplift gate.
  - Calibrated the P30 candidate inland-state regional relief amplitudes so
    shields, sag basins, old-orogen roots, rift axes, platform swells, and
    plateau margins produce a measurable regional signal without becoming
    final-map texture.  The fixture now raises inland p90-p10 relief from
    `240 m` to `333 m`, while mean absolute state relief remains only `38.8 m`.
- P31 planform reference:
  - Added `aevum.diagnostics.planform_reference.planform_reference_summary`.
    It accepts a `WorldState`, nested morphology metrics, or a P12 release
    entry.
  - Added `P31.broad_multicontinent_planform_reference`.
    A deterministic multi-continent fixture must stay inside the initial
    real-Earth planform envelope: land fraction, major component count, largest
    landmass share, exposed-land ribbon, and largest coastline complexity.
  - Added `P31.ribbon_land_planform_detector`.
    A deterministic narrow-ribbon fixture must be flagged by the ribbon metric.
  - Added `P31.overcomplex_coastline_planform_detector`.
    A broad but jagged coastline fixture must be flagged by coastline complexity
    without being treated as a pure ribbon-land failure.
  - The P31 summary carries the 8000/24000/72000 resolution ladder so high
    resolution is used as an audit policy, not as an excuse for missing process
    objects.
- Current generated-world baseline:
  - New 8000-cell smoke:
    `out_p31_planform_reference_earthlike_8000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - P31 diagnosis: land fraction `0.260` is in envelope, but component count
      `3`, largest landmass fraction `0.633`, ribbon fraction `0.501`, and
      largest coastline complexity `15.805` are out of the initial planform
      envelope.
  - Existing 24000-cell P29 baseline:
    `out_p29_inland_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P31 diagnosis: land fraction `0.253`, component count `4`, and largest
      landmass fraction `0.484` are in envelope.
    - Remaining planform failures are exposed-land ribbon `0.424` versus target
      max `0.35`, and coastline complexity `23.276` versus target max `8.0`.
    - Dominant severity is coastline complexity (`2.91x` reference max), then
      ribbon fraction (`1.21x` reference max).
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P31 --out out_bench_p31_planform_reference_20260624`
    -> `pass`, `3/3` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P30 --out out_bench_p30_inland_state_20260624_c`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py -q`
    -> `25 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
- Production direction selected from P31:
  - P32 was selected to address coastline complexity first, because it was the
    strongest P31 gap.  Candidate mechanisms had to be continent/margin
    lifecycle driven: inherited passive-margin smoothing, broad shelf/platform
    progradation, protected straits/seaways, and removal of unsupported
    inherited arc/suture slivers.
  - P33 was reserved for exposed-land ribbon reduction without forcing a single
    supercontinent: widen or merge only tectonically parented continental
    fragments, preserve real island arcs, and keep basin/seam continuity gates.

2026-06-24 - P32 object-constrained coastline simplification and first production fixture

- Context:
  - P31 established that the strongest remaining generated-world planform gap
    is largest-landmass coastline complexity, especially at the 24000-cell
    medium audit scale.
  - The residual should not be handled by renderer smoothing or arbitrary
    land/ocean flips; protected breakup seaways and shallow marginal seas must
    remain open.
- Changed:
  - Added `TerrainModule._simplify_modern_coastline_planform`, a conservative
    area-near-neutral local coastline pass.  It can drown only weak coastal
    teeth whose local perimeter contribution is positive and can fill only
    same-component shallow continental embayments.  The whole swap is reverted
    unless largest-component coastline complexity decreases, land area remains
    stable, component count does not regress, and protected breakup seaways
    remain ocean.
  - Added P32 telemetry to P12 release summaries:
    `terrain_p32_coastline_complexity_before`,
    `terrain_p32_coastline_complexity_after`,
    candidate areas, swap area, drowned cells, and filled cells.
  - Extended the P32 benchmark suite with a full production smoothing fixture
    and two focused checks:
    `P32.coastline_smoothing_reduces_complexity`,
    `P32.object_constrained_coastline_simplification` and
    `P32.protected_breakup_seaway_not_refilled`.
- Microbenchmark results:
  - `P32.coastline_smoothing_reduces_complexity`: coastline complexity
    `12.63 -> 11.71`, ribbon fraction `0.363 -> 0.329`, land fraction
    `0.252 -> 0.260`, protected seaway land fraction `0.0`.
  - `P32.object_constrained_coastline_simplification`: complexity delta
    `0.402`, land-fraction delta `0.00042`.
  - `P32.protected_breakup_seaway_not_refilled`: protected seaway remains
    ocean, complexity delta `0.405`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P32 --out out_bench_p32_coastline_complexity_20260624_b`
    -> `pass`, `3/3` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P30 --out out_bench_p30_inland_state_after_p32_20260624_c`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py -q`
    -> `25 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p29_inland_geomorphology_complexity_gates_pass -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
- Generated-world audits:
  - 8000-cell smoke:
    `out_p32_coastline_complexity_earthlike_8000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Against the previous 8000-cell P31 smoke, exposed-land ribbon improves
      from `0.501` to `0.486`, and largest coastline complexity improves from
      `15.805` to `15.443`.
