    - Land fraction is `0.260`, component count is `3`, largest land component
      is `0.648`, and largest coastline complexity remains outside the initial
      reference envelope.
    - Modern coastline smoothing telemetry shows drowned area `0.020`, fill
      area `0.0`, and payback area `0.035`.  P32 local swap telemetry is still
      `0.0`, so this generated-world improvement is mostly the broader
      mature-coastline smoothing/payback path, not yet a local P32 swap.
    - Ocean bathymetry screening is no longer the immediate blocker:
      nearshore superdeep water is `0.0`, and far-ocean shallow water is
      `0.089`, both inside the current envelope.
  - 24000-cell audit:
    `out_p32_coastline_simplification_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.250`, land components `4`, largest landmass `0.484`,
      exposed-land ribbon `0.429`, largest coastline complexity `23.526`,
      ocean basins `22`.
    - `p26_regression_gate` versus
      `out_p29_inland_relief_earthlike_24000_t2500_20260624` passes with no
      failed checks, but the primary P31 coastline/ribbon metrics do not
      improve at 24000.
- Interpretation:
  - P32 is a useful guardrail and first production fixture, not a full
    production fix for the generated-world coastline problem.
  - The 24000 residual is not dominated by removable one-cell teeth.  It is
    dominated by underwide, recently reworked accretionary/suture continental
    margins and inherited planform geometry.  The next production phase should
    move upstream: margin/platform lifecycle smoothing, parented shelf
    progradation, and accreted/suture sliver consolidation with explicit
    8000/24000 no-regression gates.

2026-06-24 - P33 parented sliver consolidation microbenchmark

- Context:
  - P32 proved that local coastline cleanup can be made safe, but the 24000-cell
    generated world still shows over-complex coastlines and too much ribbon
    land.  The next target is upstream crustal state: old accreted or sutured
    strips attached to a broad continent should not remain permanently
    classified as active terrane/suture ribbons.
- Changed:
  - Added `TectonicsModule._consolidate_parented_margin_slivers`.
  - The pass changes only crustal state (`origin`, `age`, `thickness`,
    `stability`) and never flips land/ocean cells.
  - It can mature quiet old `ARC`/`SUTURE` strips only when they are adjacent to
    a stable parent continental anchor.  Recently reworked active belts and
    detached island arcs are excluded.
  - Hooked the pass after broad continental-province maturation and before
    cratonization, so terrain later sees these old strips as margin/platform
    state instead of active accretionary/suture ribbons.
  - Added P33 telemetry to P12 release summaries:
    candidate cells, consolidated cells, area fraction, active-preserved cells,
    and detached-preserved cells.
  - Added `P33.parented_margin_sliver_consolidation`.
- Microbenchmark result:
  - Candidate old parented sliver cells: `46`.
  - Consolidated cells in one step: `6`, area fraction `0.00083`.
  - Mature accretionary/suture cells drop `46 -> 40`.
  - Shape pressure drops `0.129 -> 0.090`.
  - Active belt changed cells: `0`; detached arc changed cells: `0`; land mask
    unchanged.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P33 --out out_bench_p33_parented_sliver_20260624_c`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
- Generated-world audits:
  - The first P33 production attempt ran too early in a 2500 Myr audit and
    regressed planform metrics.  The pass is now gated to mature late-stage
    continents (`>=3000 Myr`, and at least `70%` of the configured run age).
  - 8000-cell, 2500 Myr no-regression audit:
    `out_p33_parented_sliver_earthlike_8000_t2500_20260624_b`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.260`, components `3`, exposed-land ribbon `0.486`,
      largest-landmass coastline complexity `15.443`.
    - P33 telemetry is zero, as expected under the mature-stage gate.
    - `p26_regression_gate` versus the P32 8000 baseline passes with no failed
      checks.
  - 8000-cell, 4500 Myr mature audit:
    `out_p33_parented_sliver_earthlike_8000_t4500_20260624`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.237`, components `2`, exposed-land ribbon `0.495`,
      largest-landmass coastline complexity `11.551`.
    - P33 had no mature parented candidates at final time: candidate cells `0`,
      consolidated cells `0`, active-preserved cells `9`, detached-preserved
      cells `395`.
    - Terrain detail remains dominated by arc/microcontinent land
      (`0.544` of land), while platform fraction is only `0.374`.
- Interpretation:
  - P33 is a conservative mechanism-layer repair, not yet a visual production
    victory.  It establishes the correct state transition for old parented
    slivers and records generated-world telemetry.
  - The generated-world audits show P33 currently triggers too rarely in the
    mature Earth-like output.  The dominant remaining issue is upstream:
    continental platform/parent-margin objects are underproduced, leaving too
    much exposed land classified as arc/microcontinent rather than stable
    platform, foreland, shelf, or old subdued orogen.
  - The next follow-up should therefore be parented shelf/platform progradation
    and margin lifecycle widening from existing continent objects, not another
    renderer cleanup or random land flip.

2026-06-25 - P34 parented platform/progradation mechanism and no-regression audit

- Context:
  - P33 established a safe state transition for old parented accretion/suture
    slivers, but generated-world audits showed no mature parented candidates at
    final time.  The remaining 8000-cell mature Earth-like output still has too
    much exposed arc/microcontinent land and too little stable platform/craton
    expression.
  - The target mechanism is an upstream analogue of passive-margin shelf and
    platform progradation: quiet cells adjacent to stable parent continental
    anchors may inherit continental cargo, while active rifts/ridges and
    explicit breakup corridors remain protected.
- Changed:
  - Added `_parented_platform_progradation_zone` and
    `_protected_breakup_corridor_mask` in `TectonicsModule`.
  - Wired the new zone into `_conserve_continental` as a restoration process,
    not active arc accretion.  Restored cells inherit parent origin/age/thickness
    through `_continental_margin_parent`.
  - Added P34 telemetry to P12 release summaries:
    candidate cells, restored cells, restored area fraction, and protected
    cells.
  - Added `P34.parented_platform_progradation` microbenchmark and pytest.
  - Initial production trigger from `70%` of run age was too broad and made
    mature generated-world audits excessively slow.  P34 is now final-stage
    gated (`>=98%` of configured run age) and area-capped per step.
- Microbenchmark result:
  - `P34.parented_platform_progradation` passes.
  - In the fixture, continental fraction rises `0.0272 -> 0.0294`.
  - Restored cells: `16`; all are parented gains, `0` arc-origin gains.
  - Old attached arc restored cells: `15`; old shell restored cells: `1`.
  - Protected seaway, detached arc, and active-rift gains are all `0`.
  - Protected candidate cells excluded: `6`.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P34 --out out_bench_p34_parented_platform_20260625`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p34_parented_platform_progradation_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p34_parented_platform_progradation_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `5 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Generated-world audits:
  - 2500-cell, 4500 Myr summary:
    `out_p34_parented_platform_earthlike_2500_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Runtime `76.1s`.
    - P12 decision `fail` due to existing low-resolution ocean-basin seam gate:
      `ocean basin ids break across antimeridian seam`.
    - P34 telemetry is zero, so this failure is not caused by P34.
    - Morphology is otherwise improved at this low resolution: land fraction
      `0.255`, components `2`, ribbon `0.368`, largest coastline complexity
      `7.88`.
  - 8000-cell, 4500 Myr summary:
    `out_p34_parented_platform_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Runtime `211.2s`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Metrics match the P33 8000/4500 baseline: land fraction `0.237`,
      components `2`, ribbon `0.495`, largest coastline complexity `11.551`.
    - P34 telemetry is zero: candidate cells `0`, restored cells `0`.
    - Regression gate versus P33 passes with no failed checks.
- Interpretation:
  - P34 is a valid and guarded mechanism, but it does not yet improve the
    generated world because the generated crust does not create eligible
    stable-parent/old-shell candidates by final time.
 - The next phase should not tune P34 thresholds blindly.  P35 should diagnose
   why stable parent continental anchors and old passive-platform shells are
   underproduced: likely causes are excessive late oceanic/arc exposure,
   insufficient preservation of ancient continental area, and missing
   continent-object lineage for passive margins.

2026-06-25 - P35/P36 continental-debt diagnostics, old-platform anchors, and
terrain-object recovery

- Context:
  - P34 was safe but generated-world telemetry stayed zero.  The 8000/4500
    Earth-like output had continental area `0.147`, exposed oceanic land
    `0.399`, arc/microcontinent land `0.544`, and P35 continental gap
    `0.1605`, but no eligible parented restoration candidates.
  - The suspected ordering issue was that craton/platform readiness was
    recognized too late to supply parent anchors during continental
    conservation.
- Changed:
  - Added age/thickness/reworked-aware stable-parent anchor recognition for
    P35: old, quiet, thick primordial platform cores and their supported
    platform margins can act as parent anchors in deterministic fixtures.
  - Added P35/P36 telemetry for old platform anchors and pre-conservation
    craton-readiness attempts in release summaries.
  - Added `P36.mature_platform_anchor_recovery` and
    `P36.preconserve_craton_readiness` microbenchmarks.
  - Added terrain object recovery for generated-world regression coverage:
    inactive low-rift-potential rift/sag basins can emit `interior_basin`
    objects, and stabilized highland orogen roots can emit
    `old_subdued_orogen` objects.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P36 --out out_bench_p36_mature_platform_anchor_20260625_e`
    -> `pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p34_parented_platform_progradation_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `7 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p34_parented_platform_progradation_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `8 passed`.
- Generated-world audit:
  - Final retained-code 8000/4500 summary:
    `out_p36_guarded_old_platform_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Runtime `195.5s`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Regression gate versus P35 passes with no failed checks.
    - Metrics match the P35 no-regression baseline: land fraction `0.237`,
      components `2`, ribbon `0.495`, continental area `0.147`, exposed
      oceanic land `0.399`, arc/microcontinent land `0.544`.
    - P35/P36 production telemetry remains zero: old-platform anchors `0`,
      debt candidates `0`, restored cells `0`.
  - A rejected production attempt at
    `out_p36_craton_readiness_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    raised continental area to `0.277` and reduced exposed oceanic land, but
    failed release due to severe narrow continental fragmentation: land ribbon
    `0.694`, continental components `35`, coastline complexity `23.36`.
- Interpretation:
  - P36 proves the missing process in isolation, but enabling craton/readiness
    before conservation without continent-object shape constraints creates many
    narrow preserved fragments.  The next phase should not simply raise
    continental preservation or craton targets.
 - Next required design step: shape-aware continent-object preservation and
   parent-margin restoration, with explicit caps on component count, width,
   and coastline/ribbon response before any further continental-area recovery
   is allowed in production.

2026-06-25 - P37 shape-aware restoration guard microbenchmark

- Context:
  - P36 showed that old-platform/craton readiness can identify a missing
    process, but a direct generated-world production attempt increased
    continental area by preserving many narrow fragments.  The next gate must
    therefore ask whether a candidate restoration is attached to a broad parent
    continent object, not merely whether the cell is old and parented.
- Changed:
  - Added `_shape_guard_continental_restoration(...)` in `TectonicsModule`.
    It evaluates each candidate restoration patch against its single parent
    continent component using graph-width and narrow-area ratios.
  - Added optional telemetry for shape-guard input, accepted, and rejected
    cells.  The guard is only active when
    `tectonics.enable_shape_guarded_debt_recovery` is set.
  - Added `P37.shape_guard_restoration` microbenchmark.  The fixture contains
    a broad parent continent and a narrow ribbon fragment; the guard must
    accept restoration on the broad parent and reject restoration on the
    ribbon fragment.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P37 --out out_bench_p37_shape_guard_20260625_final`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p34_parented_platform_progradation_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `8 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Interpretation:
  - P37 closes the first safety hole exposed by the rejected P36 production
    attempt: restoration can now be rejected when the parent component is a
    ribbon.  It does not yet solve generated-world continental-area recovery,
    because production old-platform recovery remains disabled.
 - Next step: connect shape-guarded recovery to continent-object lineage and
   run a guarded old-platform recovery audit.  Acceptance should require
   non-regression of land ribbon fraction, continental component count, and
   coastline complexity before any area increase is considered useful.

2026-06-25 - P38 lineage-aware restoration guard and guarded audit

- Context:
  - P37 prevented restoration around narrow parent components but did not
    distinguish stable continent-object lineage from broad orphan fragments.
    The rejected P36 production attempt showed that recovery must be tied to
    persistent continent identity, not only local graph shape.
- Changed:
  - Added `P12RunConfig.global_overrides` and CLI
    `python -m aevum.cli p12 --set-global KEY=VALUE`, so guarded tectonic
    recovery experiments can be reproduced without editing presets or source.
  - Added optional lineage gating inside
    `_shape_guard_continental_restoration(...)`.  When
    `tectonics.enable_lineage_guarded_debt_recovery` is set, a restoration
    parent component must be dominated by one previous `tectonics.continent_id`
    and that id must map to a broad `tectonics.continents` object.
  - Added P38 release telemetry for lineage-guard input, accepted, and rejected
    cells.
  - Added `P38.lineage_guard_restoration` microbenchmark.  It proves that
    shape-only guard accepts both broad lineaged and broad orphan parents, but
    lineage guard accepts only the valid broad continent object and rejects
    orphan/ribbon parents.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P38 --out out_bench_p38_lineage_guard_20260625_b`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p31_planform_reference_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p33_parented_margin_sliver_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p34_parented_platform_progradation_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `9 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Generated-world audit:
  - Guarded 2500/4500 summary:
    `out_p38_guarded_old_platform_earthlike_2500_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - P12 decision `fail` due to the existing low-resolution ocean-basin
      antimeridian seam diagnostic.
    - P35/P37/P38 telemetry remains zero, so the failure is unrelated to the
      new guards.
  - Guarded 8000/4500 summary:
    `out_p38_guarded_old_platform_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Regression gate versus P36 passes with no failed checks.
    - Metrics remain unchanged from the P36 no-regression baseline: land
      fraction `0.237`, components `2`, ribbon `0.495`, continental area
      `0.147`, exposed oceanic land `0.399`.
    - Cumulative telemetry from
      `out_p38_lineage_guarded_old_platform_cumulative_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
      shows the guarded path is active but conservative: cumulative P35 debt
      candidates `1`, restored cells `0`; P37 shape guard input `1`,
      accepted `0`, rejected `1`; P38 lineage guard input `1`, accepted `0`,
      rejected `1`.
- Interpretation:
  - P38 closes the second safety hole: production recovery can now be tied to
    broad, persistent continent objects when candidates exist.
  - Generated worlds produce too few old-platform candidates, and the only
    8000/4500 candidate is rejected by the shape/lineage guards.  P39 should
    therefore add an old-platform-anchor funnel diagnostic: count cells blocked
    by age, reworking, thickness, source origin, width, stability, and lineage
    before any further production restoration is attempted.

2026-06-25 - P39 old-platform-anchor funnel diagnostic

- Context:
  - P38 proved shape and lineage guards are safe, but it did not explain why
    generated worlds rarely enter the guarded recovery path.  The next useful
    step was diagnostic, not another threshold change.
- Changed:
  - Added shared old-platform funnel logic in `aevum/modules/tectonics.py`.
    `_stable_parent_anchor_mask(...)` and production telemetry now use the same
    source/thickness/rework/age/width/stability thresholds.
  - Added P39 release telemetry under `tectonic_object_telemetry`, including
    per-gate counts for source, thickness, quiet/reworked state, mature/old
    age, width, stability, old-platform core/margin, mature platform, and
    lineaged versus orphan process anchors.
  - Added `P39.old_platform_anchor_funnel` microbenchmark.  The fixture has a
    valid broad lineaged old platform, a broad orphan platform, and deliberate
    false candidates blocked by source, thickness, recent rework, young age,
    low stability, and narrow width.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P39 --out out_bench_p39_old_platform_funnel_20260625_b`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `6 passed`.
- Generated-world audit:
  - Guarded 8000/4500 summary:
    `out_p39_old_platform_funnel_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.237`, land components `2`, land ribbon fraction
      `0.495`, continental crust fraction `0.147`.
    - P35 gap `0.1605`, stable parent cells `27`, old-platform anchors `0`,
      debt candidates `0`, P37/P38 guard inputs `0`.
    - P39 funnel over `1036` continental cells: source-ok `808`, thick-ok
      `822`, quiet `0`, recently reworked `1036`, mature-age `41`, old-age
      `27`, width>=3 `628`, width>=4 `455`, stability>=0.30 `133`,
      stability>=0.36 `27`.
    - Old-platform core `0`, old-platform margin `0`, mature platform `0`;
      process anchors `27` are all legacy lineaged anchors, not new mature
      platform anchors.
- Interpretation:
  - The blocker is upstream continental-state history, not the new recovery
    guards.  By final time, the generated continent has been marked recently
    reworked almost everywhere; only a small remnant remains old and stable.
  - P40 should therefore repair continental age/rework/stability semantics:
    preserve quiet craton/platform interiors through passive-margin reshaping,
    avoid global rework-age resets during benign relabeling, and distinguish
    true tectonic reactivation from object maintenance.

2026-06-25 - P40/P41/P42 rework semantics and shape-supported craton guard

- Context:
  - P39 showed that old-platform recovery was blocked because the generated
    continent was almost entirely recent-reworked.  The next changes therefore
    targeted process semantics before any broad threshold retuning.
- Changed:
  - P40 separates quiet parented restoration from true active accretion:
    parented restoration and shape maintenance now inherit parent rework age,
    while explicit active accretion still stamps current rework.
  - P41 adds an opt-in mature continental-debt priority rule.  When enabled
    and parented restoration candidates exist, the conservation pass prefers
    them and defers active-only accretion candidates instead of spending the
    area budget on new arc/island crust.
  - P42 adds shape support to craton/core semantics.  Narrow continental
    ribbons keep their age/provenance archive but are capped below core
    stability, `crust_domain` no longer labels width-1/2 ribbons as craton,
    and `_maintain_cratonic_kernels(...)` no longer falls back to promoting an
    all-ribbon continent.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P40 --out out_bench_p40_rework_semantics_20260625_c`
    -> `pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P41 --out out_bench_p41_mature_debt_priority_20260625`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P42 --out out_bench_p42_shape_supported_craton_20260625_c`
    -> `pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `9 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Generated-world audits:
  - P40 guarded 8000/4500:
    `out_p40_rework_semantics_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Decision `warn`; land `0.220`, components `16`, land ribbon `0.539`,
      continental ribbon `0.283`, continental area `0.162`.
    - P39 quiet cells `117`, process anchors `119`, P35 restored `7`.
    - P40 telemetry shows `7` inherited parented restoration cells but `154`
      reactivated cells, so conservation still spent much of the mature
      deficit on active-only accretion.
  - P41 guarded 8000/4500:
    `out_p41_mature_debt_priority_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Decision `fail`; land `0.284`, components `14`, land ribbon `0.571`.
    - Almost all continental crust became ancient/stable
      (`ancient_continental_fraction_gt2500_myr=0.995`), exposing that
      craton/stability semantics were protecting narrow ribbons.
  - P41 default-enabled after P42:
    `out_p42_shape_supported_craton_p41default_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Decision `fail`; land `0.233`, components `12`, land ribbon `0.763`,
      ocean basins `21`.
    - This confirms P41 must remain opt-in until terrain/payback constraints
      stop converting deferred active accretion into highly ribboned exposed
      land.
  - P42 guarded 8000/4500:
    `out_p42_shape_supported_craton_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - Decision `warn`; land `0.242`, components `13`, land ribbon `0.479`,
      continental ribbon `0.434`, continental area `0.242`.
    - Ancient/stable fractions are no longer inflated
      (`ancient_continental_fraction_gt2500_myr=0.030`,
      `stable_craton_fraction_gt075=0.025`), but this now triggers the old
      "ancient continental crust fraction is low" warning.
    - `p26_regression_gate` versus P40 fails on continental ribbon, largest
      coastline complexity, and ocean basin count.  P42 is therefore a useful
      correction to the P41 hard failure, not a final quality gate pass.
- Interpretation:
  - The next blocker is not the old-platform anchor funnel.  The terrain
    cleanup stack removes unsupported land but then `_pay_back_modern_coastline_land(...)`
    restores area without a strong enough broad-component/embayment constraint,
    preserving many components and a high ribbon fraction.
  - P43 should constrain modern coastline payback and final component cleanup:
    payback should only fill same-component embayments adjacent to broad
    interiors/platforms, should avoid creating or preserving small separate
    components, and must be validated against the P40/P42 regression metrics.

2026-06-25 - P43 mature active-only accretion guard experiment

- Context:
  - The P42 production state has no release hard failure, but it still fails the
    P39 regression gate: land components rise from `2` to `13`, continental
    ribbon rises from `0.344` to `0.434`, largest-coastline complexity rises
    from `11.551` to `12.714`, and ocean basins collapse from `9` to `2`.
  - Telemetry shows the final conservation pass still spends most of the deficit
    on active-only accretion (`p40_parented_restoration_reactivated_cells=290`)
    while stable-parent/old-platform recovery is inactive.
- Changed:
  - Added P43 telemetry for mature active-only conservation:
    candidate cells, accepted cells, rejected cells, accepted area fraction, and
    cap area fraction.
  - Added `tectonics.enable_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard`.  It is
    default-off.  When explicitly enabled, mature active-only candidates require
    broad-parent geometric support and obey a per-step process cap.
  - Added `P43.mature_active_accretion_shape_guard` microbenchmark.  The fixture
    verifies that unsupported active-only protrusions are rejected and accepted
    mature active-only area stays below the process cap.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P43 --out out_bench_p43_mature_active_guard_20260625_d`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `10 passed`.
- Generated-world audits:
  - Default-off audit:
    `out_p43_default_off_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Metrics match the P42-style state: land `0.242`, components `13`, land
      ribbon `0.479`, continental ribbon `0.434`, continental area `0.242`,
      stable craton fraction `0.025`, ancient continental fraction `0.030`.
    - P43 telemetry is inactive: candidates `0`, accepted `0`, rejected `0`.
    - Regression gate versus P39 still fails on component count, continental
      ribbon, largest coastline complexity, and ocean-basin count.
  - Strict P43-on experiment:
    `out_p43_mature_active_guard_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - P12 decision `fail`; land ribbon `0.667`.
    - P35/P39 diagnostics reappear (`p35_stable_parent_cells=59`, P39 funnel
      continental cells `1296`) and active-only accepted cells fall to `103`,
      but the map becomes under-repaired and more ribbon-dominated.
  - Relaxed-support P43-on experiment:
    `out_p43_mature_active_guard_relaxed_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`.
    - P12 decision `fail`; land ribbon `0.763`, continental components `17`.
    - Final P43 telemetry is `0` because the earlier active-only restriction
      changed the evolutionary path before the final step; this confirmed that
      active-only restriction alone is not a safe production repair.
- Interpretation:
  - P43 is useful as a microbenchmark and telemetry hook, but it cannot be
    enabled by default until a replacement parented-recovery path exists.
  - The next blocker is conservation ordering: mature worlds need broad
    parented shelf/platform recovery before active-only arc accretion is capped.
    Otherwise the model either overfills with narrow active margins or starves
    continental repair and exposes oceanic ribbon land.
- P44 next-step test plan:
  - Add a fixture with both lineaged old-platform pockets and active-only arc
    candidates.  The accepted cells must mostly come from parented pockets,
    inherit quiet parent rework state, and improve or preserve parent width.
  - In production, parented recovery should run before active-only accretion
    during mature continental conservation, and P43's active-only guard should
    remain default-off until P44 passes 8000-cell regression gates.
  - The 8000-cell acceptance target remains: no P12 hard failure, land component
    count within P39 tolerance, continental ribbon within P39 tolerance, largest
    coastline complexity not worse than P39 tolerance, and ocean-basin count in
    the P39 regression range.

2026-06-25 - P43/P44 guarded recovery cleanup and production audit

- Context:
  - The worktree had two separate `run_p43_bench(...)` definitions.  The later
    coastline-payback suite shadowed the mature-active suite, so P43 tests and
    CLI summaries could not both represent the actual P43 acceptance surface.
  - P44 parented recovery exists and has a valid fixture, but generated-world
    audits showed it is not production-ready as a default path.  With P44 on,
    the 8000-cell Earth-like release failed: land `0.212`, components `18`,
    exposed-land ribbon `0.568`, and largest-coastline complexity `15.831`.
  - The default path also temporarily failed after an over-strict P43 payback
    constraint made `terrain_modern_coastline_payback_area_fraction=0.0`.  The
    root cause was not lack of recovery need (`~0.047-0.050` world fraction),
    but domain gating: supported water-side continental cells were mostly
    old accreted/suture collage, and the strict rule treated all such domains as
    active ribbon rather than possible welded continental margin.
- Changed:
  - Consolidated P43 into one suite with three microbenchmarks:
    `P43.mature_active_accretion_shape_guard`,
    `P43.payback_fills_broad_embayment_not_terrane_ribbon`, and
    `P43.payback_recovers_supported_accreted_collage`.
  - Kept P44 as explicit opt-in via
    `tectonics.enable_mature_parented_recovery`; production default remains off
    until an 8000-cell release audit proves it improves the generated world.
  - Added payback filter telemetry to release summaries:
    `base_ocean_cont`, `same_component`, `broad_supported`, `support_ok`,
    `domain_ok`, `geometry_ok`, and final `candidate` counts/area fractions.
  - Reworked modern coastline payback so same-component embayments backed by
    broad support or strong embayment geometry may recover old accreted/suture
    collage margins.  This models welded continental collage rather than
    treating every suture label as an active island chain.
  - Retuned `p26_regression_gate` ocean-basin acceptance so an under-partitioned
    baseline such as `2` basins does not reject a candidate in the Earth-like
    basin-count range.  The gate still rejects collapsed or heavily over-fragmented
    basin partitions.
- Verification:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P43 --out out_bench_p43_supported_collage_payback_v5_20260625`
    -> `pass`, `3/3` microbenchmarks.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P44 --out out_bench_p44_parented_recovery_after_payback_support_20260625`
    -> `pass`, `1/1` microbenchmark.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_coastline_payback_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p44_parented_recovery_precedes_active_only_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_p26_regression_gate.py -q`
    -> `16 passed`.
- Generated-world audit:
  - `out_p44_default_off_supported_collage_payback_v2_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    at 8000 cells, `t=4500 Myr`, P44 default-off and P35/P38/P39 recovery
    diagnostics enabled:
    - P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land fraction `0.244`, components `11`, exposed-land ribbon `0.478`,
      continental ribbon `0.434`, largest-coastline complexity `12.706`.
    - Payback restored `0.046` world area from `0.064` candidate area, with
      recovery need `0.100`; P44 telemetry stayed inactive (`0` restored).
    - Compared with the P43 default-off baseline, land is `+0.0027`, component
      count is `-2`, exposed-land ribbon is `-0.0002`, largest-coastline
      complexity is `-0.008`, and basin count moves from `2` to `4`.
  - `p26_regression_gate` versus
    `out_p43_default_off_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`:
    `out_p44_default_off_supported_collage_payback_v2_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625/p44_supported_collage_regression_report_vs_p43_v2.json`
    -> `pass`, failed checks `none`.
    - Remaining warnings are still real: land area is low versus Earth, ancient
      continental crust is low, exposed-land ribbon remains too high, and the
      largest coastline is over-complex.
- Interpretation:
  - P43 now restores the previous no-hard-failure production state without
    reopening protected seaways or blindly filling isolated terrane/suture
    ribbons.
  - This is still not a final Earth-like morphology pass.  The dominant residual
    driver remains young/unstable accretionary-margin and suture ribbon inside a
    single continental crust component.  The next productive phase should move
    upstream: reduce overproduction of active suture/terrane labels and build
    broad craton/platform/interior provinces before terrain cleanup has to
    compensate.
  - P44 is useful as a mechanism fixture, but enabling it by default currently
    worsens generated-world morphology.  The next P44 iteration must be judged
    by 8000-cell release metrics, not only by its fixture.

2026-06-25 - P45/P46 process-provenance localization and default mature-collage
path

- Context:
  - P45 introduced a thin process-provenance mask so broad collision/subduction
    deformation shoulders can affect relief without rewriting whole mature
    continental interiors as young `ORIGIN_SUTURE` or `ORIGIN_ARC`.
  - The first P45 default audit regressed the 8000-cell Earth-like world from
    P43/P44 `warn` to `fail`: land `0.229`, components `18`, exposed-land
    ribbon `0.637`, continental-crust area only `0.061`, and exposed oceanic
    land `0.774` of land.  Payback candidates fell from `0.064` to `0.009`
    world area.
  - Root cause: P45 correctly localized active provenance, but the mature
    continental path no longer had enough semantically quiet parent-supported
    collage to feed later continental recovery.  At 8000 cells the world then
    repaid area debt through active/oceanic arc chains instead of welded
    continental margin/platform material.
- Changed:
  - Kept P45 process-provenance localization and telemetry:
    `tectonics.last_p45_collision_process_cells`,
    `tectonics.last_p45_collision_provenance_cells`,
    `tectonics.last_p45_arc_process_cells`, and
    `tectonics.last_p45_arc_provenance_cells`.
  - Made P46 mature parent-supported accretionary/suture collage maturation part
    of the default mature-continent path by changing
    `tectonics.enable_mature_parent_supported_collage` default from off to on.
    Explicitly setting the global to `0` still disables the pass for experiments.
  - Updated the P46 microbenchmark fixture so it passes without a manual enable
    flag; this makes the test cover the production default rather than only an
    opt-in branch.
- Verification:
  - Syntax check:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/tectonics.py aevum/diagnostics/tectonics_bench.py aevum/diagnostics/release_gate.py tests/test_tectonics_bench.py`
    -> pass.
  - P45/P46 focused microbenchmarks:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p45_process_provenance_localization_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p46_mature_parent_supported_collage_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - P35-P46 focused regression plus P12 smoke:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_coastline_payback_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p44_parented_recovery_precedes_active_only_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p45_process_provenance_localization_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p46_mature_parent_supported_collage_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `14 passed`.
  - Default 8000-cell Earth-like audit:
    `out_p46_default_on_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land `0.244`, components `11`, exposed-land ribbon `0.478`,
      continental ribbon `0.434`, largest-coastline complexity `12.706`.
    - Continental-crust area `0.242`, ancient continental fraction `0.030`,
      stable craton fraction `0.025`.
    - Modern coastline payback candidate area `0.064`; restored area `0.046`.
  - Three-preset smoke after default-on P46:
    `out_p46_default_on_three_preset_3000_t4500_smoke_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Earth-like `warn`: land `0.242`, components `2`, ribbon `0.321`.
    - Arid `pass`: land `0.784`, components `3`, ribbon `0.249`.
    - Waterworld `pass`: land `0.030`, components `34`, ribbon `0.450`.
- Interpretation:
  - The production default is back to the P43/P44 no-hard-failure baseline while
    retaining P45's more geologically defensible separation between active
    process footprints and persistent crust provenance.
  - P46 is not a cosmetic terrain cleanup.  It is a tectonic-state maturation
    rule: old parent-supported accreted/suture collage becomes continental
    platform/interior before later conservation and terrain passes need to make
    land/sea decisions.
  - Remaining warnings are still real and should not be hidden: ancient/stable
    craton area is low, exposed-land ribbon is still high, and the largest
    coastline remains over-complex.  The next phase should build broader
    craton/platform/interior provinces from tectonic history rather than adding
    another late terrain smoothing pass.

2026-06-25 - P47 conservative mature cratonic shield-core target

- Context:
  - After P46, the default Earth-like output no longer hard-failed, but stable
    craton and ancient continental crust remained too low: stable craton
    fraction `0.025` of continental area and ancient `>2500 Myr` continental
    fraction `0.030`.
  - The previous `_cratonic_kernel_target_fraction(...)` was a fixed `0.025` of
    continental area.  That is too low for a mature Earth analogue with broad
    shields/platforms, and it makes old continental interiors too easy to lose
    through later rasterized plate evolution.
  - Reference logic used for this phase:
    - Treat cratons as stable continental interiors with shield/platform
      expression, not arbitrary old strips
      (`https://www.britannica.com/science/craton`).
    - Keep the distinction between rigid plate interiors and deforming boundary
      networks, consistent with resolved-topology reconstruction practice
      (`https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/pygplates_primer.html`).
- Changed:
  - Added P47 release telemetry:
    `tectonics_p47_craton_target_fraction`,
    `tectonics_p47_pre_stable_craton_fraction`,
    `tectonics_p47_promoted_craton_cells`, and
    `tectonics_p47_promoted_craton_area_fraction`.
  - Replaced the fixed cratonic target with a time-dependent mature shield-core
    target.  The first attempt targeted `~0.15` of continental area by maturity;
    the 8000-cell generated-world audit showed that was too aggressive for the
    current terrain/conservation coupling.
  - Retuned P47 to a conservative high-stability craton-core target: after
    `900 Myr` the target grows with cooling/maturation and reaches `~0.08` of
    continental area.  Broader ancient platform aprons are intentionally left for
    a later lower-stability platform phase instead of being promoted to hard
    craton.
  - Added `P47.mature_cratonic_kernel_target` microbenchmark.  It verifies that
    mature broad continental interiors can grow stable/ancient cratonic kernels,
    the target is time-dependent, and ancient ribbon continents are not promoted.
- Failed experiment recorded:
  - `out_p47_craton_kernel_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    used the aggressive `0.15` mature target and failed:
    - P12 decision `fail`.
    - Land `0.227`, components `7`, exposed-land ribbon `0.550`.
    - Continental area fell to `0.170`; exposed oceanic land rose to `0.356`.
    - Ancient fraction rose to `0.164` and stable craton to `0.151`, but the
      map regressed because parented recovery/payback was starved.
  - Interpretation: high-stability craton cores and lower-stability ancient
    platforms must be separated.  One high-stability target cannot represent
    both shield cores and platform aprons.
- Verification:
  - Syntax check:

    -> `3 passed`.

    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_coastline_payback_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p44_parented_recovery_precedes_active_only_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p45_process_provenance_localization_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p46_mature_parent_supported_collage_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p47_mature_cratonic_kernel_target_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `15 passed`.

    `out_p47_craton_kernel_conservative_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land `0.257`, components `4`, largest land component `0.550`.
    - Exposed-land ribbon `0.373`, continental ribbon `0.422`, largest-coastline
      complexity `8.039`.
    - Continental area `0.289`, ancient continental fraction `0.387`, stable
      craton fraction `0.314`, exposed oceanic land `0.000`.
  - Three-preset smoke:
    `out_p47_conservative_three_preset_3000_t4500_smoke_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Earth-like `warn`: land `0.234`, components `9`, ribbon `0.508`.
    - Arid `pass`: land `0.784`, components `3`, ribbon `0.249`.
    - Waterworld `pass`: land `0.030`, components `25`, ribbon `0.538`.
- Interpretation:
  - P47 materially improves the Earth-like generated world: it removes the
    ancient-crust warning, eliminates exposed oceanic land in the 8000-cell audit,
    reduces components from `11` to `4`, and reduces exposed-land ribbon from
    `0.478` to `0.373` without introducing hard failures.
  - The high final stable fraction is not produced by final-step promotion; the
    final P47 promoted-cell telemetry is `0`, because stable nuclei were already
    preserved earlier in the history.  That is the intended direction: old cores
    should influence subsequent evolution, not be painted on at the end.
  - Remaining warnings are now narrower:
    - ribbon is still high versus Earth-reference target;
    - largest-coastline complexity is barely above the warning threshold;
    - real lower-stability ancient platform aprons/interior basins are still not
      separately generated.
  - Next phase should be P48: split craton shield, buried platform, and interior
    basin as separate object/province states, so ancient continental crust can be
    broad without all old platform becoming high-stability craton.

2026-06-25 - P48 cratonic province object layer

- Context:
  - P47 separated high-stability craton cores from broader old continental
    crust, but terrain still had to infer `shield`, `platform`, and
    `interior_basin` mostly from per-cell stability/thickness/elevation rules.
  - That made interior continental landforms weakly parented: the generated
    terrain could contain platform/interior-basin objects without a matching
    tectonic province object explaining why those regions existed.
- Changed:
  - Added deterministic tectonic province objects:
    `tectonics.shields`, `tectonics.platforms`, and
    `tectonics.interior_basins`.
  - Added `TectonicsModule._cratonic_province_objects(...)`.  It uses broad
    continental width, old/quiet crust age, recent rework age, crust stability,
    crust domain, and relative platform thickness.  It does not mutate
    `crust.type`, thickness, origin, stability, or the land mask.
  - Added P48 release telemetry:
    `tectonics_p48_shield_object_count`,
    `tectonics_p48_shield_share_of_continental_crust`,
    `tectonics_p48_platform_object_count`,
    `tectonics_p48_platform_share_of_continental_crust`,
    `tectonics_p48_interior_basin_object_count`, and
    `tectonics_p48_interior_basin_share_of_continental_crust`.
  - Updated terrain landform parent lookup so generated `shield`, `platform`,
    and `interior_basin` landforms can inherit these tectonic province parents
    before falling back to craton/continent objects.
  - Added `P48.cratonic_province_object_layer` microbenchmark.  It verifies that
    broad old continental interiors produce shield/platform/interior-basin
    objects, active sutures and accreted terranes are excluded, object generation
    does not mutate land state, and terrain landforms inherit the new parent
    object ids.
- Iteration:
  - First 8000-cell Earth-like audit:
    `out_p48_cratonic_province_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    preserved the P47 morphology metrics but produced no tectonic
    `interior_basin` object even though terrain emitted `9` interior-basin
    landforms.
  - Retuned tectonic interior-basin selection from an overly strict absolute
    thinning rule to relative low-thickness/low-stability mature platform lows.
    This keeps the object tectonic, not terrain-painted, but allows broad sag
    provinces whose crust is only relatively lower than surrounding platform.
- Verification:
  - Syntax check:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/tectonics.py aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/release_gate.py aevum/diagnostics/tectonics_bench.py tests/test_tectonics_bench.py`
    -> pass.
  - P48 microbenchmark:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P48 --out out_bench_p48_cratonic_province_object_layer_retuned_20260625`
    -> `pass`, `1/1`.
  - Adjacent and focused regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_coastline_payback_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p44_parented_recovery_precedes_active_only_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p45_process_provenance_localization_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p46_mature_parent_supported_collage_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p47_mature_cratonic_kernel_target_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p48_cratonic_province_object_layer_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `16 passed`.
  - Retuned 8000-cell Earth-like audit:
    `out_p48_cratonic_province_retuned_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land `0.257`, components `4`, largest land component `0.550`.
    - Exposed-land ribbon `0.373`, continental ribbon `0.422`,
      largest-coastline complexity `8.039`.
    - Continental area `0.289`, ancient continental fraction `0.387`,
      stable craton fraction `0.314`, exposed oceanic land `0.000`.
    - P48 objects: `2` shields covering `0.225` of continental crust, `2`
      platforms covering `0.195`, and `2` interior basins covering `0.0026`.
    - Terrain still emits richer landform detail: `shield=5`, `platform=29`,
      `interior_basin=9`, `foreland_basin=16`, `rift_basin=31`,
      `old_subdued_orogen=23`, `orogen=10`, `arc_microcontinent=28`.
- Interpretation:
  - P48 is intentionally provenance-first: it makes terrain/compiler outputs
    more traceable without changing sea/land or elevation.  That is why the
    Earth-like morphology metrics match P47 exactly.
  - The real generated world now has nonzero tectonic interior-basin objects,
    but their area is still very small compared with terrain interior-basin
    landforms.  The next phase should connect terrain basin formation more
    directly to tectonic subsidence/sediment-basin state instead of only adding
    parent ids after the fact.
  - Remaining release warnings are unchanged: exposed-land ribbon fraction and
    largest-coastline complexity are still above Earth-reference targets; plateau
    and cryosphere landforms remain fixture-backed or absent in this generated
    world.
- Next:
  - P49 should move from object provenance to process coupling: add a
    deterministic mature-platform subsidence/sediment-basin state that can feed
    both tectonic `interior_basins` and terrain basin relief/sediment response.
  - P49 should include a generated-world gate requiring terrain
    `interior_basin` landforms to overlap or be near P48/P49 tectonic basin
    objects, without forcing all lowlands to become basins.

2026-06-25 - P49 mature-platform subsidence and sediment-basin coupling

- Context:
  - P48 made `shield`, `platform`, and `interior_basin` tectonic province
    objects visible, but it was intentionally provenance-first.  Terrain could
    still emit interior-basin relief and sediment response mostly from local
    thresholds, then attach tectonic parents afterward.
  - P49 moves the cause upstream: mature platform basins need a tectonic
    accommodation/subsidence state that terrain can consume.
  - Reference logic used for this phase:
    - Sedimentary basins are long-term subsiding crustal regions that create
      accommodation for sediment accumulation
      (`https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sedimentary_basin`,
      `https://glossary.slb.com/terms/s/sedimentary_basin`).
    - Subsidence commonly reflects extension, cooling, loading, and basin-fill
      feedback; accommodation controls sediment distribution
      (`https://c4g.lsu.edu/tectonic-subsidence/`,
      `https://www.usgs.gov/publications/controls-stratigraphic-architecture-us-atlantic-margin-processes-forming-accommodation`).
    - Keep state/history as resolved features/objects rather than one-off render
      decisions, consistent with GPlates/pyGPlates topological-model practice
      (`https://www.gplates.org/docs/pygplates/pygplates_reference`).
- Changed:
  - Added `tectonics.platform_subsidence`, a deterministic scalar field produced
    by `TectonicsModule`.
  - Added `TectonicsModule._platform_subsidence_field(...)`.  It favors broad,
    old, quiet continental interiors with relatively thin crust and moderate
    stability, weakly persists prior subsidence state, and suppresses shields,
    sutures, continental margins, LIPs, and accreted terranes.
  - Reweighted the field after the first P49 microbenchmark attempt: width and
    quiet age are mostly eligibility gates, while relative thinness and lower
    stability are the main subsidence drivers.
  - Fed `tectonics.platform_subsidence` into:
    - P48/P49 `tectonics.interior_basins` object selection;
    - `TerrainModule._continental_detail_province(...)`, allowing high
      tectonic subsidence on mature platforms to mark basin detail;
    - `TerrainModule._constrained_inland_geomorphology_relief(...)`, adding
      low-amplitude subsidence-driven basin/platform-sag relief;
    - `TerrainModule._regionalize_continental_sediment(...)`, making
      subsidence an accommodation term for sediment depocenters.
  - Added release telemetry:
    `tectonics_p49_platform_subsidence_area_fraction`,
    `tectonics_p49_platform_subsidence_mean`,
    `tectonics_p49_platform_subsidence_max`,
    `terrain_p49_subsidence_basin_sediment_mean_m`,
    `terrain_p49_subsidence_platform_sediment_mean_m`, and
    `terrain_p49_subsidence_sediment_contrast_m`.
  - Added `P49.mature_platform_subsidence_coupling` microbenchmark.  It verifies
    tectonic localization, basin-object overlap, basin detail classification,
    relief depression, sediment enrichment, preserved land mask, and terrain
    parentage to tectonic basin objects.
- Verification:
  - Syntax check:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/tectonics.py aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/release_gate.py aevum/diagnostics/tectonics_bench.py tests/test_tectonics_bench.py`
    -> pass.
  - P49 microbenchmark:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P49 --out out_bench_p49_mature_platform_subsidence_coupling_final_20260625`
    -> `pass`, `1/1`.
    - Subsiding-basin mean field `0.563`; quiet-platform mean `0.308`.
    - Shield and active-suture mean fields `0.000`.
    - Basin relief delta `-174 m`; basin sediment `2041 m` vs quiet platform
      `247 m`; land mask unchanged.
  - P35-P49 focused regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p35_continental_debt_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p36_mature_platform_anchor_recovery_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p37_shape_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p38_lineage_guard_restoration_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p39_old_platform_anchor_funnel_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p40_rework_semantics_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p41_mature_debt_priority_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p42_shape_supported_craton_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_mature_active_accretion_shape_guard_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p43_coastline_payback_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p44_parented_recovery_precedes_active_only_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p45_process_provenance_localization_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p46_mature_parent_supported_collage_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p47_mature_cratonic_kernel_target_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p48_cratonic_province_object_layer_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p49_mature_platform_subsidence_coupling_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `17 passed`.
  - 8000-cell Earth-like audit:
    `out_p49_mature_platform_subsidence_earthlike_8000_t4500_summary_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Land `0.256`, components `4`, largest land component `0.549`.
    - Exposed-land ribbon `0.374`, continental ribbon `0.422`,
      largest-coastline complexity `8.046`.
    - Continental area `0.289`, ancient continental fraction `0.387`,
      stable craton fraction `0.314`, exposed oceanic land `0.000`.
    - P49 active subsidence area `0.0035` of continental crust, mean `0.767`,
      max `0.810`.
    - P49 sediment response: basin mean `1301 m`, platform mean `458 m`,
      contrast `843 m`.

      `interior_basin` `9 -> 11`, `platform` `29 -> 27`, with main map metrics
      effectively preserved.
  - Three-preset 3000-cell smoke:
    `out_p49_mature_platform_subsidence_three_preset_3000_t4500_smoke_final_20260625`
    -> P12 decision `warn`, `0` hard failures.
    - Earth-like `warn`: land `0.212`, components `9`, ribbon `0.546`.
    - Arid `pass`: land `0.785`, components `3`, ribbon `0.248`.
    - Waterworld `pass`: land `0.030`, components `25`, ribbon `0.538`.
    - No-subsidence cases now report zero P49 terrain sediment contrast instead
