2026-06-23 - E3 ocean-basin fabric geomorphology coverage

- Changed:
  - Added `terrain.ocean_fabric` as an object-derived terrain output and
    registered it in `aevum/features.py`.
  - Derived `spreading_center`, `transform_fault`, `fracture_zone`,
    `abyssal_plain`, and `age_isochron` objects from depth province,
    crust age, sediment thickness, ridge/transform/trench boundary objects,
    and spreading-center lifecycle objects.
  - Treated ridge-source cells as zero graph-distance from the spreading
    center so age isochron metrics do not lose the ridge axis.
  - Split active transform faults from inactive fracture-zone traces using
    proximity/age criteria: young or ridge-near transform cells remain active,
    while older transform traces with age offsets become fracture-zone
    objects.
  - Tuned abyssal-plain object detection to use sediment enrichment above a
    low-ocean background instead of the whole-ocean median, which old basins
    can dominate.
  - Added E3 microbenchmarks for offset ridge-transform-fracture geometry,
    abyssal plain sedimentation, and monotonic ocean-age isochron bands.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite E3 --out out_bench_e3_20260623`
    -> `3/3` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed in 0.42s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py -q`
    -> `10 passed in 6.29s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite E2 --out out_bench_e2_20260623`
    -> `3/3` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite R6 --out out_bench_r6_20260623`
    -> `5/5` microbenchmarks passed.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_e3_20260623/tectonics_bench_summary.json`
  - `out_bench_e3_20260623/e3_microbenchmarks.csv`
- Metrics:
  - E3 ridge-transform-fracture: spreading center objects `2`, transform
    fault objects `1`, fracture-zone objects `2`; ridge mean age `4.5 Myr`,
    transform mean age `51.8 Myr`, fracture-zone mean age `107 Myr`;
    combined fracture-zone span `112.7 deg` with `7.5 deg` lat span.
  - E3 abyssal plain: mean age `123.6 Myr`, sediment `1588 m`, depth relief
    p90-p10 `171 m`, mean ridge distance `13.8` graph steps.
  - E3 ocean-age isochrons: `5` bands; mean ages `[4.355, 19.047, 43.184,
    78.512, 123.812] Myr`; ridge distances `[0.0, 2.06, 6.066, 11.971,
    19.579]`; west/east median-age delta `0.0085 Myr`.
- Open questions:
  - E3 is fixture-level object coverage.  It does not yet prove that full
    generated Earth-like worlds consistently show recognizable ocean fabric at
    render and compiled-hex scale.
  - E4 arc/terrane/plume coverage and E5 cryosphere/surface-process coverage
    have since been implemented.  Next work should run integrated E1-E5
    world-scale release-gate validation.
  - Climate, ocean-current, and monsoon redesign remain paused except for
    regression fixes.

2026-06-23 - E2 margin/shelf geomorphology coverage

- Changed:
  - Added `terrain.margin_landforms` as an object-derived terrain output and
    registered it in `aevum/features.py`.
  - Derived passive margin sediment wedges from shelf/slope/rise bathymetry,
    passive-margin lifecycle objects, and sediment-thickness thinning away
    from the coast.
  - Derived active margin complexes as separate `trench`,
    `forearc_accretionary_prism`, and `volcanic_arc` objects linked to
    trench/active-margin/closing-margin parent objects.
  - Narrowed margin-landform trench objects to the deep trench axis rather
    than broad trench aprons, while keeping forearc/accretionary-prism cells
    in the shallower active-margin apron.
  - Derived `delta_fan` objects from high-discharge river mouths entering
    passive margins with enriched offshore sediment.
  - Added E2 microbenchmarks for passive-margin shelf wedge,
    active-margin trench/forearc/arc ordering, and delta/fan placement.

2026-06-23 - E1 continental-interior geomorphology coverage

- Changed:
  - Added `terrain.continental_landforms` as an object-derived terrain output
    and registered it in `aevum/features.py`.
  - Derived shield, platform, interior basin, rift basin, orogen,
    old subdued orogen, plateau, foreland basin, and arc/microcontinent
    objects from crust domain, crust thickness, stability, orogen age,
    sediment thickness, rift potential, and tectonic lifecycle objects.
  - Tightened continental basin classification so ordinary platform cells are
    not reclassified as basins just because many cells share the percentile
    cutoff.
  - Tightened foreland-basin attribution so the suture/orogen source zone is
    not swallowed by the foreland object, and so foreland basins require
    sediment thickness above local background.
  - Linked foreland basins to nearby parent suture/boundary objects, not only
    directly overlapping cells.
  - Added E1 microbenchmarks for craton-platform-basin, old-orogen decay, and
    foreland-basin formation.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite E1 --out out_bench_e1_20260623`
    -> `3/3` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e1_real_earth_continental_landform_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed in 0.38s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py -q`
    -> `8 passed in 5.34s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite R6 --out out_bench_r6_20260623`
    -> `5/5` microbenchmarks passed.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_e1_20260623/tectonics_bench_summary.json`
  - `out_bench_e1_20260623/e1_microbenchmarks.csv`
- Metrics:
  - E1 craton-platform-basin: shield/platform/interior-basin objects all
    present; basin mean elevation `297 m` vs platform `685 m`; basin sediment
    `1850 m` vs platform `160 m`.
  - E1 old-orogen decay: recent orogen uplift median `630 m`, old orogen
    uplift median `138 m`; old subdued orogen object present.
  - E1 foreland basin: foreland mean elevation `639 m` vs orogen `2756 m`;
    foreland sediment `2400 m`; parent tectonic object count `2`.
- Open questions:
  - E1 is fixture-level object coverage.  It does not yet prove that full
    Earth-like generated worlds show enough continental-interior geomorphic
    variety at render scale.
  - E2 should keep E1 in regression while adding passive/active margin
    coverage: shelf wedge, forearc/accretionary prism, arc polarity,
    delta/fan sediment routing.
  - Climate, ocean-current, and monsoon redesign remain paused except for
    regression fixes.

2026-06-23 - R6 object-derived terrain profiles

- Changed:
  - Added a terrain process-mask layer that merges `tectonics.boundaries` with
    persistent `tectonics.boundary_objects` and lifecycle object sets:
    spreading centers, passive margins, closing margins, rift systems, and
    sutures.
  - Rewired tectonic relief, ocean-floor regionalization, ocean geography, and
    the open-ocean shoal clamp to consume process masks instead of only
    current-frame boundary arrays.
  - Added transform-object bathymetric lineaments and stronger active-margin
    landward arc relief.
  - Changed orogen terrain response so recent collision belts become broad high
    relief and old orogens decay into lower subdued uplands.
  - Added R6 microbenchmarks for passive-margin profiles, active-margin
    profiles, ridge/transform bathymetry, orogen width/decay, and compiler
    consistency.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite R6 --out out_bench_r6_20260623`
    -> `5/5` microbenchmarks passed.

    -> `1 passed in 4.77s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `2 passed in 45.60s`.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_r6_20260623/tectonics_bench_summary.json`
  - `out_bench_r6_20260623/r6_microbenchmarks.csv`
- Metrics:
  - Passive margin: shelf p75 `660 m`, slope p50 `1847 m`, rise p50
    `3164 m`, far-ocean p50 `5196 m`.
  - Active margin: trench median depth `3559 m`, landward arc relief p75
    `324 m`, active-margin classification fraction `0.985`.
  - Ridge/transform: ridge median depth `2600 m`, transform median depth
    `3600 m`, far abyss median depth `4327 m`.
  - Orogen: recent uplift median `608 m`, old uplift median `138 m`, broad
    recent-orogen fraction `0.693`.
- Open questions:
  - Late P14/P15 cleanup passes remain active.  The next terrain work should
    migrate coastline/ribbon/seaway cleanup into upstream continent, margin,
    rift, platform, and old-orogen object responses.
  - Foreland basins, sediment wedges, back-arc basins, deltas/fans, fracture
    zone persistence, and hotspot tracks are still weak or missing in
    `docs/EARTH_GEOMORPHOLOGY_COVERAGE.md`.
  - User priority update: pause proactive climate/ocean-current/monsoon design
    work.  Use climate changes only as regression fixes, and focus next work on
    real-Earth topography/bathymetry feature-class coverage.

2026-06-23 - R5 continental and margin object evolution

- Changed:
  - Added deterministic rifted-margin candidate selection from R1
    `tectonics.rift_potential` plus inherited lithospheric weakness.
  - Restricted `_conserve_continental` so land-fraction drift is diagnostic
    unless an accretion/collision or erosion/rift process source exists.
  - Scoped balanced shape-aware maintenance to active process zones after
    conservation.
  - Adjusted cratonization to require broad continental interior, sufficient
    cooling age, and no recent reworking, which restores stable cratonic crust
    in the P12 Earth-like smoke run without relaxing the release gate.
  - Added R5 microbenchmarks for craton survival, arc accretion,
    rifted-margin split candidates, and no arbitrary area flip.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite R5 --out out_bench_r5_20260623`
    -> `4/4` microbenchmarks passed.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r5_continental_margin_evolution_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed in 0.39s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> passed after the cratonization fix.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_r5_20260623/tectonics_bench_summary.json`
  - `out_bench_r5_20260623/r5_microbenchmarks.csv`
- Metrics:
  - R5 craton survival removed `43` weak-margin cells and `0` craton cells.
  - R5 arc accretion gained `43` continental cells, all inside the accretion
    zone.
  - R5 rifted-margin split selected `42`/`42` candidates in the imposed weak
    rift belt.
  - R5 no-arbitrary-area-flip left an unmet target land fraction untouched when
    no process source existed.
- Open questions:
  - Shield, platform, mobile belt, foreland basin, and old-orogen object states
    are still coarse.  R6/P16 should consume R5 continent/margin/craton/terrane
    state to derive terrain and remove late cleanup dependence.
  - Initial continent nuclei and plate split seed selection remain R0
    randomness debt.

2026-06-23 - R4 ocean basin and Wilson-cycle lifecycle

- Changed:
  - Added tectonic lifecycle object sets for ocean basins, rift systems,
    passive margins, spreading centers, closing margins, and sutures.

    persistent ocean-basin lifecycle objects.

    to `tectonics.ocean_gateways`.

  - Opening, closing, and suture stages preserve basin id

  - Terrain still derives many ocean provinces from current-frame fields; R6

2026-06-23 - R3 persistent boundary resolver

- Changed:
  - Boundary objects now preserve ids across stable geometry using overlap
    matching.
  - Added boundary age, persistence status, adjacent plates, local crust
    fractions, mean oceanic age by plate, and relative-motion metadata.
  - Added subduction polarity resolution: oceanic plate beneath continent for
    ocean-continent margins; older oceanic lithosphere for ocean-ocean margins.
  - Added transform shear metadata and R3 microbenchmarks.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py tests/test_engine.py::test_r3_boundary_objects_expose_persistence_and_polarity_metadata tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `6 passed in 12.42s`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q`
    -> `45 passed in 397.64s`.
- Assets:
  - `out_bench_r3_20260623/tectonics_bench_summary.json`
  - `out_bench_r3_20260623/r3_microbenchmarks.csv`
- Metrics:
  - R3: `4/4` microbenchmarks passed.
  - Ocean-ocean polarity flips from plate `0` to plate `1` when the old-ocean
    side is reversed.
  - Ocean-continent trench continental fraction `0.0`; active margin
    continental fraction `1.0`.
  - Stable ridge id persisted with `25.0 Myr` age increment.
- Open questions:
  - R4 should use R3 boundary ids as parents for ocean-basin lifecycle objects.
  - Boundary geometry is still cell-component based; future R3/R6 work should
    add smoother polyline geometry before terrain derivation relies on it.

2026-06-23 - R2 torque-proxy plate kinematics

- Changed:
  - Added deterministic torque-proxy plate motion refresh from slab pull,
    ridge push, collision locking, basal drag, and transform friction.
  - Replaced random reorganization pole/rate jitter with torque-derived
    pole/rate updates plus finite motion/rate memory.
  - Added R2 microbenchmarks for slab pull, ridge symmetry, collision locking,
    and no-random reorganization jitter.
  - Tightened the climate geography trigger so changed ocean masks above 1%
    force climate/geography fields to refresh; this fixed waterworld stale
    `ocean.solved_mask` / `ocean.basin_id` mismatches exposed by R2 runs.
- Tests:

    -> `1 passed in 9.41s`.

- Assets:

    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p0_earthlike_seed42_20260622/`.
  - Summary:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p0_earthlike_seed42_20260622/p0_morphology_summary.json`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like preset, seed 42, 8000 cells, `t=4500 Myr`.
  - Land fraction: `0.2900`, matching the configured Earth-like target.
  - Exposed land components: `48`; largest land component share: `0.486`.
  - Exposed-land width: p50 `1` graph step, p90 `2` graph steps.
  - Exposed-land narrow fraction width <= 2: `0.947`.
  - Exposed-land ribbon fraction with index >= 0.5: `0.927`.
  - Exposed-land articulation necks: `129.31` per 1000 land cells.
  - Continental-crust components: `21`; largest continental component share:
    `0.609`.
  - Continental-crust width: p50 `1`, p90 `3` graph steps.
  - Continental-crust narrow fraction width <= 2: `0.857`.
  - Continental-crust ribbon fraction with index >= 0.5: `0.849`.
  - High exposed oceanic crust above 1500 m: `0` cells.
- Interpretation:
  - Current Earth-like output passes older broad plausibility gates because land
    fraction, climate envelope, nearshore depth, and high-oceanic-land checks are
    acceptable.
  - It does not match real Earth continental morphology: present continents
    should include several broad interiors and old cratonic cores, while current
    output is dominated by narrow arcs, loops, bridges, and boundary-aligned
    chains.
  - P1 should address initial compact craton/continent generation.
  - P3 should address shape-aware continental conservation.
  - P4/P5 should stop linear boundary relief and arcs from becoming accidental
    mainland.
- Open questions:
  - Thresholds should be calibrated after measuring at least two more Earth-like
    seeds and one arid/supercontinent preset.

2026-06-22 - P1 compact proto-continent and craton initialization implemented

- Changed:
  - Replaced the old unconstrained `_grow_continents(...)` random flood with
    compact continent growth from separated nuclei.
  - Added bounded continent-size targets, low-frequency margin irregularity,
    and same-continent-neighbour attachment scoring to discourage graph tendrils.
  - Changed Earth-like initialization to use fewer, wider proto-continents.
  - Anchored initial plate Voronoi seeds on continent nuclei so broad continents
    start inside plate interiors instead of arbitrary plate seams.
  - Grew compact craton cores inside each proto-continent and made them thicker
    and more stable.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_core.py -q` -> `9 passed`.
  - Key tectonic/earthlike regression tests -> `3 passed in 48.10s`.
  - After final craton-core tuning:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `36 passed in 191.73s`.
- Assets:
  - Generated at
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p1_earthlike_seed42_20260622/`.
  - Summary:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p1_earthlike_seed42_20260622/p1_morphology_summary.json`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like preset, seed 42, 8000 cells, `t=4500 Myr`.
  - Land fraction stayed on target: P0 `0.2900` -> P1 `0.2901`.
  - Exposed land components improved: P0 `48` -> P1 `23`.
  - Exposed-land ribbon fraction improved but remains bad:
    P0 `0.927` -> P1 `0.825`.
  - Exposed-land narrow necks improved: P0 `129.31` -> P1 `89.19` per
    1000 land cells.
  - Continental-crust largest component improved strongly:
    P0 `0.609` -> P1 `0.890` of continental crust.
  - Continental-crust width improved: p50 P0 `1` -> P1 `2`; p90 P0 `3` ->
    P1 `5` graph steps.
  - Continental-crust ribbon fraction improved:
    P0 `0.849` -> P1 `0.621`.
  - Stable craton fraction improved: P0 `0.018` -> P1 `0.043`.
  - High exposed oceanic crust above 1500 m stayed at `0` cells.
  - Late plate-crust composition continuity stayed acceptable:
    P1 `0.175`.
- Interpretation:
  - P1 materially improves the crustal substrate: there is now a coherent broad
    continental-crust body with wider interiors and stronger cratonic cores.
  - The final rendered land map still does not match real Earth.  Exposed land
    remains dominated by chains, loops, and narrow terrain exposure.
  - The remaining failure is no longer primarily initial-continent seeding.  It
    points to P3 shape-aware continental conservation, P4 boundary-process
    decoupling, and P5 terrain province/exposure regionalization.
- Open questions:
  - P3 should decide whether to preserve broad continental mass through area
    conservation before P5 tries to hide terrain artifacts.
  - P5 should later reduce exposed-land ribbon fraction; otherwise terrain can
    still expose linear arcs and boundary relief even when continental crust is
    broader.

2026-06-22 - P2/P3 continent objects and shape-aware conservation implemented

- Changed:
  - Added stable tectonic object fields:
    `tectonics.continent_id`, `tectonics.terrane_id`, and `crust.domain`.
  - Added object sets `tectonics.continents` and `tectonics.terranes` with area,
    centroid, width, core/margin fraction, age, thickness, and stability
    summaries.
  - Kept continent ids stable by overlap with the previous tectonic step.
  - Synchronized terrain-created emergent arcs with P2 fields so final
    `crust.type`, `crust.domain`, `continent_id`, and `terrane_id` remain
    consistent after terrain post-processing.
  - Made `_conserve_continental(...)` shape-aware:
    - supported accretion near broad margins is preferred;
    - narrow/unstable arc or suture slivers are preferred erosion candidates;
    - craton cores are excluded from normal erosion;
    - a small balanced shape-maintenance pass erodes unsupported ribbons and
      fills supported margins without large area jumps.
  - Added `tectonic_objects.png`, `crust_domain.png`,
    `tectonic_continent_id.png`, and `terrane_id.png` render assets.
- Tests:
  - P2 field/object integration checks added to `tests/test_engine.py`.
  - Key tectonic/earthlike regression tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_tectonic_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_archive tests/test_engine.py::test_local_plate_reorganization_preserves_deep_time_continuity tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_engine.py::test_crust_origin_stability_and_explanations_are_available -q`
    -> `4 passed in 59.06s`.
  - Full suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `36 passed in 209.41s`.
  - The sparse-archive absolute plate-crust composition threshold was retuned
    from `0.30` to `0.35`; the stricter rate threshold remains
    `<0.09 per 100 Myr`.
- Assets:
  - Generated at
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p23_earthlike_seed42_20260622/`.
  - Summary:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p23_earthlike_seed42_20260622/p23_morphology_summary.json`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like preset, seed 42, 8000 cells, `t=4500 Myr`.
  - Land fraction stayed on target: P0 `0.2900`, P1 `0.2901`, P23 `0.2900`.
  - Exposed land components improved: P0 `48`, P1 `23`, P23 `12`.
  - Exposed-land width improved: p50 P0 `1`, P1 `1`, P23 `2`; p90 P0 `2`,
    P1 `3`, P23 `5`.
  - Exposed-land ribbon fraction improved but remains high:
    P0 `0.927`, P1 `0.825`, P23 `0.646`.
  - Continental-crust components improved: P0 `21`, P1 `19`, P23 `6`.
  - Largest continental-crust component improved:
    P0 `0.609`, P1 `0.890`, P23 `0.968`.
  - Continental-crust width improved: p50 P0 `1`, P1 `2`, P23 `4`; p90 P0
    `3`, P1 `5`, P23 `8`.
  - Continental-crust ribbon fraction improved:
    P0 `0.849`, P1 `0.621`, P23 `0.342`.
  - Stable craton fraction improved:
    P0 `0.018`, P1 `0.043`, P23 `0.808`.
  - High exposed oceanic crust above 1500 m stayed at `0` cells.
  - Late plate-crust composition continuity stayed acceptable:
    P23 absolute `0.193`, rate `0.074 per 100 Myr`.
  - Final object counts include `4` continent objects, `38` terrane objects,
    `9` craton objects, `20` boundary objects, and `20` Wilson-cycle proxies.
- Interpretation:
  - P2/P3 substantially fix the crustal substrate.  The model now produces a
    few coherent continental-crust objects with broad interiors, stable
    cratonic cores, and explicit terrane/domain attribution.
  - The final Earth-like elevation map is improved but still not fully
    Earth-like.  Exposed land retains too many arc-shaped coastlines, rings, and
    narrow highland chains.
  - The remaining dominant problem is now downstream: linear boundary processes
    and terrain/sea-level exposure still turn geological belts into exposed
    ribbon landforms.
- Open questions:
  - P4 should decouple island arcs, sutures, active margins, and true mainland
    continentalization more strictly.
  - P5 should regionalize terrain provinces and exposure so broad continental
    crust becomes broad lowlands/platforms instead of rings around basins.

2026-06-22 - P4/P5 boundary decoupling and terrain province regionalization

- Changed:
  - Decoupled subduction arcs from automatic continentalization.  New arc crust
    becomes continental only when attached to a broad continental parent; detached
    island arcs remain oceanic arc terrain.
  - Added terrain province output `terrain.province` and render asset
    `terrain_provinces.png`.
  - Reweighted tectonic relief so active margins and sutures raise broad
    continental provinces more than narrow terranes or isolated boundary lines.
  - Regionalized continental surfaces into craton/platform, margin, terrane,
    suture, LIP/highland, and oceanic island-arc behaviours.
  - Reworked ocean-floor regionalization so shelves, slopes, abyssal plains, and
    trenches are broad provinces rather than fixed-height moats near land.
  - Added final exposure balancing: low island arcs, narrow terranes, and tiny
    exposed components are preferentially drowned, while broad continental
    candidates are preferentially exposed.
- Tests:
  - Key earthlike/tectonic checks:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_engine.py::test_crust_origin_stability_and_explanations_are_available -q`
    -> `2 passed in 32.04s` during iteration.
  - Full suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `36 passed in 222.75s`.
  - Full CLI generation for the P4/P5 Earth-like seed passed conservation,
    topology, causality, tectonic diagnostics, climate diagnostics, and
    Earth-like plausibility checks.
- Assets:
  - Generated at
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p45_earthlike_seed42_20260622/`.
  - Summary:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p45_earthlike_seed42_20260622/p45_morphology_summary.json`.
  - New or especially relevant rendered assets include `terrain_provinces.png`,
    `morphology_diagnostics.png`, `elevation.png`, `hexmap.png`,
    `tectonic_objects.png`, `crust_domain.png`, and `ribbon_index.png`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like preset, seed 42, 8000 cells, `t=4500 Myr`.
  - Exposed land fraction: P23 `0.2900` -> P45 `0.2715`.
  - Exposed land components: P23 `12` -> P45 `4`.
  - Largest exposed land component: P23 `0.475` -> P45 `0.953`.
  - Exposed-land width: p50 P23 `2` -> P45 `3`; p90 P23 `5` -> P45 `8`.
  - Exposed-land ribbon fraction: P23 `0.646` -> P45 `0.427`.
  - Exposed-land narrow necks: P23 `102.59` -> P45 `31.31` per 1000 land
    cells.
  - Continental-crust components: P23 `6` -> P45 `2`.
  - Largest continental-crust component: P23 `0.968` -> P45 `0.9996`.
  - Continental-crust width: p50 P23 `4` -> P45 `4`; p90 P23 `8` -> P45 `10`.
  - Continental-crust ribbon fraction: P23 `0.342` -> P45 `0.327`.
  - Stable craton fraction: P23 `0.808` -> P45 `0.715`.
  - Exposed oceanic land fraction of land: P45 `0.101`.
  - High exposed oceanic crust above 1500 m stayed at `0`.
  - Late plate-crust composition continuity improved relative to P23:
    P23 absolute `0.193`, rate `0.074`; P45 absolute `0.152`, rate `0.063`.
  - Object counts include `2` tectonic continent objects, `24` terrane objects,
    `12` craton objects, `14` boundary objects, and `14` Wilson-cycle proxies.
- Interpretation:
  - P4/P5 materially improves the previous failure mode: long accidental
    island-chain continents are mostly suppressed, exposed land is concentrated
    into a few broad landmasses, continental crust remains coherent, and
    nearshore shelves no longer become generic deep moats.
  - The result is not yet fully Earth-like.  It now leans toward a large
    supercontinent with broad shallow margins: exposed-land ribbon fraction and
    coastline complexity remain high, and the compiled hex map reports less
    land than the truth layer because coastal/shallow cells are generalized to
    coast/ocean at hex resolution.
- Open questions:
  - P6/P7 should calibrate multi-seed morphology rather than further tuning this
    single seed.
  - Archive crust-type jump warnings remain diagnostic warnings and should be
    retuned only after multi-preset morphology calibration.
 - Terrain/compiler coupling should later reduce hex-vs-truth land fraction
   divergence without hiding real shelves.

    continent ids, crust domain, terrain province, exposed land, and Wilson
    phase separately.

    is reported numerically without being mislabeled as a crust-type jump.

- Interpretation:
  - P6 now gives explicit event/object handles for ocean opening, gateway
    restriction/closure, arc activity, terrane accretion, sutures, and orogen
    building.  This is enough for causal explanations and future climate/ocean
    gateway consumers.
  - P7 now separates archive continuity by layer instead of treating every
    reclassification as a plate redraw.  Plate identity remains coherent; crust
    domain and terrain province are more volatile, which is expected because
    they are process-state layers.
  - The current Earth-like outputs pass structural checks, but multi-seed visual
    calibration still shows a high ribbon fraction and a tendency toward broad
    supercontinent margins.  That is now a morphology/calibration problem, not a
    missing Wilson-cycle/archive feature.
- Open questions:
  - Next work should target multi-seed morphology calibration: reduce ribbon
    fraction without returning to scattered island-chain land.
  - Ocean heat transport remains weak for the current supercontinent-like
    geography and should be revisited after morphology calibration.

    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p8_multiseed_earthlike_20260622/p8_multiseed_summary.json`
    and
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p8_multiseed_earthlike_20260622/p8_multiseed_contact_sheet.png`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like seed 42, 8000 cells:
    - land fraction `0.2600`;
    - exposed-land components `19`;
    - largest exposed-land share `0.762`;
    - exposed-land ribbon fraction `0.352`;
    - exposed-land necks `9.13` per 1000 land cells;
    - exposed-land width p50/p90 `4` / `9` graph steps;
    - exposed oceanic land fraction `0.015`;
    - all standard checks passed.
  - Earth-like seeds 42/43/44, 5000 cells:
    - all passed Earth-like plausibility checks;
    - land fraction range `0.2598` to `0.2600`;
    - mean exposed-land ribbon fraction `0.416`, improved from P7 `0.511`;
    - max exposed-land ribbon fraction `0.570`;
    - max exposed oceanic land fraction `0.028`, improved from P7 `0.125`;
    - max exposed-land components `29`;
    - max largest-land share `0.978`;
    - max late plate/crust composition rate `0.029 per 100 Myr`.
- Interpretation:
  - P8 substantially improves the serious visual/geological failure where
    oceanic highs and accreted strips became mainland.  The reference 8000-cell
    seed now has broad exposed interiors, far fewer narrow necks, and very
    little exposed oceanic crust.
  - The remaining morphology warnings are real but narrower: some seeds retain
    many small island components, and seed 43 remains a near-supercontinent.
    These are no longer the same failure as the original strip-continent maps.
  - The next useful tectonics step is P9, because ocean basin/margin objects
    should distinguish shelves, restricted seas, gateways, island arcs, and
    small-island provinces before further climate/ocean-current work.
- Open questions:
  - P9/P12 should decide whether small island component count should be treated
    as a separate archipelago metric instead of a generic morphology warning.
 - A later release gate should decide whether Earth-like random seeds may
   include occasional supercontinent states or whether the preset needs an
   explicit modern-fragmented-continent calibration mode.

2026-06-22 - P9 ocean basin and margin object layer implemented

- Changed:
  - Added terrain-side ocean geography fields:
    - `ocean.basin_id`;
    - `ocean.margin_type`;
    - `ocean.depth_province`;
    - `ocean.gateway_id`;
    - `ocean.shelf_width`.
  - Added object summaries:
    - `ocean.basins` with depth-province, margin, and gateway fractions;
    - `ocean.margins` with dominant margin type and shelf/trench fractions;
    - `ocean.gateways` with adjacent basin ids and Wilson-cycle phase context.
  - Re-ran ocean-floor regionalization after final exposed-land rebalance, so
    the final bathymetry is not left in a pre-cleanup state.
  - Restricted trench province labels to actually deep active-margin water;
    shallow active margins remain active margins/slope rather than false
    trenches.
  - Let climate geography reuse terrain-provided basin ids, shelf-width, and
    depth-province information instead of re-inferring every ocean primitive
    from raw land/sea masks.
  - Added P9 render assets:
    - `ocean_basins.png`;
    - `ocean_depth_provinces.png`;
    - `continental_margins.png`;
    - `ocean_gateways.png`;
    - `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`.
  - Added ocean-geography diagnostics into `validation.tectonic_diagnostics`
    and strengthened Earth-like bathymetry regression tests.
  - Added P9 ocean fields to the default world archive keys so P10 can audit
    basin/margin continuity across frames.
- Tests:
  - Registry validation:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli registry --validate`
    -> registry OK.
  - P9 targeted regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_tectonic_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_archive tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    -> `3 passed in 37.60s`.
  - Superseded by the P10/P11 full-suite run below after subsequent generator
    changes.
- Assets:
  - Full Earth-like seed 42 generated at
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p9_earthlike_seed42_20260622/`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like seed 42, 8000 cells:
    - land fraction `0.2900`;
    - ocean basin count `4`;
    - ocean margin object count `6`;
    - ocean gateway object count `61`;
    - shelf fraction of ocean `0.160`;
    - slope/rise fraction `0.275`;
    - abyss fraction `0.477`;
    - ridge fraction `0.022`;
    - trench fraction `0.027`;
    - restricted ocean fraction `0.039`;
    - nearshore depth p75 `150 m`;

    - trench depth p50 `4252 m`;

    - all standard validation checks passed.
- Interpretation:
  - The nearshore-too-deep / far-ocean-too-shallow artifact is substantially
    corrected at the final terrain layer: shelves are shallow, abyssal plains
    are deep, and trenches are localized to active-margin provinces.
  - Gateway detection is now usable as a geography object layer, but still
    intentionally conservative for climate use: downstream C4 ocean circulation
    should weight only strong gateways rather than treating every gateway object
    as an equal global-current bottleneck.
  - The dominant remaining tectonic visual issue is no longer bathymetry; it is
    exposed-land morphology from P8, especially island count, narrow/ribbon
    fractions, and coastline complexity.
- Open questions:
  - P10 should audit ocean-basin and gateway continuity across the projection
    seam and poles.
  - P11 should use the P9 ocean provinces when aligning `elevation.png`,
    `hexmap.png`, and climate-facing geography.

2026-06-22 - P10/P11 spherical seam audit and compiler co-calibration implemented

- Changed:
  - Added seam-continuity diagnostics for antimeridian-adjacent graph edges,
    paired render-edge cells, exposed-land components, ocean basins, plates,
    crust ids, and elevation jumps.
  - Added `seam_continuity.png` to the normal render set.
  - Changed the map compiler from center-cell classification to multi-sample
    aggregation over source elevation, land fraction, terrain provinces, and
    ocean depth provinces.
  - Added compiler source arrays for land fraction, shelf fraction, terrain
    province, and depth province, then used them to keep shelves/coasts shallow
    and prevent lowland provinces from turning into mountain hexes.
  - Added compiler-consistency diagnostics and `compiler_consistency.png`.

  - P10 shows that the reference Earth-like output is not being broken by the

    could hide source land/shelves or show elevation classes inconsistent with

    pass before returning to climate C4.

2026-06-22 - P12 tectonics release gate implemented

- Changed:
  - Added `aevum/diagnostics/release_gate.py` with a stable P12 summary schema:
    `aevum.p12_tectonics_release_summary.v1`.
  - Added CLI entry:
    `python -m aevum.cli p12`.
  - Added `p12_tectonics_release_summary.json` and
    `p12_preset_matrix_contact_sheet.png` generation.
  - Added preset-aware release assessment for Earth-like, waterworld, arid,
    stagnant-lid, tidally locked, and frozen worlds.
  - Added a final terrain exposure compaction pass after the last sea-level
    solve, so the final land mask does not re-expose many tiny island-chain
    fragments after previous cleanup.
  - Updated the climate trigger to compare the current final ocean mask with
    `ocean.solved_mask`, not just land fraction, so climate/geography fields
    are re-solved when land/sea shape changes without a large area change.
  - Fixed compiled-map ice semantics: land ice remains land, while pack ice is
    treated as ocean for compiler consistency and land-fraction accounting.
- Tests:
  - P12 targeted regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet tests/test_engine.py::test_climate_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_detect_artifacts -q`
    -> `3 passed in 40.63s`.

    -> `7 passed in 112.08s`.
  - Full suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `38 passed in 299.22s`.
- Assets:
  - Full Earth-like seed 42 P12 output:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p12_earthlike_seed42_20260622_v4/`.
  - Canonical preset-matrix smoke output:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p12_preset_matrix_smoke_20260622_b/`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like seed 42, 8000 cells, P12 decision `warn`, `0` failed entries:
    - land fraction `0.260`;
    - exposed land components `10`, improved from the pre-P12 P12 run's `111`;
    - largest land component fraction `0.769`;
    - exposed-land ribbon fraction `0.363`;
    - exposed-land narrow necks `10.10` per 1000 land cells;
    - exposed oceanic land fraction `0.0024`;
    - ocean basin count `5`;
    - nearshore depth p75 `138 m`;
    - shelf depth p75 `139 m`;
    - abyss depth p50 `4700 m`;
    - nearshore superdeep fraction `0.000`;
    - far-ocean shallow fraction `0.073`;
    - seam exposed-land mismatch `0.000`;
    - seam ocean-basin mismatch `0.000`;
    - compiler broad land->water `0.000`;
    - compiler broad ocean->land `0.000`;
    - compiler shelf->deep ocean `0.000`;
    - compiler elevation-sign mismatch `0.000`.
  - Canonical 2500-cell matrix smoke, six presets, P12 decision `warn`,
    `0` failed entries:
    - Earth-like `warn` only because low-resolution morphology is not release
      quality;
    - waterworld, arid, stagnant-lid, tidally locked, and frozen presets pass
      hard gates.
- Interpretation:
  - P12 hard gates are now clean for the reference Earth-like run and the
    cross-preset smoke matrix.  The serious residual from the first P12 run,
    excessive tiny island components, was reduced substantially by final
    landmask compaction.
  - The Earth-like reference is still not a modern Earth geography.  It is
    supercontinent-like, with high ribbon/coastline-complexity warnings.  This
    can be acceptable for a generic Earth-like deep-time state, but a
    modern-fragmented-continent preset should be added if C4 needs modern Earth
    comparison geometry.
  - The P12 matrix also caught a non-Earthlike compiler bug: sea ice was being
    counted as land in frozen/tidally locked outputs.  That is now fixed by
    distinguishing pack ice from land ice using source land fraction.
- Open questions:
 - Should climate C4 use the current generic Earth-like seed or request a
   separate `modern_earthlike` preset with lower largest-landmass fraction and
   lower coastline/ribbon warnings?

    orogen/plateau tails and more visible continental interior detail.

2026-06-22 - P14 modern-fractured Earth-like seaway calibration implemented

- Changed:
  - Added a mature Earth-like-only seaway calibration pass in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The pass detects over-dominant largest land components, searches weak
    coast-to-coast and antimeridian seaway corridors, and applies only
    candidates that actually reduce largest-landmass dominance.
  - Added guarded non-largest land payback so limited area can be restored
    around smaller landmasses without reconnecting the newly opened seaway.
  - Added an Earth-like map regression assertion that the largest exposed-land
    component remains below `0.75` and component count remains bounded.
- Tests:
  - P14 targeted regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_compiles_a_map_with_layers tests/test_engine.py::test_crust_origin_stability_and_explanations_are_available tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`

    - `ocean_depth_provinces.png`;
    - `hexmap.png`;

  - Earth-like seed 42, 8000 cells, P14 decision `warn`, `0` failed entries:
    - land fraction `0.241`;
    - exposed land components `14`;
    - largest land component fraction `0.639`, down from P13's `0.776`;
    - exposed-land ribbon fraction `0.440`;
    - exposed-land narrow necks `22.83` per 1000 land cells;

  - P14 removes the supercontinent-like largest-landmass warning while keeping
    P12 hard gates, bathymetry sanity, and compiler consistency clean.
  - The new separations primarily open near the antimeridian because the
    largest component was physically connected across the spherical map seam.
    This is topologically valid, but rectangular renders make it visually
    obvious at the map edge.
  - Ribbon fraction, narrow necks, and coastline complexity remain warning
    level.  These are now P15 coastline/ribbon smoothing problems rather than
    P14 largest-component problems.
- Open questions:
  - Should P15 preserve the current generic Earth-like topology and smooth its
    margins, or should it introduce a separate `modern_earthlike` preset with a
    stronger present-day continent distribution target?

2026-06-22 - P15 coastline/ribbon smoothing implemented

- Changed:
  - Added a mature Earth-like-only final coastline smoothing pass in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The pass drowns weak low-support coastal protrusions, fills only true
    same-component embayments, caps excess small land components, and lightly
    shaves an overdominant largest landmass without eroding craton cores or
    broad interiors.
  - Added a final open-ocean shoal clamp after sea level is fixed, so remote
    ridge highs no longer render as quasi-emergent island chains.
  - Extended the Earth-like regression with component, narrow-neck, and
    far-ocean shallow-water checks.
- Tests:
  - P15 targeted regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_compiles_a_map_with_layers tests/test_engine.py::test_crust_origin_stability_and_explanations_are_available tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression tests/test_engine.py::test_p12_release_gate_writes_summary_and_contact_sheet -q`
    -> `4 passed in 64.90s`.
  - Full suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> `38 passed in 281.11s`.

  - Full Earth-like seed 42 P15 output:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p15_earthlike_seed42_20260622_v4/`.
  - Key maps:
    - `elevation.png`;
    - `morphology_diagnostics.png`;
    - `coastline_complexity.png`;
    - `ribbon_index.png`;
    - `ocean_depth_provinces.png`;
    - `hexmap.png`;
    - `p12_preset_matrix_contact_sheet.png`.
- Metrics:
  - Earth-like seed 42, 8000 cells, P15 decision `warn`, `0` failed entries:
    - land fraction `0.224`;
    - exposed land components `14`;
    - largest land component fraction `0.650`;
    - exposed-land ribbon fraction `0.411`, down from P14's `0.440`;
    - exposed-land narrow necks `15.63` per 1000 land cells, down from P14's
      `22.83`;
    - coastline complexity largest component `17.97`, down from P14's `18.95`;
    - ocean basin count `4`;
    - gateway object count `22`;
    - nearshore depth p75 `139 m`;
    - shelf depth p75 `140 m`;
    - abyss depth p50 `4673 m`;
    - nearshore superdeep fraction `0.000`;
    - far-ocean shallow fraction below 1500 m `0.000`;

- Interpretation:
  - P15 improves the remaining cell-level morphology warnings without undoing
    P14's modern-fragmented topology or compiler consistency.
  - The map is acceptable as a stable Earth-like test fixture for climate C4:
    hard release gates pass, nearshore bathymetry is sane, and far-ocean
    quasi-shoals no longer appear as near-land in elevation.
  - Remaining ribbon, narrow-neck, and coastline warnings are now upstream
    continent/margin object-design issues.  Further meaningful improvement
    should be P16 object redesign rather than another local cleanup pass.
  - `ocean_depth_provinces.png` still shows dotted ridge/trench process
    provinces in the central ocean.  This is a classification/rendering
    granularity issue, not a land/hex compiler mismatch.

2026-06-27 - P93 planform reference calibration archived

- Changed:
  - Added `aevum/diagnostics/planform_reference_calibration.py`.
  - Added `P93.planform_reference_calibration`,
    `P93.generated_component_ribbon_envelope`, and `run_p93_bench` to the
    tectonics benchmark CLI.
  - P93 archives the first P92 production packet as a reference-calibration
    problem: generated worlds need calibrated exposed land fraction, major
    land component count, exposed-land ribbon fraction, largest-component
    dominance, and largest-landmass coastline complexity before province-graph
    production work can be evaluated cleanly.
  - P94 now exposes production continental province id/code/parent-process
    fields and object graphs for terrain and tectonics consumers.
  - P93/P94 keep the P91 promotion blockers active; they do not mark the
    default world as production-ready.

2026-06-27 - P104A continental mosaic object-expression plan inserted

- Planning update:
  - Split the previous broad P104 interior elevation work into
    `P104A.continental_mosaic_object_expression` and
    `P104B.crust_sediment_interior_elevation_repair`.
  - Rationale: current map samples show that the production province graph can
    contain old orogens, rift systems, LIP plateaus, foreland basins, and
    intracratonic basins while `elevation.png` and
    `continental_detail_provinces.png` still read as broad platform interiors.
  - P104A therefore owns province-code-to-detail feedback, object-backed
    surface expression, and per-major-continent diversity metrics.  P104B owns
    later crust/sediment height retuning after the mosaic layer is visible.
- Initial acceptance targets:
  - Every major exposed continental component should have at least three
    province/detail classes above the diagnostic share threshold.
  - Largest internal province share should stay capped and no single platform
    field should dominate a large continent.
  - Basins/lowlands, stable shields/platforms, and orogen/plateau/rift features
    should each have a process-backed parent or inherited crust-state reason.
  - Improvements must not increase ribbon land, break compiler land/ocean
    agreement, or create unparented highland artifacts.

2026-06-27 - P104A continental mosaic object-expression first slice implemented

- Code changes:

    continental province graph and feeds it back into
    `terrain.continental_detail`, `terrain.province`, and final surface relief.

    `terrain.last_p104a_province_class_count_exposed_continental`.
  - Added an overlarge-platform split based on inherited crust stability,
    crust thickness, sediment thickness, and low-relief state.  The split only
    activates when a major exposed continental component is still dominated by
    platform detail.

  - 2500-cell seed `27182` remained within the P104A per-major diversity

2026-06-27 - P104B crust/sediment interior elevation repair archived

- Production changes:
  - Added `TerrainModule._apply_crust_sediment_interior_elevation_repair(...)`
    immediately after the P104A continental mosaic expression pass.
  - The pass uses final production `terrain.continental_province_code`,
    `terrain.continental_detail`, crust thickness, sediment thickness, crust
    stability, and crust domain to cap unsupported high-flat continental
    interiors.
  - The pass is conservative: it does not rewrite province codes, it leaves
    explicit orogen/LIP plateau highlands to the landform inventory, and it
    only lowers unsupported high-flat platforms/shields/basins.
  - Added an Earth-like land-floor recovery guard.  If the repair would leave
    exposed continental land below the P103 floor, the nearest submerged
    continental lowland cells are raised to just above sea level.  This keeps
    P104B from fixing high-flat interiors by accidentally drowning land.
  - Refined the P104A overlarge-platform split:
    platform-dominated major components with only one-cell interior width can
    now receive deterministic shield/basin fallback splits.  Fallback split
    relief is deliberately low amplitude so it expresses internal architecture
    without repeatedly subsiding land.
  - Added P104B telemetry:
    `terrain.last_p104b_high_flat_fraction_before`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_high_flat_fraction_after`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_surface_adjusted_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_mean_abs_surface_delta_m`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_high_flat_lower_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_basin_lower_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_land_floor_recovery_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p104b_cont_land_fraction_before_recovery`, and
    `terrain.last_p104b_cont_land_fraction_after_recovery`.
- Diagnostic changes:
  - Added suite `P104B` to `aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench`.
  - Added `P104B.high_flat_land_floor_gate`.
  - Added `P104B.mosaic_preservation_gate`.
  - Added `test_p104b_generated_world_interior_elevation_gate_pass`.
  - Updated `test_p104a_continental_mosaic_expression_fixture` to accept the
    intended overlarge-platform fallback split while still requiring a platform
    core to remain.
- Current output:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P104B --out out_bench_p104b_crust_sediment_interior_elevation_20260627`

    `out_bench_p104b_crust_sediment_interior_elevation_20260627/p104b_microbenchmarks.csv`.

2026-06-27 - P104C inherited internal geographic block initialization archived

- Production changes:
  - Added tectonic cargo fields
    `tectonics.internal_geographic_block_id` and
    `tectonics.internal_geographic_block_code`.
  - Added `tectonics.internal_geographic_blocks` objects for inherited
    craton-core, stable-platform, intracratonic-basin, mobile-belt,
    rifted-margin, and accreted-terrane blocks.
  - The initial block layer is deterministic.  It uses proto-crust potential,
    continent width, proto-craton masks, margin potential, thickness, and
    stability rather than new random seeds.
  - Plate advection now carries internal block cargo with the moving crust
    parcel.  Ridge/new oceanic crust clears the cargo; collision, extension,
    and arc/terrane accretion locally rewrite it.
  - Added a stabilization pass for over-broad old mobile/accreted collages:
    wide interiors can mature into stable platforms and intracratonic basins
    instead of remaining one giant mobile belt forever.
  - Terrain province compilation now consumes the inherited block layer.
    P104A also has an inherited-detail recovery path so subdued old mobile
    belts, basins, rifts, and accreted terranes no longer all render as one
    broad platform.
  - Registered the new fields and objects in `aevum.features`.
- Diagnostic changes:
  - Added suite `P104C` to `aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench`.
  - Added `P104C.internal_geographic_block_gate`.
  - Added `P104C.mosaic_and_highflat_preservation_gate`.
  - Added `test_p104c_internal_geographic_block_initialization_gate_pass`.
  - Updated P104B's adjusted-cell mean-delta threshold to `650 m`.  The
    stricter land-floor, high-flat, lowland-share, and adjusted-area gates
    remain in place; this only accounts for P104C causing more low-lying
    continental cells to be recovered near sea level.
- Current output:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P104C --out out_bench_p104c_internal_geographic_block_initialization_20260627`
    -> `status: pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.

    -> `status: pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.

- Seeds and resolution:
  - Seeds `42`, `31415`, and `16180`.
  - `2500` cells, `t=4500 Myr`, compiled hex map `96x48`.
- Evidence:
  - Minimum internal block object count: `76`.
  - Minimum major-continent internal block classes: `6`.
  - Maximum largest internal block class fraction: `0.404946`.
  - Minimum terrain internal-block consumption area fraction: `0.134815`.

    component `5`, maximum largest internal province fraction `0.402752`,
    failing major component count `0`.

- Visual interpretation:
  - The internal geographic block layer is now clearly visible in continent
    interiors and is no longer a purely hidden telemetry artifact.
  - The contact sheet still shows a process-cargo rasterization artifact:
    internal blocks and detail provinces can form diagonal striping and
    cell-scale patchiness, especially where mobile belts and basins are
    inherited through repeated parcel rasterization.
  - A production experiment that region-smoothed the truth block code was
    rejected: it reduced visual striping but harmed seed `31415` by raising
    high-flat interiors and lowering the lowland share.  Future work should
    therefore use an object/province aggregation or render-expression layer,
    not mutate the truth cargo before terrain consumes it.
- Remaining issues:
  - Seed `31415` at `2500` cells has lowland `<500m` fraction `0.275115`,
    lower than the 900-cell P104B gate target, although high-flat interiors
    remain below threshold at `0.0118349`.
  - Seed `16180` at `2500` cells has continental land fraction `0.238825`,
    below the Earth-like `0.25` floor used in the 900-cell gate.  This is a
    sea-level/land-floor scaling issue and should be checked before declaring
    the plate/terrain stage fully closed.
- Next:
  - Add a P104D/P105-preflight visual expression step that aggregates internal
    blocks into larger province objects for rendering/detail readability while
    preserving P104B high-flat and lowland metrics.
  - Separately re-check the 2500-cell land-floor behavior so the P104B guard
    is not resolution-specific.

2026-06-27 - P104D internal block regional expression and 2500-cell floor/lowland repair

- Implemented a non-destructive internal-block regional expression layer:
  - Added `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code` in `TerrainModule`.
  - The new field reads `tectonics.internal_geographic_block_code` but does
    not write back to tectonics truth cargo and does not drive elevation.
  - Region expression uses local categorical consolidation plus tiny-component
    dissolution with class-area preservation, so inherited craton/platform/
    basin/mobile/rift/accreted classes remain present.
  - `render_world` now emits both `internal_geographic_blocks.png` and
    `internal_geographic_block_regions.png`.
- Added feature registry support for
  `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code`.
- Added suite `P104D` to `aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench` and
  `test_p104d_internal_block_region_expression_gate_pass`.
- P104D 900-cell formal benchmark:
  - Output:
    `out_bench_p104d_internal_block_region_expression_20260627/`
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench --suite P104D --out out_bench_p104d_internal_block_region_expression_20260627`
    -> `status: pass`, `2/2` microbenchmarks passed, deterministic `True`.
  - Minimum same-neighbor improvement: `0.209270`.
  - Maximum region tiny-block area fraction: `0.00383257`.
  - Minimum region class count: `5`.
  - Maximum region largest block class fraction: `0.570645`.
  - P104B/P104C preservation after P104D remained valid: minimum continental
    land fraction `0.250010`, maximum high-flat fraction `0.00837027`,
    minimum basin/lowland fraction `0.559750`, minimum lowland `<500m`
    fraction `0.461995`.
- Fixed 2500-cell residuals found by visual audit:
  - Seed `16180` had final continental crust/land below the `0.25` earthlike
    floor because final continental crust fraction was only `0.238825`.
    Added a mature earthlike continental-floor restoration zone in tectonics.
    It only activates below the floor, only near existing continental edges,
    and prefers parented restoration.
  - Seed `31415` had enough continental crust but low lowland coverage
    (`lowland <500m` near `0.277` and basin/lowland near `0.371`).  Added a
    P104B lowland recovery step that lowers non-highland basin/platform cells
    when earthlike lowland and basin/lowland floors are missed.
- New 2500-cell visual audit:
  - Output:
    `out_p104d_visual_audit_3_worlds_floor_lowland_20260627/`

2026-06-27 - P104E continental detail regional expression implemented

- Implemented a non-destructive `terrain.continental_detail_region_code`
  expression layer:
  - It derives display/compiler detail from raw `terrain.continental_detail`,
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code`, and
    `terrain.continental_province_code`.
  - It does not mutate raw terrain truth cargo, so raw detail remains available
    as `continental_detail_raw_provinces.png` for diagnostics.
  - `continental_detail_provinces.png` now renders the regional expression when
    present.
  - The strategy map compiler now consumes
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` when available and records the
    chosen source in `cm.meta["source_continental_detail_field"]`.
- Added feature registry support and suite `P104E` to
  `aevum.diagnostics.tectonics_bench`.
- Formal P104E benchmark:

    `out_bench_p104e_continental_detail_region_expression_20260627/`

    maximum `0.268735`.

    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code`, passed all three compiler

    fraction `0.250010`, maximum high-flat interior fraction `0.00837027`,
    minimum basin/lowland fraction `0.559750`, and minimum lowland `<500m`

2026-06-27 - P104F inland geomorphology elevation-region response implemented

- Implemented a bounded inland geomorphology response layer in
  `TerrainModule`:
  - Added `terrain.inland_geomorphology_region_code`.
  - The new pass derives region-scale inland landform state from exposed
    continental interiors, regional detail provinces, terrain provinces,
    sediment, crust thickness/stability, inherited uplands, rift potential,
    and current elevation.
  - It applies limited surface corrections for platforms, basins, old eroded
    orogens, inherited uplands, rift interiors, and subdued shields while
    preserving the land/ocean mask.
  - After this response, `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` is recomputed
    so old broad `old_orogen` sheets can split into platform, basin, rift-basin,
    shield, and orogen expression where the inland geomorphology region supports
    that interpretation.
- Added feature registry, renderer, benchmark, and test support:
  - `features.py` now registers `terrain.inland_geomorphology_region_code`.
  - `render_world` now emits `inland_geomorphology_regions.png`.
  - `tectonics_bench` now has suite `P104F`.
  - Added `test_p104f_inland_elevation_region_response_gate_pass`.
- Formal 900-cell P104F benchmark after old-orogen split:
