2026-06-28 - P105A large closed-ocean connectivity guard implemented

- Problem found in the 18/24-plate large-map experiment:
  - A connected supercontinent is not itself wrong, but a large ocean-scale
    waterbody fully enclosed by land is a serious planform error.
  - Existing metrics over-counted `ocean.basin_id` classes and did not ask
    whether ocean water components were connected on the sphere.
- Implemented diagnostics:
  - `validation._ocean_geography_metrics` now reports ocean connected
    components, largest-ocean share, maximum closed-ocean candidate area, total
    secondary-ocean area, and counts of secondary components above world/ocean
    area thresholds.
  - Tectonic diagnostics now warn when a non-main ocean component exceeds the
    large closed-ocean envelope.
  - Added `test_ocean_geography_reports_large_closed_ocean_candidates`.
- Fixed field ordering:
  - `TerrainModule._ocean_geography` now masks ocean fields against final land:
    land cells get no basin/gateway id and zero depth/margin/shelf labels.
  - The terrain step refreshes final `land/ocean`, ocean geography, ocean
    fabric, margin landforms, and arc/plume landforms after P104 surface
    repairs, so exported elevation and ocean fields use the same sea-level cut.
- Implemented terrain repair:
  - Added `_connect_large_closed_oceans`, an Earth-like mature-world pass after
    P96 surface ordering and before final terrain/ocean classification.
  - The pass only targets non-main ocean components at least `2%` of world area
    or `5%` of ocean area.
  - It opens a narrow seaway from the enclosed waterbody to the main ocean along
    the lowest-cost land path, strongly penalizing cratons, stable interiors,
    high terrain, and broad continental interiors while favoring margins,
    sutures, accreted terranes, weak lithosphere, and narrow land bridges.
  - Newly opened closed-ocean seaways are recorded as
    `terrain.closed_ocean_seaway_opened_corridors` and protected alongside
    breakup seaways so later ocean-floor regionalization does not refill them.
  - Added `test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean`.
- Verification:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_ocean_geography_reports_large_closed_ocean_candidates tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_tectonic_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_archive -q`
    -> `3 passed in 44.70s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms -q`
    -> `1 passed in 17.16s`.
  - 24-plate / 4000-cell quick audit:
    `out_closed_ocean_repair_24p_4000_20260628/`.
    Final `ocean_component_count=7`,
    `largest_ocean_component_fraction_of_ocean=0.993954`,
    `max_closed_ocean_candidate_fraction_world=0.001500`,
    `closed_ocean_candidate_count_gt2pct_world=0`, and
    `closed_ocean_candidate_fraction_of_ocean=0.006046`.
    This sample had no large closed-ocean candidate at the repair point, so it
    did not need to open a seaway.
- Remaining work:
  - Re-run the original 24-plate / 16000-cell long audit as a dedicated
    performance job. The attempted full run was interrupted in late
    margin/drainage aggregation, not inside the closed-ocean repair itself.
  - Add a high-resolution P105A microbenchmark once the 16k audit runtime is
    reduced or moved to a long-run suite.
  - Separate follow-up issue: high target plate counts still collapse to too few
    active modern plates in some samples; that is a plate activation/topology
    problem, not the closed-ocean connectivity guard.

2026-06-28 - P105B patchy open-ocean shoal/ridge artifact repair

- Problem:
  - The 24-plate review map showed mid-ocean patchy shallow-looking blobs.
  - Code attribution showed these were mostly sparse `ridge` boundary/object
    cells dilated into `OCEAN_DEPTH_RIDGE`, plus open-ocean shoal cells that
    were only deepened to about `-1700m`.
  - Geological interpretation: Earth has mid-ocean ridges, aseismic ridges,
    seamount chains, and oceanic plateaus, but not broad random checkerboard
    shallow seas in the middle of abyssal basins.
- Implemented:
  - Added `_coherent_open_ocean_ridge_zone` to filter compact one-off ridge
    blobs. It requires ridge components to have enough graph elongation/area
    before they remain classified as coherent ridge province.
  - Applied that filter in `_regionalize_ocean_floor`,
    `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals`, and `_ocean_geography`.
  - Retuned far-open-ocean depth constraints:
    - coherent ridge highs are deepened to at least about `-2600m`;
    - non-ridge far-ocean shoals shallower than about `-2600m` are deepened to
      at least about `-3200m`.
  - Added `test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow`.
- Verification:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_ocean_geography_reports_large_closed_ocean_candidates -q`
    -> `3 passed in 0.64s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r6_object_derived_terrain_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed in 13.06s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `1 passed in 0.80s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow tests/test_engine.py::test_tectonic_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_archive tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms -q`
    -> `3 passed in 59.93s`.
  - 24-plate / 4000-cell quick visual audit:
    `out_ocean_shoal_ridge_fix_24p_4000_20260628/`.
    `far_ocean_shallow_fraction_lt1500m=0.0`,
    `shelf_to_abyss_depth_delta_m=4288.88`,
    `abyss_fraction_of_ocean=0.584255`, and
    `max_closed_ocean_candidate_fraction_world=0.001500`.
- Remaining work:
  - This pass intentionally suppresses noisy, compact ridge fragments in the
    generated 24-plate sample; the next improvement should rebuild modern
    mid-ocean ridges as continuous spreading-center polylines/objects rather
    than relying on sparse boundary cell dilation.
  - Add explicit oceanic plateau / hotspot seamount-chain objects before
    allowing isolated mid-ocean shallows to remain shallow.

2026-06-28 - P105C patchy open-ocean deep-pit suppression

- Problem:
  - After P105B, the map still showed sea-floor spots.  These were no longer
    shallow-sea artifacts; they were mostly dark, compact deep pits.
  - Code attribution found sparse `trench` and `transform` process cells being
    directly converted into `-5000m` trench axes, `-4200m` trench aprons, or
    `-3600m` transform zones, even when they were isolated far from coherent
    active margins.
- Implemented:
  - Added `_coherent_open_ocean_deep_zone` for trench/transform filtering.
  - Added `_component_graph_diameter_steps` and reused it for ridge, trench,
    and transform coherence checks.
  - Added `_suppress_patchy_open_ocean_depth_spots`, which raises compact
    non-preserved far-ocean deep spots back to ordinary deep-ocean depth.
  - Applied trench filtering in `_ocean_geography`,
    `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals`, and
    `_regionalize_ocean_floor`; applied transform filtering in
    `_regionalize_ocean_floor`.
  - Added `test_patchy_open_ocean_trench_fragments_do_not_create_deep_pits`.
- Verification:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_trench_fragments_do_not_create_deep_pits tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r6_object_derived_terrain_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed in 12.34s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_trench_fragments_do_not_create_deep_pits tests/test_engine.py::test_tectonic_diagnostics_cover_current_world_and_archive tests/test_engine.py::test_geography_primitives_cover_sphere_and_follow_landforms -q`
    -> `4 passed in 59.62s`.
  - 24-plate / 4000-cell quick visual audit:
    `out_ocean_patch_suppression_24p_4000_20260628/`.
    Trench fraction fell from the previous quick audit's `0.085778` to
    `0.002026`, `far_ocean_shallow_fraction_lt1500m=0.0`,
    `nearshore_superdeep_fraction_gt2500m=0.036519`, and
    `max_closed_ocean_candidate_fraction_world=0.001500`.
- Remaining work:
  - The generated ocean floor now avoids obvious point-like pockmarks, but the
    basin floor is still organized into broad faceted provinces.  That should
    be addressed by rebuilding spreading centers, fracture zones, abyssal
    plains, oceanic plateaus, and seamount chains as explicit ocean-fabric
    objects instead of relying on current-frame sparse process cells.

2026-06-28 - P105D coherent ocean-floor fabric restored after patch suppression
- Problem:
  - P105B/P105C correctly removed isolated open-ocean shallow/deep process-cell
    artifacts, but the quick 24-plate audit then looked too simple: complex
    ocean-floor fabric had been suppressed along with the speckle.
  - The root cause was that `_regionalize_ocean_floor` filtered sparse current
    ridge/trench/transform cells but did not rebuild a coherent replacement from
    persistent crust-age structure.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_ocean_age_contrast`, `_derived_open_ocean_ridge_zone`, and
    `_apply_coherent_ocean_floor_fabric`.
  - `_derived_open_ocean_ridge_zone` promotes only connected young open-ocean
    ridge axes from the crust-age field when explicit ridge objects are too
    sparse; it now selects the local youngest axis within young-crust belts
    rather than the whole young region.
  - `_apply_coherent_ocean_floor_fabric` adds continuous ridge highs,
    age-discontinuity fracture/transform lineations, and low-amplitude
    age-smoothed abyssal relief inside open ocean, while keeping trench and
    spot-suppression guards active.
  - `_ocean_geography`, `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals`, and
    `_regionalize_ocean_floor` now share the derived ridge zone so elevation
    and depth-province maps stay aligned.
- Tests:
  - Added `test_age_derived_open_ocean_ridge_restores_coherent_bathymetry`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_trench_fragments_do_not_create_deep_pits tests/test_engine.py::test_age_derived_open_ocean_ridge_restores_coherent_bathymetry tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_r6_object_derived_terrain_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `5 passed in 12.60s`.
- 24-plate / 4000-cell quick visual audit:
  - `out_ocean_fabric_restore_axis_24p_4000_20260628/`.
  - Ridge fraction restored to `0.033454` of ocean, trench fraction remains
    low at `0.002027`, `far_ocean_shallow_fraction_lt1500m=0.0`,
    `nearshore_superdeep_fraction_gt2500m=0.040708`, and
    `max_closed_ocean_candidate_fraction_world=0.001500`.
  - Ocean-fabric object count: `73` (`age_isochron=58`, `abyssal_plain=8`,
    `spreading_center=5`, `transform_fault=2`).
- Remaining work:
  - Ridge/fabric expression is now present again, but the categorical
    `ocean_depth_provinces.png` still shows some ridge patches as blocky
    polygons.  A later ocean-fabric object pass should represent long
    ridge-transform systems, fracture-zone swarms, seamount chains, and oceanic
    plateaus explicitly rather than relying only on age-derived raster fallback.

2026-06-28 - P106 ocean-fabric object pass plan archived
- Objective:
  - Move ocean-floor complexity from raster fallback into explicit geologic
    objects while preserving the P105 fixes for isolated far-ocean shallow
    blobs, deep pockmarks, and large closed-ocean artifacts.
  - Generated ocean basins should contain Earth-like categories of seafloor
    structure: continuous ridge-transform networks, fracture-zone swarms,
    abyssal-hill/age fabric, seamount chains, oceanic plateaus, trench-arc-
    backarc systems, passive-margin sediment systems, and microcontinental
    fragments where crustal lineage supports them.
- Principles:
  - No return to random ocean patches.  Every far-ocean bathymetric anomaly must
    have parentage from spreading, transform shear, age discontinuity, hotspot,
    LIP, subduction/backarc extension, sediment routing, or continental-crust
    fragmentation.
  - Generate or promote process objects first, then let those objects shape
    bathymetry, `ocean.depth_province`, `ocean.margin_type`, render maps, and
    compiler-facing terrain categories.
  - Keep sphere topology explicit: ridge, fracture, seaway, and basin objects
    must survive the longitude seam and must not be interpreted as planar
    disconnected strips.
  - Each substep gets a microbenchmark plus a 24-plate Earth-like visual audit
    using `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`, `ocean_depth_provinces.png`,
    `ocean_margin_type.png`, `crust_age.png`, and `tectonic_objects.png`.
- P106.0 baseline and acceptance metrics:
  - Add/collect metrics for ridge object count, ridge ocean fraction, transform
    fault count and length, fracture-zone count and span, trench count and
    fraction, seamount-chain count, oceanic-plateau fraction, abyssal-plain
    fraction, isolated shallow-blob count, and isolated deep-pit count.
  - Acceptance: patch artifact metrics remain near zero while at least
    spreading-center, transform/fracture, abyssal-plain, and age-fabric objects
    are visible in generated Earth-like worlds.
- P106.1 continuous ridge-axis network:
  - Rebuild ridge expression around explicit `tectonics.spreading_centers`,
    boundary objects, and crust-age young axes.
  - Depth province `OCEAN_DEPTH_RIDGE` should mark the ridge axis and immediate
    flanks, not the whole young-ocean polygon.
  - Tests: ridge median depth is shallower than old abyssal crust; ridge axes
    are elongated, seam-safe, and not inflated into broad shallow patches.
- P106.2 transform faults and fracture zones:
  - Derive transform faults from ridge offsets, plate-motion shear, and
    ridge-near age discontinuities; extend inactive traces into fracture zones
    away from active ridges.
  - Fracture zones should create narrow, coherent lineations and low-amplitude
    bathymetric modulation, not point pits.
  - Tests: `terrain.ocean_fabric` includes `transform_fault` and
    `fracture_zone`; combined fracture span is larger than isolated blobs; P105
    patchy-pit regressions continue to pass.
- P106.3 abyssal hills and age fabric:
  - Use ridge distance, crust age, and sediment smoothing to create subtle
    abyssal-hill texture: younger oceanic crust rougher, older sedimented
    abyssal plains smoother.
  - Age isochrons should guide texture direction and basin fabric rather than
    become large categorical patches.
  - Tests: young-ocean relief variance exceeds old sedimented abyssal plains,
    while abyssal plains remain regionally flat and non-speckled.
- P106.4 seamount chains, hotspot tracks, and guyots:
  - Generate chain objects from plume/hotspot parentage and plate motion; encode
    age progression from active volcanic end to older submerged guyots.
  - Most seamounts should remain submarine; only a small fraction may form
    islands where uplift and sea level support exposure.
  - Tests: seamount chains have ordered age progression, plume/hotspot parent
    IDs, and do not increase far-ocean shallow-blob metrics.
- P106.5 oceanic plateaus and LIPs:
  - Promote `DOMAIN_LIP`, `tectonics.lips`, and plume-impact contexts into
    broad oceanic plateau or aseismic-ridge objects.
  - Plateaus should be wide, coherent, shallower than adjacent abyssal plains,
    and usually submarine; area caps prevent basin-scale flooding of shallow
    province.
  - Tests: plateau area stays below cap, edge gradients are continuous, and
    parent process is plume/LIP rather than random crust noise.
- P106.6 trench-arc-backarc systems:
  - Couple closing margins to trench, forearc, island/continental arc, and
    backarc-basin objects.
  - Backarc basins can create young oceanic crust and local shallow highs, but
    should remain parented to subduction/extension context.
  - Tests: trenches are narrow and deep, spatially adjacent to active margins
    and arcs; backarc basins are not misclassified as random closed oceans.
- P106.7 passive-margin sediment systems:
  - Let shelf, slope, rise, submarine fan, and canyon/channel expression depend
    on passive-margin age, sediment supply, drainage outlets, and basin
    accommodation.
  - Tests: passive margins show variable shelf/rise width; major drainage
    outlets create larger submarine fans; nearshore-superdeep guards do not
    regress.
- P106.8 microcontinents and continental fragments:
  - Allow shallow offshore plateaus only where continental-crust lineage,
    rifted-margin fragments, or terrane provenance supports them.
  - Keep microcontinents distinct from oceanic plateaus and seamount chains.
  - Tests: far-ocean shallow continental plateaus require continental crust
    parentage; oceanic crust cannot form unparented shallow blobs.
- Recommended execution order:
  - First complete P106.0, P106.1, and P106.2, because ridge-transform-fracture
    fabric is the ocean-basin skeleton and directly addresses the current
    blocky/faceted depth-province residual.
  - Then implement P106.3 for background basin texture, followed by P106.4 and
    P106.5 for hotspot/LIP relief, P106.6 for active-margin ocean systems,
    P106.7 for passive-margin sediment expression, and P106.8 for
    microcontinental exceptions.

2026-06-28 - P106.4b parented archipelago exposure and final island trim
- Root cause:
  - The earlier ocean-floor pass intentionally capped hotspot/seamount-chain
    and oceanic-plateau relief below sea level to eliminate random far-ocean
    shallow patches.  That solved the shallow-blob problem but also made valid
    oceanic archipelagos nearly impossible in the rendered terrain.
  - Late terrain expression passes could also re-expose one-cell oceanic highs
    after `_drop_unsupported_land_components`, leaving occasional unparented
    oceanic islands in final maps.
- Design update:
  - Added sparse parented archipelago exposure from hotspot/seamount chains,
    oceanic plateaus/LIPs, and microcontinent candidates.  Exposure is budgeted
    per feature component and keeps most related cells submarine.
  - Protected small parent-backed archipelagos during final component
    compaction and unsupported-component dropping when cells carry recent
    volcanism, LIP, accreted-terrane, plume-impact, arc, or terrane parentage.
  - Added a final post-expression trim that removes only tiny oceanic land
    components without tectonic/volcanic parentage, preserving parented
    hotspot/arc/LIP/microcontinent archipelagos.
- Metrics and acceptance:
  - `validation._ocean_geography_metrics` now reports
    `parented_archipelago_component_count`,
    `parented_archipelago_area_fraction_world`, and
    `unparented_oceanic_island_component_count`.
  - `p106_full_ocean_feature_coverage` now requires at least one parented
    archipelago and zero unparented oceanic island components, in addition to
    the existing ocean-fabric and artifact guards.
- Verification:
  - Targeted fixture: a hotspot chain with 75 oceanic cells exposes only 3
    island nodes, reports `parented_archipelago_component_count=3`, and keeps
    `unparented_oceanic_island_component_count=0`.
  - Generated 36-plate, 1200-cell, 4500 Myr Earth-like run after the final trim:
    `parented_archipelago_component_count=1`,
    `unparented_oceanic_island_component_count=0`,
    `unparented_far_ocean_shallow_component_count_lt1500m=0`, and
    `isolated_far_ocean_deep_pit_component_count_gt4100m=0`.
  - Regression command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p106_ocean_feature_metrics_cover_object_categories tests/test_engine.py::test_age_discontinuity_fallback_generates_fracture_zone_objects tests/test_engine.py::test_arc_plume_landforms_promote_p106_oceanic_objects tests/test_engine.py::test_parented_hotspot_chain_can_form_limited_archipelago tests/test_engine.py::test_final_trim_preserves_parented_archipelago_and_drowns_unparented_island tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_trench_fragments_do_not_create_deep_pits tests/test_engine.py::test_age_derived_open_ocean_ridge_restores_coherent_bathymetry tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e4_real_earth_arc_plume_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    passed with `10 passed`.
- Follow-up status:
  - Superseded by the P106 closeout below.  The low-resolution-safe
    transform-tail/fracture fallback has now been added, so the 1200-cell
    generated terminal world also produces a parented `fracture_zone` object.

2026-06-28 - P106 closeout: large-grid ocean-object audit and Earth comparison
- Objective alignment:
  - The user asked to finish P106 with more initial geographic units and a
    larger map because small worlds suppress geomorphic diversity.  The
    closeout audit therefore uses an Earth-like 36-plate, 8000-cell terminal
    world instead of the earlier 12-plate/1200-cell smoke scale.
  - The goal remains ocean-floor complexity as explicit process-parented
    objects, not random shallow/deep raster patches.
- Implementation deltas since P106.4b:
  - Added a low-resolution transform-tail fallback in
    `_ocean_fabric_objects`: if active transform cells do not leave a coherent
    inactive fracture trace after the normal two-pass halo, a three-pass,
    age/ridge-distance-gated tail is promoted through the same coherence filter.
    This resolves the previous 1200-cell `fracture_zone` gap without relaxing
    the open-ocean pit filter.
  - Tightened `_suppress_patchy_open_ocean_depth_spots` so tiny far-ocean
    deep components are raised even when their graph elongation is misleading
    at sparse resolution.
  - `validation._ocean_geography_metrics` now ignores parented deep lineations
    overlapping fracture/transform/trench objects when counting isolated
    far-ocean deep pits, so real process objects are not misclassified as
    random pockmarks.
  - Large-grid terrain stage telemetry now uses approximate component summaries
    for non-final stages at `grid.n > 5000`, avoiding unnecessary BFS cost
    while retaining exact summaries at key final checkpoints.
- Final 36-plate / 8000-cell audit:
  - Output directory:
    `out_p106_final_36p_8000_20260628_c/`.
  - Primary maps:
    `elevation.png`, `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`,
    `ocean_depth_provinces.png`, `tectonic_objects.png`, `plates.png`,
    and `crust_age.png`.
  - Metrics file:
    `out_p106_final_36p_8000_20260628_c/p106_ocean_metrics.json`.
  - Key pass metrics:
    `p106_core_ocean_fabric_complete=True`,
    `p106_full_ocean_feature_coverage=True`,
    `p106_object_category_count=13`,
    `spreading_center_object_count=6`,
    `transform_fault_object_count=6`,
    `fracture_zone_object_count=3`,
    `fracture_zone_combined_span_deg=167.733`,
    `abyssal_plain_object_count=23`,
    `abyssal_hill_object_count=13`,
    `age_isochron_object_count=77`,
    `seamount_chain_object_count=11`,
    `oceanic_plateau_object_count=14`,
    `back_arc_basin_object_count=13`,
    `passive_margin_wedge_object_count=16`,
    `delta_fan_object_count=6`,
    `margin_trench_object_count=4`,
    and `parented_archipelago_component_count=24`.
  - Artifact guards:
    `unparented_oceanic_island_component_count=0`,
    `unparented_far_ocean_shallow_component_count_lt1500m=0`,
    `isolated_far_ocean_deep_pit_component_count_gt4100m=0`,
    `ridge_fraction_of_ocean=0.025960`,
    `trench_fraction_of_ocean=0.000883`,
    `far_ocean_shallow_fraction_lt1500m=0.002407`,
    `nearshore_superdeep_fraction_gt2500m=0.037835`,
    and `max_closed_ocean_candidate_fraction_world=0.009876`.
- Reference-Earth comparison assets:
  - Downloaded NOAA/NCEI ETOPO5 global relief data into
    `data/reference/etopo5/`.
  - Rendered direct ETOPO5 and ETOPO5 sampled to the Aevum 8000-cell sphere
    grid under the same segmented absolute elevation legend used by current
    `render_world`.
  - Main comparison sheet:
    `out_reference_earth_elevation_20260628/earth_8000_vs_aevum_36p_8000_segmented_elevation_legend.png`.
  - Three-way sheet:
    `out_reference_earth_elevation_20260628/earth_direct_vs_earth8000_vs_aevum36p8000_segmented_elevation_legend.png`.
  - Raster cache:
    `out_reference_earth_elevation_20260628/segmented_elevation_legend_comparison_data.npz`.
  - The comparison shows that P106 ocean objects are now richer and mostly
    parented, but it also exposes the next non-P106 terrain issue: Aevum's
    continental interiors still skew too high, with too much 500-2000 m
    yellow-green/yellow terrain and too little 0-500 m deep-green lowland
    compared with real Earth sampled to the same grid.
- Render update:
  - `render.ELEVATION_CMAP` now uses a fixed segmented absolute-height legend
    instead of per-map symmetric percentile scaling.  Land bins are expanded at
    0-200 m, 200-500 m, 500-1000 m, 1000-2000 m, 2000-3000 m,
    3000-4500 m, and 4500-6000 m, while ocean remains a fixed negative-depth
    blue ramp.  This makes Aevum and Earth elevation maps visually comparable.

    tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e4_real_earth_arc_plume_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `13 passed in 1.16s`.

    interrupted after `97 passed, 9 failed` in broader P34-P44 / plate

2026-06-28 - P107 plan archived: boundary clarity, microplates, and 24000-cell geomorphic expression
- Why a new phase is required:
  - P106 made the ocean-object layer parented and much less random, but it did
    not solve the larger tectonic readability problem.  The final 36-plate /
    8000-cell `plates.png` visually collapses to roughly 6-7 macro plates, so
    the model still loses the small-plate and microplate hierarchy seen in
    real plate models.
  - Major process belts are under-expressed or visually ambiguous: Atlantic-
    style mid-ocean ridges, Tibet-style continental collision plateaus,
    Andean-style active continental margins, and western-Pacific/first-island-
    chain-style trench-arc-backarc systems should be readable as different
    boundary provinces.
  - Ocean interiors still need smaller process-parented relief: island chains,
    submarine plateaus, microcontinents/continental fragments, narrow trenches,
    abyssal-hill fabric, and seamount/guyot chains.  P106 has objects for many
    of these, but the plate and terrain layers do not yet make them crisp.
  - `8000` cells are useful for routine regression, but they are too coarse for
    final judgment of microplates, narrow island arcs, trenches, ridge offsets,
    and small oceanic islands.  `24000` cells become the main visual acceptance
    tier for P107; `8000` remains the fast gate.
- P107 target state:
  - Preserve a ranked plate hierarchy through the terminal world:
    `macro/major` plates, `minor` plates, and protected `microplate` objects.
  - Boundary systems should produce named province objects and terrain response:
    ridge-transform-fracture networks, continental collision plateaus and
    suture belts, active continental-margin arcs, trench-arc-backarc systems,
    passive margins, and transform boundaries.
  - Plate maps, tectonic-object maps, bathymetry, elevation, and crust-age maps
    must agree: ridges should align with young oceanic crust and shallow axial
    bathymetry; trenches should be narrow and deep beside arcs; collision belts
    should create broad high plateaus plus flanking ranges/foreland basins; arc
    chains should be narrow and discontinuous rather than large mainland blobs.
  - 24000-cell audits should reveal more detail than 8000-cell audits instead
    of merely scaling the same coarse shapes.
- P107.0 baseline and instrumentation:
  - Add a terminal-world audit serializer for high-resolution runs.  It should
    save compact `.npz` arrays for at least `tectonics.plate_id`,
    `terrain.elevation_m`, `crust.type`, `crust.age_myr`,
    `ocean.depth_province`, `terrain.province`, `tectonics.boundaries`, and key
    object masks.  This prevents future analyses from depending on rendered PNG
    colors.
  - Add metrics:
    `plate_rank_counts`, `plate_area_fraction_histogram`,
    `terminal_active_plate_count`, `terminal_major_plate_count`,
    `terminal_minor_plate_count`, `terminal_microplate_count`,
    `protected_microplate_count`, `merged_microplate_count`,
    `boundary_province_object_count_by_kind`,
    `ridge_transform_network_continuity`,
    `collision_plateau_area_fraction`,
    `active_margin_arc_trench_adjacency_fraction`,
    `island_arc_chain_count`, `microcontinent_object_count`,
    `parented_oceanic_island_chain_count`, and
    `deep_trench_fraction_below_6000m`.
  - Baseline runs:
    `8000` cells / 36 seeds for fast comparison and `24000` cells / 48-60
    initial plate seeds for the first real visual audit.  Every baseline must
    preserve raw arrays and rendered assets.
  - Acceptance for P107.0 is not quality improvement yet; it is complete
    observability.  We must be able to answer how many large/minor/micro plates
    exist from arrays, not from `plates.png`.
- P107.1 ranked plate identity and merge policy:
  - Replace the single area cutoff `MIN_PLATE_AREA_FRAC = 0.008` with a ranked
    policy:
    - oceanic speckle plates without boundary/process parentage may merge;
    - island-arc, backarc, transform-bounded, rift-fragment, collision-wedge,
      microcontinent, and young-ocean microplates are protected while their
      parent process remains active or geologically recent;
    - stale microplates can be accreted into adjacent major plates through
      explicit accretion/suture events instead of silent label capture.
  - Add fields/objects such as `tectonics.plate_rank`,
    `tectonics.microplates`, `tectonics.plate_parentage`,
    `tectonics.plate_accretion_events`, and `tectonics.protected_plate_id`.
  - Tests:
    synthetic fixtures for oceanic speckle merge, island-arc microplate
    preservation, backarc microplate preservation, and continental-fragment
    accretion.  Generated-world gate should keep more terminal active plates
    than the current 6-7 visual macroplate collapse while avoiding noisy
    checkerboard plate IDs.
- P107.2 boundary-province skeleton:
  - Promote current raster/network boundaries into explicit boundary-province
    objects with length, width, age, adjacent plate IDs, crust pairing,
    kinematics, and lifecycle stage.
  - Boundary kinds:
    `mid_ocean_ridge`, `ridge_transform_fault`,
    `inactive_fracture_zone`, `ocean_ocean_subduction_trench`,
    `continental_arc_margin`, `island_arc_trench`, `backarc_spreading_center`,
    `continent_continent_collision`, `suture_zone`, `strike_slip_boundary`,
    and `passive_margin`.
  - Tests:
    ridge objects are long and seam-safe; ridge transforms offset ridge axes;
    trenches are adjacent to arcs/backarcs; collision belts connect two
    continental plates; transform boundaries are narrow and do not create
    mountain walls by default.
- P107.3 terrain response by boundary province:
  - Move the most visible high/low relief decisions from local cell thresholds
    to boundary-province templates:
    - Atlantic analogue: continuous ridge high, transform offsets, young crust,
      flanking abyssal-hill fabric, no random shallow blobs.
    - Tibet analogue: broad collision plateau, high ranges on margins, foreland
      basins and suture belts, not a single thin red line.
    - Andes analogue: narrow trench, continental arc/high range, forearc and
      backarc/foreland basin, asymmetric relief.
    - First-island-chain analogue: trench + island arc + backarc basin + small
      protected arc microplates, discontinuous islands and submarine arcs.
  - Tests:
    synthetic analog fixtures for those four named process types; generated
    world metrics must show distinct signatures instead of one generic mountain
    belt.
- P107.4 ocean interior fine structure:
  - Use P106 objects as parents but increase terrain expression at 24000:
    hotspot island/seamount chains with age progression, guyots, oceanic
    plateaus/LIPs, microcontinents, aseismic ridges, abyssal plains/hills,
    fracture-zone swarms, and narrow ultra-deep trenches.
  - Keep all features parented.  Random far-ocean shallow/deep patch guards
    remain active.
  - Tests:
    parented archipelago count increases at 24000 without increasing
    unparented shallow blobs; microcontinents require continental lineage;
    `deep_trench_fraction_below_6000m` is nonzero only in trench contexts.
- P107.5 24000-cell audit ladder:
  - Fast gate: 8000 cells, 36-48 seed plates, render `plates`, `elevation`,
    `bathymetry`, `ocean_depth_provinces`, `tectonic_objects`, `crust_age`,
    plus raw audit `.npz`.
  - Main gate: 24000 cells, 48-60 seed plates, same outputs and metrics.  This
    is the first tier allowed to answer whether island chains, microplates,
    trenches, and ridge offsets look plausible.
  - Optional offline gate: 72000 cells only after 24000 passes and only for
    visual/detail scaling, not for first-time correctness.
  - Contact sheets must include real Earth ETOPO5 sampled to the same Aevum
    grid under the segmented elevation legend.
- P107.6 acceptance criteria:
  - Terminal plate hierarchy:
    at least several major plates plus visible minor/microplates; exact Earth
    counts are not mandatory, but the terminal system must no longer collapse
    to only 6-7 macro color regions after initializing 36+ seeds.
  - Boundary clarity:
    ridge, active continental margin, continent-continent collision, island
    arc/trench/backarc, transform, and passive margin must be separately
    visible in object maps and expressed in elevation/bathymetry.
  - Ocean interior:
    parented island chains, microcontinents/oceanic plateaus, abyssal plains,
    fracture-zone lineations, and deep trenches must exist without reintroducing
    random far-ocean patches.
  - Resolution behavior:
    24000 maps must show more meaningful small-scale structure than 8000 maps,
    and raw-array metrics must confirm the improvement rather than relying on
    prettier rendering.
- Initial execution order:
  1. Implement P107.0 audit serialization and plate-rank metrics.
  2. Implement P107.1 protected microplate merge/accretion policy.
  3. Implement P107.2 boundary-province object skeleton.
  4. Implement P107.3 terrain response templates for Atlantic/Tibet/Andes/
     island-chain analogues.
  5. Implement P107.4 ocean-interior fine-structure expression.
  6. Run the P107.5 8000 -> 24000 audit ladder and compare against real Earth
     sampled to the same grid.

2026-06-29 - P107 implementation audit and next plan
- Implemented P107.0 observability:
  - Added terminal audit serialization through `aevum.diagnostics.p107_audit`.
    Each run writes raw terminal arrays to `p107_terminal_arrays.npz`, metrics
    to `p107_terminal_metrics.json`, an optional contact sheet, and a same-grid
    ETOPO5 Earth reference panel.
  - Added the `p107-audit` CLI command with configurable cells, seed plates,
    time horizon, frame count, output path, and feature flags.
  - Metrics now report terminal plate hierarchy, protected/merged
    microplates, boundary province counts, ridge/transform continuity,
    collision plateau area, arc-trench adjacency, parented ocean features, and
    trench-only ultra-deep bathymetry.
- Implemented the first production pieces of P107.1-P107.3:
  - Added terminal `tectonics.plate_rank` and
    `tectonics.protected_plate_id` fields.
  - Added protected microplate logic for process-parented small plates:
    island arcs, microcontinents/terranes, collision wedges,
    transform-bounded fragments, rift/backarc fragments, young oceanic
    microplates, and plume/plateau microplates.
  - Added explicit `microplate` and `plate_accretion_event` objects so later
    stages can distinguish protected survival from accretion or silent merge.
  - Added a guarded hierarchy-restoration split for underresolved terminal
    plate systems.  This is deterministic and biased toward existing split
    evidence, but it remains a repair layer rather than a full force-balance
    plate model.
  - Added `tectonics.boundary_province_kind` plus boundary province objects for
    mid-ocean ridges, ridge-transform faults, ocean-ocean trenches,
    continental arc margins, island-arc trenches, backarc spreading centers,
    continent-continent collisions, sutures/continental rifts, strike-slip
    boundaries, and passive margins.
  - Added gated terrain response by boundary province: shallow ridge axes,
    narrow deep trench slots, continental-arc uplift, collision plateau/range
    uplift, passive-margin lowering, and a conservative continental shallow
    payback that keeps land fraction in a plausible range without drowning
    trench systems.
- Targeted verification:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/tectonics.py
    aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py aevum/cli.py
    tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    passed.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py
    tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p20_resolved_plate_topology_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `7 passed in 0.64s`.
  - Broader historical full-suite failures were not used as P107 closeout
    evidence because they include older P34-P44 continuity and reasonableness
    tests outside this scoped repair.
- P107 audit evidence:
  - Old 24000/60 baseline:
    `out_p107_ladder_8000_24000_20260628/01_24000cells_60p`.
    Terminal land fraction `0.2487`; active/major/minor/micro/tiny plates
    `9/7/0/1/1`; protected microplates `0`; merged microplates `18`;
    ridge components `171`; largest ridge component share `0.0940`;
    ultra-deep trench fraction `0.0`.
  - New 8000/36 gate:
    `out_p107_after_p1071d_8000_20260629/00_8000cells_36p`.
    Terminal land fraction `0.2634`; active/major/minor/micro/tiny plates
    `17/9/5/3/0`; protected plate count `17`; protected microplates `3`;
    merged microplates `3`; named boundary provinces include mid-ocean ridges,
    ridge transforms, collision zones, passive margins, backarc centers,
    island-arc trenches, and ocean-ocean trenches; ultra-deep bathymetry occurs
    only in trench contexts.
  - New 24000/60 gate:
    `out_p107_final_24000_20260629/00_24000cells_60p`.
    Runtime `1759.6 s`; terminal land fraction `0.2660`;
    active/major/minor/micro/tiny plates `27/7/16/4/0`; protected plate count
    `26`; protected microplates `4`; merged microplates `14`; boundary
    province counts include `12` mid-ocean ridges, `12` ridge-transform faults,
    `13` continental arc margins, `8` continent-continent collisions, `9`
    backarc spreading centers, `5` island-arc trenches, `3` ocean-ocean
    trenches, `3` continental rifts, and `2` passive margins.
  - The 24000 audit confirms the main P107.1 target: terminal plate hierarchy no
    longer collapses to a handful of macro plates, and protected minor/micro
    plates survive without reintroducing tiny checkerboard plates.
- Remaining quality gaps after P107.0-P107.3:
  - Boundary province objects exist, but ridge networks are still fragmented:
    new 24000 ridge component count is `210` and largest component share is
    only `0.0727`.  Real Earth has a global ridge-transform system with fewer,
    longer connected axial belts, so the next model must solve network
    topology rather than just label boundary cells.
  - Deep trenches are present and correctly trench-parented, but their area is
    too small at 24000: `deep_trench_fraction_below_6000m = 0.000113`.  Trench
    depth, length, and adjacent arc/backarc expression need stronger province
    geometry.
  - Ocean interior fine structure remains only partially solved by P106 object
    parents.  The new 24000 map has parented island chains and
    microcontinents, but abyssal hills, fracture-zone swarms, aseismic ridges,
    oceanic plateaus, guyots, and hotspot-age progressions are not yet
    expressed as a coherent basin fabric.
  - The north-hemisphere supercontinent still reads too monolithic in `plates`
    and `elevation`.  P107 improved rank survival, but not enough of the
    terminal boundary graph is expressed as readable long tectonic seams inside
    or along large landmasses.
- New plan after P107 audit:
  - P108.0 boundary-network graph extraction:
    build connected province graphs from boundary cells with seam-safe
    spherical adjacency, simplify them into long ridge, trench, transform,
    collision, and passive-margin polylines, and record endpoints,
    junctions, offsets, curvature, and continuity.  Microbenchmark: a synthetic
    ridge-transform system must have low component count and high largest
    connected ridge share while preserving transform offsets.  Add explicit
    width diagnostics so an improvement in component count cannot hide a new
    failure mode where plate-boundary provinces become broad painted swaths.
    Track median/p90 boundary width in graph steps, ridge/trench/transform
    area fractions, and the fraction of boundary cells that are more than one
    to two cells from the extracted centerline.
  - P108.1 ridge-transform-fracture reconstruction:
    derive continuous mid-ocean ridge axes from plate-pair divergence and crust
    age minima, then attach transforms and inactive fracture zones as offsets
    instead of independent spots.  Generated-world gate: ridge component count
    should fall substantially from the P107 24000 value while ridge cells align
    with young crust and shallow axial bathymetry.  Visual and metric gates must
    also cap ridge/fabric width: the ridge should read as a narrow axial high
    plus flanking abyssal-hill/fracture fabric, not a wide gray mid-ocean band.
  - P108.2 trench-arc-backarc province geometry:
    expand subduction systems into ordered trench, forearc, volcanic arc,
    backarc, and accretionary-wedge bands.  Gate: ultra-deep bathymetry must be
    nonzero, trench-parented, longer and narrower; island arcs should form
    discontinuous chains, not mainland blobs.
  - P108.3 continental collision and internal seam expression:
    convert continent-continent collision objects into broad plateau/range/
    foreland systems and expose old sutures/mobile belts inside large
    continents.  Gate: each large continent should show multiple geographic
    provinces separated by natural tectonic seams instead of a single
    high-flat platform.  Add a high-mountain coherence gate: extreme elevations
    must aggregate into continuous orogenic belts, arc ranges, plateau margins,
    and rift shoulders.  The model should reduce isolated red/purple/white
    speckle peaks unless they are explicitly parented volcano/LIP/seamount
    exceptions, and should report connected high-relief belt length, largest
    high-mountain component share, and isolated extreme-peak area fraction.
  - P108.4 ocean-basin fabric model:
    use ridge age, transforms, hotspot/plume tracks, LIP events, and
    microcontinent lineage to produce abyssal plains/hills, fracture-zone
    swarms, aseismic ridges, seamount chains/guyots, oceanic plateaus, and
    limited archipelagos.  Gate: 24000 adds real small-scale structure while
    P105 guards keep unparented far-ocean shallow/deep patches at zero.
  - P108.5 24000 acceptance ladder:
    run `8000 -> 24000` with the same P107 audit serializer and include the
    same-grid Earth panel.  Promotion requires improvement in ridge continuity,
    trench extent/depth, continent internal seam readability, and ocean-basin
    fabric without losing P107's plate hierarchy gains.

2026-06-29 - P107 boundary-network repair and 24000-cell main gate
- Problem addressed:
  - The first P107 24000 gate preserved plate hierarchy but still failed the
    boundary-readability part of P107.6.  Ridge and transform evidence was
    generated as many dotted components because the same interval sampler used
    for event markers, `_thin_boundary_mask(... spacing=3)`, was also used for
    modern ridge/trench/transform networks.
  - That made a long ridge-transform system audit as hundreds of disconnected
    fragments even when the underlying raw process mask was continuous.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_continuous_boundary_skeleton` to `TectonicsModule`.  It bridges
    one-to-two-cell gaps on the spherical neighbor graph, samples large raw
    process swaths, and connects sampled cells along shortest paths within the
    raw component.  This keeps a continuous graph without retaining the entire
    broad raw ridge patch.
  - Enabled the continuous skeleton for P107 ridge and transform output when
    `tectonics.enable_p107_boundary_network_continuity` is true.  The flag
    defaults to the existing P107 ranked-plate flag in the audit path, so the
    repair is active for `p107-audit` without changing older non-P107 defaults.
  - Kept trench output narrow.  A trial that applied the same continuous
    skeleton to trenches over-deepened broad subduction swaths.  The final
    trench path uses a denser but still narrow sampled axis, while terrain
    response deepens only the trench axis and not its full neighborhood.
  - Updated P107 terrain response so ultra-deep trench relief is axis-local:
    the trench axis targets roughly `-7350 m`, while the adjacent apron is a
    shallower forearc/slope expression.  This preserves nonzero ultra-deep
    trenches without reintroducing random far-ocean deep pits.
- Microbenchmarks and tests:
  - Added tests proving that the P107 continuous boundary skeleton preserves a
    long connected ridge path that old interval thinning breaks into multiple
    components.
  - Added a one-cell-gap bridge test for the same skeleton.
  - Verification:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py
    tests/test_engine.py::test_p106_ocean_feature_metrics_cover_object_categories

    tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_ridge_fragments_do_not_remain_shallow
    tests/test_engine.py::test_patchy_open_ocean_trench_fragments_do_not_create_deep_pits
    tests/test_engine.py::test_age_derived_open_ocean_ridge_restores_coherent_bathymetry
    tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p20_resolved_plate_topology_microbenchmarks_pass
    tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e3_real_earth_ocean_basin_fabric_microbenchmarks_pass
    tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_e4_real_earth_arc_plume_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `20 passed in 1.15s`.

2026-06-29 - P108 boundary-width guard and object-layer high-mountain repair
promoted
- Scope implemented in code:
  - Added P108 audit flags and CLI switches for boundary-width and
    high-mountain-coherence checks.
  - Added boundary-width diagnostics for ridge, trench, transform, boundary
    provinces, and ocean-fabric centerlines.  The summary reports total
    ridge/trench/transform area fraction, maximum p90 width in neighbor steps,
    and maximum fraction wider than two graph steps.
  - Added high-mountain diagnostics: 3000 m and 4500 m thresholds relative to
    sea level, connected-component count, largest component share, parent
    overlap, mountain-range overlap, isolated extreme-peak fraction, small
    high-mountain component count, and unparented small component count.
  - Added P108 width guards in tectonics/terrain so continuous ridge and
    transform networks keep narrow centerlines and ocean-fabric influence is
    kept separate from plate-contact boundary width.
  - Added object-layer high-mountain repair after terrain mountain inventory
    compilation.  The repair only edits existing continental land cells, uses
    `terrain.mountain_ranges` and `tectonics.mountain_belt_id` parentage,
    bridges near-threshold crest gaps within the same mountain-range object,
    links high-peak representatives through allowed parent corridors, and caps
    only tiny non-elongated unparented 3000 m+ flecks.
  - Retuned active-orogen selection under P108 so high terrain is selected by
    connected belt objects before area capping, rather than by many isolated
    high-score raster cells.
