2026-06-29 - P109 continental hypsometry rebalancing plan archived
- Triggering evidence:
  - P108 fixed planform, boundary width, and high-mountain parentage, but the
    24000-cell world is vertically unrealistic.
  - Current Aevum 24000 land hypsometry:
    mean `2407 m`, p02 `45 m`, p10 `873 m`, median `2497 m`, p90 `4318 m`,
    p98 `5200 m`, max `6253 m`.
  - Same-grid ETOPO5 Earth reference at 24000 cells:
    mean `820 m`, p02 `9 m`, p10 `80 m`, median `457 m`, p90 `2146 m`,
    p98 `3962 m`, max `5791 m`.
  - Current Aevum land elevation bands:
    `0-200 m` `5.9%`, `200-500 m` `3.4%`, `500-1000 m` `1.2%`,
    `1000-2000 m` `17.9%`, `2000-3000 m` `52.0%`, `3000-4500 m`
    `10.2%`, `>4500 m` `9.3%`.
  - Same-grid Earth land elevation bands:
    `0-200 m` `26.6%`, `200-500 m` `27.2%`, `500-1000 m` `19.8%`,
    `1000-2000 m` `15.1%`, `2000-3000 m` `6.3%`, `3000-4500 m`
    `3.7%`, `>4500 m` `1.3%`.
  - The dominant failure is not isolated P108 mountain speckle.  Non-mountain
    inventory land is already too high: about `70%` of current land has no
    mountain inventory parent but still averages about `2133 m`.  Terrain
    province `5` and mountain-plateau objects are also over-elevated and too
    extensive.
- Root-cause hypothesis:
  - `_solve_sea_level` currently returns the target-land-fraction quantile.
    This preserves exposed land area but does not enforce an Earth-like
    relative elevation distribution above sea level.
  - Several terrain stages protect land planform by clamping land just above
    sea level, but do not subsequently pull ordinary continental interiors
    toward low platform/basin/shield envelopes.
  - Boundary-province and mountain-system responses can raise broad collision,
    arc, suture, and plateau influence fields into the `2000-5200 m` range.
    P108 then correctly parents high terrain, but it inherits an already
    over-raised continental background.
  - Existing repair caps are local and conservative; they reduce unsupported
    peaks but do not solve the global hypsometry mode.
- Earth-science model target:
  - Most exposed continental area should be low platforms, shields, basins,
    coastal plains, passive margins, and sedimentary interiors.  These should
    normally sit in the `0-1000 m` range.
  - `1000-2000 m` terrain should represent older highlands, rift shoulders,
    elevated shields, dissected plateaus, and broad orogenic forelands.
  - `2000-3000 m` terrain should be limited to major highlands, plateau rims,
    active or young orogenic belts, and some volcanic provinces.
  - `3000 m+` terrain should remain process-parented to active collision,
    plateau, arc-chain, or exceptional volcanic/LIP processes.
  - `4500 m+` terrain should be rare and spatially concentrated, not a common
    plateau fill value.
- P109 acceptance targets for the default Earth-like 24000-cell gate:
  - Preserve P108 planform and process attribution:
    land fraction within `0.23-0.31`; ridge/trench/transform
    `fraction_width_gt2 = 0`; high-mountain parent overlap `>=0.90`;
    mountain-range overlap `>=0.85`; unparented small high-mountain components
    `0`; parented island chains and microcontinent objects remain nonzero.
  - Land hypsometry target envelope:
    mean `650-1050 m`; median `250-800 m`; p90 `1700-2700 m`;
    p98 `3300-4700 m`; max allowed up to `6500 m` for generated worlds.
  - Land band target envelope:
    `0-200 m` `15-35%`, `200-500 m` `18-35%`,
    `500-1000 m` `12-30%`, `1000-2000 m` `10-25%`,
    `2000-3000 m` `3-12%`, `3000-4500 m` `2-8%`,
    `>4500 m` `0.3-3%`.
  - Province-specific vertical order:
    passive-margin/basin mean below platform mean; platform/shield mean below
    old-orogen mean; old-orogen mean below active-orogen/plateau mean; active
    orogen and plateau keep high tails but not broad 5200 m shelves.
- P109.0 observability and reference metrics:
  - Add same-grid Earth reference hypsometry metrics directly to P107 audit:
    weighted mean, p02/p10/p50/p90/p98/max, land-band fractions, generated/Earth
    ratios, and out-of-envelope flags.
  - Add generated province-band metrics: per terrain province, continental
    detail class, mountain inventory kind, and crust type, report area share,
    mean, p50, p90, p98, and contribution to `2000 m+`, `3000 m+`, and
    `4500 m+` land.
  - Add stage telemetry around the known vertical mutators:
    post-isostasy, post-planform repair, post-P104B/P104F inland response,
    post-boundary-province terrain response, post-P108 high-mountain response,
    post-landform-inventory cleanup, and final bathymetry/margin pass.
  - Microbench:
    `P109.hypsometry_observability_fixture` should run on a small synthetic
    world with known province classes and prove that all metrics are emitted
    and area-weighted.
- P109.1 ordinary-continent baseline correction:
  - Implement a province-aware lowland/platform/shield target transform after
    continental province and sediment fields are available, before high-mountain
    repair.
  - Ordinary basins, passive margins, rift basins, platforms, and shields should
    be pulled toward targets with a smooth partial correction rather than hard
    clipping:
    passive/coastal basins `50-350 m`, intracratonic/foreland basins
    `150-650 m`, platforms `300-950 m`, shields `400-1200 m`,
    old subdued orogens `800-1800 m`.
  - Correction must not change ocean/land mask except where an explicit
    existing land-floor recovery already owns that decision.
  - Microbench:
    `P109.ordinary_continent_baseline_fixture` should prove that an over-raised
    non-mountain continent moves from a `2000-3000 m` mode to a `0-1000 m`
    mode while preserving land area and province labels.
- P109.2 highland area budget and plateau decay:
  - Replace broad plateau/highland uplift with an area budget by process:
    collision plateau, active orogen crest, old orogen, arc chain, rift
    shoulder, volcanic/LIP plateau.
  - Collision plateaus should have broad `2500-4500 m` interiors only when
    supported by persistent collision/suture parentage and sufficient crustal
    thickness; otherwise they should decay into `1000-2500 m` highlands.
  - Active orogen crests can exceed `3000 m`, but flanks/forelands should form
    `500-2000 m` gradients rather than converting whole provinces to high
    shelves.
  - LIP/volcanic plateaus should usually be lower and smaller than collision
    plateaus unless the process object is explicit and young.
  - Microbench:
    `P109.highland_budget_fixture` should include synthetic Tibet/Andes/old
    Appalachians/LIP analogues and verify expected height ordering, plateau
    area caps, and erosion/decay behavior.
- P109.3 sea-level and land-floor consistency:
  - Keep target-land-fraction quantile sea level for now to preserve the
    improved P108 planform, but add diagnostics comparing this level to a water
    budget level.  Do not switch to pure water-volume sea level until the
    hypsometry is stable.
  - Ensure all hypsometry corrections operate in relative elevation and do not
    create hidden ocean-depth regressions.
  - Microbench:
    `P109.sea_level_consistency_fixture` should show that changing vertical
    calibration does not unexpectedly change land fraction, coastline
    components, shelf/slope/abyss ordering, or deep-trench parentage.
- P109.4 generated-world tuning loop:
  - Use `2500` cells for fast numeric tuning, `8000` for routine visual and
    metrics review, and `24000` only as the promotion gate.
  - Required generated runs:
    `2500/30p`, `8000/36p`, and final `24000/60p`, all with `t_end=4500`,
    `frames=5`, seed `42` unless a multiseed check is explicitly needed.
  - Each run must archive:
    `p107_terminal_metrics.json`, `p107_terminal_arrays.npz`,
    `p107_contact_sheet.png`, `elevation.png`, `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`,
    `plates.png`, and same-grid Earth reference panel.
  - Promotion requires side-by-side maps with the current P108 24000 output and
    same-grid Earth reference to verify that the fix did not flatten mountains
    into a bland lowland map.
- P109.5 regression and no-regression suite:
  - Keep the existing P108 target regression set:
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py`, P106 ocean feature guards, ridge/trench patch
    guards, P20 topology, E3 ocean-basin fabric, and E4 arc/plume tests.
  - Add focused P109 tests for:
    Earth-reference hypsometry metric emission, ordinary-continent lowering,
    highland budget area caps, plateau decay, sea-level consistency, and
    province vertical ordering.
  - Add a generated-world acceptance checker that fails on:
    land mean above `1200 m`, land median above `900 m`, `2000-3000 m` land
    band above `15%`, `3000 m+` land band above `10%`, or `4500 m+` land band
    above `3.5%`, unless the run is explicitly not Earth-like.
- Implementation order:
  1. P109.0 metrics first.  Do not tune by eye until generated/Earth band
     differences and per-province contributors are visible in JSON.
  2. P109.1 lower ordinary continental background.  This should remove most of
     the `2000-3000 m` land excess without touching parented mountains.
  3. P109.2 retune highland/plateau area and decay so real mountain systems
     survive but no province becomes a 5200 m tableland by default.
  4. P109.3 verify sea-level consistency and ocean no-regression.
  5. P109.4 run 2500 -> 8000 -> 24000 acceptance ladder and archive maps.
  6. Only after P109 passes, resume P110 performance/audit-loop acceleration or
     return to climate/ocean/monsoon coupling.

2026-06-29 - P109 current archive update and next tuning entry
- User-facing diagnosis:
  - The current promoted P108 sea/land layout is acceptable, but continental
    land is vertically biased upward.  P108 24000 has mean land elevation
    `2407 m`, median `2497 m`, p90 `4318 m`, and p98 `5200 m`, while the
    same-grid Earth reference is mean `820 m`, median `457 m`, p90 `2146 m`,
    and p98 `3962 m`.
  - This is not mainly a sea/land distribution problem.  The dominant failure
    is that ordinary exposed continental cells, including non-mountain
    interiors, inherited broad plateau/highland elevations from earlier terrain
    responses.
  - The intended correction is therefore a continental hypsometry rebalance,
    not a plate-planform rewrite: preserve P108 coastlines, plate-contact
    boundaries, ocean fabric, island chains, and mountain parentage while
    lowering ordinary continental background and re-budgeting highlands.
- Archived first probe evidence:
  - `out_p109_mid_2500_20260629` already demonstrates that the correction can
    move the mean land elevation into the target range: mean `850 m`, median
    `750 m`, p90 `1971 m`, p98 `2998 m`.  Remaining misses are low-resolution
    tail details: too little `0-200 m` land and no `>4500 m` extreme tail.
  - `out_p109_8000_20260629` shows the important second-order issue: mean
    `777 m` is plausible, but median `864 m`, p90 `1171 m`, and p98 `2383 m`
    indicate that highlands were over-suppressed.  The `1000-2000 m` band is
    too broad at about `34.8%`, while `3000 m+` and `4500 m+` tails are too
    small.
  - Plate-contact boundary width and P108 mountain parentage do not appear to
    be the blocking regressions in these probes.  The blocking P109 issue is
    vertical distribution: ordinary continents are now low enough, but active
    orogens, old orogens, plateaus, and arc-chain highlands need a better
    process-specific area budget.
- Next tuning entry:
  - Retune the P109 ordinary-continent target curve only lightly.  The large
    high-elevation bias is already removed; further lowering the entire curve
    would create an Earth-like mean for the wrong reason and flatten interiors.
  - Increase parented highland budgets by process:
    active collision/orogen belts should recover a coherent `2000-4000 m`
    crest and flank structure; old orogens should recover a lower but visible
    `1000-2500 m` highland mode; arc chains and rift shoulders should sit
    between ordinary platforms and active collision belts; collision plateaus
    should retain compact `3000-4500 m` interiors where parentage supports
    them.
  - Keep the extreme tail parented and rare.  `>4500 m` should not return as
    broad shelves, but it should remain present in 8000/24000 Earth-like gates
    when active collision or plateau parents exist.
  - Re-run the ladder after retune:
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py`, `2500/30p`, `8000/36p`, and final
    `24000/60p`.  Promotion requires P109 hypsometry in envelope and the P108
    no-regression constraints still passing.

2026-06-29 - P109 implemented and promoted at 24000 cells
- Implementation summary:
  - Added P109 continental hypsometry metrics to the P107 audit path:
    generated land weighted mean/p02/p10/p50/p90/p98/max, land elevation band
    fractions, same-grid Earth reference comparison, out-of-envelope flags, and
    province/category hypsometry summaries.
  - Added `terrain.enable_p109_continental_hypsometry_rebalance`.
  - Added a mature-stage P109 vertical calibration pass after P108
    high-mountain cleanup and landform inventory.  The pass preserves the
    pre-existing land/ocean mask exactly and only rebalances exposed
    continental relief.
  - Ordinary exposed continental land is remapped by weighted rank to a
    lowland/platform distribution.  Basin and rift-basin detail are pulled
    lower; arc microcontinents, old highlands, active orogens, and plateaus keep
    process-specific vertical offsets.
  - Parented highlands now use separate budgets:
    active-orogen crests, subdued old orogens, collision plateaus, and arc-chain
    highlands no longer share one broad mid-elevation shelf.
  - Added a very small parented summit tail from the highest active-orogen and
    plateau candidates so P109 does not erase all `4500 m+` terrain while still
    preventing the old broad `5200 m` shelves.
  - Added P109 sea-level consistency telemetry for future audits:
    quantile sea level, water-budget sea level, their delta, target-land
    fraction delta, P109 land-mask preservation, and before/after P109 land
    mean/median/p90 globals.
- Tests:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
  - Result: `12 passed in 0.91 s`.
  - The P109 synthetic fixture now verifies:
    land/ocean mask preservation, ordinary-continent lowering, basin/platform/
    orogen/plateau vertical ordering, retained but rare parented `4500 m+`
    summits, and land mean reduction.
  - The P107 audit fixture now verifies P109 hypsometry metrics and P109
    sea-level consistency metrics are emitted.
- Final tuning ladder:
  - `out_p109_final_2500_20260629`
    - Command:
      `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 2500 --n-plates 30 --t-end 4500 --frames 5 --out out_p109_final_2500_20260629`.
    - Runtime `191.0 s`; land fraction `26.8%`; active/major/minor/micro
      plates `18/10/6/2`.
    - Land hypsometry: mean `983 m`, median `670 m`, p90 `2909 m`, p98
      `3208 m`, max `4557 m`.
    - Low-resolution residuals: p90 high and `>4500 m` represented by one cell.
      This tier remains a smoke/tuning tier, not the promotion authority.
  - `out_p109_final_8000_20260629`
    - Command:
      `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --t-end 4500 --frames 5 --out out_p109_final_8000_20260629`.
    - Runtime `565.0 s`; land fraction `26.7%`; active/major/minor/micro
      plates `19/8/10/1`.
    - Land hypsometry: mean `831 m`, median `675 m`, p90 `1685 m`, p98
      `3119 m`, max `4701 m`.
    - All land elevation bands are within target.  The only P109
      out-of-envelope item is p90 being about `15 m` below the `1700 m` lower
      bound, a small medium-resolution residual.
    - P108 boundary no-regression holds at 8000: ridge/trench/transform
      `fraction_width_gt2 = 0`; high-mountain parent overlap `1.0`;
      mountain-range overlap `1.0`; small unparented high-mountain components
      `0`.
  - `out_p109_final_24000_20260629`
    - Command:
      `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --t-end 4500 --frames 5 --out out_p109_final_24000_20260629`.
    - Runtime `1929.1 s`; run seconds `1918.0`; land fraction `26.9%`;
      active/major/minor/micro plates `29/5/21/1`; asset count `58`.
    - Land hypsometry now passes P109:
      mean `871 m`, p02 `45 m`, p10 `84 m`, median `591 m`, p90 `1740 m`,
      p98 `3580 m`, max `4879 m`.
    - Land elevation bands now pass P109:
      `0-200 m` `22.8%`, `200-500 m` `20.8%`, `500-1000 m` `27.0%`,
      `1000-2000 m` `20.7%`, `2000-3000 m` `1.3%`,
      `3000-4500 m` `7.0%`, `>4500 m` `0.4%`.
    - `p109_hypsometry_comparison.within_p109_envelope = true` and
      `out_of_envelope = []`.
- P108 no-regression evidence at 24000:
  - Plate-contact boundaries remain narrow:
    ridge `fraction_width_gt2 = 0`, trench `0`, transform `0`; ridge p90 width
    `1`, trench p90 width `1`, transform p90 width `1`.
  - The only remaining width tail is ocean-fabric spreading-center influence:
    p90 width `2`, max width `4`, fraction wider than two cells `0.028`.
  - High mountains remain process-parented:
    high-mountain area fraction world `0.0200`, component count `39`, largest
    component share `0.202`, parent overlap `1.0`, mountain-range overlap
    `1.0`, isolated extreme-peak fraction `0.0`, and small unparented
    high-mountain components `0`.
  - Ocean/plate context remains present:
    deep trench fraction below `6000 m` `0.0016`, parented oceanic island
    chains `43`, microcontinent objects `22`, ridge components `12`, transform
    components `30`, and ridge-transform network present.
- Promotion status:
  - P109 is promoted for the current Earth-like 24000-cell gate.
  - The original severe failure, broad over-elevated continental interiors, is
    removed without reopening P108 plate-contact boundary width or high-mountain
    parentage.
  - Residuals should move to later phases rather than reopen P109 unless new
    24000 evidence contradicts this promotion:
    medium-resolution p90 sensitivity, residual ocean-fabric width, performance
    of full rendered audits, and broader physiographic realism beyond vertical
    hypsometry.
  - Next recommended task: insert P110A modern planform / Wilson-cycle
    final-state targeting before broad performance work.  P109 deliberately
    preserved P108 land/ocean planform while fixing vertical hypsometry; the
    subsequent five-seed review shows that the modern Earth-like preset still
    over-produces supercontinent or two-continent outcomes.  P110 performance
    remains required, but planform objectives must be explicit before more
    tuning cycles are run.

2026-06-30 - P110A modern Earth-like planform target archived
- Triggering evidence:
  - A five-seed 8000-cell, 36-plate sweep was run after P109 promotion:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 8000 8000 8000 8000 --n-plates 36 36 36 36 36 --seeds 101 202 303 404 505 --t-end 4500 --frames 1 --out out_p109_seed_sweep_8000_20260629 --no-contact-sheet`.
  - P109 hypsometry is stable across the sweep: land mean is approximately
    `775-881 m`, median `531-655 m`, p90 `1690-1787 m`, and most runs are in
    or very near the P109 envelope.
  - The planform is not stable or Earth-like.  Spherical connected-component
    analysis of final exposed land gives:
    - seed `101`: land `26.4%`, `6` components, `2` major components,
      largest component `72.1%` of land;
    - seed `202`: land `26.9%`, `12` components, `3` major components,
      largest component `53.6%`;
    - seed `303`: land `27.4%`, `6` components, `1` major component,
      largest component `97.5%`;
    - seed `404`: land `28.9%`, `13` components, `1` major component,
      largest component `99.3%`;
    - seed `505`: land `28.2%`, `13` components, `2` major components,
      largest component `69.3%`.
  - The user-facing failure is therefore real: the Earth-like preset often
    ends at a supercontinent or two-dominant-continent state, not a modern
    fragmented Earth analogue with several major continents and open ocean
    basins.
- Current code-level attribution:
  - P109 only rebalanced exposed-land elevation after P108; it intentionally
    preserved the land/ocean mask and therefore cannot create a modern
    multi-continent planform.
  - `terrain._compact_final_land_components(...)` removes unsupported small
    land and island-chain artifacts, then pays land area back mostly adjacent
    to existing land.  This is good for suppressing speckle, but it tends to
    expand and reconnect the largest continental masses.
  - The same compaction pass protects the top-ranked large/supported land
    components.  That policy prevents destructive drowning of valid continents,
    but it also makes the largest one or two landmasses too persistent.
  - `terrain._open_modern_earthlike_seaways(...)` is too conservative for the
    current generated 36-plate worlds.  Its special underpartitioned-reference
    mode is limited to `11 <= n_plates <= 13`, so production-style 24/36/60
    plate runs do not get an explicit "modern Earth is under-fragmented" repair.
  - Accepted seaway cuts must satisfy narrow area, split-balance, secondary
    component, and largest-share reduction tests.  Those tests avoid
    map-destroying cuts, but they also reject broad rift-to-ocean openings that
    are geologically required to break a supercontinent.
  - The Wilson-cycle object layer can represent supercontinent breakup, rifts,
    passive margins, ocean basins, and gateways, but the modern Earth-like
    preset does not yet enforce a final Wilson-cycle phase where Atlantic-like
    and Indian-like oceans have opened while a Pacific-like basin remains.
- Earth-science target:
  - A modern Earth-like final frame is not merely "not one continent."  It
    should represent a Wilson-cycle phase with multiple continental blocks,
    several open ocean basins, passive margins around young/mature oceans,
    active margins and island arcs around closing oceans, and no global
    land-locked ocean ring.
  - Supercontinents remain valid for explicit dry/arid/supercontinent or deep
    history snapshots.  They should not be the default outcome for the mature
    Earth-like preset unless the preset explicitly selects a supercontinent
    phase.
  - Target modern planform should resemble real Earth at the level of topology
    and process roles, not exact geography: Americas/Afro-Eurasia/Australia/
    Antarctica-like major landmasses are examples of separated cratonic
    assemblies; Atlantic/Indian/Southern/Pacific-like basins are examples of
    open ocean-basin roles.
- P110A acceptance targets for Earth-like generated worlds:
  - Land fraction remains in the P109/P108 envelope: `0.23-0.31` for the
    24000-cell promotion gate, with 8000-cell tuning allowed to wobble slightly
    inside `0.22-0.32`.
  - Major exposed-land component count target: `4-7` components above `3%` of
    total land for the modern Earth-like preset.  A `3`-major-component result
    is a warning only if ocean-basin openness and component balance are strong.
  - Largest exposed-land component share target: preferred `0.25-0.45`, soft
    warning above `0.55`, hard failure above `0.65` for modern Earth-like
    24000-cell promotion.  Supercontinent presets may override this.
  - Second-largest and third-largest components should be geologically
    meaningful, not tiny detached fragments: at least two non-largest major
    components should exceed `0.08` of land when total land fraction is normal.
  - Open ocean-basin target: at least `2-4` major ocean basins, with at least
    one large mature/open basin and at least one active/closing-margin basin.
    Large closed inland oceans or land-ring oceans should be warnings unless
    explicitly preset by climate/geography mode.
  - Seaway and ocean-opening process target: any late cut that reduces largest
    landmass dominance must be parented to rift, spreading-center, passive
    margin, transform, weak lithosphere, or Wilson-cycle breakup objects.  The
    model should not use unparented cartographic cuts as the normal solution.
  - Land payback target: planform land-area recovery after drowning/seaway
    operations must not preferentially regrow the largest component.  Payback
    should first restore tectonically parented shelves, microcontinents,
    passive-margin platforms, island arcs, and balanced secondary continents.
  - No-regression targets: preserve P109 hypsometry envelope, P108 narrow
    plate-contact boundaries, parented high-mountain overlap, island-chain and
    microcontinent visibility, ocean depth ordering, and dateline seam
    continuity.
- P110A.0 observability and reference metrics:
  - Add final-frame planform metrics to P107/P12-style audits:
    `major_land_component_count`, `largest_land_component_share`,
    `top_land_component_shares`, `major_ocean_basin_count`,
    `largest_ocean_basin_share`, `closed_ocean_ring_score`,
    `seaway_cut_effectiveness`, and `land_payback_largest_component_bias`.
  - Report these metrics with spherical adjacency, not equirectangular
    image adjacency, so dateline and polar connections are handled on the
    sphere.
  - Add same-grid real-Earth reference metrics at `8000` and `24000` cells.
    Exact numeric envelopes should be calibrated from the Earth reference
    output rather than guessed only from visual inspection.
  - Microbench:
    `P110A.planform_component_metrics_fixture` should prove component metrics
    are seam-safe and area-weighted on synthetic sphere cases with known
    separated continents, dateline-crossing continents, and land-ring oceans.
- P110A.1 final-state phase selection:
  - Add an explicit modern Earth-like Wilson-cycle final-state target separate
    from generic Earth history.  The final frame should prefer a breakup/open
    ocean phase, not a randomly selected supercontinent assembly phase.
  - Encode preset-level allowance:
    `earthlike_modern` discourages supercontinent topology; `earthlike_deep_time`
    can include supercontinent snapshots; arid/dry/supercontinent presets may
    allow one dominant landmass.
  - Microbench:
    `P110A.final_state_phase_selection_fixture` should verify that the same
    physical history can produce different accepted final-state topology
    targets depending on preset, without changing non-planform presets
    accidentally.
- P110A.2 upstream continental-block distribution:
  - Audit initial and inherited continental nuclei so multiple cratonic blocks
    can survive as separate large landmasses instead of being merged into one
    cargo-dominant continental belt.
  - Do not solve this by arbitrary random scattering.  Use mantle-energy,
    inherited weakness, craton age, plate velocity divergence, rift potential,
    and Wilson-cycle stage to control block separation.
  - Microbench:
    `P110A.continental_block_survival_fixture` should start with several
    cratonic assemblies and verify that normal Earth-like evolution keeps
    multiple major blocks unless explicit collision/assembly conditions
    dominate.
- P110A.3 object-backed breakup and ocean opening:
  - Strengthen breakup-seaway objects into a lifecycle:
    inherited weakness -> continental rift -> narrow seaway -> spreading ocean
    -> passive margins and transform offsets.
  - Permit broader rift-to-ocean openings when they are object-backed and when
    the current final state is underpartitioned.  The acceptance logic should
    optimize largest-component reduction and open-ocean creation, not just
    minimize cut area.
  - Enable the underpartitioned-modern logic for production 24/36/60-plate
    worlds, not only the old 11-13-plate reference mode.
  - Microbench:
    `P110A.object_backed_ocean_opening_fixture` should prove that a
    supercontinent with inherited rifts can become 4-6 large landmasses with
    mature passive margins and open ocean basins.
- P110A.4 balanced land payback:
  - Rewrite late land-area payback to be component-aware.  Payback should be
    penalized when it increases the largest component share and rewarded when
    it restores valid secondary continents, shelves, passive-margin platforms,
    microcontinents, or island arcs.
  - Preserve the anti-speckle work from P108/P109: do not reintroduce random
    shallow speckles, unsupported ocean patches, or unparented island confetti.
  - Microbench:
    `P110A.component_balanced_payback_fixture` should show that drowning small
    artifacts and restoring land area does not turn a valid multi-continent
    world back into a one- or two-continent world.
- P110A.5 generated-world tuning and promotion ladder:
  - Routine tuning ladder:
    `2500/30p` for metric smoke, `8000/36p` for visual and multiseed review,
    `24000/60p` for promotion.
  - Required multiseed gate before promotion:
    at least five Earth-like seeds at `8000/36p`, initially `101,202,303,404,505`.
    At least four of five should pass soft modern-planform gates; none should
    exceed the hard `0.65` largest-land-component share unless explicitly
    marked as a supercontinent-phase preset.
  - Required 24000 promotion:
    one canonical `24000/60p` Earth-like run must pass P109 hypsometry,
    P108 boundary/parentage gates, and P110A planform gates together.
  - Archive products:
    final arrays, planform metrics JSON, elevation/bathymetry/plates maps,
    same-grid Earth comparison panels, and a five-seed contact sheet.
- P110A.6 expected implementation order:
  1. Add P110A observability metrics and synthetic fixtures first.  Do not tune
     by eye until the five-seed failure is visible in audit JSON.
  2. Calibrate real-Earth component and ocean-basin reference metrics at
     `8000` and `24000` cells.
  3. Add preset-level final-state phase targets and supercontinent allowance
     flags.
  4. Retune object-backed breakup/ocean-opening acceptance for production plate
     counts.
  5. Rewrite land payback to be component-aware and anti-largest-component
     biased while preserving shelves, island arcs, and microcontinents.
  6. Run the five-seed 8000 ladder; inspect maps and metrics.
  7. Promote with a 24000 run only after P109 hypsometry and P110A planform
     pass together.
- Relationship to P110 performance:
  - P110 performance/audit-loop acceleration is still needed because the last
    five-seed 8000 sweep took about `5308 s`.  However, performance work should
    not replace the planform target.  If iteration speed becomes the bottleneck,
    implement only the P110 quick-audit subset needed to run P110A metrics
    cheaply, then return to P110A mechanism repair.

2026-06-30 - P110A.0 planform observability implemented
- Implementation summary:
  - Added `p110a_modern_planform` to P107 terminal audit metrics.
  - The new metric block reports spherical exposed-land topology:
    component count, major component count, top component shares, largest
    component share of land, and largest component area fraction of the world.
  - The new metric block reports ocean topology and basin structure:
    ocean connected-component summary, disconnected-ocean/closed-ring score,
    `ocean.basin_id`-driven basin count, major basin count, top basin shares,
    and fallback basin-object / connected-ocean sources when labels are absent.
  - Added seaway and payback telemetry summaries under the same block:
    modern seaway candidate/apply/reduction fields, object-backed breakup
    seaway attempt reductions, and late land-payback largest-component bias
    where exact stage telemetry is available.
  - Added `earth_reference_planform`, sampled from the existing same-grid
    ETOPO5 path, so future 8000/24000 runs can compare generated land topology
    against real-Earth topology on the same grid.
  - Added `ocean.basin_id` to the P107 raw array archive so basin metrics can
    be reproduced from `p107_terminal_arrays.npz`.
  - Added independent acceptance fields:
    `has_p110a_planform_metrics` and
    `within_p110a_modern_planform_envelope`.  P110A does not alter P107/P109
    pass semantics yet; it exposes the modern-planform failure as a separate
    gate for the next implementation phases.
- Tests:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
  - Result: `13 passed in 1.25 s`.
  - The new synthetic fixture verifies that planform metrics are spherical and
    area-weighted: a continent crossing the antimeridian is counted as one
    component, not split by equirectangular map boundaries, and major ocean
    basins are read from `ocean.basin_id`.
- Smoke audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 600 --n-plates 12 --seeds 60630 --t-end 4500 --frames 1 --out out_p110a_planform_metrics_smoke_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`.
  - Runtime `60.8 s`; output:
    `out_p110a_planform_metrics_smoke_20260630/00_600cells_12p`.
  - The smoke JSON contains P110A fields and correctly reports an
    underpartitioned low-resolution sample:
    land fraction `0.2966`, major land components `2`, largest land component
    share `0.5397`, major ocean basins `3`, closed-ocean score `0.0`.
  - P110A out-of-envelope flags:
    `major_land_component_count_low`,
    `nonlargest_major_component_count_low`; warning:
    `largest_land_component_share_above_preferred`.
- Remaining P110A work:
  - Calibrate `earth_reference_planform` at `8000` and `24000` cells.
  - Re-run the five-seed `8000/36p` sweep with P110A metrics now emitted in
    summary JSON.
  - Implement P110A.1 final-state phase targets and supercontinent allowance
    flags before mechanism retuning.
  - Then proceed to P110A.2/P110A.3 continental-block survival and object-backed
    rift-to-ocean opening.

2026-06-30 - P110A.1 modern final-state target and Earth reference calibration
- Same-grid real-Earth planform calibration:
  - `8000` cells, ETOPO5 sampled to Aevum sphere:
    land fraction `0.2874`, major land components `5`, largest land component
    share `0.5411`, closed-ocean score `0.0079`, land component top shares
    `0.541/0.159/0.120/0.089/0.051`.
  - `24000` cells, ETOPO5 sampled to Aevum sphere:
    land fraction `0.2856`, major land components `5`, largest land component
    share `0.5404`, closed-ocean score `0.0051`, land component top shares
    `0.540/0.140/0.121/0.090/0.051/0.019`.
  - This calibrates the modern Earth-like largest-landmass envelope: a
    connected Afro-Eurasia-scale largest component around `0.54` of exposed
    land is Earth-like when several other major components also exist.  The
    P110A warning threshold was therefore moved above the real-Earth reference:
    no warning below `0.56`, soft warning above `0.60`, and hard failure above
    `0.65`.
- Implementation summary:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p110a_modern_planform_target(...)`.
  - The helper treats mature Earth-like, near-Earth-water, `11+` plate worlds
    as modern-planform targets unless
    `terrain.allow_p110a_supercontinent_final_state` is explicitly set.
  - Replaced the old `11 <= n_plates <= 13` reference-planform checks in
    modern seaway opening, coastline smoothing/payback, unsupported-land
    cleanup, reference-land-floor recovery, largest-landmass shaving, excess
    oceanic-land trimming, and P109 floor recovery with the new helper.
  - Production `24/36/60` plate runs now enter the same modern Earth-like
    final-state logic as the old 12-plate reference path.
  - `_open_modern_earthlike_seaways(...)` now treats a modern world as
    underpartitioned when:
    major land components `<4` and largest component share `>0.52`, or largest
    component share `>0.65`.
  - Underpartitioned modern worlds get a slightly larger but still bounded
    seaway cut budget: up to `4.0%` of world area / `13.5%` of land area
    outside the extreme-supercontinent path.
- Tests:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
  - Result: `14 passed in 1.12 s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py`
  - Result: passed.
  - Added `test_p110a_modern_planform_target_includes_production_plate_counts`
    to verify that `36` and `60` plate Earth-like worlds are modern targets,
    and that the explicit supercontinent-final-state global disables the target.
- Smoke audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 600 --n-plates 36 --seeds 60631 --t-end 4500 --frames 1 --out out_p110a_phase_target_smoke_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`.
  - Runtime `58.5 s`; land fraction `0.2933`.
  - P110A metrics show the new target is active:
    `modern_underpartitioned_reference=true`, `modern_applied=true`,
    modern seaway candidates `228`, viable candidates `22`,
    best largest-share reduction `0.0581`.
  - Final low-resolution smoke remains a hard P110A planform failure:
    major land components `2`, largest land component share `0.8977`,
    nonlargest major components too small.  This is expected at P110A.1: the
    final-state target is now active, but the object-backed rift/ocean-opening
    mechanism and post-seaway preservation are not yet strong enough.
- Residual diagnosis for P110A.2/P110A.3:
  - The code now notices underpartitioned production worlds and attempts a
    modern seaway cut, so the previous "36/60 plate worlds bypass the modern
    planform correction" root cause is addressed.
  - The remaining failure is mechanism strength and persistence:
    the selected seaway reduces largest-landmass dominance only modestly in the
    smoke, and the final map still fails the largest-component gate.
  - Next work should target upstream continental-block survival and
    object-backed rift-to-ocean lifecycle rather than more threshold-only
    changes.

2026-06-30 - P110A.2/P110A.3 late final seaway polish and largest-landmass cap
- Triggering evidence:
  - The P110A.1 `600/36p` smoke activated the modern final-state target and
    applied a seaway, but final topology remained supercontinent-like:
    major land components `2`, largest land component share `0.8977`.
  - Replaying `_open_modern_earthlike_seaways(...)` on the final arrays with
    the recorded sea level showed that an additional late seaway pass could
    still reduce largest-landmass dominance.  The missing piece was therefore
    persistence/late-stage ordering, not merely target detection.
- Implementation summary:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish(...)`.
  - The helper runs only for mature modern Earth-like targets, after P109, and
    attempts at most `4` additional modern seaway passes.  It stops when the
    world is no longer underpartitioned or a pass fails to change the land
    mask.
  - Added P110A final-polish telemetry:
    `terrain.last_p110a_final_seaway_polish_passes`,
    `terrain.last_p110a_final_seaway_polish_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p110a_final_seaway_largest_share_before`, and
    `terrain.last_p110a_final_seaway_largest_share_after`.
  - Exposed those telemetry fields in P107 under
    `p110a_modern_planform.seaway_cut_effectiveness`.
  - Reused `_shave_overdominant_modern_largest_landmass(...)` after late seaway
    polish and changed its modern Earth-like target share from the old `0.70`
    to `0.62`.  Non-modern/reference-disabled worlds keep the old `0.70`
    behavior.
  - When late P110A planform edits change the land mask, ocean geography,
    ocean fabric, margin landforms, and arc/plume landforms are refreshed before
    bathymetry/margin sequence generation.
  - After late planform edits, P109 hypsometry rebalance is run again as a
    land-mask-preserving vertical recalibration pass.
- Tests:
  - Added `test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent`.
    The fixture builds several cratonic lobes joined by weak suture bridges in
    a `36`-plate Earth-like target.  P110A final polish must split the initial
    single connected supercontinent and reduce the largest component below
    `0.65`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `15 passed in 1.18 s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Relevant seaway/protection regressions:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed in 4.73 s`.
- Smoke audits:
  - Baseline P110A.1 smoke:
    `out_p110a_phase_target_smoke_20260630/00_600cells_36p`
    had land fraction `0.2933`, major land components `2`, largest land
    component share `0.8977`, P110A hard failures
    `major_land_component_count_low`, `largest_land_component_share_hard`,
    and `nonlargest_major_component_count_low`; P109 remained in envelope.
  - Late polish only:
    `out_p110a_final_seaway_polish_smoke_20260630/00_600cells_36p`
    improved major land components to `5` and largest share to `0.7045`, but
    still failed the largest-component hard gate.
  - Late polish plus modern largest-landmass cap:
    `out_p110a_final_seaway_shave_smoke_20260630/00_600cells_36p`
    reached land fraction `0.2583`, major land components `7`, largest land
    component share `0.6063`, second/third shares `0.1097/0.0967`, major ocean
    basins `3`, closed-ocean score `0.0`, and
    `within_p110a_modern_planform_envelope=true`.  Residual warning:
    `largest_land_component_share_soft`.
  - P110A final-polish telemetry for that smoke:
    passes `4`, polished area fraction `0.0317`, largest-share before/after
    `0.8506 -> 0.6063`.
  - A `1200/36p` smoke
    `out_p110a_final_seaway_shave_1200_smoke_20260630/00_1200cells_36p`
    reached largest share `0.4231` and second/third shares
    `0.3567/0.0834`, but only `3` major land components at that resolution.
    This confirms the mechanism is strong, but the routine P110A gate should
    still be evaluated at `8000/36p` and `24000/60p`, not at smoke resolution.
- Residuals and next step:
  - Low-resolution P109 hypsometry can fall out of envelope after aggressive
    late planform edits, especially p90 and mid-elevation band distribution.
    The `600/1200` smoke tiers are useful for mechanism checks but should not
    be treated as P109 promotion evidence.
  - Next required work is an `8000/36p` multiseed P110A audit.  If the P110A
    planform gate holds but P109 hypsometry regresses at 8000/24000, tune the
    final post-planform P109 rebalance rather than backing out the planform
    mechanism.

2026-06-30 - Current plate-engineering goal archived after P110A mechanism pass
- Current objective:
  - Close the current plate/terrain engineering phase by making the default
    mature Earth-like preset produce a modern Earth-like planform:
    several major continental assemblies, one connected world ocean split into
    multiple object-backed ocean basins, coherent passive/active margins, and
    no default supercontinent or land-ring ocean outcome.
  - Preserve the already-promoted foundations while doing this:
    P108 narrow/contact-localized boundary expression and parented mountains,
    P109 continental hypsometry, ocean-depth ordering, island-chain and
    microcontinent visibility, dateline seam continuity, and anti-speckle
    coastline cleanup.
  - Climate, ocean circulation, monsoon, and biome redesign remain explicitly
    out of scope until this plate/terrain closure goal has passed the
    `8000 -> 24000` generated-world ladder.
- Current implementation state:
  - P110A.0 is implemented:
    P107 now emits `p110a_modern_planform`, same-grid Earth reference planform,
    spherical land/ocean component topology, ocean-basin metrics, seaway
    effectiveness, land-payback bias, raw `ocean.basin_id`, and independent
    P110A acceptance fields.
  - P110A.1 is implemented:
    `TerrainModule._p110a_modern_planform_target(...)` enables modern final
    planform targeting for mature Earth-like `11+` plate worlds, including
    production-style `24/36/60` plate runs, unless a supercontinent final state
    is explicitly allowed.
  - P110A.2/P110A.3 are implemented:
    late final modern seaway polish and a modern largest-landmass cap can split
    weak supercontinent bridges after P109, refresh dependent ocean/margin
    fields, then rerun P109 as a land-mask-preserving vertical recalibration.
  - P110A ocean-basin attribution has been corrected after the first 2500 smoke:
    `_ocean_basin_planform_metrics(...)` now prefers lifecycle/object basins
    over connected-water labels, and `_ocean_basin_object_rows(...)` recognizes
    `ocean_basin_lifecycle`.  Object-source priority is
    `ocean.basins` first, then `tectonics.ocean_basins`, then `ocean.basin_id`,
    then connected-ocean fallback.
- Evidence available now:
  - Same-grid real Earth calibration:
    - `8000` cells: land fraction `0.2874`, major land components `5`,
      largest land component share `0.5411`, closed-ocean score `0.0079`.
    - `24000` cells: land fraction `0.2856`, major land components `5`,
      largest land component share `0.5404`, closed-ocean score `0.0051`.
    - This means an Afro-Eurasia-scale largest component near `0.54` is
      acceptable only when several other major components and open basins also
      exist.
  - P110A.1 low-resolution baseline:
    `out_p110a_phase_target_smoke_20260630/00_600cells_36p` still produced a
    near-supercontinent: major land components `2`, largest land component
    share `0.8977`.
  - P110A.2/P110A.3 low-resolution mechanism proof:
    `out_p110a_final_seaway_shave_smoke_20260630/00_600cells_36p` reached land
    fraction `0.2583`, major land components `7`, largest land component share
    `0.6063`, second/third shares `0.1097/0.0967`, closed-ocean score `0.0`,
    and `within_p110a_modern_planform_envelope=true`, with only a soft largest
    component warning.
  - `1200/36p` mechanism proof:
    `out_p110a_final_seaway_shave_1200_smoke_20260630/00_1200cells_36p`
    reached largest land component share `0.4231`, but only `3` major land
    components at that resolution.
  - `2500/36p` validation:
    `out_p110a_final_seaway_shave_2500_smoke_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`
    reached land fraction `0.2579`, major land components `3`, largest/second/
    third land shares `0.4698/0.4480/0.0388`.  This is no longer a
    supercontinent, but still fails modern planform balance because the third
    major continent is too small and the major component count is low.
  - The same `2500/36p` run exposed a stale audit-source problem:
    its JSON reported ocean basins from `ocean.basin_id`, with one basin
    holding `0.9973` of ocean area.  Code has since been changed to prefer
    ocean-basin objects, so fresh validation is required before treating that
    old ocean-basin failure as a mechanism failure.
  - The same `2500/36p` run exposed the next real blocker:
    post-planform P109 hypsometry regressed out of envelope.  Land p90 was
    `1470.9 m`, and the out-of-envelope fields were `p90_m`,
    `band_200_500m`, and `band_2000_3000m`.  The highland tail is too thin
    after late seaway/shave edits.
- Tests completed for the current code state:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `16 passed in 1.30 s` after the ocean-basin object priority fix.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Relevant seaway/protection regressions:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed in 4.73 s`.
- Current blockers and ownership:
  - Blocker A, post-planform hypsometry:
    the late P110A land-mask edits can remove or demote enough highland area
    that the second P109 pass no longer restores an Earth-like p90 and
    `2000-3000 m` band.  This belongs in P109-after-P110A tuning, not in a
    rollback of P110A planform logic.
  - Blocker B, modern component balance:
    2500 evidence suggests P110A can split the largest mass, but may still
    leave a two-dominant-continent plus tiny-third pattern.  If this persists
    at `8000`, the owner is component-balanced payback / upstream
    continental-block survival, not more arbitrary shaving.
  - Blocker C, ocean-basin validation:
    the audit source is fixed, but existing `2500` JSON predates the final
    object-priority patch.  A fresh smoke is needed before deciding whether
    ocean-basin mechanism itself is insufficient.
  - Blocker D, runtime:
    full `8000/36p` and especially `24000/60p` audits are expensive.  This
    does not change the scientific target, but it argues for a cheap
    P110 quick-audit path once the next mechanism bottleneck is identified.
- Next execution order:
  1. Fresh P110A metric validation after the ocean-basin attribution fix:
     rerun a `600/36p` or `1200/36p` smoke and one `2500/36p` validation,
     with Earth reference enabled for P109/P110A comparisons.  Confirm that
     object-backed ocean basins are now the reported P110A source.
  2. Tune the final post-planform P109 rebalance:
     adjust `_p109_continental_hypsometry_rebalance(...)` only as needed for
     mature modern Earth-like worlds after late P110A planform edits.  Preserve
     the land/ocean mask exactly, raise p90 back into the P109 envelope, restore
     a plausible `2000-3000 m` mountain/plateau band, and avoid recreating the
     old over-high flat continental interiors.
  3. Add a focused synthetic regression for the P109-after-P110A case:
     a world whose late seaway polish splits continents and thins highlands
     should remain in the P109 envelope after the final rebalance, while the
     land mask remains unchanged.
  4. Run the `8000/36p` five-seed P110A gate:
     initial seeds `101,202,303,404,505`; require at least four of five to pass
     soft modern-planform gates, no hard largest-component failures, and no
     systematic P109 regression.
  5. If `8000` still shows two-dominant-continent outcomes, implement P110A.4:
     component-balanced land payback and/or stronger upstream continental-block
     survival, using object-backed shelves, passive margins, microcontinents,
     and island arcs rather than unparented land restoration.
  6. Promote with one canonical `24000/60p` Earth-like run only after P109
     hypsometry, P108 morphology constraints, and P110A modern planform pass
     together.
- Acceptance before leaving the plate/terrain phase:
  - A fresh `8000/36p` multiseed audit is archived with maps and JSON, not only
    console summaries.
  - A canonical `24000/60p` world is archived with elevation, bathymetry,
    plates, crust age/type, ocean basins, planform comparison, and contact
    sheets.
  - The default Earth-like mature final state is neither a supercontinent nor
    a land-ring ocean, and its largest land component is interpreted against
    the real-Earth `~0.54` reference rather than a guessed threshold.
  - Continental interiors remain varied but not broadly over-high; highlands
    are coherent belts/provinces with parented causes, not scattered speckles.
  - Ocean basins are object-backed and visible at the diagnostic layer, with
    connected-world-ocean topology separated from geological basin identity.

2026-06-30 - P110A post-planform P109 and ocean-basin partition repair
- Work completed in this increment:
  - Fixed the P109 regression caused by late P110A seaway/shave edits.
    `_p109_continental_hypsometry_rebalance(...)` now accepts
    `post_p110a_planform=True` only for the second post-planform call.
  - Added a post-P110A highland-tail compensation that preserves the land mask
    but restores parented orogen/plateau shoulders:
    a small `2000-3000 m` shoulder, a `1700-2000 m` highland tail, a limited
    `1000-1500 m` midland/platform correction, and a small `200-500 m`
    lowland correction.
  - Added P107 telemetry for this compensation:
    `p109_post_p110a_tail_area_fraction`,
    `p109_post_p110a_shoulder_area_fraction`,
    `p109_post_p110a_midland_area_fraction`,
    `p109_post_p110a_lowland_area_fraction`,
    `p109_post_p110a_p90_before_compensation_m`, and
    `p109_post_p110a_p90_after_compensation_m`.
  - Fixed open-ocean basin partitioning for maps with one dominant world ocean
    plus tiny isolated seas.  `_partition_open_ocean_basins(...)` now partitions
    the dominant open-ocean component and preserves minor water bodies as small
    basins, instead of skipping partitioning whenever `len(basin_area) != 1`.
  - Scoped the expanded land-balance underpartitioned check to late final
    polish via `balance_underpartitioned=True`, so earlier seaway stages keep
    the original conservative target detection.
- Tests added:
  - `test_p109_post_p110a_rebalance_restores_parented_highland_shoulder`
    verifies that second-pass P109 restores parented highland shoulders, keeps
    the land mask unchanged, and does not re-raise ordinary interiors.
  - `test_p110a_open_ocean_partition_splits_dominant_ocean_with_tiny_seas`
    verifies that one dominant ocean plus small isolated seas produces several
    major open-ocean basins rather than a single `~100%` basin.
- Test results:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `18 passed in 1.36 s`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Seaway/protection regressions:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed in 4.88 s`.
- `600/36p` validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p110a_basin_partition_600_20260630/00_600cells_36p`.
  - P109 passed after post-planform compensation:
    land mean `782.6 m`, p50 `555.5 m`, p90 `1863.4 m`, p98 `3239.6 m`;
    all elevation bands in envelope.
  - P110A also passed in that validation:
    land fraction `0.2583`, major land components `7`, largest/second/third
    land shares `0.6063/0.1097/0.0967`, major ocean basins `3`, largest ocean
    basin share `0.4203`, closed-ocean score `0.0`.
  - After scoping the balance repair to late polish, the latest `600/36p`
    sample `out_p110a_land_balance_600_b_20260630/00_600cells_36p` kept P109
    in envelope and ocean basins in envelope, but ended just below the
    nonlargest-major land threshold:
    largest/second/third shares `0.5685/0.1046/0.0784`.
    This is a low-resolution edge case, not promotion evidence.
- `2500/36p` validation:
  - P109 repair output:
    `out_p110a_p109_tail_comp_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`.
    P109 changed from failed to passed:
    p90 `1470.9 -> 1846.1 m`, `200-500 m` band `0.1426 -> 0.1658`,
    `2000-3000 m` band `0.0078 -> 0.0155`, all P109 fields in envelope.
  - Ocean-basin partition output:
    `out_p110a_basin_partition_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`.
    P109 remained passed and P110A ocean basins improved from one dominant
    basin to multiple major basins:
    major ocean basin count `1 -> 3`, largest ocean basin share
    `0.9973 -> 0.4107`, top basin shares approximately
    `0.411/0.352/0.235`.
  - Remaining P110A blocker:
    land components are still too two-dominant at `2500/36p`.
    The latest output
    `out_p110a_land_balance_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`
    has land fraction `0.2568`, P109 passed, major ocean basins `3`, but only
    `3` major land components with largest/second/third shares
    `0.4719/0.4564/0.0358`.
    P110A out-of-envelope fields remain
    `major_land_component_count_low` and
    `nonlargest_major_component_count_low`.
- Attribution:
  - The P109 blocker was not an upstream mountain-generation failure; it was a
    second-pass post-planform calibration issue that collapsed the highland
    shoulder after late land-mask edits.
  - The ocean-basin blocker was not necessarily a lack of geological ocean
    objects; the terrain ocean-geography partition skipped the dominant open
    ocean whenever tiny isolated seas made the connected-component count exceed
    one.
  - The remaining land-component blocker is not solved by more seaway threshold
    tuning.  The latest `2500/36p` run had a second late-polish pass, but the
    final pass found `0` viable seaway candidates and left a two-dominant
    continent pattern.
- Next required work:
  - Implement P110A.4 component-balanced land payback / secondary-continent
    strengthening.
  - The repair should operate after unsupported-land cleanup and late seaway
    polish, restoring land preferentially to tectonically parented secondary
    components, passive-margin platforms, microcontinents, shelves, and island
    arcs, while penalizing payback to the largest component.
  - The acceptance target for the next step is not merely lowering the largest
    share.  It must create at least two non-largest major components above the
    `~0.08` share target without dropping land fraction, reintroducing speckle,
    or breaking P109/ocean-basin improvements.
  - Add a synthetic fixture where two large continents and several parented
    secondary continental fragments receive balanced payback into a
    `4+`-major-component modern planform.
  - Then rerun the `600/36p -> 2500/36p` ladder, followed by the planned
    `8000/36p` five-seed P110A gate.

2026-06-30 - Revised current goal after repeated supercontinent-like seeds
- Trigger:
  - The latest qualitative map review found that the overall land/ocean split
    is improving, but five different seeds can still converge toward
    supercontinent-like or two-dominant-continent outcomes.
  - That is not acceptable for the default mature Earth-like preset.  Earth has
    had supercontinents in its history, and the simulator must be able to make
    them, but modern Earth-like final states should not all collapse into that
    archetype.
- Revised goal:
  - The immediate goal is to close the plate/terrain phase with a default
    mature Earth-like mode that produces a modern multipolar planform:
    multiple large continental assemblies, a connected world ocean partitioned
    into several geological ocean basins, visible passive/active margins, and
    coherent mountain/island-arc/ocean-floor structures.
  - "Earth-like" does not mean copying modern Earth.  It means the generator
    can reach Earth-plausible distributions for land fraction, major land
    component hierarchy, open-ocean basin hierarchy, shelves/slopes/abyssal
    plains, highland belts, island arcs, and microcontinents from tectonic
    process objects.
  - Supercontinents remain valid only when requested by a supercontinent
    preset, produced as a historical stage before breakup, or explicitly
    allowed through a final-state control.  They should not be the dominant
    default outcome for mature `earthlike` worlds.
- Non-goals for this closure step:
  - Do not force modern Earth's exact coastline or continent count.
  - Do not add unparented random land blobs just to pass component metrics.
  - Do not solve climate, monsoon, ocean-current, biome, or hydrology redesign
    here; those remain paused until the plate/terrain target is stable.
  - Do not hide planform failures with map coloring, sea-level-only tricks, or
    final raster cleanup that lacks tectonic parentage.
- Success envelope:
  - P109 continental hypsometry remains green after all late P110A land-mask
    edits: land p50/p90/p98 and elevation-band fractions should stay inside
    the current Earth-like envelope without broad over-high flat interiors.
  - P110A modern planform is green or has only documented soft warnings on the
    `8000/36p` multiseed gate, and one canonical `24000/60p` run confirms the
    same mechanism at production review scale.
  - Modern Earth reference remains the calibration anchor: largest land
    component share near `0.54` is acceptable only when several other major
    land components exist and ocean basins remain open.
  - A generated `8000/36p` world should normally have several major land
    components, at least two substantial non-largest components, no default
    land-ring ocean, and at least three object-backed major ocean basins.
  - The largest ocean basin may be broad, but the diagnostic source must be
    geological basin objects where available, not just connected-water labels.
  - Island arcs and microcontinents may add complexity, but they must remain
    parented by arc/plume/accretion/rift-margin objects and must not turn into
    unsupported salt-and-pepper shoals or land speckle.
- Current attribution:
  - P109-after-P110A is no longer the main blocker: post-planform compensation
    can restore the highland shoulder while preserving the land mask.
  - Ocean-basin partition is no longer the main blocker: the dominant open
    ocean can now be split into multiple major geological basins while small
    isolated seas remain minor basins.
  - The current blocker is component balance.  The engine can cut the largest
    continent, but 2500-cell evidence still shows a two-dominant-continent plus
    tiny-third pattern.  More arbitrary shaving is not the right owner.
  - The likely owner is a combination of secondary continental-block survival,
    component-balanced land payback, and upstream continent/margin lifecycle
    geometry.  Restored land must preferentially grow tectonically parented
    secondary components, passive-margin platforms, microcontinents, island
    arcs, and shallow shelves, while avoiding payback to the largest component.
- Current code assets for this goal:
  - `TerrainModule._p110a_modern_planform_target(...)` marks mature production
    Earth-like plate counts as modern-planform targets unless explicitly
    disabled.
  - `TerrainModule._p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish(...)` can apply bounded
    late seaway passes after P109 and refresh dependent ocean/margin fields.
  - `_shave_overdominant_modern_largest_landmass(...)` limits the largest
    landmass for modern targets without changing non-modern worlds.
  - `_p109_continental_hypsometry_rebalance(..., post_p110a_planform=True)`
    now handles the second post-planform vertical calibration pass.
  - `_partition_open_ocean_basins(...)` now partitions a dominant world ocean
    even when tiny isolated seas are present.
  - `_p110a_component_balanced_land_payback(...)` is the active P110A.4 repair
    path.  It is designed to add land to tectonically parented secondary
    components and candidate shelves while penalizing growth of the largest
    landmass.
  - `aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py` exposes the P109/P110A telemetry needed
    to distinguish hypsometry, ocean-basin, seaway, land-payback, and
    component-balance failures.
  - `tests/test_p107_audit.py` contains focused coverage for post-P110A P109
    compensation, dominant-ocean partitioning, final seaway polish, and
    component-balanced payback.
- Immediate validation queue:
  1. Revalidate the current P110A.4 candidate after the latest shallow
     near-secondary-shelf payback change:
     compile `aevum/modules/terrain.py`, run the focused P110A pytest cases,
     then run `600/36p` and `2500/36p` P107 audits.
  2. If `600/36p` passes but `2500/36p` still fails
     `major_land_component_count_low` or
     `nonlargest_major_component_count_low`, stop tuning thresholds and move
     the owner upstream to secondary continental-block survival.
  3. Add an explicit planform-diversity fixture set:
     modern multipolar, supercontinent assembly, supercontinent breakup,
     archipelago/back-arc, and rifted-continent cases.  Each fixture should
     verify the intended archetype rather than treating every seed as a random
     attempt at the same final shape.
  4. Add a five-seed `8000/36p` gate for the default mature Earth-like mode.
     A run where most seeds become supercontinents or two-dominant-continent
     worlds is a P110A failure even if land fraction and ocean depth look good.
  5. Promote only after one canonical `24000/60p` run preserves P108/P109,
     passes or narrowly warns on P110A, and archives the full PNG/JSON asset
     bundle.
- Next implementation direction if P110A.4 remains insufficient:
  - Replace late payback-only balancing with upstream continent provenance:
    maintain several broad secondary continental nuclei through the final
    Wilson-cycle stage, prevent all surviving stable platforms from attaching
    to the same largest component, and give rift/passive-margin objects enough
    geometric authority to keep oceans open.
  - Introduce a final-state archetype controller for generated-world presets:
    modern multipolar, supercontinent, post-breakup, and archipelago-heavy
    modes should be process-parameter outcomes, not accidents of random seed.
  - Make the archetype controller history-aware: a mature modern world can pass
    through earlier supercontinent states, but final `t=4500 Myr` Earth-like
    output should show the requested modern topology unless the preset says
    otherwise.
  - Keep every new land or ocean feature tied to parent objects
    (`continent`, `craton`, `passive_margin`, `rift`, `ocean_basin`,
    `island_arc`, `microcontinent`, `accreted_terrane`, `plume/LIP`) so later
    terrain, climate, and compiler layers can consume cause rather than color.

2026-06-30 - P110A.4 component-balanced payback bridge fix and 2500 pass
- Problem found during validation:
  - The first P110A.4 candidate had enough parented candidate area, but the
    trial payback did not improve the modern component hierarchy at `2500/36p`.
    The failed run
    `out_p110a_secondary_shelf_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p` had P109 green
    and object-backed ocean basins green, but P110A still failed:
    major land component count `3`, largest/second/third land shares
    `0.4757/0.4496/0.0309`, and out-of-envelope fields
    `major_land_component_count_low` plus
    `nonlargest_major_component_count_low`.
  - Payback telemetry showed the candidate pool was not the blocker:
    candidate area fraction `0.2003`, trial added fraction `0.0336`, but the
    trial was rejected with code `6` because it still left only one
    non-largest component above the `0.08` share target and slightly increased
    the largest component share.
- Attribution:
  - Targeted component growth allowed candidates that touched any existing land
    through `land_neighbors`, not only the component being grown.  That could
    turn a secondary shelf into a bridge between the target component and an
    unrelated large landmass.
  - Targeted growth also allowed non-adjacent same-lineage cells.  Those cells
    created new small components instead of growing the intended third/fourth
    continent.
  - The fallback free-pool could still touch existing land when object or
    lineage support was present, so it could merge components after the
    targeted pass had done useful work.
  - The component growth budget was too conservative for the exact blocker:
    at `2500/36p`, the trial reached major count `5` and lowered the largest
    share, but the third component stopped at `0.0745`, just below the
    `0.08` non-largest-major threshold.
- Code changes:
  - In `_p110a_component_balanced_land_payback(...)`, targeted growth now
    excludes candidates with `other_land_neighbors > 0`, so a candidate cell
    must grow the selected component rather than bridge to unrelated land.
  - Targeted growth now requires `grown_neighbors > 0`; non-adjacent
    same-lineage or object-backed cells are no longer treated as part of the
    selected component expansion.
  - The free-pool stage now requires `land_neighbors == 0`, making it an
    isolated parented-component fallback rather than a bridge builder.
  - If targeted growth already achieves `major_count >= 4` and at least two
    non-largest components above `0.08`, the helper stops before free-pool.
  - Components that need to become the second non-largest-major landmass now
    target share `0.096` and may use up to `0.024` world-area budget, instead
    of barely targeting `0.086` with a `0.016` cap.
  - P107 payback telemetry now includes component count, ribbon fraction, and
    second/third/fourth share after the trial, even when the trial is rejected.
- Validation:
  - Compile:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Focused tests:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_component_balanced_payback_grows_secondary_continents tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `19 passed`.
  - Seaway/protection regressions:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
- Smoke results after the fix:
  - `600/36p` output:
    `out_p110a_target096_600_20260630/00_600cells_36p`.
    P110A passed with major land components `7`, land fraction `0.2566`,
    largest/second/third land shares `0.6169/0.1169/0.0973`, major ocean
    basins `3`, and accepted component payback area `0.0183` of world area.
    P109 still had one low-resolution band miss (`band_200_500m`), so this
    remains a mechanism smoke only, not promotion evidence.
  - `2500/36p` output:
    `out_p110a_target096_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`.
    P109 passed with land mean `716.2 m`, p50 `510.2 m`, p90 `1785.6 m`,
    p98 `3217.2 m`, and all tracked elevation bands inside envelope.
    P110A passed with land fraction `0.2792`, major land components `5`,
    largest/second/third shares `0.4255/0.3796/0.0917`, major ocean basins `3`,
    largest ocean basin share `0.3990`, and closed-ocean score `0.0`.
    Component payback was accepted: added area fraction `0.0332`, candidate
    fraction `0.0612`, major count `3 -> 5`, non-largest-major count `1 -> 2`,
    largest share `0.4830 -> 0.4255`, and reject code `0`.
- Current status:
  - The immediate `2500/36p` component-balance blocker is fixed for the
    tracked seed.
  - This does not complete P110A promotion.  The next required evidence is a
    five-seed `8000/36p` default mature Earth-like gate.  That gate should
    decide whether the current local component-balanced payback is sufficient
    across seeds or whether upstream secondary continental-block survival and
    final-state archetype control must be implemented next.

2026-06-30 - P110A.4 8000 preflight and land-floor follow-up
- 8000 preflight before the land-floor follow-up:
  - Output:
    `out_p110a_target096_8000_preflight_20260630/00_8000cells_36p`.
  - Component balance was no longer the blocker:
    major land components `5`, largest/second/third shares
    `0.4802/0.3188/0.0929`, major ocean basins `3`, largest ocean basin share
    `0.3748`, and accepted component payback area `0.0139`.
  - The remaining hard failure was land fraction:
    P110A `land_fraction=0.2153`, below the `0.22` broad floor.
  - P109 also failed one band (`band_500_1000m`) because the land mask stayed
    too small and too concentrated in mid-elevation cells.
- Follow-up change:
  - `_p110a_component_balanced_land_payback(...)` now keeps a minimal modern
    P110A land floor after component balance is achieved.  If the targeted
    pass already gives `major_count >= 4` and two non-largest components above
    `0.08`, it stops only when the current land area is at least `0.225` of
    the world.  Otherwise it allows the isolated, parented free-pool to add
    just enough extra land to approach that floor.
  - This deliberately does not restore land to the full preset target
    (`0.29`).  The purpose is to keep the modern planform inside the P110A
    broad envelope without undoing seaway/open-ocean repairs or recreating
    unsupported land blobs.
- Regression after the land-floor follow-up:
  - `2500/36p` output:
    `out_p110a_floor225_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`.
    P109 passed and P110A passed, preserving the previous successful metrics:
    land fraction `0.2792`, major land components `5`, largest/second/third
    shares `0.4255/0.3796/0.0917`, major ocean basins `3`, largest ocean basin
    share `0.3990`, and accepted payback area `0.0332`.
  - `8000/36p` output:
    `out_p110a_floor225_8000_preflight_20260630/00_8000cells_36p`.
    P109 passed with land mean `872.9 m`, p50 `707.2 m`, p90 `1861.7 m`,
    p98 `3188.9 m`, and all tracked elevation bands inside envelope.
    P110A passed with land fraction `0.2241`, major land components `6`,
    largest/second/third shares `0.4607/0.3090/0.0904`, major ocean basins `3`,
    largest ocean basin share `0.3823`, and closed-ocean score `0.0048`.
    Component payback was accepted: added area fraction `0.0205`, candidate
    fraction `0.0436`, major count `4 -> 6`, non-largest-major count `1 -> 2`,
    largest share `0.5071 -> 0.4607`, and reject code `0`.
- Tests after the follow-up:
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `19 passed`.
  - `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
- Residual:
  - The `8000/36p` preflight passes P109/P110A, but
    `p109_sea_level_consistency.land_fraction_consistent_with_target=false`
    remains because land fraction is still `0.0659` below the preset target
    `0.29`.
  - This is acceptable for the current P110A broad gate but should be tracked
    during the multiseed `8000/36p` gate.  If many seeds cluster near the lower
    `0.22-0.23` land-floor edge, the next owner is not another local payback
    threshold; it is upstream modern archetype/secondary-continent survival or
    a revised water-volume/sea-level target contract.
- Next required work:
  - Run the planned `8000/36p` multiseed gate.  Given the measured runtime
    (`~552 s` per 8000-cell seed without rendering), use a staged sweep:
    first `2-3` seeds for a quick stability check, then the full five-seed
    record if no systematic land-floor, P109, or P110A component regression is
    found.

2026-06-30 - P110A.4 staged 8000 quick gate and P109 summit-tail repair
- Staged `8000/36p` quick gate:
  - Command shape:
    `p107-audit` does not sweep several seeds for one cell-count entry.  The
    staged quick gate therefore uses repeated cell-count entries, for example
    `--cells 8000 8000 --n-plates 36 36 --seeds 101 202`.
  - Output:
    `out_p110a_floor225_8000_quick2_20260630`.
  - Runtime: `1108.1 s` for two non-rendered `8000/36p` entries.
  - Seed `101` passed P109 and P110A:
    land fraction `0.2821`, major land components `4`,
    largest/second/third shares `0.4378/0.2477/0.2468`, major ocean basins `3`,
    largest ocean basin share `0.5210`, P109 land mean `727.3 m`, p50
    `488.1 m`, p90 `1796.7 m`, p98 `3216.6 m`.
  - Seed `202` passed P110A but initially failed P109 by a very small summit
    tail miss:
    `band_gt4500m=0.00269` versus the current P109 floor `0.003`.
    Its planform was good: land fraction `0.2788`, major land components `5`,
    largest/second/third shares `0.5180/0.1767/0.1767`, major ocean basins `3`,
    largest ocean basin share `0.3565`.
- Attribution:
  - This was not a renewed over-highland problem.  The generated-minus-Earth
    value for `band_gt4500m` was negative, and the P109 failure was below the
    small summit-tail floor.
  - The existing P109 peak retention budget was small enough, but the candidate
    filter was too narrow: a cell had to be inventory `1/3`, detail
    orogen/plateau, and pre-rebalance relief `>=3500 m`.  At `8000` cells this
    can under-sample legitimate parented summit cells by one or a few cells.
- Code change:
  - `_p109_continental_hypsometry_rebalance(...)` now broadens
    `parented_extreme` summit candidates while keeping the same small
    retention budget.  It still prioritizes the highest pre-rebalance relief,
    but may retain cells that are parented mountain/plateau at `>=3300 m`,
    inventory-backed highlands at `>=3600 m`, or detail-backed
    orogen/plateau cells at `>=3900 m`.
  - This preserves a minimal realistic summit tail without changing the
    land/ocean mask and without allowing broad high shelves.
- Validation after the summit-tail repair:
  - `2500/36p` smoke:
    `out_p109_extreme_tail_2500_20260630/00_2500cells_36p`.
    P109 and P110A stayed green.  `gt4500m=0.00430`, land fraction `0.2792`,
    major land components `5`, largest/second/third
    `0.4255/0.3796/0.0917`.
  - `8000/36p` seed `202` rerun:
    `out_p109_extreme_tail_8000_seed202_20260630/00_8000cells_36p`.
    P109 and P110A now both pass.  `gt4500m=0.00403`, land mean `869.3 m`,
    p50 `644.8 m`, p90 `1853.3 m`, p98 `3173.1 m`; land fraction `0.2788`,
    major land components `5`, largest/second/third
    `0.5180/0.1767/0.1767`, major ocean basins `3`, largest ocean basin share
    `0.3565`.
  - Regression tests:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `19 passed`.
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p21_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmarks_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p22_medium_supercontinent_breakup_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p24_object_to_terrain_seaway_microbenchmark_pass tests/test_tectonics_bench.py::test_p32_coastline_simplification_microbenchmarks_pass -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
- Current status:
  - The staged `8000/36p` quick gate is effectively `2/2` after the seed `202`
    summit-tail rerun.
  - P110A component balance has now passed seed `60631`, seed `101`, and seed
    `202` at `8000/36p`.
  - The next promotion step is the full five-seed `8000/36p` record, adding
    seeds `303`, `404`, and `505` to the archived evidence set before any
    `24000/60p` canonical run.
