    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 2500 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 9 --out out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_seed707_2500_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
  - Seed sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_seed707_2500_20260630 --out out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_seed707_2500_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A threshold pass `1/1`, P110B visual candidate `1/1`,
    distribution flags `none`.
  - Terminal:
    land `0.276`; major land components `4`;
    largest/second/third land shares `0.326 / 0.223 / 0.221`;
    major ocean basins `3`; no P109/P110A warnings.
  - Historical trajectory:
    `1/11` supercontinent-like frames; frame fraction `0.091`;
    longest consecutive duration `0.0 Myr`;
    `long_lived_supercontinent_like=false`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Run a fresh rendered `8000/36p` preflight with the P110B.4.6 code path,
    then inspect visible geography: plate-boundary legibility, coherent
    mountain belts, ocean-floor diversity, and whether terminal land fraction
    remains comfortably in the Earth-reference envelope at higher resolution.

2026-06-30 - P110B.4.7 rendered 8000-cell preflight after terminal seaway repair
- Purpose:
  - Validate the P110B.4.6 terminal-seaway and lowland-tail repair at a visual
    scale using the previously problematic seed `707`.
  - Separate true planform regressions from remaining visual/rendering
    geography issues before declaring the plate-engineering phase ready for
    the next cleanup packet.
- Rendered run:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 9 --out out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_8000_seed707_render_20260630`
  - Runtime:
    `698.9 s`.
  - Output directory:
    `out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_8000_seed707_render_20260630/00_8000cells_36p`.
  - Rendered assets include:
    `p107_contact_sheet.png`, `elevation.png`,
    `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`, `plates.png`, `crust_age.png`,
    `ocean_basins.png`, `ocean_depth_provinces.png`,
    `continent_components.png`, `geography_primitives.png`,
    `morphology_diagnostics.png`, and climate-layer PNGs.
- Seed sweep:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_8000_seed707_render_20260630 --out out_p110b46_final_seaway_lowland_8000_seed707_render_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
  - Result:
    P109/P110A threshold pass `1/1`; P110B visual candidate `1/1`;
    largest land share median/p90/max `0.413 / 0.413 / 0.413`;
    largest soft-warning rate `0.0%`; distribution flags `none`.
- Terminal planform metrics:
  - Land fraction `0.2845`; major land components `5`;
    largest/second/third land shares `0.413 / 0.233 / 0.160`.
  - Major ocean basins `3`; largest ocean basin share `0.477`.
  - Terminal supercontinent-like `false`;
    modern multipolar overconnected `false`;
    terminal supercontinent score `0.195`.
  - Lineage support:
    major components `5`; continental major components `5`;
    continental lineage-supported major components `5`;
    provenance-supported major components `5`;
    independent primary lineage count `3`;
    lineage area fraction of land `0.810`.
- Historical trajectory:
  - Supercontinent-like frames `1/11`;
    frame fraction `0.091`;
    longest consecutive supercontinent-like duration `0.0 Myr`;
    maximum largest-land share `0.909`;
    median largest-land share `0.430`;
    `long_lived_supercontinent_like=false`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
- Terrain and bathymetry:
  - Land hypsometry:
    mean `791 m`; p50 `558 m`; p90 `2013 m`; p98 `3112 m`;
    max `4700 m`.
  - Land elevation bands:
    `0-200 m` `0.269`; `200-500 m` `0.160`;
    `500-1000 m` `0.315`; `1000-2000 m` `0.153`;
    `2000-3000 m` `0.065`; `3000-4500 m` `0.033`;
    `>4500 m` `0.0044`.
  - Ocean depth using actual terminal sea level
    `sea_level_m=-2383.4`:
    mean depth `2253 m`; p50 `2759 m`; p90 `3721 m`; p98 `4020 m`;
    max depth `7049 m`.
    Ocean area deeper than `5000 m` is `0.0030`; deeper than `6000 m` is
    `0.0024`.  A temporary `elevation < 0` check falsely suggested an
    overdeep ocean because the terminal sea level is below absolute zero in
    the generated height field; the correct relative-depth calculation does
    not show a global abyssal overdeepening failure.
- Visual inspection:
  - Passed:
    terminal `elevation.png` and `continent_components.png` show multiple
    major landmasses rather than a single modern supercontinent;
    `ocean_basins.png` shows three large basins with a large open-ocean basin;
    `crust_age.png` and `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png` are broadly aligned
    with oceanic age and shelf/slope/abyss organization.
  - Residual issues deferred to visual-scale cleanup:
    continental margins and ribbon index are still too extensive and jagged;
    `tectonic_objects.png` and detail-province maps remain speckled;
    high terrain is improved but mountain belts still appear blocky rather
    than consistently long, coherent orogenic belts; rendered bathymetry uses
    a 0-4000 m visual scale that saturates the deepest trenches and can make
    normal abyssal areas look too uniformly dark.
- Decision:
  - P110B.4.6 fixes the immediate supercontinent regression at 900, 2500, and
    8000 cells for the audited problem seeds.
  - Do not mark the whole plate-engineering plan complete yet.  The next work
    should move to a visual-scale geography cleanup packet focused on
    continent-margin/ribbon suppression, coherent mountain belts, clearer
    plate-boundary expression, and ocean-object mask de-speckling.

2026-06-30 - P111 visual-scale geography cleanup kickoff
- Scope:
  - The latest P110B gate proves the modern endpoint and historical
    supercontinent residence are numerically acceptable at visual-audit scale.
    The remaining defects are visual/process-resolution defects rather than
    terminal planform defects.
  - The first P111 target is high-mountain coherence: mountains should form
    range/province-scale belts, not many small parented highland spots.
- Diagnostic addition:
  - Extended P107 high-mountain coherence metrics with:
    `top3_high_mountain_component_share`,
    `small_high_mountain_component_area_fraction_of_high`, and
    `high_mountain_fragmentation_index`.
  - Rationale:
    existing metrics showed `parent_overlap=~1.0`, so they could not
    distinguish a real coherent orogen from many small parented speckles.
    The new metrics measure how much high-mountain area lives in the largest
    few components versus small fragments.
- Current `8000/36p seeds 505/606/707` P111 baseline, computed from terminal
  arrays without rerunning simulation:
  - seed `505`: high components `36`; small components `34`;
    largest high component share `0.132`; top-3 share `0.319`;
    small-component area share `0.758`; fragmentation index `1.0`.
  - seed `606`: high components `42`; small components `38`;
    largest high component share `0.104`; top-3 share `0.280`;
    small-component area share `0.640`; fragmentation index `1.0`.
  - seed `707`: high components `28`; small components `26`;
    largest high component share `0.116`; top-3 share `0.314`;
    small-component area share `0.779`; fragmentation index `1.0`.
- Interpretation:
  - The model now attaches most high mountains to plausible parent processes,
    but it fails to merge those parented highs into coherent mountain belts.
    P111 should therefore adjust terrain response and post-inventory mountain
    cleanup to connect supported highland shoulders/crests, not just suppress
    unsupported peaks.
- Initial P111 acceptance target:
  - Keep P109/P110A/P110B gates passing on the `8000/36p` three-seed baseline.
  - Reduce high-mountain fragmentation index below `0.75` for at least two of
    the three baseline seeds, with no seed above `0.90`.
  - Reduce small high-mountain component area fraction below `0.45` where
    possible, while preserving parent overlap above `0.85`.
  - Do not increase `>4500 m` land band beyond P109's upper envelope.

2026-06-30 - Plate-engineering finish target update after five-seed review
- User-facing trigger:
  - Recent maps no longer fail as obvious ribbon-worlds, and P110B.4.6/P110B.4.7
    made the terminal land/ocean split much more usable.
  - The next visible blocker is qualitative: several seed previews still read
    too much like supercontinent or two-continent worlds, not like a modern
    Earth-style distribution of large landmasses, marginal seas, island arcs,
    and open ocean basins.
  - The current land/sea distribution can look acceptable while the elevation
    field still feels too high because broad continental interiors and old
    platforms are expressed as smooth uplands rather than a mix of lowlands,
    basins, shields, old orogens, plateaus, and passive-margin plains.
- Current evidence to keep in view:
  - Current P110B.4.5/P110B.4.7-style `8000/36p` three-seed evidence is much
    better than the older five-seed gateway baseline:
    land fraction `0.283-0.311`; major land components `4-5`;
    largest land share `0.334-0.429`; major ocean basins `3`;
    no terminal or long-lived historical supercontinent flags.
  - The earlier `8000/36p` five-seed gateway baseline passed numerical gates
    but explains the user's visual concern: largest land share median `0.505`,
    max `0.548`, one member with only two major land components, and a small
    third-land share in multiple members.  That distribution can pass coarse
    "not a single supercontinent" logic while still reading as over-connected.
  - P111 high-mountain baseline remains poor even when process parentage is
    valid: `28-42` high-mountain components per seed, top-3 high-mountain share
    only `0.280-0.319`, and small high-mountain component area share
    `0.640-0.779`.
  - The P110B.4.7 seed `707` hypsometry is not globally impossible
    (`mean land=791 m`, `p50=558 m`, `p90=2013 m`, `max=4700 m`), but its
    visual problem is shape: too much moderate-high land is expressed as broad
    smooth upland, while coherent lowland belts and province partitions are not
    visually strong enough.
- Diagnosis:
  - The existing P110B gate is sufficient to reject terminal single
    supercontinents, but it is still too permissive for two-body or
    over-connected modern maps.  It does not strongly reward a balanced
    three-to-six-major-continent endpoint.
  - Internal domain seaways and terminal breakup cuts are too conservative in
    the current high-resolution evidence.  They prevent hard failures, but they
    do not yet reliably create Earth-like marginal seas, natural divides inside
    large continental assemblies, or separated but historically related
    continental blocks.
  - Terrain highness is driven less by an extreme mountain tail than by broad
    support terms: crustal/isostatic support, mature-platform support,
    lowland-tail repairs, and sea-level solving can leave large interiors as
    connected moderate upland.
  - Boundary and mountain rendering still works at cell/province-mask scale
    more than at range-system scale.  Narrowing the boundary masks alone would
    improve legibility but could erase necessary orogenic continuity unless
    the highland response is rebuilt as belt objects.
  - Ocean-object counts are now present, but visible ocean-floor hierarchy is
    still under-expressed.  Shallow areas, ridges, trenches, island chains, and
    microcontinents must be object-backed and shaped by plate history, not
    injected as final decorative patches.
- Updated plate-engineering finish line:
  - Modern planform:
    - land fraction preferred `0.26-0.31`;
    - major land component count preferred `3-6`;
    - largest land component share preferred `0.25-0.45`;
    - largest land component share `>0.55` should be a soft failure unless the
      map has strong evidence of multiple continent domains, narrow land
      bridges, and open ocean basins;
    - at least two of the second/third/fourth major land components should be
      geologically supported, not late oceanic-landform artifacts.
  - Supercontinent residence:
    - terminal supercontinent-like must remain false;
    - long-lived historical supercontinent-like must remain false;
    - finite supercontinent episodes are allowed only when followed by
      object-backed breakup, passive margins, oceanic crust formation, and
      surviving seaways.
  - Ocean topology:
    - major ocean basin count preferred `3-5`;
    - largest ocean basin share preferred `0.35-0.60`;
    - closed-ocean-ring score should remain below `0.02`;
    - disconnected or continent-enclosed ocean components must have Wilson-cycle
      or marginal-sea provenance, not final mask accidents.
  - Continental hypsometry and province expression:
    - land mean target `600-900 m`;
    - land p50 target `300-650 m`;
    - land p90 target `1600-2300 m`;
    - `0-500 m` land share should usually be at least `0.40`;
    - `>4500 m` land share should stay below the existing P109 upper envelope;
    - large continents must expose multiple physiographic provinces:
      shield/platform, sedimentary or intracratonic basin, passive-margin
      lowland, old orogen/suture, active orogen where applicable, foreland
      basin, and rift or failed-rift lowland.
  - Plate-boundary and mountain expression:
    - current boundary masks should be thinner and clearer than the old broad
      process fields, but boundary lifecycle objects must remain continuous;
    - active or recent mountain belts should be range-scale connected objects;
    - high-mountain fragmentation index target remains `<0.75` for at least
      two of three baseline seeds and no seed above `0.90`;
    - top-3 high-mountain component share should rise above `0.45` where the
      world contains active collision or Andean-style margins;
    - small high-mountain area share should fall below `0.45` without reducing
      parent-process overlap below `0.85`.
  - Ocean-floor expression:
    - abyssal plains should be broad and quiet;
    - mid-ocean ridges should be continuous with transform offsets;
    - trenches should be narrow deep features tied to subduction polarity;
    - seamount chains and oceanic plateaus should sometimes breach sea level as
      island chains when thermal age, swell height, and sea level allow it;
    - microcontinents and submerged plateaus should be rare, named/object-backed
      features, not repeated shallow-ocean speckle.
- Implementation sequence:
  - `P111.1 modern-planform distribution gate`
    - Promote the five-seed `8000/36p` review to a standing audit using fresh
      seeds, not only legacy seeds `505/606/707`.
    - Add aggregate warnings for two-body endpoints, largest-land share above
      preferred range, weak third/fourth land components, and missing
      object-backed internal seaways.
    - Do not weaken the existing P109/P110A/P110B acceptance logic.
  - `P111.2 continental hypsometry retune`
    - Reduce broad upland expression by separating stable cratonic/platform
      support from elevation uplift.
    - Strengthen passive-margin lowlands, intracratonic basins, foreland
      basins, and mature sedimentary covers as low-to-moderate elevation
      objects.
    - Preserve realistic active orogens and plateaus; do not solve the problem
      by flattening all continents.
  - `P111.3 coherent orogen and boundary-legibility repair`
    - Build mountain belts from boundary/lifecycle objects and inherited
      sutures, then rasterize them, instead of letting individual high cells
      decide coherence.
    - Narrow visible plate-boundary masks while retaining continuous archive
      objects and process ids.
  - `P111.4 terminal breakup and seaway provenance`
    - Make modern seaways and continental separations descend from rift,
      passive-margin, slab-retreat, gateway, and domain-boundary history.
    - Avoid arbitrary final land/ocean carving except as a documented repair
      fallback with explicit warning metrics.
  - `P111.5 ocean-floor hierarchy and island-chain expression`
    - Rebalance ridge, transform, trench, abyssal plain, seamount, oceanic
      plateau, microcontinent, and island-arc rendering from object fields.
    - Allow object-backed island chains where volcanic/arc/plateau elevation
      exceeds sea level, but keep unsupported shallow speckle suppressed.
  - `P111.6 24000-cell promotion`
    - Run `24000/60p` only after `8000/36p` five-seed distribution, hypsometry,
      boundary, and mountain-coherence gates are stable.
    - Use 24000 cells to verify microgeomorphology, not to hide structural
      issues that are already visible at 8000 cells.
- Microbenchmark and audit additions:
  - Add or update generated-world gates:
    - five-seed modern-planform distribution;
    - two-body endpoint detector;
    - third/fourth component support detector;
    - internal seaway provenance coverage;
    - continent province-diversity per major landmass;
    - high-mountain belt coherence;
    - boundary visible-width and continuity;
    - ocean-floor object-to-render consistency.
  - Add fixture-style microbenchmarks:
    - `atlantic_passive_margin_ridge`: broad passive margins, a continuous
      ridge, transform offsets, and abyssal plains;
    - `andean_active_margin`: trench, arc/high belt, foreland/backarc
      asymmetry, and narrow deep bathymetry;
    - `himalaya_tibet_collision`: collision belt plus limited plateau and
      foreland basin, not scattered peaks;
    - `island_arc_accretion`: island arcs can breach sea level and later accrete
      to a continent;
    - `microcontinent_and_plateau`: submerged plateau/microcontinent features
      are object-backed and area-limited;
    - `supercontinent_breakup_modern_endpoint`: finite supercontinent residence
      followed by sustained breakup, oceanic crust, passive margins, and
      multiple terminal landmasses.
- Next concrete entry point:
  - Start with `P111.1`: implement the five-seed modern-planform distribution
    audit and warnings first.  This gives the next retuning work a stable
    target so later terrain or seaway edits are judged against the same
    "Earth-like but not Earth-cloned" endpoint.

2026-06-30 - P111.1 modern-planform distribution gate archived
- Purpose:
  - Keep P110B's coarse "not a terminal supercontinent" gate comparable, while
    adding a stricter P111 review layer for modern Earth-like land/ocean
    distribution.
  - Detect exactly the failure mode raised in visual review: maps can pass P110B
    yet still read as over-connected, two-body, or weak-third-continent worlds.
- Implementation:
  - Extended `aevum/diagnostics/p110b_seed_sweep.py` with P111 thresholds:
    preferred largest-land share `<=0.45`, soft overlarge share `>0.55`,
    preferred major land components `3-6`, minimum third-land share `0.10`,
    fourth-land support `0.03`, secondary-pair support `0.16`, and two-body
    top-two share `0.88`.
  - Added per-run fields:
    `p111_modern_planform_status` and
    `p111_modern_planform_review_flags`.
  - Added aggregate fields:
    `p111_modern_planform_candidate_count`,
    `p111_modern_planform_review_count`,
    `p111_two_body_endpoint_count`,
    `p111_largest_land_component_overlarge_count`,
    `p111_largest_land_component_above_modern_preferred_count`,
    `p111_weak_third_fourth_land_component_count`,
    `p111_internal_seaway_provenance_gap_count`,
    `p111_review_flag_counts`, `p111_status_counts`, and
    `p111_distribution_flags`.
  - Added acceptance key:
    `p111_modern_planform_distribution_ready`.
  - Updated `aevum/cli.py p110b-seed-sweep` to print P111 candidate count and
    P111 distribution flags.
  - Kept P110B acceptance semantics intact: existing
    `distribution_flags` and `ready_for_p110b_promotion` still describe the
    older P110B gate, while P111 flags describe the stricter finish-line gate.
- Tests:
  - Added a two-body endpoint regression:
    `test_p111_seed_sweep_flags_two_body_endpoint_separately`.
  - Added a P110B-candidate/P111-review regression:
    `test_p111_seed_sweep_flags_overlarge_weak_tertiary_candidate`.
  - Targeted command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `9 passed`.
- Existing-output audits:
  - Legacy five-seed baseline:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p110b3_gateway_topology_8000_gate_5seed_20260630 --out out_p1111_modern_planform_gate_legacy5_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 5`
    -> P109/P110A `5/5`, P110B visual candidates `5/5`, but P111
    modern-planform candidates only `1/5`.
  - Legacy P111 flags:
    `p111_modern_planform_review_flags_present`,
    `p111_two_body_endpoint_present`,
    `p111_weak_third_fourth_land_component_present`,
    `p111_median_largest_landmass_above_modern_preferred`,
    `p111_major_land_component_count_low_present`,
    `p111_largest_landmass_above_preferred_rate_high`.
  - Legacy metrics:
    largest land share median/p90/max `0.505 / 0.533 / 0.548`;
    major land components min/median/max `2 / 4 / 4`.
  - Current three-seed baseline:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p110b45c_residence_controller_peakfloor_8000_3seed_20260630 --out out_p1111_modern_planform_gate_current3_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 3`
    -> P109/P110A `3/3`, P110B visual candidates `3/3`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `3/3`, P111 distribution flags `none`.
  - Current metrics:
    largest land share median/p90/max `0.413 / 0.426 / 0.429`;
    major land components min/median/max `4 / 4 / 5`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.1 proves that the latest P110B.4.5/P110B.4.7 code path has a better
    modern endpoint than the older gateway-topology five-seed baseline.
  - It also gives future tuning a stricter regression target: later hypsometry,
    mountain-belt, or ocean-floor changes must not drift back into the old
    P110B-pass/P111-fail regime.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Proceed to `P111.2 continental hypsometry retune`: reduce broad smooth
    interior uplands by separating stable cratonic/platform support from uplift,
    strengthening lowland basin/passive-margin objects, and preserving active
    orogen/plateau relief.

2026-06-30 - P111.2 continental hypsometry finish retune archived
- Purpose:
  - Address the remaining visual issue that some generated continents still
    read as broad smooth uplands even when global P109 hypsometry passes.
  - Keep the fix vertical and province-aware: do not change the land/ocean mask,
    and do not flatten explicit active orogens, plateaus, or high mountain
    inventory.
- Implementation:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p111_continental_hypsometry_finish_retune`.
  - The pass runs after the existing P109 final p90 floor, extreme-peak floor,
    p90 ceiling, and lowland-shoulder polishes.
  - It is gated to modern Earth-like final states through
    `_p110a_modern_planform_target`.
  - It targets only ordinary continental cells:
    platform, basin, rift-basin, arc/microcontinent, or uncategorized
    continental land.
  - It protects:
    `CONT_DETAIL_OROGEN`, `CONT_DETAIL_PLATEAU`, mountain inventory classes
    `1` and `3`, and existing `>=3000 m` highland cells.
  - It first restores ordinary `500-1050 m` platform/basin cells into the
    `200-500 m` lowland/platform band when `0-500 m` land share is below target
    or when p50/mean are high.
  - It then lowers part of the remaining `800-1750 m` ordinary tableland into a
    lower platform shoulder when mean, p50, or `500-1000 m` band share remain
    high and the p90 tail has enough room.
  - It writes P111.2 observability globals:
    `terrain.last_p1112_adjusted_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p1112_low500_fraction_before/after`,
    `terrain.last_p1112_mean_before/after_m`,
    `terrain.last_p1112_p50_before/after_m`,
    `terrain.last_p1112_p90_before/after_m`,
    `terrain.last_p1112_highland_preservation_delta_p50_m`, and
    `terrain.last_p1112_land_mask_preserved`.
  - `p107_audit` now includes those P111.2 fields in
    `p109_sea_level_consistency`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_engine.py::test_p1112_continental_hypsometry_retune_lowers_smooth_interiors_not_mountains`.
  - The fixture builds a smooth high interior continent with explicit active
    range and plateau cells.  Acceptance:
    land/ocean mask preserved; `0-500 m` share rises by more than `0.20`;
    land mean drops by more than `100 m`; highland median is unchanged; P111.2
    globals report adjustment and highland preservation.
  - Commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_p1112_continental_hypsometry_retune_lowers_smooth_interiors_not_mountains -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `10 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_runs_and_conserves_carbon tests/test_engine.py::test_determinism_same_seed -q`
    -> `2 passed in 303.74s`.
- Small generated-world audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p1112_hypsometry_retune_900_seed707_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `73.6 s`; terminal land fraction `0.238`.
  - Terminal P109:
    within P109 envelope `true`; land mean `865.6 m`; p50 `556.1 m`;
    p90 `2087.9 m`; p98 `3231.2 m`.
  - Terminal land bands:
    `0-200 m` `0.229`; `200-500 m` `0.187`; `500-1000 m` `0.313`;
    `1000-2000 m` `0.149`; `2000-3000 m` `0.066`;
    `3000-4500 m` `0.047`; `>4500 m` `0.009`.
  - P111.2 effect in that generated world:
    adjusted area `0.00446` of world area;
    `0-500 m` land share `0.397 -> 0.416`;
    land mean `868.7 -> 865.6 m`;
    p50 `556.16 -> 556.12 m`;
    p90 unchanged at `2087.9 m`;
    highland p50 delta `0.0 m`;
    land mask preserved `true`.
  - Follow-up seed sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p1112_hypsometry_retune_900_seed707_20260630 --out out_p1112_hypsometry_retune_900_seed707_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`,
    P111 modern-planform candidates `1/1`, P111 distribution flags `none`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.2 is deliberately conservative on already-good worlds: it only nudges
    a small ordinary-platform area when lowland share is just below the P111
    preferred target.
  - The function-level fixture proves the intended stronger behavior for the
    actual defect case: broad high smooth interiors get lowered while explicit
    mountains and plateaus are preserved.
  - Do not treat plate engineering as complete yet.  The next visible target is
    still P111.3: coherent orogenic belts and boundary legibility.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Proceed to `P111.3 coherent orogen and boundary-legibility repair`: use
    boundary/lifecycle objects and inherited sutures to connect high mountain
    cells into range-scale belts while narrowing noisy boundary masks.

2026-06-30 - Current plate-engineering goal lock after supercontinent-layout review
- User-facing trigger:
  - A five-seed review showed that the improved generator can now produce
    attractive land/sea distributions, but it still over-samples
    supercontinent-derived or two-dominant-continent endpoints.
  - This is not a hard validity failure by itself: Earth history includes
    supercontinent assembly and breakup, and a large Afro-Eurasia-like connected
    land domain is valid.  The defect is distributional.  The default mature
    Earth-like preset should not make most reviewed worlds read as locked
    supercontinents, enclosed-ocean worlds, or one large continent plus a weak
    secondary landmass.
- Final target for the current tectonics closure pass:
  - Generate Earth-science-plausible modern-endpoint worlds, not an Earth clone.
  - The terminal map should usually contain several broad continental domains,
    multiple open ocean basins, legible passive and active margins, coherent
    mountain systems, and object-backed ocean-floor provinces.
  - The terminal state may retain geological memory of earlier supercontinents,
    but it must show credible breakup products: passive margins, young oceanic
    crust, ridges, inherited seaways, gateways, sutures, accreted terranes, and
    continent-scale physiographic provinces.
- Non-goals:
  - Do not forbid supercontinents globally.  Supercontinent states remain valid
    for deep-time frames and explicit supercontinent presets.
  - Do not force every seed into the exact present-day Earth layout or exact
    plate count.
  - Do not solve visual realism by adding random final decorative patches.
    Islands, shallow plateaus, microcontinents, trenches, ridges, and seaways
    must have process/object provenance or be explicitly flagged as fallback
    repairs.
  - Do not promote directly to `24000` cells to hide failures already visible
    at `8000` cells.
- Current blockers to close before declaring the plate step done:
  - Endpoint diversity:
    - The current three-seed `8000/36p` baseline passes P111.1, but the broader
      visual concern remains: fresh seeds should not mostly collapse into
      supercontinent-like, two-body, or enclosed-ocean layouts.
    - Large connected land is allowed only if it exposes multiple tectonic
      domains and is balanced by other major landmasses and open ocean basins.
  - Continental elevation:
    - P111.2 reduced broad smooth uplands conservatively, but the goal remains a
      richer continental hypsometry: lowlands, platforms, sedimentary basins,
      passive-margin plains, old orogens, active belts, limited plateaus, and
      foreland basins should all be visible.
  - Boundary and mountain expression:
    - Plate boundaries are still too broad or visually noisy in places.
    - Very high terrain is still too fragmented.  Active or inherited orogens
      should read as coherent range systems and plateau margins, not as many
      small peak clusters.
  - Ocean-floor morphology:
    - Ocean interiors need a stronger hierarchy of abyssal plains, ridges,
      transforms, trenches, fracture zones, seamount chains, oceanic plateaus,
      microcontinents, island arcs, and rare emerged islands.
    - Shallow ocean patches must be either shelves, plateaus, microcontinents,
      volcanic/island-chain swells, or removed/suppressed.
  - Scale:
    - `8000` cells remains the calibration scale.
    - `24000` cells is the final visual-promotion scale for microgeomorphology,
      not the primary tuning loop.
- Acceptance targets to keep stable:
  - Planform:
    - land fraction preferred `0.26-0.31`;
    - major land components preferred `3-6`;
    - largest land component share preferred `0.25-0.45`;
    - largest land share above `0.55` is review/fail unless explicitly
      justified by strong multi-domain and open-ocean evidence;
    - top-two land share should not create a two-body endpoint;
    - major ocean basins preferred `3-5`;
    - closed-ocean-ring score remains below `0.02`.
  - Hypsometry:
    - land mean target `600-900 m`;
    - land median target `300-650 m`;
    - land p90 target `1600-2300 m`;
    - `0-500 m` land share usually at least `0.40`;
    - `>4500 m` land share stays under the P109 upper envelope.
  - Mountains and boundaries:
    - high-mountain fragmentation index `<0.75` for at least two of three
      baseline seeds and no seed above `0.90`;
    - top-3 high-mountain component share should exceed `0.45` when the world
      contains active collision or Andean-style margins;
    - small high-mountain component area share should fall below `0.45`;
    - parent-process overlap should remain above `0.85`;
    - visible boundary masks should become narrower while object/archive
      continuity remains intact.
  - Ocean floor:
    - ridges and trenches must be continuous, narrow, and process-backed;
    - abyssal plains should be broad and quiet;
    - island chains should occur from arcs, seamount chains, hotspot tracks, or
      oceanic plateaus only when elevation and sea level support emergence;
    - unsupported shallow-ocean speckle should remain suppressed.
- Development order from this point:
  - `P111.3 coherent orogen and boundary-legibility repair`
    - Convert parented but fragmented high terrain into coherent orogen belts.
    - Narrow visible boundary expression without breaking boundary objects,
      archive continuity, or terrain parentage.
  - `P111.4 endpoint breakup, seaway, and open-ocean provenance`
    - Reduce the "all seeds feel supercontinent-derived" failure mode by making
      default mature endpoints favor post-breakup multipolar layouts.
    - Strengthen rift-to-ocean, passive-margin, gateway, inherited-seaway, and
      domain-boundary provenance for terminal separations.
    - Prevent large ocean basins from becoming continent-enclosed unless they
      have marginal-sea or Wilson-cycle provenance.
  - `P111.5 ocean-floor hierarchy and island-chain expression`
    - Rebuild visible ocean-floor diversity from ridge, trench, transform,
      age-depth, hotspot, plateau, microcontinent, and arc objects.
    - Restore meaningful complex ocean geography while avoiding the earlier
      shallow-speckle artifact.
  - `P111.6 five-seed and fresh-seed promotion audit`
    - Run a fresh `8000/36p` five-seed audit after P111.3-P111.5.
    - Require P109, P110A, P110B, P111.1, P111.2, high-mountain coherence,
      boundary-legibility, ocean-floor provenance, and visual contact sheets to
      agree before promotion.
  - `P111.7 24000-cell visual promotion`
    - Run `24000/60p` only after the `8000` gates are stable.
    - Use `24000` to check finer coastline, island-chain, mountain-belt,
      fracture-zone, shelf/slope/abyss, and continental-province detail.
- Test and evidence plan:
  - Keep unit fixtures for each repair: planform distribution, hypsometry
    lowering, high-mountain coherence, boundary width/continuity, seaway
    provenance, and ocean-floor object-to-render consistency.
  - Keep generated-world smoke runs at `900` cells for fast sanity checks.
  - Use `8000/36p` fresh seed sweeps as the real calibration gate.
  - Use existing Earth-reference hypsometry and morphology fixtures as
    comparison envelopes, but judge generated worlds as plausible alternatives
    rather than literal Earth reproductions.
  - Archive every promotion run with metrics, contact sheets, and an explicit
    interpretation of what still fails visually.
- Immediate next action:
  - Start `P111.3`.  The first code inspection should trace current
    high-mountain coherence metrics, P108/P111 terrain cleanup, boundary-mask
    rendering, and mountain inventory generation.  The first implementation
    should be scoped to joining process-backed high terrain into range-scale
    belts while preserving lowland/hypsometry gains from P111.2.

2026-06-30 - P111.3 coherent-orogen belt repair first pass archived
- Purpose:
  - Address the P111 baseline failure where high mountains were mostly
    process-backed but still split into many tiny components.
  - Keep the repair object/provenance aware: use production mountain-range ids
    as the authority, lift only existing highland shoulders inside or adjacent
    to those ranges, and preserve the land/ocean mask.
- Implementation:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p111_coherent_orogen_belt_repair`.
  - The pass runs after the P109/P111.2 final hypsometry polishes and before
    final drainage/source-to-sink compilation.
  - It is gated by `terrain.enable_p108_high_mountain_coherence` and
    `terrain.enable_p111_orogen_belt_coherence` and only applies to modern
    Earth-like final states.
  - It excludes plateau inventory from crest stitching so collision plateaus do
    not become narrow artificial peak chains.
  - For each production mountain range, it identifies multiple 3000m+
    components inside the range, orders them along the range axis, and uses
    shortest paths through continental highland shoulders to fill bounded
    crest bridges.
  - Candidate bridge cells must already be land, continental crust, range
    cells or one-cell range shoulders, and generally at least `2200 m` relative
    elevation, with a lower allowance only for cells already inside the
    mountain-range inventory.
  - The pass never changes the land/ocean mask.  If the mask would change, the
    original surface is returned.
  - Added P111.3 observability globals:
    `terrain.last_p1113_bridge_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p1113_high_component_count_before/after`,
    `terrain.last_p1113_top3_high_share_before/after`,
    `terrain.last_p1113_fragmentation_index_before/after`,
    `terrain.last_p1113_range_count_considered`, and
    `terrain.last_p1113_land_mask_preserved`.
  - `p107_audit` now includes these fields inside
    `high_mountain_coherence`, including compact audit summaries.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters`.
  - The fixture constructs a continuous production mountain-range corridor with
    separated 3000m+ peak clusters and 2400m highland shoulders.  Acceptance:
    high-mountain component count falls, high-cell count rises, bridge area is
    recorded, fragmentation index improves, and land/ocean mask is unchanged.
  - Targeted commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_high_mountain_coherence_raises_belts_and_suppresses_isolated_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_post_inventory_cleanup_uses_mountain_range_parentage tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_engine.py::test_p1112_continental_hypsometry_retune_lowers_smooth_interiors_not_mountains -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
- Small generated-world audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_900_seed707_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `70.8 s`; terminal land fraction `0.272`.
  - P109 remained in envelope:
    land mean `856.8 m`, p50 `560.6 m`, p90 `2102.5 m`, max `4865.0 m`.
  - P111.3 effect in that generated world:
    bridge area fraction `0.00111` of world area;
    high-mountain components `6 -> 5`;
    top-3 high-mountain component share `0.785 -> 0.867`;
    high-mountain fragmentation index `0.715 -> 0.633`;
    range systems considered `2`;
    land mask preserved `true`.
  - Follow-up seed sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_900_seed707_20260630 --out out_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_900_seed707_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`.
  - The same 900-cell run is P111.1 review rather than P111.1 candidate because
    largest land share is `0.498`, above the stricter preferred P111 ceiling.
    Treat this as a small-audit caveat, not as an `8000/36p` distribution
    conclusion.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.3 now has a verified mechanism-level repair for the exact "parented
    but fragmented mountain" defect.
  - The generated-world audit shows the pass moving the right metrics without
    breaking P109/P110A/P110B or changing land/ocean topology.
  - This is still a first pass.  It does not yet prove that the `8000/36p`
    three/five-seed baseline satisfies the final high-mountain acceptance
    target; that needs a fresh rendered audit after P111.4/P111.5 or a targeted
    8000-cell promotion run.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Proceed to `P111.4 endpoint breakup, seaway, and open-ocean provenance`.
    The goal is to reduce remaining over-connected/supercontinent-derived
    endpoint feel by strengthening rift-to-ocean, passive-margin,
    gateway/seaway, and large-ocean provenance rather than carving arbitrary
    final channels.

2026-06-30 - P111.4 endpoint-seaway provenance first pass archived
- Purpose:
  - Close a provenance gap in the terminal seaway system.
  - `_open_modern_earthlike_seaways` could split an over-connected mature
    Earth-like endpoint, but the resulting terminal seaway was not recorded as
    a first-class object and was not protected by the existing seaway
    preservation mask.
  - This made some maps visually improved but weakly auditable: the terrain
    changed, while later diagnostics could not distinguish a process-backed
    endpoint seaway from an anonymous final mask edit.
- Implementation:
  - Added production objects under
    `terrain.p1114_modern_endpoint_seaways` for final modern endpoint seaways
    created by `_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish`.
  - Each object records:
    `kind="p1114_modern_endpoint_seaway"`, `basis`, parent continent ids,
    target component rank, P110B archetype code, cut cells, area fraction,
    largest-share before/after, domain-boundary fraction, margin/suture
    fraction, weak-lowland fraction, and provenance string.
  - Basis classification is deliberately conservative:
    `continent_domain_boundary` when the cut follows a major terminal domain
    boundary, `rift_suture_or_margin_weak_zone` when it follows margin/suture
    weak crust, otherwise `weak_lowland_bridge`.
  - `_breakup_seaway_protection_mask` now protects
    `terrain.p1114_modern_endpoint_seaways` as well as breakup and closed-ocean
    seaways.
  - `_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish` resets P111.4 endpoint objects at the
    start of each final polish so multi-frame audit runs do not accumulate
    stale endpoint objects from previous frames.
  - P111.4 objects are only recorded during final modern endpoint polish
    (`balance_underpartitioned=True`), not during earlier historical/intermediate
    calls to `_open_modern_earthlike_seaways`.
  - Fixed `terrain.last_modern_seaway_applied` so it remains true when any
    final-polish pass successfully opened a seaway, even if a later attempted
    pass found no further candidate.
  - `p107_audit` now exposes P111.4 counts, area fraction, domain-backed count,
    weak-bridge count, and top object rows in `seaway_cut_effectiveness`.
  - `p110b_seed_sweep` now extracts P111.4 endpoint-seaway counts and treats
    them as valid seaway provenance for the P111 overlarge-landmass provenance
    review flag.
- Tests:
  - Extended
    `test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent` to
    require a P111.4 endpoint-seaway object and verify that the protection mask
    contains the opened seaway cells.
  - Extended
    `test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_uses_continent_domain_boundary` to
    require `basis="continent_domain_boundary"` for a domain-boundary split.
  - Added
    `test_p111_seed_sweep_accepts_p1114_endpoint_seaway_provenance`, proving
    that a P111.4 endpoint seaway prevents the stricter P111 sweep from
    reporting `p111_overlarge_landmass_lacks_internal_seaway_provenance`.
  - Targeted commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_uses_continent_domain_boundary tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_default_allows_six_passes tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `13 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `11 passed`.
- Small generated-world audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p1114_endpoint_seaway_provenance_900_seed707_v3_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `71.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.290`.
  - P109 remained in envelope:
    land mean `872.5 m`, p50 `556.3 m`, p90 `2481.8 m`, max `4541.9 m`.
  - Terminal planform:
    major land components `6`; largest/second/third shares
    `0.494 / 0.280 / 0.103`; major ocean basins `3`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - P111.4 seaway result:
    final seaway polish passes `1`;
    polish area fraction `0.0222`;
    largest share before/after `0.905 -> 0.533`;
    `modern_applied=true`;
    P111.4 endpoint-seaway count `1`;
    P111.4 endpoint-seaway area fraction `0.0278`;
    object basis `rift_suture_or_margin_weak_zone`;
    margin/suture fraction `1.0`;
    weak-lowland fraction `1.0`.
  - P111.3 remained active in the same audit:
    high-mountain components `8 -> 5`;
    high-mountain fragmentation index `0.833 -> 0.420`.
  - Follow-up seed sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p1114_endpoint_seaway_provenance_900_seed707_v3_20260630 --out out_p1114_endpoint_seaway_provenance_900_seed707_v3_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`.
  - P111.1 remains review on this 900-cell member because largest land share
    `0.494` is above the stricter preferred P111 maximum `0.45`.
    This is acceptable as a small-audit caveat; it means P111.4 is not yet a
    finished distribution fix.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.4 now has first-class terminal seaway provenance for modern endpoint
    polish, so future audits can tell whether an over-connected endpoint was
    separated by a recorded domain/weak-zone seaway.
  - The repair does not claim that all supercontinent-derived endpoint layouts
    are solved.  It strengthens provenance and protection for cuts that already
    happen, and gives P111.4.2 a better diagnostic substrate.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Continue with `P111.4.2 generated-world endpoint distribution and
    domain-backed seaway tuning`.
  - Focus on fresh `8000/36p` evidence and improve the rate at which default
    mature endpoints land below the P111 largest-land preferred ceiling without
    adding arbitrary final cuts.

2026-06-30 - P111.4.2 strict endpoint-seaway tuning archived
- Purpose:
  - Close the specific gap left by P111.4 first pass: a generated world could
    pass P109/P110A/P110B while still sitting just above the stricter P111
    preferred largest-land ceiling (`0.45`).
  - Avoid solving that by arbitrary final cuts.  The extra cut is only allowed
    when the terminal landmass is still overpreferred, the coarse P110B gates
    are otherwise satisfied, and the candidate seaway follows domain-boundary,
    suture/margin, or weak-lowland provenance.
- Implementation:
  - Extended `_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish` so it does not stop solely
    because the P110B soft ceiling is satisfied.  It can request a P111 strict
    endpoint pass when:
    largest land share is above the P111 preferred maximum, below the coarse
    overlarge band, major-component counts are already plausible, and
    `terrain.enable_p111_strict_endpoint_polish` is enabled.
  - Extended `_open_modern_earthlike_seaways` with
    `p111_strict_preferred`.
  - Added a strict-provenance filter for candidate cuts:
    continent-domain boundary fraction, rift/suture/margin fraction, or
    weak-lowland/narrow-neck fraction must be high enough.
  - Added an anti-overfragmentation guard:
    strict endpoint cuts are rejected when the resulting largest land component
    would fall below `terrain.p111_strict_endpoint_min_largest_after`
    (default `0.30`).  This prevents the repair from turning one overlarge
    landmass into overly small peer fragments just to satisfy the upper bound.
  - Added P111.4.2 observability in `seaway_cut_effectiveness`:
    strict attempted/applied, strict cut area, preferred ceiling, minimum
    largest-after guard, and largest-share before/after.
  - P111.4 endpoint-seaway objects now record whether the cut was a strict
    P111 preferred pass and the strict largest-share thresholds used.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop`.
  - The fixture constructs a modern Earth-like endpoint that the old P110B
    stop condition would leave alone: largest land share is above `0.45` but
    below the P110B soft ceiling, and major/nonlargest component counts are
    already plausible.  A suture-backed low bridge is then opened by strict
    polish, lowering the largest component into the P111 preferred range while
    keeping it above the anti-overfragmentation floor.
  - Targeted commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_uses_continent_domain_boundary tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_default_allows_six_passes tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py::test_p111_seed_sweep_accepts_p1114_endpoint_seaway_provenance -q`
    -> `5 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `10 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_uses_continent_domain_boundary tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_default_allows_six_passes -q`
    -> `5 passed`.
- Small generated-world audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11142_strict_endpoint_polish_900_seed707_v2_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `100.4 s`; terminal land fraction `0.267`.
  - P109 remained in envelope:
    land mean `811.6 m`, p50 `558.3 m`, p90 `2398.5 m`, max `4541.4 m`.
  - Terminal planform after strict polish:
    major land components `6`; largest/second/third shares
    `0.433 / 0.275 / 0.158`; major ocean basins `3`;
    closed-ocean ring score `0.006`; terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - P111.4.2 strict result:
    strict attempted/applied `true/true`;
    strict cut area fraction `0.0067`;
    strict largest-share before/after `0.490 -> 0.439`;
    P111.4 endpoint-seaway count `4`, all recorded as
    `rift_suture_or_margin_weak_zone` in this audit.
  - Follow-up seed sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11142_strict_endpoint_polish_900_seed707_v2_20260630 --out out_p11142_strict_endpoint_polish_900_seed707_v2_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`,
    P111 modern-planform candidates `1/1`, P111 distribution flags `none`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.4.2 is a conservative generated-world endpoint repair, not a general
    continent-shape model.  It fixes the "P110B stops too early" case when a
    weak-zone seaway can make a geologically justified final separation.
  - The small audit proves the 900-cell seed-707 regression that was previously
    P111 review now passes the P111 planform sweep without losing P109
    hypsometry.
  - This does not yet prove the full `8000/36p` fresh-seed distribution.  That
    remains part of the P111 promotion audit after P111.5.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Proceed to `P111.5 ocean-floor hierarchy and island-chain expression`.
  - Focus on restoring realistic ocean-floor diversity from ridge, trench,
    transform, abyssal-plain, seamount-chain, hotspot, plateau,
    microcontinent, and arc provenance while keeping unsupported shallow-ocean
    speckle suppressed.

2026-06-30 - P111.5 ocean-floor hierarchy and island-chain expression first pass archived
- Purpose:
  - Restore ocean-floor diversity without reintroducing the old shallow-ocean
    speckle problem.
  - Previous suppression made unsupported open-ocean shoals less visible, but
    it also risked flattening object-backed features such as hotspot chains,
    plateaus, microcontinents, arcs, and trench/ridge-related relief.
  - The first pass deliberately runs after terminal planform shaping so ocean
    relief expression does not feed back into sea-level solving, seaway cuts,
    or continental component topology.
- Implementation:
  - Added a final post-planform ocean-floor expression pass:
    `TerrainModule._p1115_final_ocean_floor_hierarchy_expression`.
  - The pass is gated by `terrain.enable_p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy` and only
    applies to mature Earth-like target-land-fraction worlds near the modern
    endpoint.
  - It computes ridge, trench, transform/fracture, coast, open-ocean, and
    abyssal context from the final land/ocean mask, applies coherent
    ocean-floor fabric with `p1115_object_relief=True`, then runs unsupported
    open-ocean shoal cleanup.
  - Early regional ocean-floor passes keep the default path so object uplift
    cannot change the land/ocean mask before planform gates finish.
  - Object-backed shoal preservation is now opt-in during normal cleanup and
    automatically active only inside the P111.5 final pass.
  - Microcontinent relief preservation no longer treats broad `continent_id`
    assignment alone as sufficient provenance; it now requires stronger
    terrane, accreted-terrane, arc, plume, or LIP backing.
  - The final pass preserves trenches and pre-existing very deep cells, restores
    original land, prevents original ocean from emerging, and records whether
    the land/ocean mask was preserved.
  - Added P111.5 terrain globals:
    `terrain.last_p1115_ocean_floor_adjusted_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p1115_ocean_floor_land_mask_preserved`,
    `terrain.last_p1115_unsupported_open_ocean_shoal_area_fraction`,
    `terrain.last_p1115_object_backed_open_ocean_shoal_area_fraction`, and
    `terrain.last_p1115_unsupported_open_ocean_shoal_deepened_fraction`.
  - `p107_audit` now exposes P111.5 ocean metrics, including unsupported and
    object-backed open-ocean shoal fractions, ridge/transform/trench area,
    abyssal plain/hill area, seamount chain, oceanic plateau, microcontinent,
    island-arc area, final adjusted area, and land-mask preservation.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief`.
    The fixture places two shallow far-open-ocean cells, one plume/LIP-backed
    and one unsupported.  With P111.5 preservation enabled, the object-backed
    cell stays shallow while the unsupported cell is deepened.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask`.
    The fixture verifies that the final pass can raise object-backed oceanic
    plateau relief while keeping the original land/ocean mask unchanged and
    preserving existing deep ocean.
  - Extended the P107 terminal audit regression so required P111.5 metrics are
    present in terminal metrics, `ocean_feature_metrics`, and the acceptance
    summary.
  - Targeted command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
- Small generated-world audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy_900_seed707_v5_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `99.3 s`; terminal land fraction `0.280`.
  - Terminal planform:
    major land components `4`; largest/second/third shares
    `0.441 / 0.246 / 0.238`; major ocean basins `3`;
    largest ocean basin share `0.417`; closed-ocean ring score `0.0046`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy_900_seed707_v5_20260630 --out out_p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy_900_seed707_v5_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Ocean-floor result:
    parented oceanic island-chain count `8`;
    deep trench fraction below `6000 m` is `0.0046`;
    deep non-trench fraction below `6000 m` is `0.0`;
    unsupported open-ocean shoal fraction `0.0185`;
    object-backed open-ocean shoal fraction `0.1296`;
    final P111.5 land/ocean mask preserved `true`.
  - Rendered review assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy_900_seed707_v5_render_20260630/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.5 now has a verified first-pass mechanism for separating unsupported
    open-ocean shallow speckle from process-backed ocean-floor relief.
  - The accepted 900-cell seed-707 audit keeps the improved P111 modern
    planform while reintroducing visible ocean-floor hierarchy and preserving
    land/ocean topology.
  - This is not yet the final ocean-floor solution.  The 900-cell audit still
    shows object-backed ocean-relief metrics that are too broad
    (`p1115_object_backed_ocean_relief_fraction` around `0.958`) and emerged
    object-backed island metrics that likely overcount land-adjacent object
    masks.  Abyssal plain/hill categorization is also weak in this small run.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Proceed to `P111.5.2 ocean-object mask tightening and abyssal hierarchy`.
  - Focus on narrowing object-backed masks to real object cores and immediate
    structural halos, separating abyssal plains from abyssal hills with basin
    age/distance context, and reducing unsupported open-ocean shoals without
    erasing legitimate island chains, oceanic plateaus, microcontinents, or
    trench/ridge relief.

2026-07-01 - P111.5.2 ocean-object mask tightening and abyssal hierarchy first pass archived
- Purpose:
  - Close the largest residual from P111.5 first pass: ocean-object masks were
    too broad, so diagnostics could treat most of the ocean as object-backed
    relief.
  - Preserve legitimate ocean-floor diversity while making object masks mean
    "process core or tight structural halo" rather than an entire wide
    province.
  - Restore a visible abyssal-plain class in low-resolution generated worlds
    without calling all deep open ocean a plain.
- Implementation:
  - `_ocean_fabric_objects` now thins broad ridge provinces into spreading
    center axes using young-crust age structure and a spacing-based fallback.
    A generated ridge object no longer defaults to the whole
    `OCEAN_DEPTH_RIDGE` province.
  - `spreading_center` axes get an additional area cap/thinning pass when the
    candidate still covers an excessive share of ocean.
  - `_arc_plume_landform_objects` now narrows island-arc objects to active-core
    linework and tight halos; broad active-margin shallow belts are not all
    classified as `island_arc`.
  - Seamount-chain objects are thinned into sparse linear chains before object
    export.
  - Microcontinent objects no longer accept broad `continent_id` alone.  They
    require terrane/accreted-terrane/arc provenance, or a rifted continental
    fragment with elevated rift potential in an ocean province.
  - Abyssal-plain generation now has two fallbacks:
    relative old/deep/sediment-supported abyss cells inside a generated basin,
    and a low-resolution relative abyss selector when absolute old-ocean ages
    are unavailable in the current terminal field.
  - `p107_audit` now separates:
    `p1115_object_backed_ocean_relief_fraction` for object cores and broad
    structural cores, and
    `p1115_object_backed_ocean_support_fraction` for the one-cell support halo
    used only to decide whether a shallow open-ocean cell is process-backed.
  - The P111.5 emerged-island metric now counts only small object-dominated
    land components with plume/LIP/arc/terrane/accreted provenance, instead of
    every large land cell covered by an arc or plateau mask.
  - Added `p1115_ridge_province_area_fraction_ocean` so audits can compare
    ridge province width against actual ridge-core object width.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed`.
    The fixture proves a broad ridge province is not enough to make shallow
    open ocean object-backed in P111.5 metrics.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis`.
    The fixture constructs a wide ridge province with a young central axis and
    verifies exported `spreading_center` area is substantially narrower than
    the province.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain`.
    The fixture verifies old deep ocean can still produce an `abyssal_plain`
    object when the strict sediment threshold would otherwise suppress it.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id`.
    The fixture proves `continent_id` alone does not create open-ocean
    microcontinents, while terrane provenance still does.
  - Targeted commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask -q`
    -> `6 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id -q`
    -> `7 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `10 passed`.
- Small generated-world audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11152_ocean_object_tightening_900_seed707_v5_20260630 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `108.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.280`.
  - Terminal planform stayed unchanged from the accepted P111.5 seed-707
    reference:
    major land components `4`; largest/second/third shares
    `0.441 / 0.246 / 0.238`; major ocean basins `3`;
    largest ocean basin share `0.417`; closed-ocean ring score `0.0046`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11152_ocean_object_tightening_900_seed707_v5_20260630 --out out_p11152_ocean_object_tightening_900_seed707_v5_sweep_20260630 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Ocean-floor metric movement relative to P111.5 first pass:
    object-backed ocean-relief core fraction `0.958 -> 0.585`;
    emerged object-backed island fraction `0.232 -> 0.0033`;
    ridge-core area fraction `0.385 -> 0.193`;
    island-arc area fraction `0.282 -> 0.139`;
    microcontinent area fraction `0.057 -> 0.037`;
    abyssal-plain area fraction `0.000 -> 0.023`;
    abyssal-hill area fraction `0.0108 -> 0.0170`.
  - Residuals:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal fraction rose from `0.0185` to `0.0339`
    because fewer shallow cells are now allowed to hide behind over-broad
    object masks.  This is diagnostically healthier but still needs a broader
    8000-cell distribution check.
    The support halo remains broad in the 900-cell audit (`0.929`), mostly
    because one-cell halos are coarse at this resolution.  This should be
    judged again at 8000 cells before further tightening.
  - Rendered review assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11152_ocean_object_tightening_900_seed707_v5_render_20260630/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.5.2 fixes the biggest audit-quality bug in the ocean-floor hierarchy:
    broad provinces no longer masquerade as precise object-backed relief.
  - The accepted 900-cell audit keeps the P111 modern-planform candidate while
    improving object specificity and restoring a low-resolution abyssal-plain
    class.
  - This is still not a final promotion.  It is a mechanism proof and small
    generated-world check.  The next required evidence is a fresh `8000/36p`
    audit to see whether object cores, support halos, abyssal plain/hill
    fractions, and unsupported shoal rates remain plausible at calibration
    resolution.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Run an `8000/36p` P111 ocean-floor promotion audit after P111.5.2.
  - If it passes P109/P110A/P110B/P111 and ocean-floor metrics, proceed to
    higher-resolution visual review.
  - If it fails, tune only the specific distribution residual shown by the
    audit: likely support-halo width, unsupported shoal cleanup, abyssal
    plain/hill balance, or seamount/island-arc object density.

2026-07-01 - P111.5.3 preterminal/final volcanism split and object-core preservation archived
- Purpose:
  - Fix the 8000-cell residual where final ocean-floor cleanup either preserved
    too much shallow open-ocean relief, or, after over-tightening
    `volcanism_age`, degraded the terminal planform gates.
  - Separate two roles that had been conflated:
    preterminal ocean-floor fabric may use broad volcanic history as a process
    input during sea-level and basin formation, but the final P111.5
    mask-preserving ocean-floor expression must not treat broad
    `volcanism_age >= 0` as object-backed shallow relief.
  - Preserve object cores without preserving a one-cell halo as shallow
    open-ocean bathymetry.
- Implementation:
  - `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals` now preserves only
    arc/plume/LIP/microcontinent object cores and provenance-backed cells.  It
    no longer treats `terrain.ocean_fabric` ridge/transform/fracture masks as
    a reason to keep shallow open-ocean shoals, and it no longer expands the
    preserved object mask by a generic one-cell halo.
  - `_apply_coherent_ocean_floor_fabric` now has a split volcanism policy:
    non-final/preterminal fabric can still respond to broad
    `volcanism_age >= 0` so planform formation remains stable, while
    `p1115_object_relief=True` only uses explicit volcano/plume/plume-impact
    sources or recent plume-proximal volcanism.
  - Final seamount/hotspot expression is narrower and deeper:
    P111.5 object-relief uses a sparser hotspot axis, a tighter chain band,
    lower uplift, and a deeper seamount-chain cap so hotspot tracks read as
    submarine relief rather than broad shallow platforms.
  - `_arc_plume_landform_objects` now exports hotspot-track objects through a
    narrowed track axis instead of exposing the full plume-influence patch as a
    seamount-chain object.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo`.
    The fixture proves a LIP/plume-impact object core is preserved while an
    adjacent non-object shallow open-ocean cell is deepened.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric`.
    The fixture proves broad volcanism can shape preterminal ocean fabric but
    does not create final P111.5 object-backed relief by itself.
  - Regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `20 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `124.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.268`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_900_seed707_20260701 --out out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_900_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Ocean-floor metrics: open-ocean shoal `0.0243`, unsupported shoal
    `0.0076`, object-backed shoal `0.0167`, seamount-chain area `0.0683`,
    final land/ocean mask preserved `true`.
- 8000-cell calibration audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `1177.5 s`; terminal land fraction `0.265`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Rendered review assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_8000_seed707_render_20260701/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`.
    Key single-panel assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_8000_seed707_render_20260701/elevation.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11153_preterminal_volcanism_split_8000_seed707_render_20260701/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`.
  - Planform metrics: major land components `5`;
    largest/second/third shares `0.303 / 0.221 / 0.165`;
    major ocean basins `3`; largest ocean basin share `0.394`;
    closed-ocean ring score `0.0245`; terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - Ocean-floor metrics: open-ocean shoal `0.0745`, unsupported shoal `0.0429`,
    object-backed open-ocean shoal `0.0316`,
    object-backed ocean-relief core `0.2378`, support halo `0.4685`,
    seamount-chain area `0.0158`, abyssal plain `0.0616`,
    abyssal hill `0.2009`, ocean p50 depth `2600 m`, ocean p90 depth
    `3471 m`, final land/ocean mask preserved `true`.
  - 8000-cell comparison:
    - P111.5.2 broad-volcanism planform: largest land `0.303`,
      closed-ocean score `0.0245`, open-ocean shoal `0.3654`,
      seamount-chain area `0.0943`.
    - Over-tightened v2: largest land `0.486`, closed-ocean score `0.1103`,
      open-ocean shoal `0.2541`, seamount-chain area `0.1967`;
      failed P110B/P111 seed-sweep review.
    - P111.5.3 split: largest land `0.303`, closed-ocean score `0.0245`,
      open-ocean shoal `0.0745`, seamount-chain area `0.0158`;
      passes P110B/P111 seed-sweep review.
- Checklist status after P111.5.3:
  - Improved: open-ocean shallow speckle and broad seamount/hotspot patches are
    substantially reduced; planform gates are restored; object-backed relief is
    much closer to real object cores.
  - Still incomplete:
    mid-ocean ridges are present but not yet visually dominant as continuous
    ridge systems; trenches are present but need stricter attachment to
    subduction boundaries; boundary output still reads as scattered pixels and
    short segments rather than stable line objects; mountain belts still need a
    main-crest/tributary/foreland hierarchy; continental interiors still need a
    stronger plateau/basin/plain/craton hierarchy.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Start the release-checklist pass with boundary and oceanic-crust structure:
    make boundary outputs line-object centric, make ridge/trench bathymetry
    more legible and attached to boundary processes, and add explicit
    age-away-from-ridge diagnostics before another broad terrain retune.

2026-07-01 - P111.6 boundary/oceanic-crust structure observability and ridge expression first pass archived
- Purpose:
  - Convert the new release checklist into auditable metrics for boundary
    linework, trench attachment, age-controlled oceanic bathymetry, and
    age-away-from-ridge symmetry.
  - Make mid-ocean ridges more legible in final bathymetry without changing the
    terminal land/ocean mask or regressing P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates.
  - Explicitly separate a safe default pass from an experimental age-depth
    hardening pass.  A too-strong default age-depth floor failed P109 at
    `900/36p`, so it is kept behind an opt-in global until the crust-age
    generation layer is redesigned.
- Implementation:
  - `p107_audit` now writes `p1116_boundary_linework`:
    ridge, transform, trench, convergent, and collision/suture linework stats
    include component count, largest/top-3 component share, isolated-cell
    fraction, endpoint fraction, branch fraction, same-kind neighbor count, and
    a line-coherence score.
  - `p107_audit` now writes `p1116_oceanic_crust_structure`:
    age-depth correlation, age-distance-from-ridge correlation,
    old-minus-young median depth, far-minus-near ridge median age,
    young-near-ridge fraction, old-far-from-ridge fraction,
    nonridge-minus-ridge median depth, and trench-minus-nontrench median depth.
    The age-depth sample is restricted to abyss/ridge oceanic basin provinces,
    so shelves, slopes, restricted seas, and trenches do not dominate the
    diagnostic.
  - `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals` now lets the final P111.5
    open-ocean ridge cleanup keep ridge highs near `2200 m` rather than
    forcing them to `2600 m`.  Preterminal cleanup keeps the old `2600 m`
    guard.
  - `_apply_coherent_ocean_floor_fabric` gives final P111.5 ridge fabric a
    shallower target/cap so ridge systems read more clearly in bathymetry.
  - Added optional `terrain.enable_p1116_age_depth_final_expression`; when
    explicitly enabled it deepens old oceanic crust more strongly in the final
    fabric pass, but it is default-off because the first generated-world
    experiment failed P109 at 900 cells.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure`.
    The fixture builds a ridge, bilateral age field, and attached subduction
    trench, then verifies the new metrics report linework, trench attachment,
    age-depth coupling, age-distance coupling, ridge shallowing, and trench
    deepening.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_open_ridge_cleanup_keeps_legible_mid_ocean_high`.
    The fixture proves final P111.5 ridge cleanup keeps a ridge high around
    `2200 m`, while the preterminal cleanup still enforces the older `2600 m`
    floor.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_ocean_fabric_deepens_old_oceanic_crust`.
    The fixture covers the opt-in experimental age-depth final expression.

- 900-cell generated-world check:

    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111

    ridge line-coherence score `0.754`; trench line-coherence score `0.0178`;
    trench-subduction attachment `0.902`; deep-trench attachment `1.0`;
    nonridge-minus-ridge median depth `360 m`.
  - The failed experiment
    `out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_900_seed707_v2_20260701` is retained as
    negative evidence: enabling a hard final age-depth floor pushed P109 out of
