    crust-age/ocean-basin generation rather than being applied as a terminal
    depth clamp.
- 8000-cell calibration audit:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `877.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.265`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Rendered review assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_8000_seed707_render_20260701/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_8000_seed707_render_20260701/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p1116_boundary_crust_structure_8000_seed707_render_20260701/tectonic_boundaries.png`.
  - Gate stability:
    land fraction `0.265`; largest/second/third land shares
    `0.303 / 0.221 / 0.165`; closed-ocean ring score `0.0245`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - Ocean-floor metrics:
    open-ocean shoal `0.1500`, object-backed shoal `0.1070`,
    unsupported shoal `0.0430`, ridge area `0.1123`, seamount area `0.0158`.
    Relative to P111.5.3, the object-backed shoal increase is expected from
    making ridges shallower; unsupported shoal stays essentially unchanged.
  - P111.6 oceanic-crust metrics:
    age-depth correlation `0.306`, age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.928`,
    old-minus-young median depth `696 m`, far-minus-near ridge median age
    `53.4 Myr`, young-near-ridge fraction `0.991`, old-far-from-ridge fraction
    `0.970`, ridge median depth `2335 m`, nonridge-minus-ridge median depth
    `809 m`.
  - P111.6 boundary-linework metrics:
    ridge line-coherence score `0.331`, ridge component count `15`, ridge top-3
    component share `0.569`; trench line-coherence score `0.015`, trench
    component count `165`, trench isolated-cell fraction `0.774`;
    trench-subduction attachment `0.923`, deep-trench attachment `1.0`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6 first pass succeeds as observability and ridge-expression work:
    it adds release-checklist metrics, keeps all existing gates green, improves
    ridge bathymetric visibility, and confirms the age/ridge-distance signal is
    already present at 8000 cells.
  - It does not solve boundary line-object generation.  The new metrics prove
    the most serious remaining blocker is trench/boundary continuity:
    trenches are mostly isolated or tiny components, even though many are near
    subduction context.  This matches the visual `tectonic_boundaries.png`.
  - Strong age-depth correction should not be terminal terrain polish.  It must
    be implemented upstream in crust-age generation and ocean-basin spreading
    geometry so it does not disturb land hypsometry gates.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.2 should build/derive boundary line objects from current boundary
    pixels and process context, starting with trench/subduction continuity and
    then ridge/transform polyline simplification.  Required targets:
    reduce trench isolated-cell fraction, reduce trench component count, raise
    trench line-coherence score, keep trench-subduction attachment high, and
    preserve P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.2 trench/subduction boundary line-object pass archived

- Objective:
  - Convert the worst trench residual from raw sampled boundary pixels into a
    stable semantic line object without disturbing the accepted modern planform,
    land hypsometry, ridge expression, or ocean-crust age diagnostics.
  - Keep raw boundary telemetry visible so upstream boundary sampling quality is
    not hidden by the new object layer.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_coherent_subduction_trench_axis` in `terrain.py`.  It derives a
    narrow trench-axis candidate from raw trench boundary cells and trench
    boundary provinces, using subduction and active-margin cells only as nearby
    support for short gap bridging.  It explicitly avoids using broad
    `tectonics.closing_margins` objects as trench-axis anchors.
  - Added `_bridge_nearby_line_cells` to fill one- and two-cell gaps between
    nearby sampled trench anchors, followed by a non-anchor width peel so
    bridge cells do not turn the object into a broad raster patch.
  - `terrain.margin_landforms` now emits trench objects from the derived trench
    axis rather than requiring the current elevation/depth-province pass to
    already contain very deep trench cells.  This removes the previous circular
    dependency where a trench had to be bathymetrically deep before it could be
    a trench object.
  - `terrain.ocean_fabric` uses the derived trench axis only as an avoidance
    mask so ocean-floor fabric does not paint through trench objects.
  - The new axis is deliberately not yet wired back into `ocean.depth_province`
    or final bathymetry.  Early v2-v4 experiments did that and either made the
    trench province too broad or shifted the planform/land gate.  P111.6.3 must
    reintroduce bathymetric expression with a narrower, explicitly gated
    terrain pass.
  - `p107_audit` linework schema is now
    `aevum.p1116_boundary_linework.v2`.  Main trench linework prefers
    `terrain.margin_landforms:trench`, falls back to `ocean.depth_province ==
    trench`, then to raw boundary pixels.  The report also keeps
    `raw_trench_boundary`, `object_trench`, and `depth_trench_province`
    side-by-side.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line`.
    The fixture creates a sampled trench/subduction belt and verifies the new
    helper increases connected line cells while reducing component count and
    isolated-cell fraction.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter`.
    The fixture proves audit v2 uses the object trench as the main trench
    linework layer while preserving raw scattered trench telemetry.
  - Focused regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - Broader focused suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_open_ridge_cleanup_keeps_legible_mid_ocean_high tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_ocean_fabric_deepens_old_oceanic_crust tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `23 passed`.  The combined focused set is `26 passed`.
- Negative experiments kept as evidence:
  - `out_p11162_trench_line_objects_900_seed707_20260701` and v2/v3/v4 show
    why the object line must not yet feed terminal bathymetry directly:
    broad closing-margin support or trench aprons can push `ocean.depth_province
    == trench` to unrealistic areas, disturb land/planform gates, or convert the
    largest land component into a P110B review case.
  - The accepted v5 keeps P111.6.2 as an object/audit-layer fix and leaves
    bathymetric trench expression for P111.6.3.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11162_trench_line_objects_900_seed707_v5_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `88.2 s`; terminal land fraction `0.268`.
  - Follow-up sweep:

    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111

  - P111.6.2 900-cell trench signals:

    line-coherence score `0.0178`; object trench component count `16`, object
    isolated-cell fraction `0.0`, object line-coherence score `0.362`, object
    trench-subduction attachment `0.982`.
  - Depth caveat: `depth_trench_province` is empty at 900 in the accepted v5,
    and trench median depth is not yet a useful bathymetric signal.  This is an

- 8000-cell calibration audit:

    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `823.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.265`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Rendered review assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_render_20260701/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_render_20260701/tectonic_boundaries.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_render_20260701/object_masks.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11162_trench_line_objects_8000_seed707_render_20260701/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`.
  - Gate stability:
    land fraction `0.265`; largest/second/third land shares
    `0.303 / 0.221 / 0.165`; P109/P110A/P110B/P111 all green.
  - P111.6.2 8000-cell trench signals:
    raw trench component count `166`, raw isolated-cell fraction `0.783`, raw
    line-coherence score `0.013`; object trench component count `49`, object
    isolated-cell fraction `0.0`, object line-coherence score `0.118`, object
    trench-subduction attachment `0.963`.
  - Preserved P111.6 oceanic-crust signals:
    age-depth correlation `0.307`, age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.928`,
    old-minus-young median depth `696 m`, far-minus-near ridge median age
    `53.6 Myr`, young-near-ridge fraction `0.988`, old-far-from-ridge fraction
    `0.969`, nonridge-minus-ridge median depth `809 m`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.2 succeeds as an object-layer repair: raw trench pixels are still
    available as evidence, but the main checklist layer now has non-isolated
    trench line objects attached to subduction context while preserving all
    existing gate stability.
  - P111.6.2 does not close the physical trench checklist.  The bathymetry still
    lacks stable, narrow, deep troughs along those line objects; the 8000
    bathymetry render confirms this visually, and `trench_minus_nontrench`
    remains negative because trench objects are not yet driving depth.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.3 should attach `terrain.margin_landforms:trench` line objects back
    into ocean-floor depth expression with a narrow axis/apron model:
    axis-only `OCEAN_DEPTH_TRENCH`, shallow forearc/slope apron outside the
    trench province, explicit max-area guards, and P109/P110A/P110B/P111
    preservation checks at 900 and 8000 cells.  Success requires visible
    bathymetric trenches, positive trench-minus-nontrench depth contrast, and no
    return of broad trench provinces or planform regressions.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.3 final trench bathymetry expression archived

- Objective:
  - Close the physical trench checklist gap left by P111.6.2: trench line
    objects should not only exist in `terrain.margin_landforms`, they should
    become narrow, deep bathymetric troughs and a stable final
    `ocean.depth_province == trench` layer.
  - Preserve the accepted P109/P110A/P110B/P111 planform gates and avoid the
    earlier failure mode where trench support masks widened into broad abyssal
    provinces.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p11163_final_trench_axis` in `terrain.py`.  It derives a terminal
    trench axis from `terrain.margin_landforms:trench`, filters it through
    coherent open-ocean deep-zone context, uses short line bridging only where
    needed, peels non-line-like cells, and enforces a maximum axis area.
  - Added `_p11163_apply_final_trench_bathymetry`.  The pass is terminal-only
    and default-on through `terrain.enable_p11163_trench_bathymetry_expression`;
    it deepens the trench axis to a narrow `5600 m` target, preserves adjacent
    slope/apron expression without turning the apron into trench province, and
    caps apron area against both world/ocean area and axis-area ratios.
  - Added `_p11163_mark_final_trench_depth_province`.  It marks only final,
    sufficiently deep axis cells as `OCEAN_DEPTH_TRENCH` and active margin,
    preserving land and leaving shelves/slopes/ridges to their existing
    provinces.
  - Wired the terminal ocean-floor hierarchy so `margin_landforms` can feed the
    final bathymetry pass and the final ocean geography field update.  The pass
    is deliberately not active during nonterminal frames, because preterminal
    terrain feedback can alter later planform evolution.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province`.
    The fixture builds a synthetic open-ocean trench object, verifies land-mask
    preservation, checks that the trench axis becomes deep, asserts the axis
    remains under the area guard, and confirms final depth-province marking.
  - Focused regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure -q`
    -> `5 passed`.
  - Broader focused suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_open_ridge_cleanup_keeps_legible_mid_ocean_high tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_ocean_fabric_deepens_old_oceanic_crust tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `21 passed`.  The combined P111.6.3 focused coverage is `26 passed`.

    `0.928`, old-minus-young median depth `702 m`, far-minus-near ridge median
    age `53.6 Myr`, young-near-ridge fraction `0.988`, old-far-from-ridge
    fraction `0.969`, ridge median depth `2335 m`, nonridge-minus-ridge median
    depth `809 m`, trench median depth `5000 m`, nontrench ocean median depth
    `2442 m`, trench-minus-nontrench median depth `+2558 m`.
  - Ocean-floor object signals preserved:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal fraction `0.0425`, object-backed open-ocean
    shoal fraction `0.1058`, object-backed ocean relief fraction `0.2536`,
    emerged object-backed island fraction of world `0.00038`, and final
    ocean-floor land-mask preservation `true`.
- Visual interpretation:
  - `p107_array_contact_sheet.png` shows a plausible modern multi-landmass
    planform, readable ridge/age structure, visible deep trench bands, and
    controlled unsupported ocean shoals.
  - `bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png` now shows narrow dark troughs instead
    of only diffuse abyssal texture.  The troughs are attached to object-layer
    trench context, but their geometry still inherits cell-level fragmentation.
  - `ocean_depth_provinces.png` confirms the final trench province is no longer
    empty and no longer a broad province blanket.  Some shelf/ridge edges still
    show comb-like artifacts, which should be handled by boundary and province
    line simplification rather than more depth clamping.
  - `object_masks.png` confirms semantic coverage is broad, but ridge,
    fracture-zone, trench, and island-arc objects still read as rasterized
    cell chains rather than clean polylines.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.3 closes checklist item 2 at the current calibration scale:
    trenches now attach to subduction context and become stable, narrow, deep
    bathymetric troughs without broadening the trench province or regressing
    planform gates.
  - It does not close checklist item 7.  The same audit proves the next
    bottleneck is geometric continuity: object trench and depth trench province
    still have `49` components at 8000 cells, and raw boundary pixels remain
    extremely fragmented.
  - The correct next work is not another terminal depth polish.  It is
    polyline-style boundary/province generation: simplify ridge-transform
    networks, convert convergent arcs into coherent line objects before terrain
    response, and then let mountain-belt hierarchy consume those line objects.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.4 should target boundary/province line simplification and coherence.
    Required targets: lower trench/ridge/convergent component counts, keep
    isolated fractions near zero for semantic line objects, keep trench and
    ridge bathymetric signals, reduce comb-like province edges, preserve
    P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates, and retain age-depth/age-distance structure.
  - After P111.6.4, move to coherent mountain-belt hierarchy and continental
    interior physiographic provinces.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.4 boundary-linework object-source first pass archived

- Objective:
  - Start moving checklist item 7 from raw raster boundary pixels toward
    semantic line objects without undoing the P111.6.3 trench bathymetry pass.
  - Make the audit distinguish final object-line quality from raw boundary
    sampling quality for ridge, transform, and trench.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p11164_bridge_line_endpoints` in `terrain.py`.  The helper connects
    short gaps between semantic line endpoints only inside an already allowed
    process-supported corridor, caps added area against anchor area, and peels
    newly added branchy cells.  It is currently used conservatively for ridge
    axis cleanup in `_ocean_fabric_objects`.
  - Deliberately did not apply the new endpoint bridge to trench production in
    the accepted v3 path.  P111.6.4 v1/v2 experiments widened trench objects
    enough to trip the P111.6.3 final-axis area guard, which removed final
    depth-trench province cells at 900 cells.  Trench keeps the P111.6.2/6.3
    path until there is a stronger subduction-arc polyline model.
  - Updated `_p1116_boundary_linework_metrics` to schema
    `aevum.p1116_boundary_linework.v3`.  The main ridge linework now prefers
    `terrain.ocean_fabric:spreading_center`, then ridge depth province, then
    raw ridge boundary.  The main transform linework now prefers
    `terrain.ocean_fabric:transform_fault/fracture_zone`, then raw transform
    boundary.  Trench keeps the P111.6.2 behavior of preferring
    `terrain.margin_landforms:trench`, then depth trench province, then raw
    trench boundary.
  - The audit now reports `ridge_source`, `raw_ridge_boundary`,
    `object_ridge`, `depth_ridge_province`, `transform_source`,
    `raw_transform_boundary`, and `object_transform` side by side, matching the
    existing trench raw/object/depth split.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_line_endpoint_bridge_connects_short_semantic_gaps`.
    The fixture verifies the new endpoint bridge reduces component count and
    isolated-cell fraction while avoiding newly branchy bridge cells.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_audit_prefers_object_ridge_linework_over_raw_scatter`.
    The fixture proves schema v3 uses the object ridge as the primary ridge
    linework layer while preserving raw scattered ridge telemetry.
  - Updated the existing P111.6.2 trench audit regression to expect linework
    schema v3.
  - Focused P111.6 regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_line_endpoint_bridge_connects_short_semantic_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_audit_prefers_object_ridge_linework_over_raw_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure -q`
    -> `6 passed`.
  - Broader focused suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_open_ridge_cleanup_keeps_legible_mid_ocean_high tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_ocean_fabric_deepens_old_oceanic_crust tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_line_endpoint_bridge_connects_short_semantic_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_audit_prefers_object_ridge_linework_over_raw_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`

    `out_p11164_linework_coherence_900_seed707_v2_20260701` kept gates green
    but over-bridged trench objects.  `depth_trench_province` became empty,
    deep-trench attachment became `0.0`, and trench-minus-nontrench depth
    turned negative at 900 cells.  This proves trench line simplification must
    be driven by a stronger subduction-arc geometry model, not by the generic
    endpoint bridge.

    keep ridge-transform offsets attached, convert subduction arcs into
    ordered line objects before terrain response, and avoid broadening trench
    provinces.

    transform object component count without dropping transform coverage, keep
    ridge and trench bathymetry unchanged, and preserve P109/P110A/P110B/P111
    gates at 900 and 8000 cells.
  - P111.6.6 should then target convergent/subduction arc ordered polylines
    feeding both trench and mountain-belt hierarchy.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.5 ridge-transform junction repair archived

- Objective:
  - Repair the main P111.6.4 object-source linework regression: transform
    objects were too disconnected from ridge objects once the audit stopped
    relying on raw boundary unions.
  - Preserve P111.6.3 trench bathymetry, ridge bathymetry, oceanic crust
    age-depth structure, and P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates.
- Implementation:
  - Updated `_ocean_fabric_objects` so raw process-backed transform cells that
    touch the semantic ridge axis are preserved as `transform_fault` object
    cells even when the adjacent oceanic crust is older than the previous
    active-transform age window.
  - Allowed ridge/transform junction cells to overlap in the object layer.
    At this grid resolution, forcing transform cells out of the ridge axis
    breaks ridge-offset transform polylines at their natural junctions.  The
    repair is still constrained to raw transform source cells and a one-pass
    ridge-axis neighbourhood, with an area guard and thinning fallback for
    unusually broad connector masks.
  - Left trench generation unchanged from the accepted P111.6.3/P111.6.4 path.
    P111.6.4 showed that generic endpoint bridging can over-widen trench
    objects and delete the final depth-trench province through the area guard.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11165_ridge_adjacent_transform_survives_old_crust_age_window`.
    The fixture builds an old-ocean transform crossing a ridge and verifies
    that ridge-adjacent transform cells and ridge/transform junction cells
    survive as `transform_fault` objects.
  - Focused regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11165_ridge_adjacent_transform_survives_old_crust_age_window tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_audit_prefers_object_ridge_linework_over_raw_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - Broader focused suite:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_open_ridge_cleanup_keeps_legible_mid_ocean_high tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_ocean_fabric_deepens_old_oceanic_crust tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11165_ridge_adjacent_transform_survives_old_crust_age_window tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_line_endpoint_bridge_connects_short_semantic_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_audit_prefers_object_ridge_linework_over_raw_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`

  - P111.6.5 8000-cell linework movement relative to P111.6.4:

    `2442 m`; trench-minus-nontrench median depth `+2558 m`; age-depth

    `809 m`.

    the transform repair does not create broad abnormal transform swaths or
    disturb bathymetry, oceanic age belts, or trench bands.

    line chains.  Ridge-transform junctions are now semantically attached, but
    convergent/subduction arcs, trench segmentation, and mountain-belt parents
    remain the dominant object-geometry residual.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.5 is a stage pass for ridge-transform attachment in checklist item
    7.  It fixes the main object-source transform/ridge disconnect introduced
    by the stricter P111.6.4 audit semantics.
  - It does not close item 7 overall.  The next blocker is ordered convergent
    and subduction polyline generation: trench objects still have `49`
    components at 8000 cells, and mountain belts cannot become coherent
    real-Earth-like orogens until their convergent parent lines are coherent.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.6 should build ordered convergent/subduction arc polylines feeding
    both `terrain.margin_landforms:trench` and later mountain-belt hierarchy.
    Required targets: reduce object trench/convergent component count, keep
    trench province narrow and deep, preserve P111.6.5 ridge-transform
    adjacency, and keep P109/P110A/P110B/P111 green at 900 and 8000 cells.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.6 conservative subduction/trench arc refinement archived

- Objective:
  - Reduce final terrain-object trench fragmentation without repeating the
    P111.6.4 failure mode where generic endpoint bridging broadened trench
    objects and removed the final depth-trench province.
  - Preserve P111.6.3 narrow/deep trench bathymetry, P111.6.5
    ridge-transform junction attachment, oceanic crust age-depth structure,
    and P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p11166_refine_subduction_trench_arc`, a guarded terminal refinement
    step inside `_coherent_subduction_trench_axis`.
  - The helper starts from the already accepted trench axis, allows only
    one-cell-neighbourhood bridges around that axis, limits endpoint bridging
    to short gaps, re-runs the coherent open-ocean trench thinning pass, and
    accepts only when component count falls while area growth stays bounded.
  - Added `p11166_trench_arc_refinement` audit fields reporting whether the
    refinement was accepted plus before/after component count and ocean-area
    fraction.  These metrics are included in both raw audit metrics and the
    compact summary.
- Calibration notes:
  - Early P111.6.6 variants are retained as negative evidence.  Support-limited
    bridging produced no measurable movement, and the first area cap rejected a
    useful narrow bridge.  The accepted version uses axis-neighbourhood
    bridging plus a conservative ocean-area cap of `0.094` for the trench axis.
  - This is deliberately a local trench repair, not a general convergent
    boundary solution.  It cannot fix raw convergent scatter or mountain-belt
    parent-line fragmentation by itself.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11166_trench_arc_refinement_bridges_short_gap_without_broadening`.
    The fixture removes short gaps from a hand-built trench chain and verifies
    that P111.6.6 accepts a smaller-component result while preserving the
    original axis and bounding area growth.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11166_trench_arc_refinement_bridges_short_gap_without_broadening tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - Broader focused suite with P111.5/P111.6/P110B regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_open_ridge_cleanup_keeps_legible_mid_ocean_high tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_final_ocean_fabric_deepens_old_oceanic_crust tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_broad_volcanism_only_affects_preterminal_ocean_fabric tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11166_trench_arc_refinement_bridges_short_gap_without_broadening tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11165_ridge_adjacent_transform_survives_old_crust_age_window tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_line_endpoint_bridge_connects_short_semantic_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11164_audit_prefers_object_ridge_linework_over_raw_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_coherent_trench_axis_bridges_sampled_subduction_line tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11162_audit_prefers_object_trench_linework_over_raw_boundary_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11163_final_trench_objects_drive_narrow_bathymetry_and_depth_province tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework_and_oceanic_age_structure tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `30 passed`.

  - Accepted command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_900_seed707_v4_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `89.3 s`; terminal land fraction `0.268`.

    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - P111.6.6 900-cell signals: refinement accepted; trench components
    `16 -> 13`; trench ocean-area fraction `0.0864 -> 0.0926`; object-source
    ridge-transform adjacency `0.773`; trench-subduction attachment `0.984`;
    deep-trench attachment `1.0`; trench median depth `5000 m`;
    trench-minus-nontrench median depth `+3111 m`.
- 8000-cell calibration audit:
  - Accepted command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `821.8 s`; terminal land fraction `0.265`.
  - Follow-up sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Rendered review assets:
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_render_20260701/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_render_20260701/tectonic_boundaries.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_render_20260701/object_masks.png`,
    `/Users/rayw/Projects/aevum/out_p11166_subduction_arc_polyline_8000_seed707_render_20260701/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`.
  - Gate stability:
    land fraction `0.265`; largest/second/third land shares
    `0.303 / 0.221 / 0.165`; closed-ocean ring score `0.0245`;
    P109/P110A/P110B/P111 all green.
  - P111.6.6 8000-cell trench movement relative to P111.6.5:
    refinement accepted; trench-axis components `49 -> 44`; trench
    ocean-area fraction `0.0364 -> 0.0376`; object trench cells `214 -> 221`;
    object trench line score `0.118 -> 0.151`; depth-trench cells
    `152 -> 157`; depth-trench component count `49 -> 45`; depth-trench line
    score `0.129 -> 0.159`.
  - Preserved ridge, trench, and crust signals:
    object-source ridge-transform adjacency `0.724`; trench-subduction
    attachment `0.964`; deep-trench attachment `0.944`; trench median depth
    `5000 m`; trench-minus-nontrench median depth `+2558 m`; age-depth
    correlation `0.297`; age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.929`;
    old-minus-young median depth `702 m`; nonridge-minus-ridge median depth
    `809 m`.
  - Residual boundary signals:
    raw convergent boundary linework remains poor, with `260` cells,
    `167` components, isolated fraction `0.412`, and line-coherence score
    `0.031`.  This now dominates checklist item 7 more than trench depth or
    ridge-transform attachment.
- Visual interpretation:
  - `p107_array_contact_sheet.png` remains stable relative to P111.6.5: no
    broad trench apron regression, no ocean-age disruption, and no visible
    open-ocean shoal blow-up.
  - `object_masks.png` shows a modestly more connected trench object layer,
    but it still reads like raster cell chains rather than geologic arcs.
  - `tectonic_boundaries.png` makes the next residual clear: raw convergent
    pixels are still too scattered to serve as the parent geometry for coherent
    mountain belts or island arcs.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.6 is a stage pass for trench-object fragmentation.  It moves the
    object/depth trench metrics in the intended direction while preserving all
    gates and the P111.6.5 ridge-transform repair.
  - It does not close the boundary-linework checklist.  The remaining issue is
    upstream ordered convergent/subduction geometry, especially the parent
    lines that should drive mountain systems, island arcs, and trench arcs.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.7 should build ordered convergent/subduction parent polylines before
    terrain response.  Required targets: reduce raw convergent component and
    isolated fractions, preserve P111.6.6 narrow/deep trenches, preserve
    P111.6.5 ridge-transform adjacency, create better parent lines for
    mountain-belt main crests and island arcs, and keep P109/P110A/P110B/P111
    green at 900 and 8000 cells.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.7 ordered convergent/subduction parent line first pass archived

- Objective:
  - Add explicit tectonic parent-line semantics for convergent and subduction
    systems, instead of letting P111.6 linework diagnostics depend on raw
    scattered boundary pixels.
  - Preserve P111.6.6 trench bathymetry, P111.6.5 ridge-transform attachment,
    oceanic crust age-depth structure, and P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p11167_ordered_convergent_parent_axis`, which builds local,
    support-constrained parent axes from convergent/subduction/trench/
    active-margin evidence.  It connects ordered seeds within process support
    components and rejects broad area growth.
  - Added `convergent_parent` and `subduction_parent` entries to
    `tectonics.boundary_objects`.  These are object-layer parent geometry, not
    replacements for the raw `tectonics.boundaries` arrays.
  - Added `p11167_convergent_parent_linework` audit metrics with before/after
    component and cell counts for convergent and subduction parent refinement.
  - Updated P111.6 boundary-linework audit so the main `convergent` linework
    metric prefers the tectonic parent object when available, while keeping
    `raw_convergent_boundary` and `object_convergent_parent` side by side.
- Calibration notes:
  - The first terrain-coupled attempt let `subduction_parent` and
    `convergent_parent` feed terrain process masks directly.  It improved
    linework but over-uplifted high terrain at 900 cells: P109 failed on the
    `3000-4500m` band, age-depth correlation collapsed, and P110B/P111 could
    not promote the run.  That path was rejected.
  - The accepted P111.6.7 path keeps parent lines as semantic/audit objects
    only.  Terrain coupling is deferred to a guarded P111.6.8 pass with
    explicit mountain-belt, island-arc, and hypsometry checks.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11167_ordered_convergent_parent_axis_bridges_local_arc_gaps`,
    which verifies that sparse convergent evidence can be connected inside a
    local support corridor without broadening into an area mask.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11167_audit_prefers_convergent_parent_linework_over_raw_scatter`,
    which verifies that the P111.6 audit prefers parent-object convergent
    linework while retaining raw scatter diagnostics.

    -> `5 passed`.
  - Broader focused suite with P111.5/P111.6/P110B regressions:

    P110B, and P111 all green in the one-run sweep.
  - P111.6.7 24000-cell linework signals:
    convergent parent accepted; subduction parent accepted; convergent parent
    components `46 -> 18`; subduction parent components `127 -> 54`;
    raw convergent has `378` components, isolated fraction `0.398`, and line
    score `0.019`; parent-object convergent has `12` components, isolated
    fraction `0.0`, and line score `0.469`.

    ridge-transform adjacency `0.643`; trench-subduction attachment `0.992`;
    deep-trench attachment `1.0`; age-depth correlation `0.428`;
    age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.897`; old-minus-young median depth
    `695 m`; nonridge-minus-ridge median depth `809 m`.
- Visual interpretation:
  - The `24000/60p` elevation map now reads as a multi-continent world rather
    than a simple supercontinent.  It has several large landmasses, a polar
    continent, island chains, and visible ocean-basin structure.
  - The bathymetry shows much clearer ridge/fracture fabrics and broad abyssal
    provinces than early maps.  It still has a somewhat tiled/patchy texture
    and some shelf/slope bands are visually coarse.
  - `tectonic_boundaries.png` still exposes the raw-boundary problem: raw
    convergent/trench pixels remain scattered.  `object_masks.png` shows the
    semantic layer is better, but trenches and orogens are still fragmented.
  - The main remaining visual blocker is no longer planform.  It is guarded
    coupling from parent lines into coherent mountain systems, island arcs,
    and trench arcs.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.7 is a stage pass for checklist item 7 object semantics and audit
    visibility.  It proves ordered convergent/subduction parent linework can be
    represented without destabilizing planform gates.
  - It does not finish terrain expression.  The rejected v1 shows that directly
    feeding parent lines into existing terrain masks is too blunt and can break
    hypsometry.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.8 should couple parent lines into terrain through guarded orogenic
    hierarchy: main crest, branch ranges, foreland slopes, island arcs, and
    trench arcs.  Required targets: preserve P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates,
    prevent `3000-4500m` over-uplift, reduce high-mountain fragmentation,
    improve trench/arc continuity, and keep the P111.6.7 parent-line audit
    evidence side by side with raw boundary evidence.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.8 guarded parent-line orogenic hierarchy first pass archived

- Objective:
  - Use P111.6.7 convergent/subduction parent lines in terrain without
    repeating the rejected direct terrain-mask coupling that over-uplifted the
    `3000-4500m` land band.
  - Add a stable terrain-side semantic layer for main crests, branch ranges,
    and foreland slopes.  This layer should be visible in audit metrics and
    array archives, but it should not directly change the final elevation in
    this first pass.
  - Preserve P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates and keep P111.6 ridge, transform,
    trench, convergent, and oceanic-crust metrics side by side with raw
    boundary evidence.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks` in `TerrainModule`.
    It reads `tectonics.boundary_objects` kinds `convergent_parent` and
    `subduction_parent`, intersects their narrow influence bands with the
    already-capped active-orogen inventory, and compiles three guarded masks:
    `orogen_crest`, `orogen_branch_range`, and `orogen_foreland_slope`.
  - Added the production field `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy`, with code
    `1` for foreland slope, `2` for branch range, and `3` for main crest.
    This field is produced by terrain, registered in `features.py`, archived by
    P107 raw arrays, and reported by terminal audit metrics.
  - Added `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy` metrics:
    accepted flag, parent-line cell and area counts, active-orogen candidate
    count, parent overlap fraction, crest/branch/foreland cell counts,
    crest/branch/foreland area fractions, component counts, and field-backed
    area fractions.
  - Added object-mask support for `orogen_crest`, `orogen_branch_range`, and
    `orogen_foreland_slope` under `terrain.continental_landforms`.
  - Deliberately did not add `convergent_parent` or `subduction_parent` back
    into generic `_tectonic_process_mask` calls.  The P111.6.7 v1 failure showed
    that path is too broad and can break P109/P110B/P111 gates.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland`,
    which verifies that parent lines create non-empty crest/branch/foreland
    masks with capped area fractions and a non-empty hierarchy field.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`,
    which verifies terminal-audit reporting and field-backed area fractions.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11167_ordered_convergent_parent_axis_bridges_local_arc_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11167_audit_prefers_convergent_parent_linework_over_raw_scatter tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_high_mountain_coherence_raises_belts_and_suppresses_isolated_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_post_inventory_cleanup_uses_mountain_range_parentage tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters -q`
    -> `7 passed`.
  - Broader audit/sweep regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `76 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `137.0 s`; terminal land fraction `0.268`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_900_seed707_20260701 --out out_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_900_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - P111.6.8 signals:
    accepted `true`; parent-line cell count `229`; parent-line area fraction
    `0.255`; active-orogen candidate cells `50`; parent overlap fraction
    `0.900`; crest cells `29` / area `0.032`; branch cells `12` / area
    `0.013`; foreland cells `45` / area `0.050`; crest/branch/foreland
    component counts `5 / 3 / 10`.
  - Object inventory signal:
    terminal `terrain.continental_landforms` contains `5` `orogen_crest`
    objects, `3` `orogen_branch_range` objects, and `10`
    `orogen_foreland_slope` objects.
  - Gate stability:
    P109 hypsometry remains within envelope; terminal land hypsometry has mean
    `824 m`, p50 `548 m`, p90 `2201 m`, p98 `3229 m`, max `4865 m`; P109
    final p90 ceiling area is `0.0`.
    P111 run status remains `p111_modern_planform_candidate`, with
    largest/second/third land component shares `0.415 / 0.286 / 0.182`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.8 is a semantic terrain-coupling stage, not a final mountain-shape
    stage.  It establishes where coherent main crests, branch ranges, and
    foreland slopes should be, while preserving current gates.
  - The broad P111.6.7 parent objects remain too wide to drive terrain
    directly: the 900-cell parent-line area fraction is still `0.255`.  The
    new hierarchy caps the actual terrain-semantic response to much narrower
    area fractions before any elevation response is allowed.
  - High-mountain coherence still needs work.  In the 900-cell audit,
    P111.3 reduces 3000m+ highland components from `9` to `6`, but the final
    high-mountain fragmentation index is still `0.650` and small high-mountain
    component share remains high.  The new hierarchy provides the missing
    parent authority for a narrower follow-up repair.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.9 should use `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` to tighten the
    final high-mountain repair: lift only existing highland shoulders inside
    hierarchy code `3` and selected code `2` cells, connect nearby parented
    high components along the crest, keep foreland code `1` as a slope/basin
    context rather than a peak source, and require P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates
    plus a lower high-mountain fragmentation index.  It should not widen
    parent lines or reintroduce direct generic process-mask terrain coupling.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.9 hierarchy-gated orogen repair guard archived

- Objective:
  - Wire `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` into the final P111 high-mountain
    coherence repair without recreating the rejected broad parent-line uplift.
  - Permit main-crest and selected branch-range cells to guide highland
    shoulder bridge selection, while preventing foreland-slope code `1` from
    becoming a peak source.
  - Preserve P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates and expose whether the hierarchy
    bridge was used, reverted by hypsometry guard, or safely skipped.
- Implementation:
  - Extended `_p111_coherent_orogen_belt_repair` with optional
    `orogenic_parent_hierarchy`.
  - The repair now treats hierarchy code `3` as preferred main-crest authority
    and code `2` as secondary branch-range authority.  It may add a small
    number of bridge cells only when those cells are already highland shoulders
    near production mountain ranges.
  - Hierarchy code `1` foreland cells are excluded from `candidate_shoulder`,
    so they can remain slope/basin context but cannot be raised into 3000m+
    bridge peaks.
  - Added a P109 tail guard around the bridge application.  If bridge uplift
    would push land p90 above `2700 m` or the `3000-4500m` land band above
    `9%`, the bridge is reverted and reported.
  - Added audit fields under `high_mountain_coherence`:
    `p11169_hierarchy_used`,
    `p11169_hierarchy_bridge_area_fraction`,
    `p11169_hierarchy_bridge_cell_count`,
    `p11169_legacy_bridge_area_fraction`,
    `p11169_foreland_peak_cell_count`,
    `p11169_fragmentation_delta`,
    `p11169_crest_high_overlap_fraction`,
    `p11169_bridge_restricted_to_peak_hierarchy`, and
    `p11169_hypsometry_guard_reverted`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks`.
    It constructs a parented crest/branch/foreland corridor, verifies that
    hierarchy-supported bridge cells can connect high-mountain components, and
    verifies that foreland cells stay below `3000 m`.
  - Regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - Broader audit/sweep regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `77 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world checks:
  - Rejected v1:
    `out_p11169_hierarchy_gated_orogen_repair_900_seed707_20260701` used the
    hierarchy too narrowly, weakened the old P111.3 bridge, and failed P109 on
    `p90_m` plus `band_3000_4500m`.
  - Rejected v4/v5:
    looser bridge candidates preserved P109 but the hypsometry guard reverted
    all bridge cells; the terminal run still became a P110B review because the
    one-run terminal-supercontinent diagnostic triggered.
  - Accepted v6 command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p11169_hierarchy_gated_orogen_repair_900_seed707_v6_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `133.5 s`; terminal land fraction `0.251`.
  - Accepted v6 sweep:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p11169_hierarchy_gated_orogen_repair_900_seed707_v6_20260701 --out out_p11169_hierarchy_gated_orogen_repair_900_seed707_v6_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - Accepted v6 P109 signals:
    land p90 `2581 m`, p98 `3157 m`, `3000-4500m` land band `0.022`,
    `gt4500m` band `0.0044`, all within envelope.
  - Accepted v6 P111.6.9 signals:
    hierarchy used `true`; hypsometry guard reverted `false`; foreland peak
    count `0`; bridge-restricted-to-peak-hierarchy `true`.  The seed707
    terminal geometry had no safe continuous crest path, so bridge area was
    `0.0` and fragmentation delta was `0.0`; this is an intentional no-op
    rather than an unsafe forced connection.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.9 is a guarded repair pass, not a guarantee that every generated
    seed will add bridge cells.  It adds the missing control logic so future
    cases with a real crest/branch shoulder corridor can connect nearby
    high-mountain components while keeping forelands and P109 hypsometry safe.
  - The accepted seed707 generated-world check shows that the pass safely
    declines to bridge when the high components do not have a continuous
    land/crest support path.  This avoids drawing artificial mountain chains
    across gaps just to improve a fragmentation metric.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.10 should improve the upstream crest/branch geometry rather than
    forcing downstream terrain bridges: make `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy`
    components more line-continuous where the tectonic parent line and land
    support are genuinely continuous, then rerun the hierarchy-gated repair.
    Required evidence: lower crest component count or better crest linework,
    nonzero safe hierarchy bridge on at least one calibration case, foreland
    peak count `0`, and P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates green.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.10 upstream orogenic hierarchy linework archived

- Objective:
  - Improve `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` continuity upstream, before
    the final terrain repair attempts high-mountain bridge uplift.
  - Prevent parent-axis gaps from being prematurely classified as foreland
    slope, because that blocks legitimate crest/branch support paths.
  - Preserve the P111.6.9 safety contract: no foreland peak creation, no
    generic parent-line uplift, and no P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gate regression.
- Implementation:
  - Added a local `bridge_peak_hierarchy` refinement inside
    `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks`.
  - The refinement connects only short crest/branch gaps inside
    `cont_land & parent_near & non_foreland` support, with a small area budget
    (`0.8%` of world area) and a second cap pass after refinement.
  - Added `peak_link_support` so axis-adjacent parent-line gaps remain eligible
    for crest/branch refinement instead of being assigned to foreland before
    linework bridging.
  - Added P111.6.10 audit signals under `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`:
    `p111610_refinement_accepted`,
    `p111610_crest_components_before_refine`,
    `p111610_crest_components_after_refine`,
    `p111610_branch_components_before_refine`,
    `p111610_branch_components_after_refine`,
    `p111610_bridge_cell_count`, and
    `p111610_bridge_area_fraction`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111610_parent_hierarchy_refinement_bridges_short_axis_gap`.
    It constructs a parent-line axis with a short active gap and verifies that
    the hierarchy refinement bridges the gap neighborhood, reduces crest
    component count, and reports nonzero P111.6.10 bridge cells.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`
    to cover the new P111.6.10 audit fields.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111610_parent_hierarchy_refinement_bridges_short_axis_gap tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters -q`
    -> `5 passed`.
  - Broader audit/sweep regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `78 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111610_orogen_hierarchy_linework_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `133.1 s`; terminal land fraction `0.251`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111610_orogen_hierarchy_linework_900_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111610_orogen_hierarchy_linework_900_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - P111.6.10 generated-world signals:
    refinement accepted `true`; bridge cells `5`; bridge area fraction
    `0.00556`; crest components `4 -> 3`; branch components `2 -> 3`;
    final crest area fraction `0.0345`, branch area `0.0156`, foreland area
    `0.0422`.
  - Gate and terrain safety signals:
    P109 is within envelope; land p90 `2581 m`, p98 `3157 m`,
    `3000-4500m` land band `0.022`, `gt4500m` band `0.0044`.
    P111.6.9 still reports foreland peak count `0`, bridge restricted to peak
    hierarchy `true`, and hypsometry guard reverted `false`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.10 achieved upstream linework progress on seed707: the hierarchy
    layer now adds explicit bridge cells and reduces crest fragmentation before
    final terrain repair.
  - The final P111.6.9 terrain bridge remains `0.0` on seed707 because the
    terminal high-mountain shoulders still do not form a safe bridge target.
    That is acceptable for this step; the upstream semantic geometry is now
    measurably more continuous without compromising gates.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.11 should use the improved hierarchy layer to improve visible
    mountain-system expression without broad uplift: either tune highland
    shoulder eligibility so P111.6.9 can bridge only where crest/branch
    hierarchy and existing 2000-3000m shoulders overlap, or add a separate
    non-elevation audit for crest-line continuity on 8000/24000 renders before
    further elevation edits.  Required evidence: P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates
    green, foreland peak count `0`, and either nonzero safe terrain bridge on
    a generated-world calibration case or a documented reason to defer bridge
    uplift in favor of visual/object diagnostics.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.11 orogen bridge path diagnostics archived

- Objective:
  - Explain the residual P111.6.9 no-op cases before further terrain uplift:
    distinguish "there are no crest/branch shoulder candidates" from "there
    are candidates, but the allowed support graph is discontinuous".
  - Preserve the P111.6.9 safety contract: no broad parent-line uplift, no
    foreland peak creation, and no P109/P110A/P110B/P111 regression.
  - Provide medium-resolution evidence for whether the hierarchy bridge can
    safely operate on generated worlds.
- Implementation:
  - Added P111.6.11 diagnostics inside `_p111_coherent_orogen_belt_repair`.
    The repair now records bridge-candidate area, crest/branch hierarchy
    shoulder cells, high-mountain component-pair count, safe path count,
    blocked pair count, diagnostic system count, and whether bridge uplift was
    deferred because no safe support path existed.
  - The path audit now evaluates every adjacent high-component pair in the
    ordered mountain system.  The existing bridge cell budget still limits
    only actual uplift; diagnostics are not truncated by that budget.
  - Exposed the new fields under `high_mountain_coherence` in P107 terminal
    metrics and compact summaries:
    `p111611_bridge_candidate_cell_count`,
    `p111611_bridge_candidate_area_fraction`,
    `p111611_peak_hierarchy_shoulder_cell_count`,
    `p111611_high_pair_count`,
    `p111611_safe_path_count`,
    `p111611_blocked_high_pair_count`,
    `p111611_diagnostic_system_count`, and
    `p111611_bridge_deferred_no_safe_path`.
- Tests:
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks`
    to assert nonzero bridge candidates, nonzero hierarchy shoulder
    candidates, nonzero safe paths, and no deferred-no-path flag in a
    synthetic connected crest/branch corridor.
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111611_orogen_repair_reports_blocked_hierarchy_path_without_forced_uplift`.
    It constructs two disconnected highland lobes inside one mountain-range id
    and verifies that the repair reports blocked high-component pairs without
    forcing uplift across unsupported lowland gaps.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence`
    to verify that P111.6.11 fields are written into terminal audit metrics.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111610_parent_hierarchy_refinement_bridges_short_axis_gap tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111611_orogen_repair_reports_blocked_hierarchy_path_without_forced_uplift tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters -q`
    -> `7 passed`.
  - Broader regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `79 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111611_orogen_bridge_diagnostics_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `104.8 s`; terminal land fraction `0.251`.
  - Sweep command:

    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111

- 8000-cell generated-world check:

    -> runtime `919.2 s`; terminal land fraction `0.299`.

    -> rendered `11` QA assets.

    P109 within envelope with no out-of-envelope entries; land mean `734 m`,
    p50 `521 m`, p90 `2006 m`, p98 `3100 m`, max `4700 m`.
    P111.6.9 hierarchy bridge area `0.00187`, bridge cells `15`, foreland
    peak count `0`, hypsometry guard reverted `false`.

    bridge candidates `312`; candidate area fraction `0.0390`; crest/branch
    hierarchy shoulder cells `243`; high-component pairs `32`; safe paths
    `18`; blocked pairs `14`; diagnostic systems `3`;
    deferred-no-safe-path `false`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.11 satisfies the required evidence without broadening elevation
    response: the same diagnostic explains a safe no-op at 900 cells and a
    nonzero safe bridge at 8000 cells, while all gates remain green.
  - Further elevation edits should be based on these diagnostics.  Cases with
    high candidate count but low safe-path count need upstream line/object
    support repair; cases with safe paths but low visual continuity can tune
    shoulder uplift or rendering of crest/branch hierarchy.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.12 should use the P111.6.11 diagnostics to improve visible
    mountain-system expression on 8000 renders without raising broad
    interiors.  Preferred next actions are: render/inspect the 8000 P111.6.11
    contact sheet, add an explicit crest/branch line overlay or object QA
    layer if visual continuity is hard to read, and only then tune shoulder
    uplift for systems with nonzero safe paths and foreland peak count `0`.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.12 orogenic hierarchy QA overlay archived

- Objective:
  - Make the P111.6.8/P111.6.10 orogenic semantic layer directly visible in
    8000/24000 array-render QA packs.
  - Let visual review compare crest/branch/foreland semantics against
    elevation without changing terrain elevations.
  - Preserve the P111.6.11 safety posture: use diagnostics and visual overlays
    before further uplift tuning.
- Implementation:
  - Extended `aevum.diagnostics.p107_array_render.render_p107_array_assets`
    to render `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` when the field is present in
    the P107 terminal array archive.
  - Added `orogenic_parent_hierarchy.png`, a coded semantic map:
    `0 none`, `1 foreland slope`, `2 branch range`, `3 main crest`.
  - Added `orogenic_parent_hierarchy_overlay.png`, which draws the same
    hierarchy over the elevation raster so reviewers can see whether semantic
    crests and branches align with high terrain and whether foreland remains
    lower.
  - Updated the P107 array contact sheet to include the hierarchy panel and to
    support a dynamic number of panels instead of a fixed 3x2 layout.
- Tests:
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_array_render.py::test_p107_array_renderer_writes_visual_qa_pack`
    with a synthetic `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` field and assertions
    that both new PNG assets are written.

    -> `1 passed`.
  - Broader related regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `80 passed`.
- 8000-cell render verification:
  - Reused the accepted P111.6.11 terminal arrays:
    `out_p111611_orogen_bridge_diagnostics_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p`.
  - Render command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111611_orogen_bridge_diagnostics_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111612_orogen_hierarchy_overlay_8000_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> rendered `13` QA assets.

    and
    `out_p111612_orogen_hierarchy_overlay_8000_seed707_render_20260701/orogenic_parent_hierarchy_overlay.png`.

    crest `260` cells (`0.0325`).
