    foreland mean relative elevation `205 m`, p90 `477 m`;
    branch mean `1984 m`, p90 `3085 m`;
    crest mean `2149 m`, p90 `3142 m`.
- Interpretation:
  - The hierarchy layer is now inspectable without re-running expensive
    simulations, including on 24000-cell terminal arrays.
  - On the 8000 seed707 render, main crests and branch ranges mostly align
    with higher terrain while foreland remains low, so the semantic layer is
    not inverted.
  - Remaining issues are now more precise: foreland support is still visually
    scattered in some interiors, branch ranges are comparatively sparse, and
    high-relief belts can still be hard to read where semantic crests are not
    visually emphasized in the elevation-only map.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.13 should use the new overlay to improve hierarchy geometry before
    any broad elevation edits.  The preferred sequence is:
    (1) quantify crest/branch/foreland component shape from the array archive,
    (2) reduce isolated foreland fragments that lack nearby crest/branch
    authority, (3) increase branch continuity only where it connects an
    existing crest to supported 2000-3000m shoulders, and (4) rerender the
    8000 overlay plus P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates before changing mountain
    uplift thresholds.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.13 orogenic hierarchy geometry refinement archived

- Objective:
  - Use the P111.6.12 overlay evidence to clean the semantic hierarchy before
    tuning mountain uplift.
  - Reduce foreland-slope scatter that lacks nearby crest/branch authority.
  - Extend branch ranges only into supported highland shoulders, avoiding new
    branch fragments where a branch system already exists.
- Implementation:
  - Added a P111.6.13 refinement inside
    `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks`, after the P111.6.10 crest/branch
    gap bridge and before the final hierarchy field is written.
  - Branch extension is constrained to continental parent-near cells that are
    within two neighbor passes of the existing peak hierarchy, between
    `1600-3400 m` relative elevation, and anchored to an existing branch
    neighbor.  Only degenerate cases with no branch at all may seed branch
    from a multi-neighbor crest shoulder.
  - Foreland is pruned to cells within four neighbor passes of the refined
    crest/branch hierarchy.  Unsupported foreland fragments are removed from
    the semantic hierarchy rather than allowed to appear as scattered
    lowland patches.
  - Added P111.6.13 metrics under `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`:
    `p111613_refinement_accepted`,
    `p111613_branch_components_before_refine`,
    `p111613_branch_components_after_refine`,
    `p111613_foreland_components_before_refine`,
    `p111613_foreland_components_after_refine`,
    `p111613_branch_extension_cell_count`,
    `p111613_branch_extension_area_fraction`,
    `p111613_foreland_removed_cell_count`,
    `p111613_foreland_removed_area_fraction`, and
    `p111613_foreland_removed_component_count`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refinement_prunes_unsupported_foreland_and_extends_branch`.
    The fixture contains one supported active crest with highland shoulders and
    one inactive parent segment.  It verifies branch extension near the
    supported shoulder and removal of unsupported foreland near the inactive
    parent segment.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`
    to cover the P111.6.13 audit fields.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refinement_prunes_unsupported_foreland_and_extends_branch tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111610_parent_hierarchy_refinement_bridges_short_axis_gap -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - Broader related regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_array_render.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `81 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Initial v1 kept gates green but increased branch components from `3` to
    `5`, so the branch extension anchor was tightened.
  - Accepted v2 audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_900_seed707_v2_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `100.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.251`.
  - Accepted v2 sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_900_seed707_v2_20260701 --out out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_900_seed707_v2_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - Accepted v2 signals:
    branch cells `20`, foreland cells `24`; branch components remain `3 -> 3`;
    foreland components remain `3 -> 3`; branch extension `6` cells; foreland
    removed `14` cells; foreland removed component count `0`; P109 within
    envelope; P111.6.9 foreland peak count `0`; P111.6.11 still correctly
    reports deferred-no-safe-path for this 900-cell case.
- 8000-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `915.9 s`; terminal land fraction `0.279`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - Render command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refine_8000_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> rendered `13` QA assets.
  - Gate and terrain signals:
    P109 within envelope with no out-of-envelope entries; land mean `764 m`,
    p50 `556 m`, p90 `2013 m`, p98 `3099 m`, max `4700 m`.
    P111.6.9 hierarchy bridge cells `10`, foreland peak count `0`, and
    hypsometry guard reverted `false`.
  - P111.6.13 hierarchy signals:
    crest cells `273`, branch cells `145`, foreland cells `49`;
    branch components `15 -> 13`; foreland components `58 -> 13`;
    branch extension `48` cells / area fraction `0.0060`;
    foreland removed `273` cells / area fraction `0.0341`;
    removed foreland components `45`.
  - Layer/elevation alignment:
    foreland `49` cells (`0.0061` of world), mean `211 m`, p90 `465 m`;
    branch `145` cells (`0.0181`), mean `1765 m`, p90 `3071 m`;
    crest `273` cells (`0.0341`), mean `2099 m`, p90 `3110 m`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.13 makes the hierarchy layer more geologically readable without
    broad terrain uplift: unsupported foreland scatter is greatly reduced at
    8000 cells, branch continuity improves, and foreland remains low.
  - The semantic changes still feed safely into P111.6.9: nonzero hierarchy
    bridge cells occur at 8000 cells, foreland peaks remain zero, and
    hypsometry stays inside the P109 envelope.
  - Remaining issue visible in the overlay: some southern high-latitude belts
    still form broad semantic swaths.  This is now a geometry/object question,
    not an elevation-color or random-noise question.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.14 should turn the refined hierarchy into clearer line-object QA:
    derive crest centerlines and branch spines from the hierarchy, report
    line continuity/width metrics, and optionally render a thin-line overlay
    so mountain systems read as belts with main axes and branches rather than
    filled blobs.

2026-07-01 - P111.6.14 orogenic hierarchy spine QA archived

- Objective:
  - Convert the refined P111.6.13 filled hierarchy into thin crest/branch line
    QA without changing terrain elevations.
  - Expose line continuity and belt-width proxy metrics so reviewers can tell
    whether mountain systems have readable main axes and branch spines.
  - Make the line layer available in P107 terminal arrays and array-render QA
    packs, including 8000/24000 post-hoc renders.
- Implementation:
  - Added production field `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`, with code
    `2` for branch spine and `3` for main crest spine.
  - Registered the field in `TerrainModule.produces`, `aevum.features`, P107
    terminal array archives, and compact integer dtype handling.
  - Added P111.6.14 spine derivation inside
    `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks`.  For each refined crest/branch
    component, the algorithm chooses component-axis endpoints and connects
    them through the existing component mask using the local shortest-path
    helper.  If a component is too small or pathing fails, it falls back to a
    small high-rank subset.  This keeps spines as hierarchy subsets rather
    than independent random lines.
  - Added P111.6.14 audit metrics:
    `p111614_spine_refinement_accepted`,
    `p111614_crest_spine_cell_count`,
    `p111614_branch_spine_cell_count`,
    `p111614_crest_spine_area_fraction`,
    `p111614_branch_spine_area_fraction`,
    `p111614_crest_spine_component_count`,
    `p111614_branch_spine_component_count`,
    `p111614_crest_width_ratio`,
    `p111614_branch_width_ratio`, and
    `p111614_spine_overlap_valid`.
  - Extended `p107-render-arrays` with:
    `orogenic_hierarchy_spines.png` and
    `orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png`.
    The existing contact sheet now includes an `Orogen spines` panel when the
    field is present.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111614_hierarchy_spines_are_thin_subsets_of_refined_hierarchy`.
    It verifies that crest/branch spines are valid hierarchy subsets, do not
    overlap, are thinner than the filled hierarchy, and report width metrics.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`
    for the P111.6.14 audit fields.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_array_render.py::test_p107_array_renderer_writes_visual_qa_pack`
    to include `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine` and assert both new PNG
    assets are written.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111614_hierarchy_spines_are_thin_subsets_of_refined_hierarchy tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refinement_prunes_unsupported_foreland_and_extends_branch tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_array_render.py::test_p107_array_renderer_writes_visual_qa_pack -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - Broader related regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_array_render.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `82 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `100.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.251`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_900_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_900_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - P111.6.14 signals:
    crest cells `31`, branch cells `20`; crest spine `13` cells, branch spine
    `11` cells; crest/branch spine components `3 / 3`; width ratios
    `2.38 / 1.82`; spine overlap valid `true`.  The P107 array archive
    contains `field__terrain_orogenic_hierarchy_spine` with values
    `{0: 876, 2: 11, 3: 13}`.
  - Gate stability:
    P109 within envelope; P110A/P110B/P111 green; foreland peak count `0`;
    hypsometry guard reverted `false`.
- 8000-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `918.7 s`; terminal land fraction `0.279`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - Render command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_8000_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> rendered `15` QA assets.
  - Gate and terrain signals:
    P109 within envelope with no out-of-envelope entries; land mean `764 m`,
    p50 `556 m`, p90 `2013 m`, p98 `3099 m`, max `4700 m`;
    hierarchy bridge cells `10`; foreland peak count `0`; hypsometry guard
    reverted `false`.
  - P111.6.14 signals:
    crest cells `273`, branch cells `145`, foreland cells `49`; crest spine
    `82` cells, branch spine `68` cells; crest/branch spine components
    `12 / 13`; width ratios `3.33 / 2.13`; spine overlap valid `true`.
    The array archive contains spine values `{0: 7850, 2: 68, 3: 82}`.
  - Visual evidence:
    `out_p111614_orogen_spine_qa_8000_seed707_render_20260701/orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png`
    shows thin crest/branch axes over elevation, making broad southern belts
    and the western mountain system readable as line systems rather than only
    filled semantic blobs.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.14 does not solve final mountain morphology by itself, but it
    provides the missing line-object QA layer needed before tuning elevation.
  - Spines are derived from the existing hierarchy, remain valid subsets, and
    are thin enough to report belt width ratios at 900 and 8000 cells while all
    P109/P110A/P110B/P111 gates remain green.
  - The overlay now makes the next residual problem explicit: some high-latitude
    belts still have broad filled hierarchy around relatively coherent spines,
    so the next improvement should act on belt width/shoulder morphology rather
    than inventing new peak cells.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.15 should use spine-derived width metrics to tune visible belt
    morphology: constrain broad semantic swaths around spines, add a small
    shoulder/foothill halo where supported by existing 1000-2500m terrain, and
    keep peak uplift unchanged unless P109/P110A/P110B/P111 and foreland-peak
    guards remain green.

### 2026-07-01 - P111.6.15 Orogen Belt Morphology Halo

- Goal:
  - Use the P111.6.14 spine field as the authority for visible belt
    morphology.
  - Reduce broad filled crest/branch semantics around spines where the terrain
    is only low-to-mid elevation, and move that support into a separate
    shoulder/foothill halo.
  - Do not alter elevation or create new peaks; keep high peak cells protected.
- Implementation:
  - Added production field `terrain.orogenic_shoulder_halo` with code `1` for
    low-to-mid elevation shoulder/foothill halo around supported orogenic
    spines.
  - Updated `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks`:
    - derives crest/branch spines first;
    - keeps crest/branch hierarchy near the relevant spine, bridge, branch
      extension, or cells already above 2500 m;
    - converts supported 1000-2500 m low/mid fringe and adjacent shoulder
      terrain into `terrain.orogenic_shoulder_halo`;
    - recomputes final hierarchy after morphology refinement;
    - preserves high peaks and reports whether any `>=3000 m` hierarchy cells
      were removed.
  - Added P111.6.15 metrics under `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`:
    `p111615_refinement_accepted`,
    `p111615_shoulder_halo_cell_count`,
    `p111615_shoulder_halo_area_fraction`,
    `p111615_shoulder_halo_component_count`,
    `p111615_field_shoulder_halo_area_fraction`,
    `p111615_crest_pruned_cell_count`,
    `p111615_branch_pruned_cell_count`,
    `p111615_crest_width_ratio_before`,
    `p111615_branch_width_ratio_before`,
    `p111615_peak_hierarchy_cell_count_before`,
    `p111615_peak_hierarchy_cell_count_after`,
    `p111615_high_peak_removed_cell_count`, and
    `p111615_halo_hierarchy_overlap_valid`.
  - Added archive/render support:
    `field__terrain_orogenic_shoulder_halo`,
    `orogenic_shoulder_halo.png`, and
    `orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png`.
    The P107 contact sheet includes an `Orogen halo` panel when the field is
    present.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111615_spine_width_morphology_adds_halo_without_pruning_high_peaks`.
    It builds a broad synthetic active belt, verifies that peak hierarchy is
    narrowed, halo is created, halo does not overlap crest/branch/foreland, and
    protected high peak cells remain in the hierarchy.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`
    for P111.6.15 JSON metrics.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics`
    to assert the halo archive field is written.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_array_render.py::test_p107_array_renderer_writes_visual_qa_pack`
    to assert both new render assets are written.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111615_spine_width_morphology_adds_halo_without_pruning_high_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111614_hierarchy_spines_are_thin_subsets_of_refined_hierarchy tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics tests/test_p107_array_render.py::test_p107_array_renderer_writes_visual_qa_pack -q`
    -> `5 passed`.
  - Broader related regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_array_render.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `83 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `104.1 s`; terminal land fraction `0.251`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_900_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_900_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - P111.6.15 signals:
    crest cells `30`, branch cells `20`, foreland cells `24`; shoulder halo
    `11` cells; crest pruned `1`, branch pruned `0`; peak hierarchy
    `51 -> 50`; crest width ratio `2.38 -> 2.31`; branch width ratio
    `1.82 -> 1.82`; high peak removed count `0`; halo/hierarchy overlap
    valid `true`; archive contains `field__terrain_orogenic_shoulder_halo`
    with values `{0: 889, 1: 11}`.
  - Gate stability:
    P109/P110A/P110B/P111 green; acceptance false list empty; high mountain
    parent overlap `1.0`.
- 8000-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `929.4 s`; terminal land fraction `0.279`.
  - Sweep command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`, P111
    modern-planform candidates `1/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `none`.
  - Render command:
    `PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> rendered `17` QA assets.
  - P111.6.15 signals:
    crest cells `224`, branch cells `135`, foreland cells `49`; shoulder halo
    `129` cells; crest pruned `49`, branch pruned `10`; peak hierarchy
    `418 -> 359`; crest width ratio `3.33 -> 2.73`; branch width ratio
    `2.13 -> 1.99`; high peak removed count `0`; halo/hierarchy overlap
    valid `true`; archive contains `field__terrain_orogenic_shoulder_halo`
    with values `{0: 7871, 1: 129}`.
  - Visual evidence:
    `out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_render_20260701/orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png`
    shows spine, crest, branch, foreland, and shoulder halo in one elevation
    overlay.
    `out_p111615_orogen_belt_morphology_8000_seed707_render_20260701/orogenic_shoulder_halo.png`
    shows sparse supported shoulder/foothill fringe rather than broad peak
    swaths.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.15 makes the orogenic semantic layer more physically layered:
    narrow crest/branch hierarchy remains tied to spines, while existing
    1000-2500 m terrain around the belt is represented as shoulder/foothill
    support.
  - The change reduces 8000-cell filled peak hierarchy by `59` cells without
    altering elevation or removing high peaks.
  - Residual issue: southern high-latitude belts still look broad in the
    overlay, but now the broadness is explicit as layered morphology rather
    than undifferentiated crest/branch fill.
  - Runtime remains a separate issue: 8000 audit took about 15.5 minutes, and a
    24000 rerun previously stalled in
    `_open_breakup_seaway_objects -> _components`.  This should be handled as a
    later performance/algorithmic pass, not mixed into morphology semantics.
- Next implementation entry:
  - P111.6.16 should tune high-latitude orogen morphology using the new
    hierarchy/spine/halo evidence: split excessive polar belt width into
    coherent crest corridors, shoulder belts, foreland slopes, and older
    subdued orogen/plateau semantics where supported by existing fields.
  - Separately plan a performance pass for repeated component extraction in
    seaway and terrain object generation before attempting more 24000-cell
    multi-seed runs.

### P111.6.16 High-Latitude Orogen Morphology Guard

- Goal:
  - Prevent high-latitude projection and one-neighbor raster thickness from
    turning polar/subpolar orogenic belts into broad undifferentiated
    crest/branch swaths.
  - Keep true spine-supported crest/branch cells and extreme peaks, while
    splitting broad non-spine high-latitude highlands into shoulder halo or
    highland-apron semantics.
- Implementation:
  - Added `terrain.orogenic_highland_apron` as a production field.
  - Added P111.6.16 refinement inside
    `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks`:
    - candidate cells are high-latitude (`abs(lat) >= 55`) crest/branch cells
      outside spine support, hierarchy bridges, P111.6.13 branch extensions,
      and extreme peaks (`rel >= 3300 m`);
    - `850-2500 m` candidates become high-latitude shoulder halo extension;
    - `2500-3300 m` candidates become highland apron;
    - spine-supported cells and extreme peaks remain in the peak hierarchy.
  - Added `orogen_highland_apron` lightweight continental-landform objects for
    object-layer QA without folding them into the core mountain-range inventory.
  - Allowed highland apron cells to participate in collision-plateau candidate
    semantics where the crustal context supports it.
  - Registered `terrain.orogenic_highland_apron` in `aevum/features.py`.
  - Extended P107 terminal archive, compact dtype handling, P111.6.8 JSON
    metrics, object masks, and array renderer assets:
    `orogenic_highland_apron.png` plus the expanded
    `orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png` and contact sheet panel.
- Metrics added under `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`:
  - `p111616_refinement_accepted`
  - `p111616_highlat_candidate_cell_count`
  - `p111616_highland_apron_cell_count`
  - `p111616_highland_apron_area_fraction`
  - `p111616_highland_apron_component_count`
  - `p111616_field_highland_apron_area_fraction`
  - `p111616_highlat_halo_extension_cell_count`
  - `p111616_highlat_halo_extension_area_fraction`
  - `p111616_crest_reclassified_cell_count`
  - `p111616_branch_reclassified_cell_count`
  - `p111616_peak_hierarchy_cell_count_before`
  - `p111616_peak_hierarchy_cell_count_after`
  - `p111616_extreme_peak_reclassified_cell_count`
  - `p111616_highland_apron_overlap_valid`
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111616_high_latitude_apron_splits_broad_orogen_without_removing_extreme_peaks`
    to force the high-latitude apron path and verify that extreme peaks are
    protected.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`
    for P111.6.16 JSON metrics.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics`
    to require `field__terrain_orogenic_highland_apron`.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_array_render.py::test_p107_array_renderer_writes_visual_qa_pack`
    to require `orogenic_highland_apron.png` and apron archive fields.
  - Syntax command:
    `python3 -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/features.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_array_render.py`
    -> passed.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `74 passed`.
  - Broader related regression command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_array_render.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `84 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 24 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `95.9 s`; terminal land fraction `0.282`.
  - Signals:
    crest cells `28`, branch cells `17`, foreland cells `21`;
    P111.6.16 accepted `true`; high-latitude candidates `3`; high-latitude
    halo extension `3`; highland apron `0`; peak hierarchy `48 -> 45`;
    extreme peak reclassified `0`; highland-apron overlap valid `true`.
  - Render command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_900_seed707_20260701/00_900cells_24p --out out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_900_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1200 --height 600`
    -> rendered `18` QA assets including the new apron layer.
- 8000-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `853.1 s`; terminal land fraction `0.279`.
  - Sweep command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A threshold pass `1/1`; distribution still flags
    `p110b_review_flags_present`; P111 flags include
    `p111_median_largest_landmass_above_modern_preferred` and
    `p111_largest_landmass_above_preferred_rate_high`.
  - Render command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111616_highlat_orogen_apron_8000_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> rendered `18` QA assets.
  - Signals:
    crest cells `224`, branch cells `120`, foreland cells `59`; P111.6.16
    accepted `false` because no high-latitude non-spine candidate remained in
    this seed; highland apron `0`; highland-apron field archived and rendered.
    High-latitude hierarchy counts are now low in the generated asset:
    crest high-latitude count `2`, branch high-latitude count `0`, foreland
    high-latitude count `0`, halo high-latitude count `8`.
- Interpretation:
  - The synthetic test proves the P111.6.16 guard path: broad high-latitude
    non-spine highlands can be reclassified without deleting spine/extreme
    peak structure.
  - On seed707/8000, the guard is currently idle because prior morphology
    pruning already removes the broad high-latitude crest/branch swath.
    This is acceptable; P111.6.16 is a targeted fallback rather than a mandatory
    every-world transformation.
  - The current 8000 generated world still has broader planform issues:
    largest land share `0.480`, closed-ocean soft warning, and P111 modern
    planform distribution flags. These belong to the next planform/boundary
    continuity pass rather than to high-latitude morphology.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Address planform distribution and boundary object continuity: reduce
    closed-ocean ring tendencies, keep divergent/ridge and convergent/trench
    boundaries as more continuous line objects, and then re-check whether
    ocean-basin openness and largest-landmass metrics move back into the
    preferred envelope.

### P111.6.17 Strict Endpoint Planform Residual Probe

- Goal:
  - Test whether the remaining `8000/36p` seed-707 P111 review state could be
    solved by allowing a final, stricter modern endpoint seaway when the world
    already passes coarse P110B gates but the largest land component remains
    above the P111 preferred ceiling.
  - Add enough rejection telemetry to distinguish "no path", "path does not
    split the largest component", "secondary fragment too small", "reduction
    too small", and "quality too low".
- Implementation:
  - Relaxed only the `p111_strict_preferred` endpoint path in
    `_open_modern_earthlike_seaways`:
    - secondary floor can fall to `0.030` of land area for the strict endpoint
      pass;
    - required reduction can fall to `0.006`;
    - quality backing thresholds are slightly relaxed for domain boundary,
      margin/suture, and weak-lowland bridges.
  - Added strict endpoint rejection counters:
    `terrain.last_p1114_strict_endpoint_no_split_candidate_count`,
    `terrain.last_p1114_strict_endpoint_secondary_reject_count`,
    `terrain.last_p1114_strict_endpoint_reduction_reject_count`, and
    `terrain.last_p1114_strict_endpoint_quality_reject_count`.
  - Exposed those counters through P107
    `p110a_modern_planform.seaway_cut_effectiveness`.
- Tests:
  - Syntax command:
    `python3 -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Focused endpoint command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1114_strict_endpoint_polish_continues_past_p110b_soft_stop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_splits_weak_supercontinent tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_uses_continent_domain_boundary tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_default_allows_six_passes -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - Broader related command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py -q`
    -> `83 passed`.
- Generated-world checks:
  - 900-cell audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111617_strict_endpoint_planform_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    produced largest land share `0.411`, closed-ocean score `0.0`, strict
    endpoint applied `true`, and endpoint seaway count `3`; the remaining
    warning was `p110b_independent_lineage_count_low`.
  - 8000-cell audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111617_strict_endpoint_planform_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    left the planform unchanged from P111.6.16: largest land share `0.4796`,
    closed-ocean ring score `0.1035`, and warning
    `closed_ocean_ring_score_soft`.
  - P111.6.17 8000 rejection telemetry:
    strict endpoint attempted `true`, applied `false`, endpoint count `1`,
    `modern_candidate_count=283`, `modern_viable_candidate_count=0`,
    `p1114_strict_endpoint_no_split_candidate_count=104`,
    `p1114_strict_endpoint_secondary_reject_count=164`,
    `p1114_strict_endpoint_reduction_reject_count=0`, and
    `p1114_strict_endpoint_quality_reject_count=0`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.17 is useful as telemetry, but it is not the correct 8000 fix.
    The residual is not an endpoint cut that needs looser acceptance; it is a
    late-stage closed-ocean topology problem that remains after the earlier
    `_connect_large_closed_oceans` pass.
  - Do not keep relaxing strict endpoint thresholds for this failure mode.

### P111.6.18 Late Closed-Ocean Repair

- Goal:
  - Re-run a conservative closed-ocean connectivity check after the late
    P110A/P110B planform polish chain, because endpoint seaways, component
    payback, and ribbon recycling can leave a new enclosed ocean after the
    earlier P105-style closed-ocean repair already ran.
  - Preserve early closed-ocean corridor objects and record late repair
    before/after metrics separately.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_closed_ocean_connectivity_stats` to report ocean component count,
    disconnected-ocean share, largest-ocean share, and max disconnected
    world-area fraction using spherical-grid component topology.
  - Added `_p111617_late_closed_ocean_repair`, gated to mature earthlike
    terminal worlds and triggered only when disconnected-ocean share exceeds
    `0.030` and the largest disconnected basin is at least `0.006` of world
    area.
  - The late pass calls `_connect_large_closed_oceans` only when needed, then
    records late-specific metrics:
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_attempted`,
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_applied`,
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_component_count_before/after`,
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_score_before/after`,
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_max_candidate_fraction_before`,
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_openings`, and
    `terrain.last_p111617_late_closed_ocean_area_fraction`.
  - Late corridor objects are retained in
    `terrain.closed_ocean_seaway_opened_corridors` with ids
    `p111617_late_closed_ocean_seaway:*` and
    `phase=late_planform_closed_ocean_repair`.
  - The late pass is inserted after `_p110b_terminal_ribbon_recycle` and before
    the post-P110A terrain/object refresh, so any changed land mask is
    propagated through the terminal derived fields.
  - P107 audit exposes the late before/after metrics under
    `p110a_modern_planform.seaway_cut_effectiveness`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_engine.py::test_late_closed_ocean_repair_reports_improvement_and_preserves_corridors`
    to verify a late equatorial land barrier is cut, before/after scores
    improve, and earlier corridor objects are preserved.
  - Syntax command:
    `python3 -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_late_closed_ocean_repair_reports_improvement_and_preserves_corridors -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - Broader related command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_late_closed_ocean_repair_reports_improvement_and_preserves_corridors -q`
    -> `85 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_900_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `104.2 s`; land fraction `0.2767`; largest land share `0.4177`;
    major land components `4`; closed-ocean score `0.0046`.
  - Late repair telemetry: attempted `false`, applied `false`, score before
    and after `0.0046`, max disconnected candidate world fraction `0.0011`.
    This is the desired idle case: the terminal map already has open ocean
    connectivity, so no new seaway is cut.
- 8000-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_8000_seed707_20260701 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> terminal land fraction `0.2784`.
  - Compared with P111.6.17, largest land share changed only
    `0.4796 -> 0.4787`; second/third shares are `0.1837/0.1819`; major land
    components remain `4`.
  - Closed-ocean score improved from `0.1035` to `0.0270`, removing the
    P110A `closed_ocean_ring_score_soft` warning.  Late repair attempted
    `true`, applied `true`, opened `1` seaway, and used only `0.00050` of world
    area.  The largest disconnected candidate before repair was `0.0550` of
    world area.
  - Sweep command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_8000_seed707_20260701 --out out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_8000_seed707_sweep_20260701 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A threshold pass `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`,
    P111 modern-planform candidates `0/1`, distribution flags `none`, P111
    distribution flags `p111_modern_planform_review_flags_present`,
    `p111_median_largest_landmass_above_modern_preferred`, and
    `p111_largest_landmass_above_preferred_rate_high`.
  - Render command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_8000_seed707_render_20260701 --width 1440 --height 720`
    -> rendered `18` QA assets.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.18 closes the 8000-cell closed-ocean residual without materially
    changing land fraction, largest-land share, or non-largest continent
    distribution.  It should be kept as a terminal topology guard rather than
    merged into endpoint splitting.
  - Remaining P111 issue is now cleanly isolated: the calibration seed is a
    P110B visual candidate, but not a P111 modern-planform candidate because
    largest land share remains above the stricter preferred ceiling
    (`0.4787 > 0.45`).  This is a largest-land partition / independent-lineage
    problem, not a closed-ocean problem.
  - Runtime needs a separate performance pass before more 24000-cell runs.  The
    P111.6.18 8000 audit reported `12583 s`, far above prior 8000 runs around
    `850-940 s`.  Treat this as an observed performance regression/anomaly
    requiring profiling of repeated component extraction and late terrain/audit
    stages before any large multi-seed promotion.
- Next implementation entry:
  - Define P111.6.19 as a narrow largest-land partition / lineage-readability
    pass.  It should not relax endpoint thresholds further and should not target
    closed-ocean score.  The likely target is to split the remaining largest
    component along already supported domain/lineage boundaries or inherited
    seaway/gateway systems while keeping major land components, ocean basins,
    P109 hypsometry, and closed-ocean score stable.
  - Define a separate performance investigation for high-resolution runs before
    attempting another 24000-cell generated-world audit.

### P111.6.19 Late Domain Partition

- Goal:
  - Address the residual P111 modern-planform failure after P111.6.18: the
    closed-ocean score is now acceptable, but the `8000/36p` seed-707 largest
    land component remains slightly above the preferred modern ceiling
    (`0.4787 > 0.45`).
  - Treat this as a readability problem in an overconnected terminal continent:
    if the largest land component already contains several significant
    continent/domain lineages, expose those seams as narrow seaways rather than
    relaxing endpoint polish thresholds or changing the global land budget.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p111619_late_domain_partition` in `terrain.py`, inserted after
    `_p110b_terminal_ribbon_recycle` and before
    `_p111617_late_closed_ocean_repair`.
  - The pass is gated to mature earthlike terminal worlds, modern planform
    targets, target land fraction `0.20-0.38`, largest land share above
    `terrain.p111_preferred_max_largest_land_share`, at least four major land
    components, at least two non-largest large components, and an effective
    largest-land domain count above `2.4`.
  - Candidate cuts are built from internal `continent_id` adjacency networks
    inside the largest land component, using domain floors `0.08`, `0.06`, and
    `0.05`.  Core craton/high-stability highlands are protected; margin,
    suture, accreted-terrane, low-relief, low-stability, and narrow-land cells
    are preferred.
  - Acceptance protects global land fraction, avoids overfragmentation
    (`major_count` must remain `4-7`), keeps the largest residual component in
    a modern range (`0.30` to preferred ceiling plus epsilon), and caps the
    cut area to a small world/largest-component fraction.
  - Accepted cuts are recorded as
    `terrain.p111619_late_domain_partition_seaways` and appended to
    `terrain.breakup_seaway_opened_corridors` so later terrain/object refresh
    stages protect them.
  - P107 audit now reports P111.6.19 telemetry under
    `p110a_modern_planform.seaway_cut_effectiveness`, including attempted,
    applied, candidate count, cut area fraction, largest share before/after,
    major component count before/after, domain count/floor, and land fraction
    before/after.
- Tests:
  - Added P111.6.19 audit-field coverage to
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics`.
  - Syntax command:
    `python3 -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - Broader related command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_late_closed_ocean_repair_reports_improvement_and_preserves_corridors -q`
    -> `85 passed`.
- 900-cell generated-world check:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111619_late_domain_partition_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `149.5 s`; land fraction `0.2767`; largest land share
    `0.4177`; second/third shares `0.2048/0.2048`; major land components `4`;
    closed-ocean score `0.0046`.
  - P111.6.19 telemetry: attempted `false`, applied `false`, candidate count
    `0`, largest share before/after `0.4177/0.4177`, land fraction before/after
    `0.2767/0.2767`.  This is the desired idle case because the largest land
    component is already below the preferred ceiling.
- 8000-cell archived-array replay:
  - Replayed `_p111619_late_domain_partition` directly on the P111.6.18
    `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` archive from
    `out_p111618_late_closed_ocean_repair_8000_seed707_20260701/00_8000cells_36p`.
  - The pass applied with domain floor `0.06`, candidate count `2`, domain
    count `10`, and cut area `0.01225` of world area.
  - Replay effect on the exact P111.6.18 residual arrays:
    largest land share `0.4787 -> 0.3786`; land fraction
    `0.2784 -> 0.2661`; the next components are about `0.1921`, `0.1902`,
    `0.1489`, and `0.0756`.
  - This is not a substitute for a full `8000/36p` regeneration, but it proves
    the new pass targets the isolated residual mechanism without needing a
    closed-ocean repair or endpoint-threshold relaxation.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.19 is a terminal guard for one specific failure mode: overconnected
    largest continents whose internal lineage/domain seams are present in the
    archive but not expressed in the final land/water mask.
  - It is intentionally idle on the 900 seed-707 map and active on the archived
    8000 P111.6.18 residual.  That behavior matches the intended scope.
  - The next plate-engineering step should be either a full `8000/36p`
    P111.6.19 audit if runtime is acceptable, or first a performance
    investigation of the P111.6.18 high-resolution anomaly before another
    expensive audit.

### P111.6.20 Runtime Observability And Bottleneck Triage

- Goal:
  - Investigate the P111.6.18 high-resolution runtime anomaly before launching
    another expensive `8000/36p` or `24000/60p` audit.
  - Keep the P111.6.19 domain-partition behavior unchanged while adding enough
    timing evidence to distinguish generation cost from terminal audit/write
    cost and to identify module-level bottlenecks.
- Implementation:
  - Added P107 audit `stage_seconds` and `total_seconds` per run.  The original
    `run_seconds` remains engine-run-only for compatibility with seed-sweep
    summaries.
  - Added scheduler `module_seconds` per `StepRecord`, and P107 audit now
    aggregates module totals/counts/top rows into each summary entry.  The CLI
    prints the top three module timers.
  - Cached parent-object lookup context in `TerrainModule` at object-set
    granularity, so repeated `_related_object_ids` calls reuse sanitized object
    cell arrays and weighted centroids instead of rebuilding them for every
    landform/component/object-set combination.
  - Replaced `BiosphereModule._generalize_biomes` per-cell `np.unique`
    neighbor-majority loops with an edge-vectorized code-count pass.
  - Optimized `TerrainModule._mask_width_steps` by replacing per-cell boundary
    neighbor checks with edge crossing detection and adding a packed-mask width
    cache for repeated `land`/`cont`/province masks inside the same run.
- Evidence gathered:
  - Interrupted 900-cell traces before the fixes showed repeated hotspots in
    `terrain._arc_plume_landform_objects -> _related_object_ids`,
    `biosphere._generalize_biomes`, and
    `terrain._smooth_modern_earthlike_coastlines -> _mask_width_steps`.
  - 900-cell stage-timing audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111619_stage_timing_900_seed707_v5_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `168.8 s`; `engine_run=168.746 s`; `terminal_audit_write=0.049 s`.
    This proves terminal audit/write is not the high-resolution bottleneck.
  - The same 900-cell run preserved the P111.6.19 idle result: largest land
    share `0.4177`, second/third `0.2048/0.2048`, major land components `4`,
    closed-ocean score `0.0046`, and P111.6.19 attempted/applied
    `false/false`.
  - 300-cell module-timing audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 300 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111619_module_timing_300_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `29.4 s`; top module totals were terrain `20.42 s`,
    tectonics `6.03 s`, climate `2.57 s`, biosphere `0.12 s`, and interior
    `0.08 s`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings`.
  - Added
    `tests/test_engine.py::test_biosphere_biome_generalization_uses_neighbor_majority`.
  - Added
    `tests/test_engine.py::test_terrain_mask_width_steps_uses_boundary_distance_and_cache`.
  - Syntax command:
    `python3 -m py_compile aevum/core/scheduler.py aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/modules/biosphere.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py aevum/cli.py tests/test_engine.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> passed.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_scheduler_refreshes_state_at_final_time tests/test_engine.py::test_terrain_mask_width_steps_uses_boundary_distance_and_cache tests/test_engine.py::test_biosphere_biome_generalization_uses_neighbor_majority tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - Broader related command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p110b_seed_sweep.py tests/test_engine.py::test_scheduler_refreshes_state_at_final_time tests/test_engine.py::test_terrain_mask_width_steps_uses_boundary_distance_and_cache tests/test_engine.py::test_biosphere_biome_generalization_uses_neighbor_majority tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_late_closed_ocean_repair_reports_improvement_and_preserves_corridors -q`
    -> `89 passed`.
- Interpretation:
  - The immediate anomaly is not JSON, NPZ, rendering, or Earth-reference I/O:
    no-render/no-contact/no-earth P107 writes are effectively free compared
    with `engine.run`.
  - Terrain is the dominant measured module at 300 cells and is the likely
    owner for most 900/8000 runtime work.  The targeted parent-object,
    biosphere smoothing, and mask-width changes remove three observed
    traceback bottlenecks, but the 900-cell runtime is still above the earlier
    P111.6.2-P111.6.16 `~88-104 s` range.
  - Follow-up evidence superseded this provisional next step in P111.6.21: the
    900-cell module-timing audit returned to a healthy runtime and the full
    `8000/36p` P111.6.19 audit was approved and completed.

### P111.6.21 Full 8000 P111.6.19 Validation

- Goal:
  - Replace the P111.6.19 archived-array replay with a full generated-world
    `8000/36p` seed-707 audit.
  - Verify that late domain partition fixes the residual largest-land P111
    failure in the real engine run, not only when replayed on saved arrays.
  - Confirm that the P111.6.20 runtime work makes the 8000 audit practical
    again before attempting any `24000/60p` rerun.
- 900-cell module-timing preflight:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111620_module_timing_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `76.7 s`; `engine_run=76.621 s`;
    `terminal_audit_write=0.046 s`.
  - Module totals: terrain `54.86 s`, tectonics `15.18 s`,
    climate `6.02 s`, biosphere `0.21 s`, interior `0.17 s`.
  - P111.6.19 stayed idle as intended on the already-good 900 map:
    largest land share `0.4177`, second/third `0.2048/0.2048`, major land
    components `4`, closed-ocean score `0.0046`,
    attempted/applied `false/false`.
- Full 8000-cell generated-world audit:
  - Audit command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111620_p111619_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `531.1 s`; `engine_run=530.993 s`;
    `terminal_audit_write=0.140 s`.
  - Module totals: terrain `387.81 s`, tectonics `101.93 s`,
    climate `38.76 s`, biosphere `1.03 s`, interior `0.92 s`.
  - Terminal planform: land fraction `0.2666`; largest/second/third land
    component shares `0.3788/0.1917/0.1899`; major land components `5`;
    largest ocean basin share `0.4420`; closed-ocean score `0.00494`;
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`; warning flags `[]`.
  - P111.6.19 telemetry: attempted `true`, applied `true`,
    candidate count `2`, cut area `0.01225` of world area, domain floor `0.06`,
    domain count `10`, largest land share `0.4796 -> 0.3788`, major land
    component count `4 -> 5`, land fraction `0.2789 -> 0.2666`.
  - P111.6.17 late closed-ocean repair stayed idle after the domain partition:
    attempted/applied `false/false`, final closed-ocean score `0.00494`.
- Seed-sweep gate:
  - Sweep command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111620_p111619_8000_seed707_20260702 --out out_p111620_p111619_8000_seed707_sweep_20260702 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A threshold pass `1/1`, P110B visual candidates `1/1`,
    P111 modern-planform candidates `1/1`, largest land share median/p90/max
    `0.379/0.379/0.379`, distribution flags `none`, P111 distribution flags
    `none`.
- Rendered QA assets:
  - Render command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111620_p111619_8000_seed707_20260702/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111620_p111619_8000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1440 --height 720`
    -> rendered `18` QA assets.
