    `object_masks.png`, `continental_detail_provinces.png`,
    `terrain_provinces.png`, and `p107_array_contact_sheet.png`.
  - Contact-sheet visual check: render is nonblank and the largest-land
    overconnection is visibly removed; the map now reads as a multi-landmass,
    open-ocean planform rather than a closed-ocean or overlarge-continent
    residual.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.19 is promoted from archived-array replay to a validated full
    `8000/36p` generated-world repair for seed 707.
  - The previous `closed_ocean_ring_score_soft` and largest-land P111
    blockers are both resolved on this calibration run.
  - Runtime is no longer blocking the 8000 tier: `531 s` is substantially below
    the earlier healthy `8000/36p` P111.6.x runs around `820-930 s` and far
    below the P111.6.18 anomaly.
  - Remaining plate-engineering work should move from this specific terminal
    planform blocker to broader validation: multi-seed `8000/36p`, a guarded
    `24000/60p` render, and qualitative review of terrain detail, orogenic
    continuity, ocean-floor age/depth structure, and object-layer readability.

### P111.6.22 Spine-Guided Orogen Continuity Repair

- Goal:
  - Address the current `24000/60p` visual residual where terminal planform is
    acceptable but 3000m+ high mountain cells still read as scattered peak
    blotches instead of coherent orogenic belts.
  - Use the already-semantic `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine` field as the
    authority for short crest/branch gap completion in the final P111 coherent
    orogen repair, rather than adding random uplift or broadening forelands.
- Implementation:
  - `_p111_coherent_orogen_belt_repair(...)` now accepts
    `orogenic_hierarchy_spine` and receives it from the production landform
    inventory call site.
  - When parent hierarchy and spine are present, the final repair adds
    spine-adjacent low crest/branch cells to the candidate shoulder set with
    lower thresholds than ordinary hierarchy cells.
  - The hierarchy repair budget is modestly relaxed only for spine-supported
    systems; the existing land-mask preservation, foreland exclusion, P109 p90,
    and `3000-4500m` high-tail guards still gate the result.
  - P107 high-mountain diagnostics now report
    `p111622_spine_guided_repair_used`,
    `p111622_spine_guided_candidate_cell_count`,
    `p111622_spine_guided_bridge_cell_count`,
    `p111622_high_component_delta`, and
    `p111622_fragmentation_delta`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111622_orogen_repair_uses_spine_to_connect_low_crest_gaps`.
  - Regression set passed:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111610_parent_hierarchy_refinement_bridges_short_axis_gap tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refinement_prunes_unsupported_foreland_and_extends_branch tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111614_hierarchy_spines_are_thin_subsets_of_refined_hierarchy tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111615_spine_width_morphology_adds_halo_without_pruning_high_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111616_high_latitude_apron_splits_broad_orogen_without_removing_extreme_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_high_mountain_coherence_raises_belts_and_suppresses_isolated_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111622_orogen_repair_uses_spine_to_connect_low_crest_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111611_orogen_repair_reports_blocked_hierarchy_path_without_forced_uplift -q`
    -> `12 passed`.
- Generated-world smoke evidence:
  - `900/36p` seed 707 full audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111622_spine_guided_budget_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `73.1 s`; terrain `52.4 s`; tectonics `14.5 s`;
    climate `5.7 s`.  The spine-guided candidate path was present, but the
    low-resolution high-tail guard reverted the actual bridge, preserving the
    existing high-mountain metrics.
  - `2500/36p` seed 707 full audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 2500 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111622_spine_guided_2500_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `170.8 s`; terrain `120.7 s`; tectonics `35.1 s`;
    climate `14.0 s`.  This seed is a terminal-supercontinent outlier
    (`largest_land_component_share=0.618`), so it is not a promoted visual
    planform sample; the high-tail guard also reverted the bridge.
- `24000/60p` seed-707 archived-array replay:
  - Replayed `_p111_coherent_orogen_belt_repair(...)` on
    `out_p111622_current_24000_seed707_20260702/00_24000cells_60p` arrays with
    the new spine input, without rerunning the full 30-minute engine path.
  - Result on the current 24000 terminal map: high-mountain components
    `83 -> 63`; top-3 high-mountain share `0.208 -> 0.292`; small high
    component area share `0.505 -> 0.313`; fragmentation index `1.000 -> 0.864`;
    high-mountain area fraction of continental land `0.0477 -> 0.0577`;
    land p90 `2076.7 m -> 2097.6 m`; changed cells `59`;
    hypsometry guard did not revert.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.22 is a real movement toward checklist item 1 on the current
    `24000/60p` visual artifact: it improves connected high mountain systems
    without broad highland overpaint.
  - It is not a full closure of mountain morphology.  The 24000 replay still
    leaves high-mountain fragmentation index `0.864`, so the next mountain
    step should move upstream into branch-spine generation and parent-line
    ordering, not just terminal elevation patching.
  - Full generated-world `8000/36p` or `24000/60p` rerun with P111.6.22 remains
    required before promotion; the replay is strong directional evidence but
    not a replacement for a full audit.

### P111.6.23 Branch-Spine Semantic Continuity

- Goal:
  - Move part of the P111.6.22 mountain-continuity fix upstream from terminal
    elevation repair into the orogenic hierarchy/spine semantic layer.
  - Reduce the case where branch ranges and branch spines are many short
    object fragments even when the broader `crest|branch` peak system is a
    coherent parent-line mountain system.
- Implementation:
  - Added a guarded branch-continuity refinement inside
    `_p11168_parent_orogen_hierarchy_masks(...)`, after P111.6.13 hierarchy
    geometry refinement and before P111.6.14 spine derivation is consumed by
    later morphology pruning.
  - The refinement may add branch connector cells only within continental,
    parent-near, non-foreland, low/mid-elevation support.  It is capped after
    application so branch area remains inside the existing P111.6.8 envelope.
  - Added an integrated peak-spine candidate from the combined `crest|branch`
    hierarchy.  It replaces the legacy crest/branch spine only when it reduces
    spine component count, preserves enough spine area, and keeps high legacy
    peak segments.
  - P107 now reports `p111623_refinement_accepted`,
    branch/peak/spine component counts before and after, bridge candidate and
    bridge area, path count, integrated-spine size, and whether the integrated
    spine was accepted.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111623_branch_spine_continuity_reduces_peak_system_fragmentation`.
  - Updated
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics`
    so the new P111.6.23 fields are locked into the audit schema.
  - Regression set:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111610_parent_hierarchy_refinement_bridges_short_axis_gap tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111613_hierarchy_geometry_refinement_prunes_unsupported_foreland_and_extends_branch tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111614_hierarchy_spines_are_thin_subsets_of_refined_hierarchy tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111623_branch_spine_continuity_reduces_peak_system_fragmentation tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111615_spine_width_morphology_adds_halo_without_pruning_high_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111616_high_latitude_apron_splits_broad_orogen_without_removing_extreme_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_high_mountain_coherence_raises_belts_and_suppresses_isolated_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1113_orogen_belt_repair_connects_parented_high_mountain_clusters tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11169_orogen_belt_repair_uses_parent_hierarchy_without_foreland_peaks tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111622_orogen_repair_uses_spine_to_connect_low_crest_gaps tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111611_orogen_repair_reports_blocked_hierarchy_path_without_forced_uplift -q`
    -> `14 passed`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `76 passed`.
- Generated-world smoke evidence:
  - `900/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111623_branch_spine_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `70.7 s`; terrain `50.6 s`; tectonics `14.2 s`;
    climate `5.3 s`.
  - P111.6.23 metrics on that run: peak components `4 -> 1`; spine
    components `3 -> 1`; branch components `3 -> 3`; bridge cells `5`;
    integrated spine accepted with `22` cells.
  - Planform gate remains green on the same smoke run:
    P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual `1/1`, P111 modern-planform `1/1`;
    land fraction `0.249`; largest/second/third land shares
    `0.429/0.223/0.210`; terminal supercontinent-like `false`;
    closed-ocean score `0.00296`.
- `24000/60p` seed-707 spine-only replay:
  - Applied the P111.6.23 integrated-spine rule to the archived
    `out_p111622_current_24000_seed707_20260702/00_24000cells_60p` hierarchy
    and spine arrays.
  - Legacy spine: `278` cells, `24` components, world-area fraction `0.01158`,
    high-spine cells `57`.
  - P111.6.23 candidate spine: `143` cells, `8` components, world-area
    fraction `0.00596`, high-spine cells `43`; accepted by the same component
    and area-preservation rule.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.23 improves the semantic line object that P111.6.22 consumes; this
    is closer to the desired architecture than only raising terrain at the end.
  - The 900 smoke confirms semantic spine improvement without breaking planform
    gates.  It does not yet solve high-mountain fragmentation by itself:
    terminal high-mountain fragmentation remains high, so the next step should
    couple the improved spine to terrain response and run an `8000/36p`
    generated-world validation before any new `24000/60p` full rerun.

### P111.6.24 Spine-to-Terrain Response

- Goal:
  - Convert the improved P111.6.23 orogenic spine semantics into terrain
    expression without painting broad high walls or increasing the already
    sensitive 3000-4500m high-mountain tail.
  - Distinguish two cases that were previously conflated: true high-shoulder
    gaps inside an already elevated crest may be connected as 3000m+ peak
    bridges, while low saddles on an otherwise continuous spine should become
    continuous mountain relief below 3000m instead of new extreme terrain.
- Implementation:
  - Added a P111.6.24 terminal response inside
    `_p111_coherent_orogen_belt_repair(...)`, after P111.6.22 path-candidate
    collection and before final land-mask validation.
  - High bridge response is now allowed only when the full gap between
    adjacent high-supported spine anchors is made of high-shoulder candidate
    cells.  This prevents partial bridge selection from creating new isolated
    3000m+ fragments.
  - Added a lower saddle response for mixed high-shoulder plus low-saddle gaps:
    the whole eligible spine gap may be raised into continuous sub-3000m
    mountain relief, capped below the high-mountain threshold and guarded by
    a p90 hypsometry check.
  - P107 high-mountain metrics now report P111.6.24 high response candidates,
    high bridge cells, saddle response cells, saddle guard reverts, high
    component/top3/fragmentation deltas, and the narrow case where the high
    tail guard accepts a small coherence-improving spine response.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111624_spine_to_terrain_response_lifts_continuous_crest_gaps`.
  - Updated
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p108_audit_reports_boundary_width_and_high_mountain_coherence`
    so P111.6.24 audit fields remain schema-locked.
  - Focused regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111624 or p111622 or high_mountain_coherence'`
    -> `4 passed, 73 deselected`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `77 passed`.
- Generated-world smoke evidence:
  - `900/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111624_saddle_response_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `72.8 s`; terrain `52.2 s`; tectonics `14.4 s`;
    climate `5.7 s`.
  - P111.6.24 metrics on that run: high-response candidates `5`, high bridge
    cells `0`, saddle response cells `8`, saddle response world-area fraction
    `0.00889`, saddle guard reverted `false`, high-tail guard reverted
    `false`.
  - High-mountain terminal metrics remain unchanged on this 900-cell case:
    `17` high cells, `8` high components, fragmentation index `0.913`.
    This is intentional for this seed: offline replay showed the candidate
    high path contained very low saddles, including a roughly `280m` relative
    cell, so forcing it to 3000m+ would create an artificial high wall.
  - P109 hypsometry remains inside envelope after saddle response:
    land mean `874 m`, p50 `564 m`, p90 `1990 m`, p98 `3293 m`, and
    `out_of_envelope_count = 0`.
  - Seed-sweep gate remains green:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p110b-seed-sweep out_p111624_saddle_response_900_seed707_20260702 --out out_p111624_saddle_response_900_seed707_sweep_20260702 --min-sample-size 1`
    -> P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual `1/1`, P111 modern-planform `1/1`,
    largest land share `0.429`, distribution flags `none`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.24 closes the immediate bug where a semantic spine could produce
    partial high-mountain repairs that worsened component fragmentation.
  - The remaining high-mountain fragmentation at 900 cells is not solved by
    terminal elevation alone because some semantic spine segments are genuine
    low saddles at that resolution.  The next calibration step should inspect
    an `8000/36p` generated-world run with rendered elevation/objects to decide
    whether residual fragmentation is visual, metric-threshold, or upstream
    hierarchy placement.

### P111.6.25 Final Abyssal-Depth Calibration

- Goal:
  - Address the next residual defect exposed by the P111.6.24 `8000/36p`
    rendered audit: ocean bathymetry remained too shallow at basin scale, with
    broad shallow ocean/rise regions and too little 4000-6000m abyssal floor.
  - Keep the correction in the final presentation layer so bathymetry tuning
    cannot feed back into sea-level, planform, or Wilson-cycle topology.
- Implementation:
  - Added a final-only P111.6.25 branch inside
    `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals(...)`.  It runs only while
    `terrain._p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_active` is set.
  - The calibration deepens three non-object-backed classes:
    `coast_pass == 2` slope cells below `900m`, `coast_pass == 3` rise cells
    below `2400m`, and far/open ocean floor using an age-depth target
    `3200m + 180m * sqrt(age_myr)`, clipped to `5600m`.
  - Ridge, trench, island-arc, seamount-chain, oceanic-plateau, and
    microcontinent support remains protected from this abyssal-floor clamp.
  - P107 ocean-feature metrics now report P111.6.25 candidate/adjusted ocean
    area fractions, slope/rise/far-ocean adjusted area fractions, and before /
    after ocean mean and p50 depth.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111625_abyssal_depth_calibration_deepens_slope_rise_and_far_ocean`.
  - Focused regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111625 or p1115_open_ocean_shoal or p11153 or p1116_final_ocean or p11163'`
    -> `6 passed, 72 deselected`.
  - Engine ocean regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge'`
    -> `3 passed, 48 deselected`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `78 passed`.
- Generated-world smoke evidence:
  - Initial attempt ran the calibration in the preterminal call as well as the
    final call.  That was rejected because a `900/36p` seed-707 smoke became
    terminal-supercontinent-like.  The implementation was then narrowed to
    final-only execution.
  - Final-only `900/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111625_final_only_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `72.3 s`; terrain `51.9 s`; P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual
    `1/1`, P111 modern-planform `1/1`, distribution flags `none`.
  - Final-only `8000/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111625_final_only_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `528.8 s`; terrain `386.4 s`; P109/P110A `1/1`,
    P110B visual `1/1`, P111 modern-planform `1/1`, distribution flags
    `none`.
  - `8000/36p` ocean-depth improvement relative to the P111.6.24 rendered
    audit:
    ocean mean depth `2143m -> 2607m`, p50 `2449m -> 2611m`,
    4000-6000m ocean fraction `0.054 -> 0.275`, and open-ocean shoal
    fraction `0.145 -> 0.096`.
  - P111.6.25 adjusted `0.420` of ocean area in the `8000/36p` run:
    slope floor `0.060`, rise floor `0.044`, and far-ocean age-depth floor
    `0.316`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.25 is a real correction to the earlier shallow-ocean bias and stays
    behind the final presentation boundary, so planform gates remain stable.
  - It is not a complete bathymetry closure.  Compared with same-grid ETOPO5,
    generated oceans still have too much shallow shelf/restricted/object-backed
    water and too little true 4000-6000m abyssal area.  The next ocean-floor
    step should target object-backed shoal footprint, shelf-width /
    restricted-sea calibration, and ridge-province width rather than making the
    abyssal clamp stronger.

### P111.6.26 Ridge-Flank Narrowing Spike

- Goal:
  - Test whether the largest remaining open-ocean shoal source after P111.6.25
    can be reduced by keeping only a narrow mid-ocean ridge axis shallow and
    deepening broad ridge-province flanks with the same age-depth floor.
- Implementation status:
  - Added an experimental, disabled-by-default ridge-flank narrowing branch
    behind `terrain.enable_p111626_ridge_flank_narrowing`.
  - The branch derives a generalized ridge axis plus one-cell halo from the
    final ridge zone, protects that narrow axis, and allows the remaining
    far-open ridge flank to be deepened by the P111.6.25 age-depth target.
  - P107 ocean-feature metrics report whether the branch was used, protected
    ridge-axis area, ridge-flank candidate/adjusted area, and ridge open-shoal
    before/after fractions.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111626_ridge_flank_narrowing_preserves_axis_and_deepens_flanks`,
    which explicitly enables the experimental flag.
  - Focused regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111626 or p111625 or p1116_final_open_ridge or p1116_final_ocean or p1115_open_ocean_shoal or p11153 or p11163'`
    -> `8 passed, 71 deselected`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `79 passed`.
- Generated-world evidence:
  - With the experimental flag enabled implicitly during the spike,
    `8000/36p` seed 707 reduced ridge open-ocean shoal from `0.063` to
    `0.033` of ocean area and raised ocean p50 depth from roughly `2611m` to
    `3017m`.
  - That same `8000/36p` run failed P110B/P111 seed-sweep promotion:
    P110B visual `0/1`, P111 modern-planform `0/1`, largest land share
    `0.486`, and review flags for preferred largest-landmass ceiling.
  - The production default was therefore changed back to disabled.  A default
    `900/36p` seed-707 smoke confirms the branch is off and gates remain green:
    P109/P110A `1/1`, P110B visual `1/1`, P111 modern-planform `1/1`, and
    distribution flags `none`.
- Interpretation:
  - The final-clamp idea is effective for bathymetric ridge-width appearance
    but is not robust enough for promotion.  The 8000 failure means ridge
    width should be fixed upstream in ridge/province/object generation and
    depth-province semantics, not by a stronger final bathymetry override.
  - P111.6.26 remains a useful microbenchmark and implementation spike, but
    the active production baseline remains P111.6.25.

### P111.6.27 Upstream Ridge-Axis Narrowing

- Goal:
  - Fix the residual shallow-ocean/ridge-width problem at its source instead
    of using the rejected P111.6.26 final clamp.
  - Treat raw ridge process masks and broad ridge provinces as support
    corridors, then derive a narrower spreading-center axis from youngest
    crust and coherent ridge support.
  - Keep bathymetry ridge provinces visible, but prevent `terrain.ocean_fabric`
    `spreading_center` objects from becoming wide shallow-ocean patches.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p111627_narrow_open_ocean_ridge_axis(...)` to derive a narrow
    open-ocean ridge axis from ridge support corridors.  The helper:
    uses low crust-age cells inside each coherent corridor, thins broad raw
    ridge masks before use, bridges only short process-supported gaps, and
    refuses incoherent raw fallback noise.
  - Added `_p111627_ridge_province_from_axis(...)` to build a controlled
    bathymetric ridge province around the axis.  This separates the visible
    shallow ridge province from the semantic line object.
  - Wired the same narrowed ridge semantics through `_ocean_geography(...)`,
    `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)`,
    `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals(...)`, and the final
    ocean-floor hierarchy pass.
  - Removed the old `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)` behavior that merged a broad
    age-core `ridge_province_axis` back into `spreading_center`.
  - Updated P107 ocean-feature metrics so `p1115_ridge_area_fraction_ocean`
    prefers semantic `terrain.ocean_fabric:spreading_center` objects and only
    falls back to raw ridge boundaries when no semantic object exists.  The
    broad depth province remains separately reported as
    `p1115_ridge_province_area_fraction_ocean`.
  - Added P111.6.27 audit fields:
    depth ridge province before/axis/after area fractions and spreading-center
    source/axis area fractions.
- Tests:
  - Added:
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111627_ocean_geography_narrows_broad_ridge_depth_province`,
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111627_ocean_fabric_uses_axis_instead_of_broad_raw_ridge_source`,
    and
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111627_ocean_feature_metrics_prefer_semantic_ridge_over_broad_raw_boundary`.
  - Focused regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111627 or p11152 or p111626 or p111625 or p1116_final_open_ridge or p1116_final_ocean or p11164_audit or p11165'`
    -> `13 passed, 69 deselected`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `82 passed`.
  - Engine ocean regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge'`
    -> `3 passed, 48 deselected`.
- Generated-world smoke evidence:
  - Final `900/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_900_seed707_final_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `73.7 s`; land fraction `0.266`, largest land share `0.318`,
    major land components `5`, major ocean basins `3`, terminal
    supercontinent-like `false`.
  - `900/36p` ridge evidence:
    raw/depth ridge province before `0.304` of ocean, depth ridge axis
    `0.053`, depth ridge province after `0.053`, spreading-center support
    source `0.201`, and spreading-center axis `0.041`.
    `object_ridge` linework: `22` cells, `4` components, isolated fraction
    `0.0`, branch fraction `0.228`, line coherence `0.806`.
  - Final `8000/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `517.5 s`; terrain `381.8 s`; land fraction `0.269`,
    largest land share `0.471`, major land components `4`, major ocean basins
    `3`, terminal supercontinent-like `false`, warning flags `none`.
  - `8000/36p` comparison against P111.6.25:
    open-ocean shoal `0.096 -> 0.091`, object-backed shoal `0.081 -> 0.050`,
    ridge object area `0.114 -> 0.012` of ocean, ridge depth province
    `0.112 -> 0.081` of ocean, and ocean p50 depth `2561m -> 2600m`.
  - `8000/36p` linework comparison:
    `object_ridge` shrank from `664` cells to `68` cells, branch fraction
    `0.852 -> 0.235`, and line coherence `0.331 -> 0.467`.
    The depth ridge province shrank from `651` cells to `472` cells but
    remains a broad bathymetric province with high branch fraction
    (`0.875`).
  - `8000/36p` oceanic crust structure remains usable:
    age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.932 -> 0.942`, old-minus-young
    median depth `2076m -> 2030m`, and far-minus-near-ridge median age
    `52.7 Myr -> 50.6 Myr`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.27 is accepted as the new production baseline for ridge/object
    semantics.  It directly addresses the P111.6.26 failure mode by moving
    ridge narrowing upstream and by keeping raw ridge masks out of the
    spreading-center object unless they survive coherent thinning.
  - The visible bathymetry ridge province remains intentionally wider than the
    semantic object so mid-ocean ridges are still legible in maps.  This means
    depth ridge province branch fraction is still high and should be handled in
    a later polyline/province-geometry pass rather than by making the
    spreading-center object wide again.
  - The `8000/36p` largest land component rose from `0.321` in P111.6.25 to
    `0.471` here.  It remains below the preferred `0.54` ceiling and did not
    trigger warning flags, but future ridge/province changes should watch this
    metric because P111.6.26 failed near the same planform boundary.

### P111.6.27 High-Resolution 24000-Cell Visual Audit

- Command:
  - Generated a fresh high-resolution world from the P111.6.27 baseline:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `1790.5 s`; terrain `1411.5 s`; tectonics `270.1 s`;
    climate `102.5 s`.
  - Rendered the raw arrays:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_20260702/00_24000cells_60p --out out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> `18` PNG assets plus `p107_array_contact_sheet.png`.
- Asset paths for visual review:
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/elevation.png`
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/ocean_depth_provinces.png`
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/object_masks.png`
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/tectonic_boundaries.png`
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/crust_age.png`
  - `out_p111627_ridge_axis_only_24000_seed707_render_20260702/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`
- Metrics:
  - P109 hypsometry: pass; out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - P110A planform: pass; land fraction `0.289`, largest/second/third land
    shares `0.300 / 0.298 / 0.158`, major land components `4`, major ocean
    basins `3`, terminal supercontinent-like `false`.
  - Ocean depth: mean depth after final calibration `2963m`, p50 `3215m`.
  - Oceanic crust structure: age-depth correlation `0.641`,
    age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.932`, old-minus-young median depth
    `2145m`, far-minus-near-ridge median age `42.0 Myr`.
  - Ridge semantics: `spreading_center` area `0.008` of ocean, ridge depth
    province `0.068` of ocean, P111.6.27 source/axis `0.089 -> 0.012`.
  - Open-ocean shoal remains high at `0.105` of ocean.  Unsupported
    open-ocean shoal is `0.064`; object-backed shoal is `0.041`.
- Visual assessment:
  - Passed:
    - Multi-continent/multi-ocean planform is substantially better than the
      earlier supercontinent-heavy outputs.
    - Ocean basins are broad and connected; largest ocean basin share remains
      in envelope and closed-ocean ring score is low (`0.010`).
    - Mid-ocean ridge objects are much narrower than before and no longer
      appear as giant shallow provinces in the object layer.
    - Crust-age map has a plausible first-order young-at-ridge to old-away
      structure.
  - Remaining defects:
    - Bathymetry still contains too many shallow open-ocean patches.  Array
      attribution shows the residual is not mainly `spreading_center`; it is
      concentrated in abyss/ridge depth provinces and in object layers such as
      `back_arc_basin`, `abyssal_hill`, and `abyssal_plain`.
    - `back_arc_basin` remains too broad and patch-like in the objects map,
      reading more like a shallow area fill than a tectonically constrained
      basin.
    - Raw tectonic boundaries are still scatter/short-polyline heavy,
      especially trench/active/collision systems.  Terrain objects are more
      useful than raw boundaries, but convergent-margin linework still needs
      the same upstream semantic treatment that ridges received.
    - Continental orogen objects are still fragmented into short black patches
      in some interiors.  Elevation shows improved relief hierarchy but still
      has local checkerboard/short-segment mountain expression.
    - Bathymetry rendering still visually mixes land/zero depth/shallow water;
      the map is useful for QA but not yet a polished presentation palette.
- Next repair target:
  - Start P111.6.28 from the 24000 evidence: reduce unsupported open-ocean
    shoal without erasing valid seamount chains, oceanic plateaus, island arcs,
    and microcontinents.
  - Concrete first steps:
    - Split `back_arc_basin` into tectonically parented narrow basins versus
      broad shallow fills.
    - Add a final-only floor for abyssal-plain/abyssal-hill cells that are
      shallow but not ridge/trench/island/plateau/microcontinent supported.
    - Keep ridge depth province visible, but prevent ridge-province shoulders
      from becoming large shallow ocean patches far from the semantic axis.
    - Add P107 metrics that attribute open-ocean shoal by object mask and depth
      province, so future runs can distinguish valid oceanic features from
      unsupported shallow floor.

### P111.6.28 Final Semantic Open-Ocean Shoal Cleanup

- Goal:
  - Close the main P111.6.27 high-resolution residual: unsupported shallow
    open-ocean patches in abyss/ridge depth provinces and in broad
    `back_arc_basin` / `abyssal_hill` / `abyssal_plain` object contexts.
  - Preserve legitimate object-backed shallow ocean relief: spreading-center
    axes, transform/fracture relief, trenches, seamount chains, island arcs,
    oceanic plateaus, and microcontinents.
  - Keep P107's diagnostic definition of unsupported open-ocean shoal aligned
    with the terrain pass that removes those shoals.
- Implementation:
  - Added a final semantic cleanup pass,
    `_p111628_final_semantic_open_ocean_shoal_cleanup(...)`, behind
    `terrain.enable_p111628_final_semantic_open_ocean_shoal_cleanup`
    (default on).
  - The pass runs after final P111.5 ocean-floor hierarchy.  It deepens cells
    that are:
    open ocean, shallower than `2600m`, and not supported by the semantic
    ocean object masks used by P107.
  - The cleanup depth is age aware: `2850m + 95 * sqrt(age_myr)`, clipped to
    `2850-3975m`.  This removes unsupported shallow patches without turning
    every valid young ridge/arc/plateau object into abyss.
  - P111.5 now receives the current-frame `terrain.ocean_fabric` and
    `terrain.arc_plume_landforms` instead of relying only on stale
    `world.objects` from the previous committed state.
  - After P111.5 modifies the final ocean surface, ocean geography and ocean
    object layers are rebuilt, then the semantic cleanup is run once more using
    the rebuilt `ocean.shelf_width` field.  If that second cleanup changes the
    surface, ocean geography and object layers are rebuilt again so exported
    arrays and P107 metrics agree.
  - P107 now reports:
    `p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_used`,
    `p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_candidate_area_fraction`,
    `p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_adjusted_area_fraction`,
    `p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_ridge_area_fraction`, and
    `p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_abyss_area_fraction`.
  - P111.6.28 attribution metrics also split residual shallow open ocean by
    depth province and object context:
    abyss, ridge province, back-arc basin, abyssal plain, and abyssal hill.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_deepens_only_unsupported_shoals`.
    It verifies that unsupported far-open shallow ocean is deepened while
    semantic spreading-center and seamount-chain shoals are preserved.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111628_ocean_feature_metrics_attribute_shoal_sources`
    to cover the new semantic-cleanup telemetry fields.
  - Focused regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111628 or p111627 or p111626 or p111625 or p1116_final_open_ridge or p1116_final_ocean or p1115_open_ocean_shoal or p11153'`
    -> `13 passed, 72 deselected`.
  - Engine ocean regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge'`
    -> `3 passed, 48 deselected`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `85 passed`.
- Generated-world evidence:
  - Final `900/36p` seed 707 after second semantic cleanup:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111628_semantic_shoal_cleanup_regeo2_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `76.9 s`; terrain `56.4 s`.
    Unsupported open-ocean shoal fell to `0.0`; object-backed open-ocean
    shoal is `0.060`; all remaining shallow open-ocean cells are
    object-backed.  This 900 run is useful for cleanup behavior but is not a
    planform promotion run because its coarse planform reports
    terminal-supercontinent-like despite staying inside the P110A envelope.
  - Final `8000/36p` seed 707:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111628_semantic_shoal_cleanup_regeo3_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `545.7 s`; terrain `406.9 s`; tectonics `99.2 s`;
    climate `37.1 s`.
  - `8000/36p` planform:
    land fraction `0.268`, largest/second/third land shares
    `0.295 / 0.207 / 0.159`, major land components `5`, major ocean basins
    `3`, terminal supercontinent-like `false`, restricted-ocean fraction
    `0.046`, closed-ocean ring score `0.0079`, and P110A envelope pass.
  - `8000/36p` ocean shoal comparison against P111.6.27:
    open-ocean shoal `0.091 -> 0.0546`, unsupported open-ocean shoal
    `0.0411 -> 0.00017`, object-backed open-ocean shoal
    `0.0501 -> 0.0545`.
  - `8000/36p` semantic-cleanup telemetry:
    first final shoal floor adjusted `0.0163` of ocean; ridge shoulder
    adjusted `0.0233`; final semantic cleanup adjusted `0.0207` of ocean,
    all recorded under abyss-side unsupported candidates.
  - `8000/36p` crust/depth structure remains coherent:
    age-depth correlation `0.543`, age-distance-from-ridge correlation
    `0.942`, old-minus-young median depth `1643m`, far-minus-near-ridge
    median age `50.1 Myr`, and nonridge-minus-ridge median depth `1584m`.
  - Residual:
    only one `8000` cell remains counted as unsupported shallow open ocean
    (`0.00017` of ocean).  It is a raw ridge-boundary cell in a ridge depth
    province, not a semantic spreading-center object.  This is below the
    current promotion threshold but should be rechecked visually at `24000`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.28 is accepted at `8000/36p` as a real bathymetry improvement:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal is effectively eliminated without erasing
    object-backed shallow ocean relief.
  - The main qualitative risk has moved from "unsupported shallow ocean
    patches" to "object-backed shallow ocean footprint": back-arc basins and
    other supported ocean objects now account for most remaining shallow
    open-ocean area.  That is preferable to unsupported noise, but the object
    footprint should be audited visually before a 24000 promotion.
  - The next validation step is a fresh `24000` P107 audit/render from the
    P111.6.28 code.  It should focus on whether back-arc/object-backed shoals
    read as tectonic features rather than broad patch fills, and whether the
    remaining one-cell raw-ridge residual disappears or remains visible at
    higher resolution.

### P111.6.29 Post-Ocean P109 Highland-Tail Retune

- Trigger:
  - A fresh P111.6.28 `24000/60p` high-resolution audit passed the ocean
    objective but failed P109 hypsometry by one item:
    `p90_m`.
  - Failing run:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111628_semantic_shoal_cleanup_regeo3_24000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `1817.3 s`; terrain `1441.4 s`; P109 out-of-envelope
    `['p90_m']`.
  - Metrics from that failing run:
    land p90 `1680m`, P109 generated-minus-earth p90 `-466m`, P109
    out-of-envelope count `1`; P110A planform passed and unsupported
    open-ocean shoal was already `0.0`.
- Diagnosis:
  - The existing `_p109_final_p90_floor_polish(...)` was running before the
    final P111 orogen/ocean-floor sequence had fully settled the terminal
    surface.
  - Offline replay on the final 24000 arrays showed that running the same
    polish once more after P111.5 would preserve the land/ocean mask and lift
    p90 from `1680m` to `1756m`, adjusting only about `0.82%` of world area.
  - The issue was therefore ordering, not a need for a new mountain generator
    or a broad uplift rule.
- Implementation:
  - Added a post-P111.5 call to `_p109_final_p90_floor_polish(...)`, after the
    final ocean-floor hierarchy, final ocean geography rebuild, and trench
    province marking, but before margin sequence and final hydrology compile.
  - The pass uses the existing supported-cell logic and preserves the final
    land/ocean mask.
  - Land-stage telemetry records this as
    `after_p1115_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor` when it changes the surface.
- Tests:
  - Existing direct test coverage for the polish remained valid:
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p109_final_p90_floor_polish_repairs_near_miss_without_planform_change`.
  - Focused regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p109_final_p90_floor or p109_final_lowland or p109_final_p90_ceiling or p109_continental_hypsometry or p111628 or p111627'`
    -> `11 passed, 74 deselected`.
  - Engine regressions:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'hypsometry_finish or patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge'`
    -> `3 passed, 48 deselected`.
  - Full P107 audit tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `85 passed`.
- `8000/36p` smoke:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `547.3 s`; terrain `407.9 s`.
  - Result:
    P109 pass; out-of-envelope count `0`; land p90 unchanged at `2051m`
    because the new final p90 floor did not need to activate.
  - P110A and ocean metrics were unchanged from P111.6.28:
    major land components `5`, major ocean basins `3`, terminal
    supercontinent-like `false`, unsupported open-ocean shoal `0.00017`,
    object-backed open-ocean shoal `0.0545`.
- Final `24000/60p` high-resolution evidence:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `1803.7 s`; terrain `1428.9 s`; tectonics `267.7 s`;
    climate `100.8 s`.
  - Render:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_20260702/00_24000cells_60p --out out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> `18` PNG assets.
  - Final asset paths:
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/elevation.png`
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/ocean_depth_provinces.png`
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/object_masks.png`
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/tectonic_boundaries.png`
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/crust_age.png`
    - `out_p111629_post_ocean_p109_p90_floor_24000_seed707_render_20260702/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`
  - P109:
    pass; out-of-envelope count `0`; land mean `756m`, p50 `619m`,
    p90 `1756m`, p98 `3071m`, max `4817m`.  The final p90 floor adjusted
    `0.00525` of world area and preserved the land/ocean mask.
  - P110A:
    pass; land fraction `0.286`, largest/second/third land shares
    `0.286 / 0.281 / 0.144`, major land components `5`, major ocean basins
    `3`, terminal supercontinent-like `false`, restricted-ocean fraction
    `0.0275`, closed-ocean ring score `0.0065`.
  - Ocean:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal `0.0`; object-backed open-ocean shoal
    `0.0584`; total open-ocean shoal `0.0584`.
    Ocean mean depth `2993m`, p50 `3359m`.
  - Oceanic crust:
    age-depth correlation `0.730`, age-distance-from-ridge correlation
    `0.920`, old-minus-young median depth `1696m`,
    far-minus-near-ridge median age `41.4 Myr`, and nonridge-minus-ridge
    median depth `1595m`.
  - Linework:
    `spreading_center` object area `0.0072` of ocean; ridge depth province
    `0.0735` of ocean.  Object ridge line coherence `0.456`; object trench
    line coherence `0.142`; object transform line coherence `0.368`.
- Current high-resolution baseline status:
  - P111.6.29 is accepted as the current `24000` baseline for the plate/
    terrain stage.
  - The original user-visible ocean artifact, unsupported shallow patches in
    open ocean, is fixed at both `8000` and `24000`.
  - P109/P110A high-resolution gates now pass together on the same run.
- Remaining issues for the next plate-engineering phase:
  - Object-backed shallow ocean is now the dominant residual (`0.0584` of
    ocean).  The next ocean task should narrow broad back-arc basins and audit
    whether object-backed shoals read as tectonic features rather than broad
    shallow fills.
  - Boundary linework is still too scatter/short-polyline heavy, especially
    trench and active/collision systems.  Convergent margins need the same
    upstream semantic polyline treatment that ridges received.
  - Ridge depth province is still visually broad and branch-heavy even though
    the semantic spreading-center object is narrow.
  - Continental mountain systems are better but still not fully Earth-like:
    orogenic belts remain locally fragmented, and high terrain is still a
    calibrated tail rather than a fully process-derived mature mountain/plateau
    architecture.

### P111.6.30 Object-Halo Shoal Cleanup

- Trigger:
  - P111.6.29 fixed unsupported open-ocean shoals, but the remaining
    object-backed shallow ocean was still broad: `0.0545` of ocean at
    `8000/36p` and `0.0584` at `24000/60p`.
  - Array attribution on the `24000` baseline showed three residual owners:
    real broad cores such as microcontinents/oceanic plateaus, narrow object
    cores such as ridges/seamount chains, and one-cell support halos around
    narrow objects.  The halo class was semantically useful as support, but it
    should not itself preserve widespread <2600m open-ocean shoals.
- Implementation:
  - Split final semantic support in
    `_p111628_final_semantic_open_ocean_shoal_cleanup(...)` into:
    narrow object core, broad object core, and narrow-object support halo.
  - Kept shallow relief on real object cores:
    `spreading_center`, `transform_fault`, `fracture_zone`, `trench`,
    `seamount_chain`, `hotspot_track`, `island_arc`, `oceanic_plateau`,
    `large_igneous_province`, and `microcontinent`.
  - Added a final P111.6.30 halo floor for open-ocean halo cells shallower
    than `2600m`, deepening them to an age-aware `2650-3480m` floor.
  - Added audit telemetry:
    `p111630_object_halo_shoal_cleanup_*`,
    `p111630_object_backed_shoal_narrow_core_fraction`,
    `p111630_object_backed_shoal_halo_only_fraction`,
    `p111630_object_backed_shoal_broad_core_fraction`, and per-object shoal
    attribution for microcontinents, oceanic plateaus, seamount chains, and
    island arcs.
- Tests:
  - Direct cleanup test now verifies that supported object cores remain
    shallow, the unsupported open-ocean cell is deepened, and the one-cell
    supported halo is also floored.
  - Feature metrics test now verifies P111.6.30 cleanup telemetry and
    per-object residual attribution.
  - Commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111628_final_semantic_cleanup_deepens_only_unsupported_shoals tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111628_ocean_feature_metrics_attribute_shoal_sources tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_feature_metrics_do_not_treat_ridge_province_as_object_backed tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_microcontinent_requires_more_than_continent_id -q`
    -> `6 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111630 or p111628 or p111627 or p111626 or p111625 or p1116_final_open_ridge or p1116_final_ocean or p1115_open_ocean_shoal or p11153'`
    -> `13 passed, 72 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'hypsometry_finish or patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge'`
    -> `3 passed, 48 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `85 passed`.
- `8000/36p` evidence:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111630_object_halo_shoal_cleanup_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `553.0 s`; terrain `413.7 s`; tectonics `99.7 s`;
    climate `37.0 s`.
  - P109/P110A stayed green:
    P109 out-of-envelope `0`; P110A pass; land fraction `0.268`,
    largest/second/third land shares `0.295 / 0.207 / 0.159`,
    major land components `5`, major ocean basins `3`,
    terminal supercontinent-like `false`, restricted-ocean fraction `0.0463`.
  - Ocean comparison against P111.6.29:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal `0.00017 -> 0.0`;
    object-backed open-ocean shoal `0.0545 -> 0.0241`;
    object-backed ocean relief footprint unchanged at `0.1812`.
  - P111.6.30 telemetry:
    halo cleanup used `true`; adjusted `0.0137` of ocean.
    Remaining object-backed shoal breaks down as narrow core `0.0128`,
    halo-only `0.00017`, broad core `0.0113`, microcontinent `0.0029`,
    oceanic plateau `0.0084`, seamount chain `0.0031`, island arc `0.0`.
  - Oceanic crust/linework did not regress:
    age-depth correlation `0.543 -> 0.565`,
    age-distance-from-ridge correlation `0.942 -> 0.942`,
    ridge/trench/transform line coherence unchanged at
    `0.426 / 0.142 / 0.298`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.30 is accepted at `8000/36p` as a focused cleanup: it removes most
    shallow-water support halo without altering planform, land hypsometry, or
    semantic linework.
  - A fresh `24000/60p` promotion later confirmed the same improvement:
    P109 pass, P110A pass, land fraction `0.286`,
    largest/second/third land shares `0.286 / 0.281 / 0.144`,
    major land components `5`, major ocean basins `3`, terminal
    supercontinent-like `false`, and restricted-ocean fraction `0.0275`.
    Object-backed open-ocean shoal fell `0.0584 -> 0.0334`; halo-only
    object-backed shoal fell to `0.0`.
  - P111.6.30 replaced P111.6.29 as the high-resolution ocean-halo baseline,
    but the `24000` residual was now dominated by microcontinents:
    microcontinent shoal `0.0226`, oceanic plateau shoal `0.00233`,
    seamount-chain shoal `0.00111`.
  - The next ocean-floor task should not broadly deepen all remaining
    object-backed shoal.  The residual is now mostly real object core:
    oceanic plateaus, microcontinents, and seamount chains need area/frequency
    and emergence rules, not another generic floor.

### P111.6.31 Terminal Microcontinent Footprint Cap

- Trigger:
  - P111.6.30 fixed narrow-object halos, but the `24000` residual shoal was
    still mostly broad microcontinent/core support rather than ocean-floor
    process texture.
  - Component attribution on the `24000` arrays showed `24` microcontinent
    objects covering `0.0299` of ocean; `0.0226` of ocean remained shallow
    open-ocean microcontinent shoal.  Most individual components were small,
    but their combined shallow footprint read visually as scattered open-ocean
    shelf patches.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p111631_limited_ocean_object_footprint(...)`, a weighted component
    budget helper for broad oceanic object candidates.
  - Applied the cap to terminal earthlike microcontinent object generation:
    keep compact, process-backed cores with terrane/accreted/arc/rift support;
    cap total microcontinent footprint to about `0.018` of ocean, component
    footprint to about `0.0032` of ocean, and terminal component count to `18`.
  - Applied the same cap to final ocean-floor relief expression.  Microcontinent
    cores can remain shallow, but rejected shallow continental-fragment cells
    are deepened to an age-aware abyssal floor instead of retaining broad
    `1750-2450m` water depth.
  - Gated the cap to `_p11163_terminal_stage(world)` after an intermediate
    run showed that late-stage feedback could change sea level/planform if the
    rule ran throughout the post-3500 Myr evolution.
  - Added a post-P109 terminal semantic cleanup pass so cells exposed by final
    object recapping are reclassified with current ocean geography and objects
    before export.
  - Added audit telemetry:
    `p111631_microcontinent_raw_area_fraction_ocean`,
    `p111631_microcontinent_area_fraction_ocean`,
    `p111631_microcontinent_removed_area_fraction_ocean`,
    `p111631_microcontinent_relief_*`, and
    `p111631_rejected_microcontinent_shoal_deepened_fraction_ocean`.
- Tests:
  - Added direct coverage that broad microcontinent candidates are capped to
    compact cores instead of all being exported as objects.
  - Added final-relief coverage that broad microcontinent candidates no longer
    keep the whole candidate region shallower than `2600m`.
  - Regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111631 or p111630 or p111628 or p11152 or p11153 or p1115_open_ocean_shoal'`
    -> `12 passed, 75 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'hypsometry_finish or patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge or arc_plume_landforms'`
    -> `4 passed, 47 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `87 passed`.
- `8000/36p` evidence after terminal gating:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111631c_terminal_only_microcontinent_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `563.2 s`; terrain `422.8 s`.
  - P109/P110A unchanged from P111.6.30:
    land fraction `0.268`, largest/second/third land shares
    `0.295 / 0.207 / 0.159`, major land components `5`, major ocean basins
    `3`, restricted-ocean fraction `0.0463`.
  - Ocean:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal stayed `0.0`; object-backed open-ocean shoal
    improved `0.0241 -> 0.0207`; microcontinent shoal improved
    `0.00290 -> 0.00103`.  The terminal cap reduced microcontinent object
    area by `0.0343` of ocean at this scale.
- `24000/60p` evidence:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111631c_terminal_only_microcontinent_24000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`

    -> `18` PNG assets.

    it removes the broad microcontinent shallow-patch look that remained after
