    are now structural rather than color/bathymetry artifacts:
    trench/active-margin linework is too fragmented, and high mountains still
    form many small peak components instead of long, parented orogenic systems.

### P111.6.32 Parent-Guided Terminal Trench Linework

- Problem:
  - P111.6.31c fixed broad open-ocean shallow microcontinent artifacts, but
    terminal trench objects remained too fragmented.  On the accepted
    `24000/60p` baseline, `terrain.margin_landforms` trench linework had
    `66` components and line-coherence only `0.122`, despite
    `trench_subduction_attachment_fraction=0.990`.
  - Code attribution:
    `p107_boundary_linework` correctly preferred `terrain.margin_landforms`
    trench objects, so the issue was not rendering or audit selection.
    `_coherent_subduction_trench_axis` used ordered
    `subduction_parent/convergent_parent` objects only as weak support near
    raw trench/province cells; it did not use them to bridge parent-supported
    short gaps or prune scattered terminal trench fragments.
- Implementation:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p111632_parent_guided_trench_axis`.
  - The refinement is terminal-stage only and requires existing trench anchors:
    parent lines alone cannot create new trench objects.
  - It builds a narrow allowed corridor from:
    existing trench axis, `subduction_parent`, and only the part of
    `convergent_parent` near subduction/trench support.
  - It bridges short endpoint gaps with `_p11164_bridge_line_endpoints`, runs
    the existing deep-zone linearity filter, removes unsupported components,
    prunes branchy newly-added cells, and accepts only if:
    component count decreases, area stays inside a guarded range, trench area
    remains narrow, and parent overlap does not regress.
  - Added audit telemetry under `p111632_parent_trench_linework`:
    accepted, component before/after, ocean-area before/after, parent-overlap
    before/after, added area, and removed area.
- Tests:
  - Added direct coverage that a terminal subduction parent line can bridge
    short trench gaps and reduce components without excessive area growth.
  - Added direct coverage that parent objects alone do not create trench when
    no trench anchor exists.
  - Regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111632 or p11167 or p11166 or p11163 or p11162 or p1116_audit_reports_boundary_linework'`
    -> `22 passed, 67 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `89 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py -q -k 'hypsometry_finish or patchy_open_ocean or age_derived_open_ocean_ridge or arc_plume_landforms'`
    -> `4 passed, 47 deselected`.
- `8000/36p` evidence:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `554.5 s`; terrain `416.1 s`.
  - Render:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_20260702/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> `18` PNG assets.
  - Key asset paths:
    - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702/elevation.png`
    - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`
    - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702/tectonic_boundaries.png`
    - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702/object_masks.png`
    - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`
  - Acceptance stayed stable:
    P109 within envelope, P110A within modern-planform envelope, land fraction
    unchanged at `0.268`, major land components `5`, major ocean basins `3`.
  - Ocean/shoal side effects stayed small:
    unsupported open-ocean shoal remained `0.0`; object-backed open-ocean
    shoal changed only `0.02066 -> 0.02083`; microcontinent shoal remained
    `0.00102`.
  - Trench linework improved:
    components `48 -> 33`; line coherence `0.142 -> 0.340`; top-3 component
    share `0.154 -> 0.375`; endpoint fraction `0.344 -> 0.196`;
    attachment and deep attachment stayed `1.0`.
  - P111.6.32 telemetry:
    accepted `true`; component before/after `48 -> 33`;
    trench ocean-area fraction `0.0422 -> 0.0487`;
    parent overlap `0.935 -> 0.944`; added area `0.00649` of ocean;
    removed area `0.0`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.32 is accepted at `8000/36p` as a structural linework improvement.
    It makes trench/active-margin expression more arc-like without changing
    the planet-scale planform or reopening the shallow-patch problem.
  - It is not yet promoted to the `24000/60p` visual baseline.  The next
    promotion step is a `24000` audit/render if trench linework remains the
    highest-priority issue; otherwise proceed to high-mountain/orogen
    coherence, where visual residuals are now more apparent.

#### P111.6.32 `24000/60p` promotion check

- Command:
  `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
  -> runtime `1913.3 s`; terrain `1519.7 s`; tectonics `280.0 s`;
  climate `106.8 s`.
- Render:
  `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_20260702/00_24000cells_60p --out out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
  -> `18` PNG assets.
- Key asset paths:
  - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_render_20260702/elevation.png`
  - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_render_20260702/bathymetry_shelf_slope_abyss.png`
  - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_render_20260702/tectonic_boundaries.png`
  - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_render_20260702/object_masks.png`
  - `out_p111632_parent_trench_linework_24000_seed707_render_20260702/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`
- Acceptance:
  - P109 stayed within envelope, out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - P110A stayed within the modern-planform envelope, warning count `0`.
  - Sea level and planform were unchanged from P111.6.31c:
    sea level `-2828.4m`; land fraction `0.303`;
    largest/second/third land shares `0.292 / 0.277 / 0.153`;
    major land components `5`, major ocean basins `3`;
    terminal-supercontinent-like `false`.
- Ocean/shoal side effects:
  - Unsupported open-ocean shoal improved `0.000060 -> 0.0`.
  - Object-backed open-ocean shoal increased modestly
    `0.01039 -> 0.01302`.
  - Microcontinent shoal increased `0.00119 -> 0.00388` but stayed far below
    the pre-P111.6.31 broad-shoal failure mode.
  - Oceanic plateau and seamount-chain shoal fractions stayed unchanged
    (`0.00239` and `0.00090`).
- Trench linework:
  - P111.6.32 accepted at `24000/60p`.
  - Components improved `66 -> 55`; line coherence `0.122 -> 0.161`;
    top-3 component share `0.133 -> 0.178`;
    endpoint fraction `0.356 -> 0.266`.
  - Trench area grew only `0.01847 -> 0.01978` of ocean.
  - Parent overlap improved `0.822 -> 0.834`; trench attachment stayed
    `0.991`, deep-trench attachment stayed `1.0`.
  - Ridge/transform/convergent line-coherence metrics were unchanged.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.32 is promoted as the current `24000` terminal linework baseline
    because it passes P109/P110A, removes the one-cell unsupported shoal
    residual, and improves trench connectivity without broadening into a
    province-scale band.
  - The improvement is intentionally modest at high resolution.  It does not
    close the structural checklist by itself: trench/active-margin linework is
    more readable but still not as clean as real subduction arc systems.
  - The next blocker for plate-engineering closure is now high-mountain and
    orogenic-system morphology: high mountains remain too fragmented, with
    weak main-ridge/branch/foreland expression and insufficient continuous
    orogenic belts.

### P111.6.33 Planned: Orogen Belt Morphology Closure

- Current-state attribution from the P111.6.32 `24000/60p` arrays:
  - Terminal high mountains (`>=3000m` relative to sea level) occupy only
    `0.0108` of world area but are split into `59` components.
  - Extreme mountains (`>=4500m`) occupy `0.00125` of world area and are split
    into `22` components.
  - High-mountain parentage is not the blocker:
    `high_mountain_parent_overlap_fraction = 1.0`, and mountain-range overlap
    is `0.985`.
  - The semantic orogenic hierarchy is much more coherent than the high-tail
    terrain response:
    crest hierarchy has `702` cells, `8` components, top-3 share `0.913`;
    branch hierarchy has `248` cells, `39` components, top-3 share `0.456`;
    crest/branch spine has `234` cells but `38` components.
  - High-mountain cells overlap the orogenic hierarchy, but not enough of the
    hierarchy becomes readable terrain:
    high cells overlap crest by `0.446`, branch by `0.181`, and spine by
    `0.288`.
  - The top three peak-hierarchy systems each contain many separate high
    subcomponents: `18`, `19`, and `9` high components inside otherwise
    coherent crest/branch systems.
- Negative result:
  - A quick offline replay that simply raised `2400-3000m` saddle cells along
    short paths did not materially improve high-mountain fragmentation:
    best tested case only changed high components `59 -> 57`.
  - Therefore the next fix should not be a wider threshold patch that forces
    low saddles into `3000m+` terrain.  That would risk artificial high walls
    and violate the earlier P111.6.24 guard rationale.
- Design target:
  - Treat the orogen as a morphology system, not only a high-elevation mask.
  - Produce readable continuous mountain belts with:
    main ridge line, branch ranges, high shoulders, foreland slope, and apron.
  - Keep extreme peaks as local maxima on a continuous belt, rather than trying
    to make all `3000m+` cells one connected object.
- Planned implementation route:
  1. Add an orogenic-belt morphology metric beside
     `high_mountain_coherence`, measuring continuity of `1800m+`, `2400m+`,
     and `3000m+` relief within `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy` and
     `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`.
  2. Improve `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine` generation so branch spines
     are fewer, longer, and more nested under coherent crest systems.
     Current branch spine has `23` components at `24000`; this is a structural
     input to broken terrain expression.
  3. Add a graded terrain response:
     - crest spine: continuous `2600-3300m` high ridge where existing relief
       and parent score justify it;
     - branch spine: `1800-2800m` branch ranges;
     - shoulder halo: `900-2200m` flanks and foothills;
     - foreland/apron: low-gradient transition, explicitly below high-peak
       threshold.
  4. Keep P109/P110A and land-mask guards:
     p90/p98 land hypsometry must not move outside envelope, high-tail area
     cannot jump sharply, and foreland cells must not become new peaks.
  5. Validate in tiers:
     targeted tests for a synthetic crest/branch system, `900/36p` smoke,
     `8000/36p` generated-world audit/render, then only promote after a
     `24000/60p` run.
- Intended success signal:
  - Orogen belts should read as continuous systems in `elevation.png`,
    `orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png`, and
    `orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png`.
  - The existing `3000m+` high-mountain fragmentation metric may improve, but
    it is no longer the sole acceptance signal; real Earth-like mountain belts
    can contain discrete high peaks embedded in continuous lower relief.

#### P111.6.33a/b checkpoint: belt morphology metric and first terrain response

- Implemented P111.6.33a audit metric:
  - Added `p111633_orogenic_belt_morphology` with schema
    `aevum.p111633_orogenic_belt_morphology.v1`.
  - The metric reports component/area/top-3/small-fragment stats for:
    peak hierarchy, crest, branch, foreland, spine, spine crest/branch,
    shoulder halo, and highland apron.
  - It also reports relief-tier continuity for `1800m+`, `2400m+`,
    `3000m+`, and `4500m+` inside the peak hierarchy:
    coverage, spine overlap, crest/branch coverage,
    components per parent hierarchy component, and a graded belt-continuity
    score.
  - Current P111.6.32 `24000/60p` baseline from the new metric:
    graded belt-continuity score `0.288`; hierarchy components `7`;
    spine components `22`; spine components per hierarchy component `3.14`;
    relief components `1800m:50`, `2400m:58`, `3000m:46`;
    high-peak fragmentation pressure `6.57`; worst parent hierarchy contains
    `19` high subcomponents; near-high saddles `49` cells.
- Implemented P111.6.33b first terrain response:
  - Added `terrain.enable_p111633_belt_relief_response` default-on gate.
  - The pass works only inside `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy`
    crest/branch cells, near `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`, and excludes
    foreland/collision-plateau inventory.
  - It first tries to connect existing `1800m+` relief segments along spine
    and parent-hierarchy paths; if no safe full path exists, it can still lift
    adjacent low-saddle cells as a local belt shoulder response.
  - Target relief remains below `3000m`, so the pass does not create new
    high-mountain pixels; it is intended to make continuous belts/shoulders
    more readable before any later high-tail refinement.
  - Guardrails:
    land/ocean mask must be preserved; land p90 cannot exceed
    `max(2700m, p90_before + 80m)`; `2400-3000m` land band cannot exceed
    `max(0.120, band_before + 0.035)`; violations revert this response.
  - Added telemetry:
    response used, candidate/bridge cell count and area fraction,
    `1800m` and `2400m` component count before/after, and guard reverted.
- Tests:
  - Added audit schema coverage for P111.6.33 fields and telemetry.
  - Added synthetic terrain coverage where disconnected `1800m+` crest
    segments become more continuous while no new `3000m+` high peaks appear.
  - Regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111633 or p111624 or p111622 or high_mountain_coherence'`
    -> `6 passed, 85 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `91 passed`.
- `900/36p` generated-world smoke:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111633_belt_relief_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `78.9s`; terrain `57.8s`; tectonics `14.8s`.
  - P110A stayed inside the modern-planform envelope.
  - P111.6.33 response was used; candidate cells `9`, bridge cells `4`;
    guard reverted `false`.
  - `1800m+` parent-hierarchy components improved `5 -> 4`;
    `2400m+` components stayed `4 -> 4`.
  - Terminal P111.6.33 morphology after the response:
    graded belt-continuity score `0.587`;
    relief components `1800m:4`, `2400m:4`, `3000m:2`;
    relief top-3 shares `1800m:0.950`, `2400m:0.900`;
    high-peak fragmentation pressure `0.667`;
    worst parent high-subcomponent count `1`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.33a/b is a valid first implementation checkpoint, not a `24000`
    promotion.
  - The response is intentionally conservative.  It improves lower-relief
    belt continuity and shoulder readability without using a broad
    `3000m+` uplift patch.
  - Remaining risk is visual: `24000` high mountains are still expected to
    look too fragmented until the next step improves spine generation and
    produces longer coherent branch/main-ridge systems.
- `8000/36p` validation of P111.6.33b:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111633_belt_relief_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `569.0s`; terrain `427.6s`.
  - Render:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111633_belt_relief_8000_seed707_20260702/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111633_belt_relief_8000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> `18` PNG assets.
  - P109 and P110A stayed inside envelope.
  - Belt relief response was used; candidate cells `40`, bridge cells `8`;
    `1800m+` components `24 -> 19`, `2400m+` components `23 -> 17`;
    guard reverted `false`.
  - Terminal morphology: hierarchy components `19`, spine components `7`,
    graded belt-continuity score `0.561`, high-peak fragmentation pressure
    `0.895`, high-mountain components `19`.
  - Visual result:
    lower-relief belts improved, but the map still showed many small final
    peak-hierarchy remnants without spine support.  This confirmed that the
    next fix had to be upstream hierarchy/spine cleanup, not another elevation
    uplift patch.

#### P111.6.33c checkpoint: final spine/hierarchy coherence

- Implemented P111.6.33c final spine refinement:
  - Added `terrain.enable_p111633c_final_spine_refinement` default-on gate.
  - After P111.6.15/P111.6.16 pruning and high-latitude reclassification, the
    pass audits final `crest|branch` components against the current spine.
  - Tiny final peak components with no spine support and no `>=3300m` extreme
    cell are reclassified out of peak hierarchy:
    below `2500m` becomes shoulder halo; `2500-3300m` becomes highland apron.
  - Final crest/branch spines are then recomputed as thin paths on the final
    hierarchy and merged with preserved legacy spine cells.
  - Added telemetry:
    final-spine refinement accepted, peak components before/after,
    spineless peak components before/after, orphan peak/high/extreme cells
    reclassified, and final spine cell/component count.
- Tests:
  - Extended audit schema coverage for the P111.6.33c telemetry.
  - Regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111614 or p111615 or p111616 or p111623 or p111633 or p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics'`
    -> `7 passed, 84 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `91 passed`.
- `900/36p` smoke:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111633c_final_spine_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `72.3s`; terrain `53.4s`.
  - P109 and P110A stayed inside envelope.
  - P111.6.33c accepted; final spine cells `29`, final spine components `3`.
  - No orphan peak fragments required reclassification in this seed.
  - P111.6.33b response still worked: bridge cells `4`, `1800m+`
    components `5 -> 3`.
- `8000/36p` validation after P111.6.33c:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111633c_final_spine_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `533.9s`; terrain `400.7s`.
  - Render:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111633c_final_spine_8000_seed707_20260702/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111633c_final_spine_8000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> `18` PNG assets.
  - P109 and P110A stayed inside envelope.
  - Final spine refinement accepted:
    peak components `16 -> 6`, spineless peak components `11 -> 0`,
    orphan peak cells reclassified `11`, orphan high/extreme cells
    reclassified `0 / 0`.
  - P111.6.33b belt response became more effective on the cleaned hierarchy:
    bridge cells `11`; `1800m+` components `22 -> 14`;
    `2400m+` components `20 -> 12`.
  - Terminal morphology improved versus P111.6.33b:
    hierarchy components `19 -> 12`, graded belt-continuity score
    `0.561 -> 0.672`, high-peak fragmentation pressure `0.895 -> 0.750`,
    worst parent high-subcomponents `4 -> 3`, relief components
    `1800m:19 -> 14`, `2400m:17 -> 12`, `3000m:17 -> 9`,
    high-mountain components `19 -> 12`, high-mountain fragmentation
    `0.493 -> 0.438`.
- Interpretation:
  - P111.6.33c fixes the observed final hierarchy/spine mismatch at `8000`.
  - This is still not full plate-engineering closure: broad continental
    interior physiographic provinces and 24000-scale visual quality remain to
    be checked.
  - Next validation step is a `24000/60p` promotion check for P111.6.33c.  If
    the high-resolution map preserves the 8000 gains and passes P109/P110A,
    the remaining plate-engineering work can shift from active orogen
    coherence to continental interior province expression.

#### P111.6.33c `24000/60p` promotion check

- Command:
  `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 24000 --n-plates 60 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
  -> runtime `2110.2s`; terrain `1685.3s`; tectonics `301.1s`;
  climate `116.4s`.
- Render:
  `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_20260702/00_24000cells_60p --out out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
  -> `18` PNG assets.
- Key asset paths:
  - `out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_render_20260702/elevation.png`
  - `out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_render_20260702/orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png`
  - `out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_render_20260702/orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png`
  - `out_p111633c_final_spine_24000_seed707_render_20260702/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`
- Acceptance:
  - P109 stayed within envelope, out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - P110A stayed within the modern-planform envelope.
  - Land fraction moved from the P111.6.32 baseline `0.303` to `0.286`,
    still target-consistent.
  - Major land components stayed `5`; largest/second/third land shares
    became `0.343 / 0.203 / 0.156`.
  - Unsupported open-ocean shoal stayed `0.0`.
  - Object-backed open-ocean shoal improved `0.0130 -> 0.0109`.
  - Parent trench overlap improved `0.834 -> 0.892`.
- P111.6.33c telemetry:
  - Final spine refinement accepted.
  - Peak components `4 -> 4`; spineless peak components `0 -> 0`;
    orphan peak/high/extreme cells reclassified `0 / 0 / 0`.
  - Final spine cells `335`; final spine components `23`.
- Orogen/high-mountain effects versus the P111.6.32 `24000` baseline:
  - High-mountain components `59 -> 60`, so raw component count did not
    improve.
  - High-mountain top-3 share improved `0.296 -> 0.368`.
  - High-mountain fragmentation index improved `0.925 -> 0.789`.
  - Extreme-mountain components improved `22 -> 20`.
  - P111.6.33b bridge response connected many lower-relief belts:
    `1800m+` components `82 -> 48`, `2400m+` components `65 -> 50`.
  - Terminal belt morphology remains insufficient:
    hierarchy components `4`, spine components `23`, spine per hierarchy
    component `5.75`; relief components remain high
    (`1800m:48`, `2400m:50`, `3000m:57`); high-peak fragmentation pressure
    `14.25`; worst parent hierarchy still contains `25` high subcomponents.
- Visual interpretation:
  - `orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png` is cleaner than before: main
    crest/branch semantics are readable and no longer dominated by orphan
    hierarchy fragments.
  - `elevation.png` is not yet acceptable as a closure result.  High terrain
    still appears as peak clusters and blocky high patches instead of
    continuous ridge systems with branch ranges, shoulders, and foreland
    gradients.
  - Therefore P111.6.33c is accepted as an implementation checkpoint but not
    promoted as final plate-engineering closure.

### P111.6.34 Planned: Spine-Aligned Elevation Morphology

- Problem now isolated:
  - The semantic layer is much cleaner than the high-elevation field.
  - At `24000`, high-mountain parentage is not the issue; remaining failure is
    the terrain response from orogenic spine/hierarchy into actual elevation.
  - P111.6.33b intentionally avoided `3000m+` forcing, which was correct for
    hypsometry, but final visual closure now needs a shape-aware elevation
    response that organizes existing high terrain around ridge axes.
- Design target:
  - Turn each major orogenic hierarchy component into a terrain province with:
    continuous main ridge axis, discontinuous but aligned high peaks, branch
    ranges, shoulders, and foreland slope.
  - Preserve P109/P110A: do not raise broad continental interiors; do not
    increase high-tail area sharply; do not create new land.
- Proposed implementation:
  1. Add a P111.6.34 audit section measuring per-orogen elevation morphology:
     ridge-axis relief continuity, high-peak distance-to-spine distribution,
     branch/shoulder/foreland gradient ordering, and high-cell clustering per
     parent component.
  2. Add an axis-distance field from final `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`
     within each parent hierarchy component.
  3. Redistribute existing orogenic relief locally:
     - near crest spine: preserve/shape highest local peaks and fill short
       saddles only when supported by neighboring high terrain;
     - branch spine: keep moderate relief and align secondary ridges;
     - shoulder halo: enforce monotonic taper below high-mountain threshold;
     - foreland/apron: low-gradient transition.
  4. Use conservation-style guards:
     local orogen p90/p98 cannot jump beyond small thresholds; total land
     p90/p98 must stay within P109 envelope; high area in `3000-4500m` cannot
     increase without a matching reduction of isolated high cells.
  5. Validate by:
     synthetic ridge/branch terrain tests, `900/36p`, `8000/36p` render, then
     a final `24000/60p` check.

#### 2026-07-02 expert review update: orogenic morphology convergence

- External review summary:
  - Good:
    the orogenic hierarchy concept is now clear; main belts are more linear
    than before; morphology and elevation are broadly coupled; the current
    layer is a plausible expandable intermediate semantic layer.
  - Mixed:
    main crest and branch-range organization are still not strong enough;
    foreland slope and apron placement need more regular spatial relation to
    the crest/branch system; some local classification remains too fragmented.
  - Priority fixes:
    spine continuity and smoothness; polar/map-edge overclassification;
    excessive raster/grid texture.
- Immediate status:
  - P111.6.34 has been implemented in code as a conservative
    spine-aligned elevation response and a new audit block
    `p111634_spine_aligned_elevation_morphology`.
  - Unit coverage currently verifies the audit schema and the synthetic terrain
    behavior: branch-axis uplift stays below `3000m`, off-axis orphan high
    cells can be softened, and land/ocean mask is preserved.
  - `900/36p` seed `707` smoke passed P109 and P110A and did not trip the new
    P111.6.34 guard.  The `8000/36p` validation is still the promotion gate;
    do not claim P111.6.34 closure until that run and a rendered visual review
    are complete.
- `8000/36p` seed `707` validation result after this update:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111634_spine_aligned_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `599.8s`; terrain `451.7s`; tectonics `104.8s`;
    climate `40.6s`.
  - Render:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111634_spine_aligned_8000_seed707_20260702/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111634_spine_aligned_8000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
    -> `18` PNG assets.
  - P109 and P110A stayed inside envelope.  Land major components stayed `5`;
    largest land share `0.312`; ocean connectivity largest share `0.990`.
  - P111.6.34 response was used and not reverted:
    axis candidates `33`, raised cells `20`, off-axis high candidates `6`,
    softened cells `6`; high components inside the P111.6.34 audit improved
    `16 -> 15`; high near-spine fraction improved `0.911 -> 0.978`.
  - Mixed result:
    P111.6.34 correctly attaches high terrain more tightly to the spine, but
    the broader P111.6.33 morphology metrics still show excessive fragmentation
    at this seed: high-peak fragmentation pressure `2.500`, worst parent high
    subcomponents `6`, and `3000m+` relief components `15`.
  - Visual review:
    the map remains recognizably improved as a semantic layer, but rendered
    elevation still shows short/dashed ridge segments, polar/edge
    overclassification, and cell-scale diagonal raster texture.
  - Conclusion:
    P111.6.34 is a valid implementation checkpoint but not a closure result.
    The next implementation step should be P111.6.35 spine linework smoothing
    and continuity, not stronger elevation forcing.

### Revised P111.6.34+ Closure Plan

The expert feedback changes the closure criterion from "semantic orogenic
hierarchy exists" to "semantic hierarchy behaves like coherent terrain linework
and renders without obvious grid artifacts."  The next phases should therefore
be ordered as follows.

#### P111.6.34: finish spine-aligned elevation validation

- Scope:
  - Keep the existing conservative response:
    crest-spine saddles may enter the low `3000m+` range only when already
    supported by nearby high terrain; branch spines should form continuous
    `1800-2900m` secondary ranges; unsupported off-axis high pixels should be
    demoted into shoulder/highland classes.
  - Do not use broad random uplift or global smoothing.
- Required evidence:
  - Full P107 tests pass.
  - `900/36p` and `8000/36p` pass P109/P110A.
  - P111.6.34 audit reports:
    response used, guard not reverted, high near-spine fraction not worse,
    axis-raised cells nonzero when eligible, and no broad high-tail expansion.
  - Rendered `8000` review shows high terrain more attached to crest/branch
    axes than P111.6.33c.
- Failure criteria:
  - If `high_component_count` rises substantially, if P109 p90/p98 moves out
    of envelope, or if elevation remains visibly patchy while the semantic
    spine is clean, move the next fix upstream to P111.6.35 rather than adding
    more elevation forcing.

#### P111.6.35: spine linework smoothing and continuity

- Problem:
  - Current final spines can still be many short components inside a single
    parent orogen.  This makes main crests and branch ranges read as dashed
    raster chains instead of continuous mountain systems.
- Design target:
  - Treat each parent orogen as a linework system:
    one or a few dominant main-crest polylines, branch polylines attached to
    the main axis, and limited terminal splays.
  - Smooth linework on the sphere using graph paths and geodesic adjacency,
    never planar image assumptions.
- Implementation plan:
  1. Add a spine-linework audit:
     component count per parent orogen, endpoints per component, mean curvature
     proxy, short-component fraction, branch attachment fraction, and
     antimeridian/polar continuity flags.
  2. Build a parent-orogen graph from `crest|branch` cells:
     weighted nodes by relief, parent-line support, boundary proximity, and
     existing spine support; weighted edges by same-parent adjacency and
     geodesic smoothness.
  3. Extract a main crest path per parent component:
     choose high-relief/parent-supported anchors, connect them with
     shortest paths constrained to the parent hierarchy, then prune teeth and
     one-cell side branches.
  4. Attach branch ranges:
     branch paths must terminate on or near the main crest, preserve existing
     high terrain where present, and avoid creating isolated branch islands.
  5. Reclassify tiny unattached spine fragments:
     convert unsupported remnants to shoulder halo or highland apron unless
     they contain extreme peaks or clear parent-boundary support.
- Tests:
  - Synthetic curved-orogen fixture where a noisy dashed spine becomes one
    smooth connected crest with two attached branches.
  - Antimeridian fixture where the spine crosses longitude wrap without a
    rendered edge break.
  - Polar fixture where high-latitude belts remain narrow line systems rather
    than broad caps.
  - Generated-world `8000` gate: spine components per hierarchy component
    decreases, branch attachment fraction increases, and P109/P110A remain
    inside envelope.

#### P111.6.36: polar and map-edge overclassification repair

- Problem:
  - High latitude and rectangular render edges amplify local adjacency noise.
    This can produce broad orogenic classification at poles or edge-adjacent
    fragments that look like map artifacts.
- Design target:
  - Keep legitimate polar/subpolar orogens, arcs, trenches, and highlands, but
    require stronger process support before classifying broad polar or
    map-edge areas as crest/branch.
- Implementation plan:
  1. Add diagnostics:
     polar crest/branch area fraction, polar width ratio, edge-band mismatch,
     edge-fragment count, and polar spine-to-hierarchy width ratio.
  2. Add stricter support tests for high-latitude crest/branch cells:
     require parent-line support, final spine proximity, extreme-peak support,
     or coherent neighbor-majority support.
  3. Convert weak polar/edge peak hierarchy to shoulder halo, highland apron,
     foreland slope, or ordinary continental province depending on relief and
     adjacency.
  4. Validate with both graph metrics and rendered PNGs; the antimeridian and
     poles must be checked on spherical adjacency, not only projected images.
- Tests:
  - Synthetic polar broad-cap fixture.
  - Synthetic antimeridian parent-orogen fixture.
  - Generated-world `8000` and final `24000` visual check:
     no broad polar crest blankets; no left/right edge discontinuity in
     elevation, spine overlay, or object masks.

#### P111.6.37: anti-raster terrain expression

- Problem:
  - Even with better semantics, the rendered elevation can retain cell-scale
    blockiness: square-looking ridges, jagged patch margins, and checker-like
    class boundaries.
- Design target:
  - Move final terrain expression from per-cell class response toward
    province/linework response:
    continuous ridge axes, smooth shoulders, coherent forelands, and local
    relief texture that follows terrain objects rather than independent noise.
- Implementation plan:
  1. Add visual/array metrics:
     local class-boundary jaggedness, one-cell island fraction, relief
     gradient coherence around spine, and high-elevation patch compactness.
  2. Use object-aware smoothing only within compatible classes:
     spine axis, branch spine, shoulder halo, foreland slope, highland apron,
     craton/platform, basin/plain.  Do not blur coastlines, trenches, ridges,
     or plate boundaries.
  3. Add anisotropic smoothing around orogens:
     along-spine continuity stronger than cross-spine smoothing; shoulders and
     forelands taper away from spine.
  4. Add render-side QA:
     compare `elevation.png`, `orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png`, and
     `orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png` at `8000` and `24000`.
- Tests:
  - Synthetic jagged-ridge fixture where linework stays intact but one-cell
    high patches reduce.
  - Generated-world gates:
    P109/P110A unchanged, high-tail area not inflated, ridge/branch continuity
    improves, and visual review shows less grid texture.

#### Promotion sequence after the expert review

1. Complete current P111.6.34 `8000/36p` validation and render review.
2. If P111.6.34 improves elevation attachment but spine remains visibly dashed,
   implement P111.6.35 before any more elevation tuning.
3. Run P111.6.36 if polar/edge artifacts remain in either `8000` or `24000`.
4. Run P111.6.37 only after semantic linework is acceptable; otherwise
   smoothing will hide, not fix, structural problems.
5. Final plate-engineering closure requires a `24000/60p` rendered review where:
   main crests are continuous systems, branch ranges attach naturally,
   foreland/apron zones are spatially ordered, polar/edge overclassification is
   controlled, and the terrain no longer reads as cell-scale raster patches.

#### 2026-07-02 P111.6.35 implementation checkpoint

- Implemented a first P111.6.35 spine-linework smoothing pass:
  - Gate:
    `terrain.enable_p111635_spine_linework_smoothing`, default on.
  - Placement:
    after P111.6.33c final spine cleanup and before final
    `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine` export.
  - Behavior:
    identifies dashed spine components inside a parent-supported orogenic
    system, builds a small candidate graph from existing `crest|branch`,
    parent-near support, and cells adjacent to existing spine, and connects
    nearby fragments with shortest paths on the spherical graph.
  - The pass can add a very small number of semantic bridge cells to branch
    hierarchy, but it does not change elevation and it does not create land.
  - Guards:
    bridge is accepted only if peak component count does not increase, spine
    component count decreases or short-component/branch-attachment metrics
    improve, and final crest/branch area remains within the existing P111.6
    hierarchy envelope (`crest <= 0.036`, `branch <= 0.042` world area).
  - Halo/apron overlap cleanup:
    accepted P111.6.35 bridge cells are removed from P111.6.15/P111.6.16
    shoulder/apron telemetry masks so semantic layers do not overlap.
- Added audit metrics:
  - New top-level block:
    `p111635_orogenic_spine_linework`.
  - Metrics include:
    spine components per peak-hierarchy component, short-spine component
    fraction, endpoints per component, branch attachment fraction, polar spine
    area fraction, linework continuity score, plus P111.6.35 bridge/path
    telemetry.
  - P111.6.35 telemetry is also exposed inside
    `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`.
- Tests:
  - Added a synthetic linework smoothing fixture where parent-supported spine
    fragments are connected without exceeding crest/branch area caps.
  - Added P111.6.35 audit schema coverage.
  - Regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111635 or p111623 or p11168_parent_line_hierarchy_builds_guarded_crest_branch_foreland or p111615_spine_width'`
    -> `5 passed, 90 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `95 passed`.
- `900/36p` seed `707` smoke:
  - Command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 900 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111635_spine_linework_900_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> runtime `85.8s`; terrain `63.4s`; tectonics `15.6s`.
  - P109 and P110A stayed inside envelope.
  - Land fraction `0.293`; major land components `4`; largest land component
    share `0.303`; ocean connectivity largest share `0.995`.
  - P111.6.35 linework audit:
    linework continuity score `0.667`; spine components per peak hierarchy
    component `1.5`; short spine component fraction `0.0`; endpoints per
    component `2.0`; branch attachment fraction `1.0`; polar spine fraction
    `0.0`.
  - P111.6.35 smoothing did not activate in this low-resolution seed:
    accepted `false`, bridge cells `0`, path count `0`.  This is acceptable
    for the 900 smoke because the linework already had no short components and
    fully attached branches; it is not promotion evidence.
  - P111.6.34 response attempted but was guard-reverted in this 900 run.  This
    did not break P109/P110A, but it reinforces that the next meaningful gate
    must be the `8000/36p` linework/elevation visual comparison.
- Current interpretation:
  - P111.6.35 is implemented and unit-tested as a guarded semantic linework
    pass.
  - The 900 smoke only proves it is not obviously destabilizing the generated
    world.
  - The next required step is an `8000/36p` validation and render review against
    `out_p111634_spine_aligned_8000_seed707_render_20260702`.  Promotion should
    require improved or unchanged P109/P110A, reduced spine components per
    peak hierarchy where eligible, and visibly smoother spine/elevation
    alignment.

#### 2026-07-02 P111.6.35 `8000/36p` validation

- Command:
  `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 8000 --n-plates 36 --seeds 707 --frames 5 --out out_p111635_spine_linework_8000_seed707_20260702 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
  -> runtime `589.2s`; terrain `441.9s`; tectonics `104.8s`;
  climate `39.7s`.
- Render:
  `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-render-arrays out_p111635_spine_linework_8000_seed707_20260702/00_8000cells_36p --out out_p111635_spine_linework_8000_seed707_render_20260702 --width 1600 --height 800`
  -> `18` PNG assets.
- Acceptance:
  - P109 stayed within envelope, out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - P110A stayed within the modern-planform envelope, out-of-envelope count
    `0`.
  - Land fraction `0.2866`; major land components `4`; largest/second land
    shares `0.389 / 0.362`; ocean connectivity largest share `0.988`.
- P111.6.35 linework result:
  - Smoothing accepted.
  - Spine components `8 -> 5`.
  - Short spine components `3 -> 0`.
  - Branch attachment fraction `0.700 -> 1.000`.
  - Bridge cells `8`; bridge candidates `311`; path count `3`.
  - Top-level linework metrics:
    continuity score `0.778`; spine components per peak hierarchy component
    `1.000`; short-spine fraction `0.000`; endpoints per component `3.333`;
    branch attachment `1.000`; polar spine area fraction `0.160`.
- Coupled elevation effects relative to P111.6.34 `8000`:
  - P111.6.34 axis response still used and guard not reverted.
  - Ridge-axis relief continuity improved `0.301 -> 0.325`.
  - High near-spine fraction improved `0.978 -> 0.990`.
  - High far-from-spine fraction improved `0.022 -> 0.010`.
  - Spine `2400m+` coverage improved `0.446 -> 0.604`.
  - Worst parent high subcomponents improved `6 -> 4`.
  - Lower-relief `1800m+` components improved `12 -> 8`.
  - However, graded belt-continuity score decreased `0.632 -> 0.585`, and
    `2400m+` / `3000m+` component counts stayed high (`13` / `15`).
- Visual interpretation:
  - `orogenic_hierarchy_spines.png` and the contact sheet show more coherent
    semantic linework than P111.6.34, especially in the long southern belt and
    mid-latitude branch systems.
  - At contact-sheet scale, the improvement is local rather than dramatic.
  - Elevation still reads with strong diagonal grid texture and patchy
    high-relief expression.
  - Polar/subpolar and map-edge classifications remain too broad or fragmented
    in places; this matches the expert-review priority list.
- Conclusion:
  - P111.6.35 is accepted as a checkpoint: it improves linework semantics while
    preserving P109/P110A.
  - It is not plate-engineering closure.  The next implementation step should
    be P111.6.36 polar/map-edge overclassification repair, followed by
    P111.6.37 anti-raster terrain expression.

#### 2026-07-02 updated closure plan after expert morphology review

- Expert review summary:
  - Good:
    the orogenic hierarchy now has a clear mountain-belt concept; main ranges
    are more linear than before; morphology and elevation are coupled well
    enough for an extensible middle semantic layer.
  - Adequate but incomplete:
    main-crest and branch-range organization is still not strong enough;
    foreland slope and apron positions need more regular process ordering;
    some local classes remain too fragmented.
  - Must repair before plate-engineering closure:
    spine continuity and smoothness, polar/map-edge overclassification, and
    visible raster/grid texture.
- Current checkpoint:
  - P111.6.35 is the current accepted `8000/36p` linework baseline.
  - It fixed short spine components in seed `707` (`3 -> 0`) and improved
    branch attachment (`0.700 -> 1.000`), but the rendered terrain still shows
    patchy high-relief cells, diagonal grid texture, and broad polar/subpolar
    class noise.
  - A first P111.6.36 implementation scaffold exists in code for conservative
    polar/edge peak reclassification.  It still needs focused tests, a
    `900/36p` smoke, and an `8000/36p` rendered validation before it can be
    called accepted.

Updated status against the eight core plate/terrain checklist items:

1. Continuous mountain systems along convergent boundaries:
   stage partial.  P111.6.35 improved linework semantics, but the rendered
   main crest, branch ranges, and high-relief envelope still need stronger
   object-level continuity.
2. Trenches attached to subduction boundaries:
   stage pass.  Keep P111.6.3/P111.6.32 behavior protected while changing
   orogenic terrain.
3. Mid-ocean ridges visible in bathymetry:
   stage pass.  Do not retune in the current mountain/terrain pass except for
   regression checks.
4. Ocean crust age controls sea depth:
   stage pass for current scope.  Preserve age-depth coupling while validating
   terrain changes.
5. Ocean crust age increases away from ridges:
   stage pass for current scope.  Preserve ridge-bilateral age structure.
6. Objects-layer semantics are fixed:
   partial pass.  Boundary/ocean objects are mostly stable; mountain-system
   objects still need stricter distinction among main crest, branch range,
   highland apron, foreland, plateau, and ordinary interior highland.
7. Boundary pixels behave like line objects:
   partial pass.  Trench/ridge/subduction linework has improved, but orogenic
   spine linework still needs final smoothing and object promotion.
8. Continental interiors have geomorphic hierarchy:
   partial pass.  Cratons, basins, platforms, plateaus, highlands, and orogens
   exist semantically, but visual terrain still reads too rasterized in places.

Immediate execution order:

1. P111.6.36 polar and map-edge overclassification repair
   - Finish the existing conservative repair pass.
   - Candidate cells:
     weak high-latitude or render-edge crest/branch cells without spine
     support, spine-near support, extreme-peak support, or coherent parent-line
     support.
   - Allowed action:
     reclassify weak peak hierarchy to shoulder halo or highland apron; do not
     lower elevation yet and do not touch true spine/extreme cells.
   - Required tests:
     audit schema coverage, synthetic polar broad-cap fixture, synthetic
     map-edge fragment fixture, and a no-extreme-reclassification guard.
   - Generated validation:
     `900/36p` smoke first; then `8000/36p` seed `707` with rendered assets.
   - Acceptance:
     P109/P110A unchanged, polar peak area lower or unchanged, edge peak
     fragment count lower or unchanged, extreme reclassified cells `0`, and
     no visible broad polar crest blanket in PNG review.
2. P111.6.37a spine object promotion and smoothness
   - Promote final orogenic spines from cell masks into explicit object-like
     graph paths per parent orogen.
   - Main crest:
     one dominant smooth path per parent belt where possible, with limited
     terminal splays.
   - Branch ranges:
     must attach to main crest or to a valid secondary spine; free-floating
     high-relief branch islands should be demoted or connected only when
     process-supported.
   - Metrics:
     linework continuity score target `>= 0.82` at `8000/36p`, short-spine
     component fraction `0`, branch attachment `>= 0.95`, and no increase in
     high-relief component fragmentation.
3. P111.6.37b anti-raster terrain expression
   - Apply object-aware terrain response after semantic linework is stable.
   - Smooth along mountain-system axes more than across axes.
   - Preserve trenches, ridges, coastlines, and plate boundaries; smoothing is
     allowed only within compatible terrain provinces.
   - Metrics:
     reduce one-cell highland/peak islands, reduce diagonal/grid texture,
     preserve high-near-spine fraction, and recover or improve the graded
     belt-continuity score lost in P111.6.35.
4. P111.6.38 foreland/apron spatial ordering
   - Make mountain belts read as crest -> branch/highland -> foreland/apron
     -> basin/plain transitions rather than shuffled classes.
   - Use distance from spine, parent-orogen side, slope direction, basin
     adjacency, and crustal province context.
   - Acceptance:
     foreland/apron zones should sit outside the orogenic core in coherent
     bands, not as random halos; local class islands should decrease.
5. P111.6.39 high-resolution promotion review
   - Run `8000/36p` seed `707` after each step.
   - When `8000` is stable, run `24000/60p` rendered review.
   - Final closure requires:
     P109/P110A pass, no regression in ocean/trench/ridge checklist items,
     continuous mountain systems, clear spine/branch/foreland ordering,
     controlled polar/edge classification, and no obvious raster grid texture.

Do not resume climate, ocean current, or monsoon work until this plate/terrain
closure pass has either reached P111.6.39 promotion or has a clearly documented
remaining geology blocker.

#### 2026-07-02 P111.6.36 implementation and validation checkpoint

- Implemented P111.6.36 as a conservative polar/map-edge peak-hierarchy
  cleanup:
  - Gate:
    `terrain.enable_p111636_polar_edge_overclassification_repair`, default on.
  - Placement:
    after P111.6.35 spine linework smoothing and before final hierarchy/spine
    export.
  - Candidate cells:
    weak polar or render-edge `crest|branch` cells without direct spine
    support, without one-cell spine-near support, below the extreme-peak
    threshold, and lacking enough parent/core or neighbor support.
  - Action:
    reclassify weak peak cells to shoulder halo or highland apron; do not
    change elevation, land/ocean state, ridge/trench/ocean structure, or true
    spine cells.
  - Guards:
    reject if any `>=3300m` extreme peak would be reclassified, if peak
    component count expands beyond a small tolerance, or if hierarchy overlaps
    shoulder/apron masks after the operation.
- Added a default-on P111.6.16 ablation gate for testing:
  `terrain.enable_p111616_highlat_morphology_refinement`.
  Production default remains unchanged.  The gate allows P111.6.36 to be
  tested against a residual high-latitude broad-peak fixture that P111.6.16
  would normally clean earlier.
- Added audit support:
  - New top-level metric:
    `p111636_polar_edge_orogen_overclassification`.
  - Summary fields include:
    polar peak/spine area fractions, polar peak-to-spine width ratio,
    edge peak component counts, candidate/reclassified counts, polar/edge
    reclassified counts, extreme reclassification count, and non-expansion
    booleans.
  - P111.6.36 telemetry remains exposed inside
    `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`.
- Tests:
  - Added P111.6.36 telemetry assertions to the P111.6.8 parent hierarchy audit
    schema test.
  - Added a top-level P111.6.36 audit metric schema test.
  - Added a high-latitude residual broad-peak behavior fixture:
    with P111.6.16 disabled, P111.6.36 reclassifies weak polar peak width into
    highland apron, reduces polar peak area, preserves the extreme crest cell,
    and leaves no apron/hierarchy overlap.
  - Regression commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111636 or p111616_high_latitude_apron or p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics or p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics'`
    -> `5 passed, 92 deselected`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `97 passed`.
- `900/36p` seed `707` smoke:

    -> runtime `83.5s`; terrain `61.6s`; tectonics `15.2s`;

    diagnostics, passes focused/full tests, keeps P109/P110A stable, and
    removes a real weak edge peak at `8000` without extreme-peak damage.
