    fragments, but long unsupported interior strips remained, especially in
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code`.
  - A later terminal elevation smoother cannot make continental interiors look
    geologically organized if the render/compiler semantic fields still expose
    inherited strip cargo as long raster-aligned belts.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p120_continental_semantic_geometry_repair` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The repair is default-on through
    `terrain.enable_p120_continental_semantic_geometry_repair`.
  - Scope:
    unsupported continental interiors only: continental land with no
    orogenic hierarchy, no spine, no shoulder/foreland apron, no mountain
    inventory, and no raw orogen/plateau detail.
  - Inputs:
    `terrain.province`, raw `terrain.continental_detail`,
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code`,
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code`,
    `terrain.inland_geomorphology_region_code`, and
    `terrain.continental_province_code`.
  - The repair builds a semantic guide from production continental provinces
    and inland geomorphology:
    shield -> craton core/shield; platform -> stable platform/platform;
    intracratonic or foreland basin -> intracratonic basin/basin;
    rift or passive margin -> rifted margin/rift basin; supported high-relief
    old/active orogens remain protected by the unsupported-interior mask.
  - The helper identifies elongated components with the same P120 thresholds
    used by the audit (`>=8` cells, `>=0.006` unsupported-interior share,
    elongation `>=3.4`) and only edits cells with local guide support.
  - Guard policy is field-level:
    each semantic field is accepted only if its elongated area does not
    increase, tiny area does not materially regress, and either elongated or
    tiny area improves.  A field that worsens is reverted without blocking
    improvements in another field.
  - The pass does not modify elevation, sea level, crust type, raw tectonic
    cargo, or land/ocean planform.
  - Added P120 repair telemetry to
    `p111635_orogenic_spine_linework`: accepted/guard flags, candidate area,
    per-field changed area, and per-field elongated/tiny before/after.
- Validation:
  - Added
    `test_p120_continental_semantic_geometry_repair_breaks_unsupported_stripe`,
    using a synthetic rectangular grid with a long unsupported platform stripe
    and province-guided basin cut points.  It verifies that detail/internal
    semantic stripes are broken while a protected orogenic cell is unchanged.
  - Extended
    `test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics` to assert the
    P120 repair telemetry is exported into P107 metrics.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - Focused tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p120_continental_semantic_geometry_repair_breaks_unsupported_stripe tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `131 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- Process-level validation policy:
  - Validation used independent CLI processes with one output directory per
    run.  Thread-count environment variables were set to `1`.  No batch used
    more than the user-approved process-level parallelism budget.
- 900-cell on/off probe:
  - Outputs:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_on_900_seed707_20260703` and
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_off_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - The 900-cell sample did not expose the target elongated defect:
    P120 unsupported elongated fractions were `0.0`, and terminal arrays were
    identical.  This tier is therefore useful as a smoke test but not as a
    quality gate for P120.
- 2500-cell representative validation:
  - Default-on output:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_fieldguard_on_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline/default-off output:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_off_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `143.9s` on the default-on run versus `140.1s` baseline.
  - P120 repair accepted with no guard revert.
  - Only `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code` changed:
    `47` cells, changed area fraction `0.0188`.
  - Unsupported internal-block region elongated area:
    `0.2138 -> 0.0978`.
  - P120 helper telemetry for its stricter candidate mask:
    internal elongated `0.1750 -> 0.0708`, internal tiny
    `0.2001 -> 0.1084`.
  - `terrain.province`, `terrain.continental_detail_region_code`,
    elevation, land fraction, linework score, short-spine fraction, and branch
    attachment were unchanged.
- 8000-cell representative validation:
  - Default-on output:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_fieldguard_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_observability_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Rendered review:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_fieldguard_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `473.7s`; terrain `327.7s`, tectonics `104.4s`, climate
    `38.9s`, comparable to the P120 observability run.
  - P120 repair accepted with no guard revert.
  - Changed arrays:
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code` (`124` cells),
    `terrain.province` (`115` cells), and
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` (`65` cells).  No elevation,
    crust, sea-level, or planform arrays changed.
  - Unsupported `terrain.province` elongated area:
    `0.1432 -> 0.0085`.
  - Unsupported internal-block region elongated area:
    `0.1874 -> 0.0433`.
  - Unsupported detail-region elongated area stayed stable:
    `0.09416 -> 0.09415`.
  - Region tiny deltas improved:
    detail tiny delta `-0.1280 -> -0.1619`, internal tiny delta
    `-0.1177 -> -0.1375`.
  - Orogenic linework invariants were unchanged:
    linework continuity `0.6980`, short-spine fraction `0.0`, branch
    attachment `1.0`.
- Decision:
  - P120 repair is accepted default-on as a semantic region-field repair.  It
    addresses the most severe remaining upstream source of unsupported
    continental long strips without touching elevation or tectonic truth
    fields.
- Known limitation and next entry:
  - `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` still has unsupported elongated
    area around `0.094` in the 8000 representative sample.  This is below the
    internal-block defect that P120 fixed, but it remains visible in the
    continental-detail render.
  - Elevation still shows some diagonal/raster texture because P120 changes
    semantic compiler fields, not the already-built surface.  A later pass
    should decide whether to feed repaired semantic regions back into inland
    elevation response or to add a province-level terrain response that uses
    repaired region geometry before final elevation.
  - P121 should target detail/elevation coupling rather than more terminal
    smoothing: use repaired semantic regions to organize shield/platform/basin
    surfaces and reduce residual diagonal interior relief while preserving
    mountain belts and hypsometry gates.

#### 2026-07-03 P121 semantic-region elevation response

- Motivation:
  - P120 repaired the continental semantic region fields, but `elevation.png`
    was still built mostly from earlier surface response stages.  The visible
    result was that some continental interiors still carried diagonal/raster
    relief texture even where the semantic fields had become more block-like.
  - P121 is a guarded terrain-expression pass, not another label repair:
    use repaired semantic regions to damp unsupported interior relief texture
    while preserving real orogenic belts, sea-level topology, and P109
    hypsometry.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p121_semantic_region_elevation_response` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The pass is default-on through
    `terrain.enable_p121_semantic_region_elevation_response`.
  - Pipeline order:
    P119 terminal unsupported-interior smoothing ->
    P120 semantic-region repair ->
    P121 semantic-region elevation response ->
    later spine cleanup stages.
  - Scope:
    continental land only, no mountain inventory, no orogenic hierarchy,
    no spine, no shoulder halo, no highland apron, and no sea/land boundary
    changes.  It edits only `terrain.elevation_m`.
  - The compatibility graph reads repaired `terrain.province`,
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code`, and
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code`; cells are smoothed only across
    compatible semantic neighbors.
  - Detail-specific response caps:
    basins and rift basins get stronger downward accommodation; platform,
    shield, and arc-microcontinent get weaker smoothing; low-relief old-orogen
    semantic regions are allowed only when they lack real orogenic support and
    remain below `1400m`.
  - Hypsometry guard:
    original `>=1000m` cells cannot be lowered below the 1000m band boundary,
    and the local `1000-2000m` continental band area cannot materially shrink.
    This was added after the first low-orogen trial pushed the P109
    `band_1000_2000m` metric out of envelope.
  - Safety guard:
    preserve land mask, never edit ocean or real orogenic-support cells, reduce
    measured compatible-edge roughness, do not increase non-orogenic
    `>=500m` or `>=1000m` highland area, do not raise land P90, and keep land
    P50 above a conservative floor.
  - Added P121 telemetry to `p111635_orogenic_spine_linework` and compact P107
    summaries: accepted/guard flags, candidate/selected/adjusted area,
    compatible-edge count, roughness before/after, non-orogenic highland
    before/after, mean/max delta, and P50/P90 before/after.
- Validation:
  - Added `test_p121_semantic_region_elevation_response_is_guarded`, which
    builds a synthetic rough continental interior, verifies roughness reduction,
    preserves a protected orogenic cell, and checks land mask plus non-orogenic
    highland guardrails.
  - Extended
    `test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics` to assert P121
    telemetry export.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - Focused tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p121_semantic_region_elevation_response_is_guarded tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - Full tests after final band guard:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `132 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell representative validation:
  - Accepted output:
    `out_p121_semantic_region_elevation_low_orogen_on_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline/off output:
    `out_p121_semantic_region_elevation_off_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `149.0s`; terrain `95.8s`, tectonics `36.9s`, climate
    `15.1s`.
  - P121 accepted with no guard revert and preserved the land mask.
  - Changed arrays:
    `terrain.elevation_m` changed `140` cells.  The old off comparison also
    reported a small `object__volcanic_arc` mask difference (`5` cells), so the
    accepted 2500 evidence is treated as a metric/field-invariant validation
    rather than an exact all-array equivalence proof.
  - Compatible-edge roughness:
    `181.1m -> 125.7m`.
  - Non-orogenic highland area:
    `>=500m` `0.0152 -> 0.0128`; `>=1000m` `0.00240 -> 0.00120`.
  - P109 stayed inside envelope; out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - Orogenic linework stayed unchanged:
    linework continuity `1.0`, short-spine fraction `0.0`, branch attachment
    `1.0`.
- 8000-cell representative validation:
  - Final accepted output:
    `out_p121_semantic_region_elevation_bandguard_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline:
    `out_p120_semantic_geometry_repair_fieldguard_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Rendered review:
    `out_p121_semantic_region_elevation_bandguard_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `487.2s`; terrain `336.8s`, tectonics `107.0s`, climate
    `40.6s`.
  - P121 accepted with no guard revert and preserved the land mask.
  - Only `terrain.elevation_m` changed: `559` cells, max absolute edit
    `296.0m`, adjusted area fraction `0.0699`.
  - Compatible-edge roughness:
    `201.0m -> 143.1m`.
  - Non-orogenic highland area:
    `>=500m` `0.02224 -> 0.01737`; `>=1000m` stayed `0.00525`.
  - P109 recovered after band guard:
    out-of-envelope count `0`, `within_p109_envelope=True`.
  - P109 band deltas remained in envelope, including
    `band_1000_2000m = -0.0667`.
  - Land hypsometry stayed controlled:
    P50 `485.8m -> 473.0m`, P90 unchanged at `2349.5m`.
  - Orogenic linework stayed unchanged:
    linework continuity `0.6980`, short-spine fraction `0.0`, branch
    attachment `1.0`.
- Decision:
  - P121 is accepted default-on as a conservative elevation-expression pass.
    It measurably reduces unsupported interior roughness while preserving P109,
    planform, and mountain-linework gates.
- Known limitation and next entry:
  - Full-map visual change is intentionally subtle: at 8000 cells the pass
    changes `559` cells, enough to reduce measured roughness but not enough to
    completely remove all diagonal continental texture at overview scale.
  - Remaining visible diagonal structure is likely upstream: old-orogen/detail
    region geometry and earlier elevation coupling still classify broad
    diagonal belts before terminal response.  A stronger next step should move
    earlier than P119-P121 and rework the inland/old-orogen detail generator
    itself, or add province-object elevation response before final surface
    polishing.

#### 2026-07-03 P122 orogenic detail footprint compaction

- Motivation:
  - The P121 8000-cell visual audit showed that elevation roughness had been
    reduced, but large diagonal low-to-mid relief belts still appeared as broad
    `orogen` semantic regions.
  - Direct attribution on
    `out_p121_semantic_region_elevation_bandguard_on_8000_seed707_20260703`
    showed that unsupported detail-orogen cells were almost gone, so the
    remaining problem was not "orogen without any support".  Instead, the
    orogenic support/detail footprint itself was too broad: many low-relief
    cells sat in platform/rift/basin-like province contexts while still being
    expressed as `CONT_DETAIL_OROGEN`.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p122_orogenic_detail_footprint_compaction(...)` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The pass is default-on through
    `terrain.enable_p122_orogenic_detail_footprint_compaction`.
  - Pipeline order is now:
    P119 unsupported-interior smoothing ->
    P120 semantic-region geometry repair ->
    P122 orogenic-detail footprint compaction ->
    P121 semantic-region elevation response ->
    later spine cleanup stages.
  - Scope:
    render/compiler semantic fields only:
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code`,
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code`, and `terrain.province`.
    It does not directly edit elevation, sea level, crust type, plate IDs, or
    tectonic truth cargo.
  - Core preservation:
    cells on sufficiently expressed spine/core orogen support remain
    `orogen`.  Low-relief non-core cells are eligible only when their province
    or inland context points to platform, shield, intracratonic basin,
    foreland basin, rift system, passive margin lowland, or comparable inland
    geomorphology.
  - Repair behavior:
    eligible low-relief orogen cells are re-expressed as platform, shield,
    basin, or rift/passive basin.  Internal-block and terrain-province render
    codes are synchronized only inside the selected candidate cells, avoiding
    accidental changes to submerged or non-continental cells.
  - Guard:
    preserve core orogen detail, never alter non-continental cells, do not
    increase total detail-orogen area, and require either low-relief or noncore
    orogen area to decrease.  The first 2500-cell trial exposed that the guard
    was too narrow and that internal/terrain synchronization was touching
    non-selected cells; both issues were fixed before acceptance.
  - Added P122 telemetry to `p111635_orogenic_spine_linework`:
    accepted/guard flags, candidate area, changed area by semantic field,
    detail-orogen area before/after, low-relief orogen area before/after,
    noncore orogen area before/after, and preserved core-orogen cell count.
- Validation:
  - Added
    `test_p122_orogenic_detail_footprint_compaction_preserves_spine_core`,
    which builds a synthetic low-relief platform/rift stripe incorrectly
    labeled as `orogen`, verifies that P122 demotes the flanks, and preserves
    the spine core.
  - Extended
    `test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics` to assert
    P122 telemetry export.
  - Focused tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p120_continental_semantic_geometry_repair_breaks_unsupported_stripe tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p122_orogenic_detail_footprint_compaction_preserves_spine_core tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p121_semantic_region_elevation_response_is_guarded tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - Final full tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `133 passed`.
  - Array-render smoke:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell representative validation:
  - Accepted output:
    `out_p122_orogenic_detail_compaction_on_2500_seed707_20260703_v2`.
  - P122-off comparison:
    `out_p122_orogenic_detail_compaction_off_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `146.5s`; terrain `94.3s`, tectonics `36.2s`, climate
    `14.9s`.
  - P122 accepted with no guard revert.
  - P122 compacted the semantic footprint:
    detail-orogen area `0.1492 -> 0.0928`,
    low-relief orogen area `0.1108 -> 0.0544`,
    noncore orogen area `0.1092 -> 0.0528`.
  - Final changed arrays versus P122-off:
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` `141` cells,
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code` `120` cells,
    `terrain.province` `107` cells, and downstream
    `terrain.elevation_m` `132` cells through P121 response.
  - P109 stayed inside envelope with out-of-envelope count `0`.
- 8000-cell representative validation:
  - Accepted output:
    `out_p122_orogenic_detail_compaction_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Rendered review:
    `out_p122_orogenic_detail_compaction_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline:
    `out_p121_semantic_region_elevation_bandguard_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `497.1s`; terrain `343.6s`, tectonics `108.5s`, climate
    `42.1s`.
  - P122 accepted with no guard revert.
  - P122 compacted the semantic footprint:
    detail-orogen area `0.1146 -> 0.0711`,
    low-relief orogen area `0.08136 -> 0.03787`,
    noncore orogen area `0.08111 -> 0.03762`,
    preserved core-orogen cell count `268`.
  - Final all-continental-crust orogen share decreased from `999` cells
    (`0.12485` world area) to `651` cells (`0.08136` world area).  The final
    continental-land orogen count stayed `141` cells (`0.01762` world area),
    so this pass mainly fixes broad continental-crust/shelf semantic
    overexpression, while visible land elevation change remains subtle.
  - P121 downstream roughness improved relative to the P121 baseline:
    compatible-edge roughness `201.0m -> 143.1m` became
    `182.5m -> 127.5m`.
  - Non-orogenic `>=500m` highland area after P121 decreased slightly
    `0.01737 -> 0.01700`; `>=1000m` stayed `0.00525`.
  - P109 stayed inside envelope with out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - Orogenic linework stayed unchanged:
    linework continuity `0.6980`, short-spine component count `0`, branch
    attachment `1.0`.
  - Final changed arrays versus P121 baseline:
    `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` `348` cells,
    `terrain.internal_geographic_block_region_code` `281` cells,
    `terrain.province` `273` cells, and downstream
    `terrain.elevation_m` `326` cells with max absolute delta `250m`.
- Decision:
  - P122 is accepted default-on as a guarded semantic-footprint repair.  It
    meaningfully reduces broad low-relief/noncore orogen overexpression and
    improves P121 roughness without moving P109 or linework gates.
- Known limitation and next entry:
  - P122 mostly improves continental-crust semantic expression and downstream
    roughness.  It does not yet change the land-visible orogen footprint in
    the seed-707 8000-cell sample, so the overview `elevation.png` remains
    visually similar.
  - The next quality step should move from semantic compaction to actual
    land-visible orogenic expression: either lift/organize underexpressed
    spine cores into clearer ridge-and-branch belts, or split broad lowland
    mountain-inventory support into spine, foreland, basin, and platform
    subobjects before the terminal elevation response.

#### 2026-07-03 P123 spine high-relief continuity expression

- Motivation:
  - After P122, the main remaining land-visible issue was not missing spine
    semantics.  The final spine linework existed and was mostly aligned with
    high terrain, but high-relief expression was still fragmented along the
    ridges.
  - Correcting the attribution required using `terrain.elevation_m -
    sea_level_m`.  The 8000-cell P122 sample had sea level
    `-2226.94m`; treating array elevation as already relative incorrectly made
    the spine look far too low.
  - With the correct sea-level reference, the 8000-cell P122 sample showed
    good median spine relief but fragmented high-relief components:
    `spine_relief_2400_component_count=10`,
    `spine_relief_3000_component_count=17`,
    `high_component_count=16`, and
    `ridge_axis_relief_continuity_score=0.5146`.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_expression(...)` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - The pass is default-on through
    `terrain.enable_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_expression`.
  - Pipeline placement:
    after P1138/P1139/P1140 spine cleanup and P116 candidate promotion, before
    later terminal land/ocean cleanup.  This means P123 reads the final
    promoted spine graph.
  - Scope:
    existing continental-land spine cells only; no new spine cells, no
    semantic relabeling, no ocean edits, no land-mask change.
  - Candidate selection:
    only bridge-like spine saddle cells with at least two same-spine neighbors
    and double-sided high-relief support are eligible.  This replaced the first
    broader candidate definition after a 2500-cell trial showed a guard revert:
    the broad definition could select ridge extensions rather than saddle
    bridges.
  - Elevation response:
    crest-spine bridge saddles may be lifted toward `3000m` relative relief;
    branch-spine saddles may be lifted toward `2400m`.  Lift is capped
    (`820m` crest, `460m` branch) and total adjusted area is capped by both
    world area and spine area.
  - Guard:
    require improved high-relief continuity, preserve the land mask, never
    lower terrain, prevent excessive new high components, and keep P90/P98 plus
    high-elevation land bands within conservative bounds.
  - Added P123 telemetry to
    `p111634_spine_aligned_elevation_morphology`: accepted/guard flags, land
    mask, candidate/adjusted area, 2400m and 3000m spine component counts,
    2400m coverage, high-component counts, mean/max lift, and P90/P98
    before/after.
- Validation:
  - Added
    `test_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_expression_fills_spine_saddle`,
    which builds a long crest spine with two high segments separated by one
    low saddle and verifies that P123 connects the high-relief components while
    preserving non-spine cells and the land mask.
  - Extended
    `test_p111634_audit_reports_spine_aligned_elevation_morphology_metrics`
    to assert P123 telemetry export.
  - Focused tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p120_continental_semantic_geometry_repair_breaks_unsupported_stripe tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p122_orogenic_detail_footprint_compaction_preserves_spine_core tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_expression_fills_spine_saddle tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p121_semantic_region_elevation_response_is_guarded tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111634_audit_reports_spine_aligned_elevation_morphology_metrics tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics -q`
    -> `6 passed`.
  - Final full tests:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `134 passed`.
  - Array-render smoke:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell representative validation:
  - Accepted output:
    `out_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_bridge_on_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline:
    `out_p122_orogenic_detail_compaction_on_2500_seed707_20260703_v2`.
  - Runtime was `144.2s`; terrain `93.3s`, tectonics `35.4s`, climate
    `14.4s`.
  - P123 accepted with no guard revert and preserved the land mask.
  - It adjusted `16` cells, affecting only `terrain.elevation_m`; max lift was
    `615.2m`.
  - High-relief continuity improved:
    `spine_relief_2400_component_count` `9 -> 6`,
    `spine_relief_3000_component_count` `11 -> 7`,
    `high_component_count` `11 -> 7`,
    `spine_relief_2400_coverage_fraction` `0.4814 -> 0.5696`,
    and `ridge_axis_relief_continuity_score` `0.3170 -> 0.5064`.
  - Crest-spine median relief moved `2573m -> 3000m`; branch-spine median
    relief stayed `1919.7m`.
  - P109 stayed inside envelope with out-of-envelope count `0`; linework
    continuity, short-spine count, and branch attachment stayed unchanged.
- 8000-cell representative validation:
  - Accepted output:
    `out_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_bridge_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Rendered review:
    `out_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_bridge_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Baseline:
    `out_p122_orogenic_detail_compaction_on_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime was `488.8s`; terrain `337.8s`, tectonics `107.0s`, climate
    `41.1s`.
  - P123 accepted with no guard revert and preserved the land mask.
  - It adjusted `24` cells, affecting only `terrain.elevation_m`; max lift was
    `820m`.
  - High-relief continuity improved conservatively:
    `spine_relief_3000_component_count` `17 -> 14`,
    `high_component_count` `16 -> 15`,
    and `ridge_axis_relief_continuity_score` `0.51456 -> 0.51480`.
  - `spine_relief_2400_coverage_fraction` stayed `0.8054`; P123 was mostly a
    3000m crest-saddle repair in this sample.
  - P90 and P98 land relief stayed unchanged at the audit precision:
    P90 `2349.45m`, P98 `3126.08m`.
  - P109 stayed inside envelope with out-of-envelope count `0`.
  - Orogenic linework stayed unchanged:
    linework continuity `0.6980`, short-spine count `0`, branch attachment
    `1.0`.
  - Elevation bands moved only within high-relief categories:
    `2000-3000m` land share `0.07546 -> 0.06958`,
    `3000-4500m` `0.06688 -> 0.07320`,
    `>4500m` unchanged.
- Decision:
  - P123 is accepted default-on as a conservative saddle-bridge repair.  It is
    effective at 2500 cells and safe at 8000 cells, but intentionally small at
    8000 because the guard prevents creating broad artificial highlands.
- Known limitation and next entry:
  - P123 improves high-relief continuity where a bridge saddle is already
    clearly supported by nearby high spine cells.  It does not yet solve
    broader spine shape smoothness, short local direction changes, or overly
    angular linework.
  - The next plate-engineering quality step should focus on the geometry of
    the spine objects themselves: smoother trunk/branch paths and more regular
    foreland/apron placement, rather than further terminal elevation lifting.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.12 ocean-fabric summary exact fast path

- Motivation:
  - P112.11 left `semantic_object_build` as the dominant terrain cost, and the
    post-trim ocean-fabric subprofile showed `component_object_summaries` as
    the largest ocean-fabric substage.
  - This pass only accepted exact formula-preserving rewrites, because
    performance work must not degrade rendered geography while the plate
    engineering quality gates are still active.
- Implementation:
  - `_dominant_basin_parent` now computes per-basin parent area with one
    `np.bincount` over valid cells instead of repeatedly scanning the full mask
    once per basin id.
  - Ocean-fabric derived fractions now sum over component-local indices
    (`flag[comp]`) instead of building full-grid boolean intersections for each
    fraction.
  - No ocean-fabric masks, thresholds, parent object semantics, or terrain
    fields were changed.
- Validation:
  - Added:
    `test_terrain_dominant_basin_parent_matches_reference_loop`.
  - The new test compares the optimized parent-basin lookup to an inline copy
    of the previous unique-id loop, including sparse basin ids and invalid
    cells.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_dominant_basin_parent_matches_reference_loop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_age_contrast_matches_reference_neighbor_loop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_thin_broad_ridge_province_to_axis tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11152_ocean_fabric_objects_fallback_old_abyssal_plain -q`
    -> `4 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `108 passed`.
- 900-cell performance validation:
  - Baseline:
    `out_p113_full_norender_900_seed707_20260703`.
    Runtime `64.1s`; terrain `41.2s`; semantic object build `21.43s`;
    post-trim ocean fabric `3.13s`; component summaries `1.60s`.
  - Accepted output:
    `out_p112_ocean_fabric_summary_fastpath_900_seed707_20260703`.
    Runtime `55.5s`; terrain `36.1s`; semantic object build `18.78s`;
    post-trim ocean fabric `2.66s`; component summaries `1.40s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Acceptance booleans were unchanged (`19/23` true in both summaries).
- Conclusion:
  - This is a safe algorithmic acceleration, not an approximation: it should
    not change rendered map quality because terminal arrays were byte-equivalent
    under the same seed and settings.
  - Remaining acceleration targets are unchanged but better isolated:
    continue with `semantic_object_build` helpers that still rebuild object
    masks, then profile/optimize `terminal_bathymetry_polish`.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.13 terminal bathymetry subprofile

- Motivation:
  - After P112.12, `terminal_bathymetry_polish` remained the second largest
    terrain stage.  The stage bundled land planform cleanup, object rebuilds,
    ocean-floor expression, and semantic shoal cleanup, so optimizing it
    directly would have mixed unrelated causes.
- Implementation:
  - Added `terrain_internal_profile.subprofiles.terminal_bathymetry_polish`
    with eight sub-stages:
    `p109_terminal_rebalance`, `p110_p111_planform_polish`,
    `planform_change_object_rebuild`, `p109_finish_polish`,
    `orogen_belt_repair`, `p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy`,
    `post_ocean_p109_floor`, and `p111631_semantic_shoal_cleanup`.
  - Updated the P107 timing test to assert that the terminal bathymetry
    subprofile is present in both aggregate and terminal-world metrics.
  - Updated the P107 CLI output to print a `bathymetry profile:` top-stage line
    when the subprofile is available.
- Validation:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `108 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/cli.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - CLI smoke:
    `.venv/bin/python -m aevum.cli p107-audit --cells 90 --n-plates 8 --seeds 1709 --t-end 40 --frames 1 --out out_p112_terminal_bathymetry_subprofile_smoke_20260703 --no-render-world-assets --no-contact-sheet --no-earth-reference`
    -> pass and printed `bathymetry profile: ...`.
- 900-cell attribution:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_terminal_bathymetry_subprofile_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `55.3s`; terrain `36.0s`; tectonics `13.7s`; climate `5.2s`.
  - `terminal_bathymetry_polish` total `6.46s`, with top sub-stages:
    `p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy` `3.04s`,
    `planform_change_object_rebuild` `1.82s`,
    `p110_p111_planform_polish` `1.22s`,
    `orogen_belt_repair` `0.32s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus P112.12:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Acceptance booleans remained unchanged (`19/23` true).
- Conclusion:
  - P112.13 is observation-only and does not change generated maps.
  - The next performance target is now concrete:
    first inspect `_p1115_final_ocean_floor_hierarchy_expression`, then the
    object rebuild path triggered by planform changes.  Any accepted
    optimization must keep the same terminal-array equivalence gate unless it is
    explicitly a quality-changing plate-engineering pass.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.14 P1115 rebuild attribution

- Motivation:
  - P112.13 initially made `p1115_ocean_floor_hierarchy` look like the largest
    terminal-bathymetry substage, but that label covered both the P1115 ocean
    floor expression and the object rebuilds triggered after P1115 changed the
    surface.
  - Before optimizing, the attribution needed to distinguish actual bathymetry
    math from repeated semantic object reconstruction.
- Implementation:
  - Added an optional `profile_stage_seconds` map to
    `_p1115_final_ocean_floor_hierarchy_expression`.
  - The P1115 internal subprofile records:
    `eligibility_return`, `prepare_arrays`, `boundary_zone_derivation`,
    `coherent_ocean_floor_fabric`, `open_ocean_shoal_deepen`,
    `semantic_shoal_cleanup`, `deep_preserve`, `final_trench_bathymetry`, and
    `land_mask_guard`.
  - Split the surrounding terminal-bathymetry attribution into:
    `p1115_ocean_floor_expression`,
    `p1115_surface_change_object_rebuild`,
    `p1115_semantic_shoal_cleanup`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup_object_rebuild`, and
    `p1115_trench_depth_province_mark`.
  - Updated `test_p1115_final_ocean_floor_expression_preserves_land_mask` to
    exercise and assert the P1115 subprofile while preserving the existing
    land-mask and deep-preserve checks.
- Validation:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p1115 or p1116_final_ocean or p111628 or p11153 or p11152'`
    -> `12 passed, 96 deselected`.
  - After splitting the terminal P1115 rebuild attribution:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p1115 or p1116_final_ocean or p111628 or p11153 or p11152 or audit_summary_records_stage_timings'`
    -> `13 passed, 95 deselected`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `108 passed`.
- 900-cell attribution:
  - First P1115 internal subprofile output:
    `out_p112_p1115_subprofile_900_seed707_20260703`.
    It showed that the true P1115 function total was only `0.36s`, with:
    `open_ocean_shoal_deepen` `0.13s`,
    `boundary_zone_derivation` `0.12s`,
    `coherent_ocean_floor_fabric` `0.10s`,
    `semantic_shoal_cleanup` `0.01s`.
  - Refined rebuild-attribution output:
    `out_p112_p1115_rebuild_attribution_900_seed707_20260703`.
    Runtime `55.4s`; terrain `36.0s`; tectonics `13.8s`; climate `5.2s`.
  - Refined terminal-bathymetry top stages:
    `planform_change_object_rebuild` `1.83s`,
    `p1115_surface_change_object_rebuild` `1.44s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup_object_rebuild` `1.22s`,
    `p110_p111_planform_polish` `1.21s`,
    `p1115_ocean_floor_expression` `0.36s`,
    `orogen_belt_repair` `0.32s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_p1115_subprofile_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Acceptance booleans remained unchanged (`19/23` true).
- Conclusion:
  - P1115's actual bathymetry expression is not the bottleneck at 900 cells;
    the bottleneck is repeated post-surface-change object reconstruction.
  - The next safe acceleration target is a reusable/conditional terminal object
    rebuild helper for the repeated
    `_ocean_geography` -> `_ocean_fabric_objects` ->
    `_margin_landform_objects` -> `_arc_plume_landform_objects` chain.
  - Any rebuild optimization must continue using terminal-array equivalence as
    the acceptance gate, because these objects feed later bathymetry and audit
    semantics.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.15 terminal ocean semantic context helper

- Motivation:
  - P112.14 identified repeated terminal object reconstruction as the real
    terminal-bathymetry hotspot.  Before adding cache or conditional reuse, the
    repeated rebuild chain needed one canonical helper so later optimization has
    a single acceptance surface.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_rebuild_ocean_semantic_context(...)`.
  - The helper preserves the original rebuild order exactly:
    `_ocean_geography` -> `_ocean_fabric_objects` ->
    `_margin_landform_objects` -> `_arc_plume_landform_objects`.
  - Replaced the repeated terminal rebuild blocks after:
    planform-change polish, P1115 surface change, P1115 semantic cleanup,
    P111631 post-P109 semantic cleanup, P111637A terminal spine land
    consistency, and P111637B final spine elevation consistency.
  - Initial semantic-object construction and the post-trim ocean-fabric
    profiled path remain explicit because they have different profiling needs.
- Validation:
  - Added:
    `test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain`.
  - The test compares helper output to the previous manual chain for ocean
    geography fields, ocean geography objects/diagnostics, ocean fabric,
    margin landforms, and arc/plume landforms.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `109 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_terminal_context_helper_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `54.4s`; terrain `35.4s`; tectonics `13.5s`; climate `5.1s`.
  - Versus P112.14 rebuild-attribution run:
    runtime `55.4s -> 54.4s`,
    terrain `36.0s -> 35.4s`.
    This is treated as normal run-to-run variance rather than a claimed
    algorithmic speedup, because the helper is a mechanical refactor.
  - Terminal-bathymetry profile remained structurally the same:
    `planform_change_object_rebuild` `1.81s`,
    `p1115_surface_change_object_rebuild` `1.42s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup_object_rebuild` `1.20s`,
    `p110_p111_planform_polish` `1.20s`,
    `p1115_ocean_floor_expression` `0.36s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_p1115_rebuild_attribution_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Acceptance booleans remained unchanged (`19/23` true).
- Conclusion:
  - P112.15 is a safe preparatory refactor, not the final acceleration.
  - The next step can add subprofile or caching at the helper boundary:
    first measure which helper child dominates across terminal rebuilds
    (`ocean_geography`, `ocean_fabric`, `margin_landforms`, or
    `arc_plume_landforms`), then consider exact reuse only when the surface and
    semantic inputs are proven unchanged.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.16 terminal ocean semantic context child profile

- Motivation:
  - P112.15 created a safe terminal rebuild helper, but the helper still hid
    which child stage dominated the repeated rebuild cost.
  - This pass adds attribution only; it does not cache, skip, or approximate any
    object rebuild.
- Implementation:
  - `_rebuild_ocean_semantic_context(...)` now accepts optional
    `profile_stage_seconds` and `profile_prefix` keyword arguments.
  - Each helper invocation records:
    `ocean_geography`, `ocean_fabric`, `margin_landforms`, and
    `arc_plume_landforms`, prefixed by call site.
  - Terminal rebuild call sites now use prefixes:
    `planform_change`, `p1115_surface_change`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup`, `p111631_pre_cleanup`,
    `p111631_post_cleanup`, `p111637a_spine_land`, and
    `p111637b_spine_elevation`.
  - The terrain internal profile now emits
    `subprofiles.terminal_ocean_semantic_context` when at least one terminal
    context rebuild occurs.
  - Updated
    `test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain` to assert
    the helper child-profile keys while preserving exact output equivalence.
- Validation:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `109 passed`.
- 900-cell attribution:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_terminal_context_child_profile_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `54.7s`; terrain `35.5s`; tectonics `13.5s`; climate `5.2s`.
  - `terminal_bathymetry_polish` remained structurally unchanged:
    `planform_change_object_rebuild` `1.81s`,
    `p1115_surface_change_object_rebuild` `1.42s`,
    `p110_p111_planform_polish` `1.21s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup_object_rebuild` `1.21s`,
    `p1115_ocean_floor_expression` `0.36s`.
  - New `terminal_ocean_semantic_context` total: `4.47s`.
    Top child stages:
    `planform_change.arc_plume_landforms` `0.64s`,
    `planform_change.ocean_fabric` `0.57s`,
    `p1115_surface_change.arc_plume_landforms` `0.50s`,
    `p1115_surface_change.ocean_fabric` `0.45s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup.arc_plume_landforms` `0.43s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup.ocean_fabric` `0.39s`,
    `planform_change.margin_landforms` `0.32s`,
    `planform_change.ocean_geography` `0.28s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_terminal_context_helper_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Acceptance booleans remained unchanged (`19/23` true).
- Conclusion:
  - The terminal rebuild hotspot is now localized: `arc_plume_landforms` and
    `ocean_fabric` dominate repeated rebuild time, with geography and margin
    landforms secondary.
  - The next exact acceleration target should be a cache or conditional reuse
    check specifically for terminal `arc_plume_landforms`/`ocean_fabric`, keyed
    by surface/land-mask and relevant crust/sediment inputs.  It must keep the
    P112 terminal-array equivalence gate.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.17 exact terminal context cache

- Motivation:
  - P112.16 showed that terminal object rebuilds are repeated several times
    during late bathymetry polish.  Some late cleanup passes request the same
    ocean semantic context without changing the surface or crust inputs.
  - This pass adds only an exact cache: it must never reuse context after an
    input change and must preserve terminal-array identity.
- Implementation:
  - `_rebuild_ocean_semantic_context(...)` now accepts an optional `cache`.
  - Cache keys are conservative:
    same `world`, same `grid`, same grid size, same `world.time_myr`, same
    `sea_level`, and exact array equality for `surface`, `crust_type`,
    `crust_domain`, `crust_origin`, `crust_thick`, and `sediment`.
  - Cached inputs are stored as snapshots, so later in-place changes to caller
    arrays invalidate the cache.
  - Cache hits are recorded in
    `terminal_ocean_semantic_context` as `<prefix>.context_cache_hit`.
  - Terminal rebuild call sites share one per-step `terminal_context_cache`.
- Validation:
  - Updated
    `test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain` so the
    first helper call is compared against the manual chain and the second
    identical call proves a cache hit with identical normalized output.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `109 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_terminal_context_exact_cache_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `55.0s`; terrain `35.7s`; tectonics `13.6s`; climate `5.2s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_terminal_context_child_profile_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Top-level acceptance metadata remained unchanged.
  - The exact cache produced one observed hit:
    `p111631_pre_cleanup.context_cache_hit`.
  - `terminal_ocean_semantic_context` total changed only
    `4.47s -> 4.45s`, and total runtime remained within run-to-run noise.
- Conclusion:
  - P112.17 is a safe, result-preserving optimization, but it is not a major
    speedup for the 900-cell seed-707 audit because most terminal rebuilds
    happen after real surface changes.
  - The next meaningful acceleration target should move below this helper:
    reduce repeated work inside `arc_plume_landforms` and `ocean_fabric`,
    especially shared distance/geography scans, while continuing to require
    exact terminal-array equivalence for default runs.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.18 terminal ocean context detail profile

- Motivation:
  - P112.17 proved that exact whole-context caching is safe but has only a
    small effect on the 900-cell seed-707 audit.
  - The next acceleration target needs finer attribution inside
    `ocean_fabric` and `arc_plume`, without changing terrain generation or
    double-counting the existing top-level terminal-context profile.
- Implementation:
  - Added `terminal_context_child_stage_seconds` in `TerrainModule.step`.
  - Added a new terrain subprofile:
    `terminal_ocean_semantic_context_detail`.
  - `_rebuild_ocean_semantic_context(...)` now accepts
    `child_profile_stage_seconds` and forwards it to:
    `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)` with `<callsite>.ocean_fabric.*` keys, and
    `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)` with `<callsite>.arc_plume.*` keys.
  - `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)` keeps its existing profile keys unchanged for
    post-trim callers, while supporting an optional prefix for terminal detail.
  - `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)` now has optional no-op-by-default
    profile markers for:
    `source_influence`, `plume_lip_volcano_masks`, `arc_backarc_masks`,
    `plume_hotspot_masks`, `seamount_hotspot_axes`, `microcontinent_masks`,
    `component_object_summaries`, and `object_sort`.
  - Existing `terminal_ocean_semantic_context` remains the top-level elapsed
    profile, so its total is not intentionally double-counted by child stages.
- Validation:
  - Updated
    `test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain` to
    verify both the unchanged top-level profile keys and the new child detail
    keys.  Cache hits produce no child detail, as expected.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `109 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_terminal_context_detail_profile_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `56.6s`; terrain `36.9s`; tectonics `14.0s`; climate `5.2s`.
    The small slowdown is expected for added profiling instrumentation and
    should not be treated as a model regression.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_terminal_context_exact_cache_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Top-level acceptance metadata remained unchanged.
  - `terminal_ocean_semantic_context_detail` total: `3.12s`, `48` stages.
    Aggregated by family:
    `arc_plume` `1.66s`, `ocean_fabric` `1.46s`.
  - Aggregated by call site:
    `planform_change` `1.26s`,
    `p1115_surface_change` `1.00s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup` `0.86s`.
  - Aggregated by internal stage:
    `arc_plume.component_object_summaries` `1.18s`,
    `ocean_fabric.component_object_summaries` `0.75s`,
    `ocean_fabric.ridge_axis_zones` `0.36s`,
    `ocean_fabric.prepare_sources` `0.29s`,
    `arc_plume.arc_backarc_masks` `0.19s`,
    `arc_plume.plume_lip_volcano_masks` `0.17s`.
- Conclusion:
  - The most important remaining terminal-context performance target is not
    the distance field itself.  It is repeated component/object-summary work,
    especially parent-mask dilation and parent-object attachment inside
    `arc_plume` and `ocean_fabric`.
  - P112.19 should target exact object-summary acceleration: reduce repeated
    per-component full-grid masks/dilations and parent lookup overhead while
    keeping terminal arrays and object metadata stable for the audit seed.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.19 direct component masks for object summaries

- Motivation:
  - P112.18 showed that object-summary construction dominates the remaining
    terminal ocean-context detail profile.
  - The safest first optimization is mechanical: remove per-component full-grid
    temporary masks where a component-cell list already provides the exact same
    information.
- Implementation:
  - In `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)`, component parent masks now use
    `_dilated_bool(grid, comp, passes=parent_expand)` directly instead of
    building a full-grid `comp_mask` and then calling `_dilate_mask(...)`.
  - In `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)`, the same direct component dilation
    is used for parent masks.
  - Arc/plume `land_fraction`, `ocean_fraction`, and `ridge_fraction` now use
    local component indexing (`flag[comp]`) instead of full-grid
    `comp_mask & flag` expressions.
  - `plume_seed_cells` is computed once per arc/plume object rebuild instead of
    once per summarized component.
  - Parent-object lookup, parent-mask dilation radius, component sorting,
    object IDs, object limits, and all model thresholds remain unchanged.
- Validation:
  - Added
    `test_terrain_component_summary_direct_masks_match_full_mask_reference` to
    lock the equivalence assumptions:
    direct component-cell dilation equals full-mask dilation, and local
    component area fractions equal the old full-grid mask fractions.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_component_summary_direct_masks_match_full_mask_reference tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `110 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_object_summary_direct_masks_seedcache_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `54.9s`; terrain `35.6s`; tectonics `13.6s`; climate `5.2s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_terminal_context_detail_profile_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Top-level acceptance metadata remained unchanged.
  - Compared with P112.18:
    `semantic_object_build` `19.21s -> 18.50s`,
    `terminal_bathymetry_polish` `6.59s -> 6.34s`,
    `terminal_ocean_semantic_context` `4.62s -> 4.41s`,
    `terminal_ocean_semantic_context_detail` `3.12s -> 2.95s`,
    `post_trim_ocean_fabric` `2.71s -> 2.61s`.
  - Aggregated terminal-context detail:
    `arc_plume.component_object_summaries` `1.18s -> 1.11s`,
    `ocean_fabric.component_object_summaries` `0.75s -> 0.71s`,
    `ocean_fabric.ridge_axis_zones` `0.36s -> 0.35s`,
    `ocean_fabric.prepare_sources` `0.29s -> 0.28s`.
- Conclusion:
  - P112.19 is a safe, result-preserving micro-optimization.  It reduces some
    object-summary overhead and removes unnecessary full-grid component masks,
    but the remaining profile is still dominated by parent-object attachment
    and component summary construction.
  - The next safe target should avoid changing geology: add exact
    instrumentation around parent-object lookup inside object summaries, then
    optimize only the proven repeated pieces such as parent-mask dilation,
    overlap lookup, and nearest-parent fallback.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.20 parent-object lookup detail profile

- Motivation:
  - P112.19 reduced obvious full-grid component-mask overhead, but object
    summaries still dominate terminal ocean-context detail.
  - Before changing parent metadata or lookup mechanics, parent-object lookup
    needed its own attribution so we do not optimize the wrong part of the
    object-summary loop.
- Implementation:
  - `_related_object_ids(...)` now accepts optional
    `profile_stage_seconds` and `profile_prefix`.
  - The profile records:
    `overlap_index`, `direct_overlap`, `expanded_mask`,
    `expanded_overlap`, `nearest_centroid`, `nearest_context_rows`, and
    `nearest_scan_sort` when those paths execute.
  - Added a separate `terminal_parent_object_lookup` subprofile for terminal
    ocean-context helper calls.
  - Added a separate `post_trim_ocean_fabric_parent_lookup` subprofile for the
    post-trim ocean-fabric path.
  - Kept `terminal_ocean_semantic_context_detail` and
    `post_trim_ocean_fabric` as their original coarse child-stage profiles, so
    parent lookup detail does not double-count inside those totals.
  - Default `_related_object_ids(...)` callers remain unprofiled unless an
    explicit profile dictionary is passed.
- Validation:
  - Added
    `test_terrain_related_object_ids_profile_matches_unprofiled_lookup`.
    It verifies that direct-overlap and expanded-overlap profile calls return
    exactly the same parent IDs as unprofiled calls.
  - Updated
    `test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain` to verify
    parent lookup profile propagation through the terminal context helper and
    no child/profile emission on exact cache hits.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_related_object_ids_profile_matches_unprofiled_lookup tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `111 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_parent_lookup_separate_profile_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `54.8s`; terrain `35.6s`; tectonics `13.6s`; climate `5.1s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_object_summary_direct_masks_seedcache_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Top-level acceptance metadata remained unchanged.
  - Clean parent lookup attribution:
    `terminal_parent_object_lookup` total `0.135s`;
    `post_trim_ocean_fabric_parent_lookup` total `0.196s`.
  - Terminal parent lookup aggregated by stage:
    `direct_overlap` `0.099s`,
    `overlap_index` `0.035s`,
    `expanded_mask` `0.001s`,
    `expanded_overlap` `0.000s`.
  - Terminal parent lookup aggregated by family:
    `arc_plume` `0.086s`,
    `ocean_fabric` `0.049s`.
  - Post-trim ocean-fabric parent lookup:
    `overlap_index` `0.135s`,
    `direct_overlap` `0.060s`,
    expanded fallback near zero.
- Conclusion:
  - Parent-object lookup is not the leading remaining performance target in the
    900-cell seed-707 audit.  It is useful to keep profiled, but it does not
    explain most of `component_object_summaries`.
  - The next exact target should stay inside component summaries but look
    beyond parent lookup: component extraction/sorting, repeated area/statistic
    reductions, centroid calculations, plume-distance summaries, and
    object-list construction.
  - Any further optimization must continue to use terminal-array equivalence as
    the hard gate, and should also compare object metadata if the next pass
    changes summary construction rather than only instrumentation.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.21 component-summary detail profile

- Motivation:
  - P112.20 showed that parent-object lookup is measurable but not the leading
    remaining object-summary cost.
  - The next step is to profile the non-parent work inside object summaries
    without changing object construction semantics.
- Implementation:
  - Added a separate `terminal_object_summary_detail` subprofile for terminal
    ocean-context helper calls.
  - Added a separate `post_trim_ocean_fabric_object_summary` subprofile for the
    post-trim ocean-fabric path.
  - `_rebuild_ocean_semantic_context(...)` now accepts
    `object_summary_profile_stage_seconds` and forwards it to
    `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)` and `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)`.
  - `_ocean_fabric_objects(...)` now optionally records:
    `component_extract_sort`, `parent_mask`, `base_stats`,
    `parent_basin_shape`, and `object_fields`.
  - `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)` now optionally records:
    `component_extract_sort`, `parent_mask`, `base_stats`,
    `parent_continent_volcanism`, `plume_distance`,
    `plume_age_correlation`, and `object_fields`.
  - Parent lookup remains in its own P112.20 subprofiles, so nested timing does
    not double-count parent lookup inside the new object-summary detail totals.
  - Exact cache hits still emit no child, parent, or object-summary detail.
- Validation:
  - Updated
    `test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain` to verify
    object-summary detail propagation through the terminal context helper and
    no object-summary detail emission on exact cache hits.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`

    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `111 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:

    `out_p112_component_summary_detail_profile_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `54.9s`; terrain `35.7s`; tectonics `13.6s`; climate `5.1s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_parent_lookup_separate_profile_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Top-level acceptance metadata remained unchanged.
  - `terminal_object_summary_detail` total `1.705s`, `36` stages.
    Aggregated by internal stage:
    `object_fields` `0.588s`,
    `plume_age_correlation` `0.369s`,
    `parent_mask` `0.222s`,
    `plume_distance` `0.203s`,
    `base_stats` `0.162s`,
    `component_extract_sort` `0.092s`,
    `parent_basin_shape` `0.037s`,
    `parent_continent_volcanism` `0.032s`.
  - `terminal_object_summary_detail` aggregated by family:
    `arc_plume` `1.035s`,
    `ocean_fabric` `0.670s`.
  - `terminal_object_summary_detail` aggregated by call site:
    `planform_change` `0.680s`,
    `p1115_surface_change` `0.553s`,
    `p1115_semantic_cleanup` `0.473s`.
  - `post_trim_ocean_fabric_object_summary` total `1.175s`.
    Aggregated by internal stage:
    `object_fields` `0.736s`,
    `parent_mask` `0.173s`,
    `base_stats` `0.129s`,
    `component_extract_sort` `0.071s`,
    `parent_basin_shape` `0.066s`.
- Conclusion:
  - The dominant remaining object-summary cost is not component extraction or
    parent lookup.  It is object field construction/statistics, plus arc/plume
    plume-distance and plume-age correlation summaries.
  - P112.22 should target exact summary construction improvements, starting
    with low-risk local optimizations:
    vectorize plume-distance summaries where object metadata can be proven
    stable within tolerance, and reduce repeated per-component percentile/
    fraction work only with object-metadata comparison in addition to terminal
    array equivalence.

#### 2026-07-03 P112.22 plume-distance vectorization

- Motivation:
  - P112.21 identified arc/plume object-summary work as a remaining measurable
    cost, with `plume_distance` contributing about `0.203s` inside terminal
    object-summary detail on the 900-cell seed-707 audit.
  - This is a low-risk candidate because each component needs the nearest plume
    seed distance for each cell, and the old implementation performed that
    calculation with an explicit per-cell loop.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_nearest_seed_distance_deg(...)` to compute nearest angular seed
    distances for a component with one vectorized dot-product matrix.
  - Replaced the old per-cell plume-seed loop in
    `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)` with the helper.
  - The helper preserves the old first-tie behavior by using `np.argmax(...)`
    along the same ordered seed list and returns the nearest seed used for
    parent plume linkage.
- Validation:
  - Added `test_terrain_nearest_seed_distance_matches_reference_loop`, which
    compares the helper against the old loop at `1e-12` absolute tolerance.
  - Added
    `test_p11222_arc_plume_vectorized_plume_distance_preserves_objects`, which
    monkeypatches `_nearest_seed_distance_deg(...)` back to the old loop and
    recursively verifies that `_arc_plume_landform_objects(...)` emits the same
    object list, including floats and NaNs.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m py_compile aevum/modules/terrain.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
    -> pass.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_nearest_seed_distance_matches_reference_loop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_ocean_semantic_context_helper_matches_manual_chain tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_audit_summary_records_stage_timings -q`
    -> `3 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_terrain_nearest_seed_distance_matches_reference_loop tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11222_arc_plume_vectorized_plume_distance_preserves_objects -q`
    -> `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `113 passed`.
- 900-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p112_plume_distance_vectorized_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `54.379s` versus `54.853s` for
    `out_p112_component_summary_detail_profile_900_seed707_20260703`.
  - Module totals:
    terrain `35.050s` versus `35.664s`;
    tectonics `13.727s` versus `13.617s`;
    climate `5.134s` versus `5.092s`.
  - Terminal array equivalence versus
    `out_p112_component_summary_detail_profile_900_seed707_20260703`:
    all `76` common arrays in `p107_terminal_arrays.npz` matched exactly, with
    no missing or extra keys.
  - Top-level metrics excluding `terrain_internal_profile`, `assets`, and
    `array_archive` had `0` changed fields; maximum numeric difference was
    `0.0`.
  - Acceptance metadata remained unchanged:
    `p107_0_observability_complete=True`,
    `has_8000_tier=False`,
    `has_24000_tier=False`,
    `quality_improvement_not_claimed=True`.
  - Profile movement:
    `semantic_object_build` `18.515s -> 18.092s`,
    `terminal_bathymetry_polish` `6.387s -> 6.233s`,
    `planform_seaway_cleanup` `2.726s -> 2.722s`,
    `terminal_ocean_semantic_context_detail` `2.996s -> 2.836s`,
    `terminal_object_summary_detail` `1.705s -> 1.558s`,
    `post_trim_ocean_fabric_object_summary` `1.175s -> 1.173s`.
  - Terminal object-summary detail by internal stage:
    `object_fields` `0.588s -> 0.586s`,
    `plume_age_correlation` `0.369s -> 0.367s`,
    `parent_mask` `0.222s -> 0.223s`,
    `base_stats` `0.162s -> 0.163s`,
    `component_extract_sort` `0.092s -> 0.092s`,
    `plume_distance` `0.203s -> 0.058s`,
    `parent_basin_shape` `0.037s -> 0.038s`,
    `parent_continent_volcanism` `0.032s -> 0.032s`.
  - Terminal object-summary detail by family:
    `arc_plume` `1.035s -> 0.887s`;
    `ocean_fabric` `0.670s -> 0.671s`.
- Conclusion:
  - This is a result-preserving optimization: terminal arrays, non-profile
    metrics, acceptance metadata, helper output, and object metadata were all
    checked against the previous implementation.
  - The local plume-distance cost is substantially lower, but total runtime is
    still dominated by broader terrain semantic rebuild and bathymetry polish
    work.  The next exact target should be `object_fields` construction and
    repeated per-object statistic reductions, with the same object-metadata
    equivalence gate.

#### 2026-07-03 P124 orogenic spine geometry regularization

- Motivation:
  - After P122 compacted overwide orogenic detail footprints and P123 improved
    spine-aligned high-relief expression, the remaining visible issue was local
    spine linework irregularity: short one-cell gaps, excess endpoints, and
    slightly angular main-crest/branch organization.
  - The repair must not create new broad mountain blobs or semantic spine cells
    that later terrain cleanup turns into low-relief terrain.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p124_orogenic_spine_geometry_regularization(...)` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - P124 now runs late, after P1134 low-resolution terminal cleanup and before
    final drainage/packaging, so it evaluates the final `surface_new` rather
    than an earlier terrain state.
  - The helper fills only conservative, high-relief, nonpolar, hierarchy-backed
    spine gaps:
    `continental_land`, `hierarchy >= 2`, final relative relief at least
    `2400m`, and existing crest/branch neighbor support.
  - The selected area is capped by
    `min(0.0040 * world_area, 0.35 * spine_area)`.
  - Guard conditions reject any change that increases component count, short
    components, or endpoint pressure, reduces branch attachment, reduces the
    linework score, or introduces new spine cells outside the parent hierarchy
    peak support.
  - When P124 is accepted, `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine` is updated and
    P1149/P115 spine object inventories are rebuilt so the object layer matches
    the final field.
  - Added P124 telemetry to `p111635_orogenic_spine_linework`:
    accepted/guard flags, candidate and added area, component/endpoint/short
    counts, branch attachment, and linework score before/after.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `test_p124_orogenic_spine_geometry_regularization_fills_supported_gap`.
  - Extended
    `test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics` to assert P124
    telemetry.
  - Validation commands:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `135 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p124_spine_geometry_regularization_on_2500_seed707_20260703_v6`.
  - Runtime `146.2s`.
  - P124 accepted with `7` candidate cells and `1` added spine cell.
  - Spine endpoints improved `7 -> 6`; component count stayed `2`;
    short component count stayed `0`; linework score stayed `1.0`.
  - P123-aligned relief metrics did not regress:
    ridge-axis relief continuity score `0.5126`, relief-2400 component count
    `6`, relief-3000 component count `7`.
  - Array comparison against
    `out_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_bridge_on_2500_seed707_20260703`
    showed only `field__terrain_orogenic_hierarchy_spine` changed
    (`1` cell); `terrain.elevation_m` changed `0` cells.
- 8000-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p124_spine_geometry_regularization_on_8000_seed707_20260703_v2`.
  - Render:
    `out_p124_spine_geometry_regularization_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `465.4s`, comparable to the P123 8000 run.
  - P124 accepted with `33` candidate cells and `20` added spine cells.
  - Added cells had final relative relief range `2454.7m` to `4684.1m`.
  - Spine endpoints improved `17 -> 16`; component count stayed `6`;
    short component count stayed `0`; branch attachment stayed `1.0`.
  - Linework continuity score improved `0.6980 -> 0.7338`.
  - P123-aligned relief continuity improved `0.5148 -> 0.5266`; relief-2400
    and relief-3000 component counts stayed `10` and `14`.
  - Array comparison against
    `out_p123_spine_high_relief_continuity_bridge_on_8000_seed707_20260703`
    showed only `field__terrain_orogenic_hierarchy_spine` changed
    (`20` cells); `terrain.elevation_m` changed `0` cells.
- Current conclusion:
  - P124 is a conservative semantic linework cleanup.  It improves final spine
    continuity without altering elevation or broad planform, and it is now safe
    against the earlier failure mode where pre-cleanup spine additions could
    become low-relief semantic debt after terminal terrain cleanup.
  - Remaining plate-engineering work should move to larger-scale organization:
    stronger main-crest/branch hierarchy, less polar overclassification, and
    reducing visible raster/angular artifacts at the rendered level.

#### 2026-07-03 P125 terminal orogenic semantic land consistency

- Motivation:
  - The P124 8000-cell validation exposed a concrete terminal consistency bug:
    some cells that were below final sea level still carried exported orogenic
    semantics in `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy`,
    `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`, and
    `terrain.orogenic_shoulder_halo`.
  - This is not an elevation problem.  It is stale semantic debt after final
    terrain cleanup, and it can pollute objects, overlays, morphology, hex
    compilation, and later climate/water routing.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency(...)` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - P125 runs after P124 and before final drainage/packaging, so it sees the
    final `surface_new` and final `sea_level`.
  - It clears only submerged cells, defined as `surface_new - sea_level < 0`,
    from four terminal orogenic semantic fields:
    `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy`,
    `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`,
    `terrain.orogenic_shoulder_halo`, and
    `terrain.orogenic_highland_apron`.
  - It does not alter `terrain.elevation_m`, crust fields, land/ocean state, or
    bathymetry fields.
  - Guards reject shape mismatches, any positive semantic expansion, and any
    change to land-cell semantics.
  - When hierarchy/spine semantics are cleared, P1149/P115 orogenic spine
    object inventories are rebuilt so exported objects match the terminal
    fields.
  - Added P125 telemetry under
    `p111636_polar_edge_orogen_overclassification`, with direct final counts
    for submerged hierarchy/spine/halo/apron cells and full before/after
    telemetry.  A compact subset is also exposed under
    `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `test_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_clears_submerged_only`.
  - Extended P111.6.8 and P111.6.36 audit tests to assert P125 telemetry.
  - Focused validation:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p125 or p111636_audit_reports_polar_edge_overclassification_metrics or p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics'`
    -> `3 passed, 133 deselected`.
  - Full related validation:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `136 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `146.2s`.
  - P125 had no terminal candidates:
    accepted `False`, guard `False`, candidate cells `0`.
  - Final direct audit counts were all zero:
    submerged hierarchy `0`, submerged spine `0`, submerged halo `0`,
    submerged apron `0`, and submerged terminal orogenic semantic cells `0`.
  - This confirms the pass is a no-op on the 2500 case where the terminal
    semantics were already land-consistent.
- 8000-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Render:
    `out_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `479.7s`, comparable to the P124 8000 run.
  - P125 accepted with `41` terminal submerged semantic candidates
    (`0.00512` world-area fraction).
  - Cleared cells:
    hierarchy `25`, spine `13`, halo `16`, apron `0`.
  - Final after-counts are all zero:
    submerged hierarchy `0`, submerged spine `0`, submerged halo `0`,
    submerged apron `0`, and submerged terminal orogenic semantic cells `0`.
  - Edge-related cleanup was substantial (`25` cleared edge cells);
    polar cleanup was `0`.
  - Land semantics were preserved exactly:
    `p125_land_semantic_cleared_cell_count = 0`.
  - Array comparison against
    `out_p124_spine_geometry_regularization_on_8000_seed707_20260703_v2`
    showed only the intended fields changed:
    `field__terrain_orogenic_parent_hierarchy` (`25` cells),
    `field__terrain_orogenic_hierarchy_spine` (`13` cells), and
    `field__terrain_orogenic_shoulder_halo` (`16` cells).
    `field__terrain_elevation_m` changed `0` cells, and
    `field__terrain_orogenic_highland_apron` changed `0` cells.
  - Visual review of `p107_array_contact_sheet.png`, `elevation.png`,
    `orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png`, and
    `orogenic_parent_hierarchy_overlay.png` showed no elevation or planform
    regression; the orogenic overlays now stay on final land.
- Current conclusion:
  - P125 fixes the strongest remaining terminal semantic inconsistency after
    P124 without changing terrain geometry.
  - The next work item can return to the broader quality issues: stronger
    main-crest/branch organization, more regular foreland/apron placement,
    reduced polar/edge overclassification in the generator itself, and lower
    raster/angular artifacts in rendered linework.

#### 2026-07-03 P126 terminal orogenic fringe support regularization

- Motivation:
  - After P125, the 8000-cell terrain still had locally fragmented fringe
    semantics around otherwise acceptable mountain systems.
  - Quantitative residuals on
    `out_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_8000_seed707_20260703`
    showed the main hierarchy was stable but the fringe classes were noisy:
    foreland `48` cells in `15` components with `8` tiny components, shoulder
    halo `117` cells in `44` components with `27` tiny components, and highland
    apron `19` cells in `7` components with `3` singletons.
  - The spine linework did not need another structural pass at this point:
    spine components were `6`, short spine components were `0`, branch
    attachment was `1.0`, and P124's linework score was `0.7338`.
- Implementation:
  - Added `_p126_terminal_orogenic_fringe_support_regularization(...)` in
    `aevum/modules/terrain.py`.
  - P126 runs after P125 and before final drainage/packaging.
  - It is a semantic-only deletion pass.  It does not alter
    `terrain.elevation_m`, crust fields, bathymetry fields, spine semantics, or
    `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy >= 2`.
  - It clears:
    - detached or weakly supported tiny foreland components
      (`hierarchy == 1`);
    - shoulder-halo components of at most two cells;
    - highland-apron singleton components.
  - The pass is guarded by strict non-expansion checks, no peak-hierarchy
    changes, no spine changes, and conservative area caps:
    at most `1.0%` of world area, at most `42%` of total fringe area, and
    per-field caps for foreland/halo/apron.
  - Added P126 telemetry under `p11168_orogenic_parent_hierarchy`:
    accepted/guard flags, candidate and cleared cell counts, component counts
    before/after, tiny-component counts before/after, and peak/spine unchanged
    counters.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `test_p126_terminal_orogenic_fringe_support_regularization_clears_fragments`.
  - Extended
    `test_p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics` to assert P126
    telemetry.
  - Focused validation:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p126 or p11168_audit_reports_parent_orogen_hierarchy_metrics'`
    -> `2 passed, 135 deselected`.
  - Full related validation:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `137 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p126_terminal_orogenic_fringe_support_regularization_2500_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `138.9s`.
  - P126 found `27` candidate fringe cells, but guard-reverted because the
    candidate area fraction was `0.01079`, just above the `1.0%` world-area cap.
  - Array comparison against
    `out_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_2500_seed707_20260703`
    showed `0` changed cells in all exported arrays, including elevation and
    all orogenic semantic fields.
  - This is the intended low-resolution behavior: do not delete a large
    fraction of coarse fringe semantics just because they are cell-small.
- 8000-cell validation:
  - Output:
    `out_p126_terminal_orogenic_fringe_support_regularization_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Render:
    `out_p126_terminal_orogenic_fringe_support_regularization_render_8000_seed707_20260703`.
  - Runtime `489.0s`.
  - P126 accepted with `50` candidate cells
    (`0.00625` world-area fraction).
  - Cleared cells:
    foreland `11`, halo `36`, apron `3`.
  - Component improvements:
    foreland components `15 -> 8`, tiny foreland components `8 -> 2`;
    halo components `44 -> 17`, tiny halo components `27 -> 0`;
    apron components `7 -> 4`, tiny apron components `3 -> 0`.
  - Guard invariants held:
    peak-hierarchy changed cells `0`, spine changed cells `0`.
  - Array comparison against
    `out_p125_terminal_orogenic_semantic_land_consistency_8000_seed707_20260703`
    showed only the intended fields changed:
    `field__terrain_orogenic_parent_hierarchy` (`11` foreland cells),
    `field__terrain_orogenic_shoulder_halo` (`36` cells), and
    `field__terrain_orogenic_highland_apron` (`3` cells).
    `field__terrain_elevation_m` and
    `field__terrain_orogenic_hierarchy_spine` changed `0` cells.
  - Visual review of `p107_array_contact_sheet.png`,
    `orogenic_parent_hierarchy_overlay.png`,
    `orogenic_shoulder_halo.png`, and `orogenic_highland_apron.png` showed
    reduced fringe speckle without weakening the main crest/branch/spine
    organization.
- Current conclusion:
  - P126 addresses the local classification fragmentation part of the expert
