    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell P1145-enabled probes:
  - Commands used `--set-global terrain.enable_p1145_whole_mask_spine_planner=1`.
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p143_class_aware_p1145_enabled_2500_seed909_20260704`
    - `out_p143_class_aware_p1145_enabled_2500_seed1111_20260704`
  - seed `909`:
    - P143 class repair needed: `true`;
    - P1145 no longer exits as `already_good`;
    - reject reason becomes `no_multi_spine_support_component`;
    - support component count `16`, multi-spine support count `0`;
    - class score stays `0.450 -> 0.450`;
    - class small-area fraction stays `1.000 -> 1.000`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P143 class repair needed: `true`;
    - P1145 no longer exits as `already_good`;
    - reject reason becomes `no_multi_spine_support_component`;
    - support component count `16`, multi-spine support count `0`;
    - class score stays `0.450 -> 0.450`;
    - class small-area fraction stays `1.000 -> 1.000`.
- Current conclusion:
  - P143 fixes the first planner attribution bug: class-bad cases are no
    longer silently treated as already good when P1145 is enabled.
  - The remaining blocker is deeper: P1145 searches paths between combined
    spine components, but the bad cases have one combined component per support
    region while crest/branch class components are fragmented inside it.
  - Next step P144 should add class-component path planning: build repair
    options from crest-spine components and branch-spine components separately,
    promote existing hierarchy cells along class paths when needed, and accept
    only repairs that improve class-aware metrics without raising junction
    pressure or breaking P128 polyline readiness.

#### 2026-07-04 P144 class-component spine path planning

- Motivation:
  - P143 showed that class-bad seeds no longer fail as `already_good`, but the
    existing planner still cannot act because it only searches between combined
    spine components.
  - P144 adds explicit crest/branch class-component path options so the planner
    can try to repair fragmented class linework without depending on combined
    component splits.
- Implementation:
  - Extended `plan_whole_orogen_spine_mask(...)` with three bridge classes:
    - existing external branch bridge cells;
    - crest-class bridge cells promoted inside existing crest hierarchy;
    - branch-class bridge cells promoted inside existing branch hierarchy.
  - Class path options are generated independently for crest and branch spine
    components, using shortest paths constrained to the existing class hierarchy
    component.
  - Final trial profiles now evaluate the union of external and class bridges.
  - P143 class-safe acceptance remains active:
    - combined score may not regress;
    - class-aware score may not regress;
    - class small-area fraction may not increase;
    - material improvement may come from class-aware metrics.
  - Added reject reason `19 = no_class_path_options`.
  - Exposed P144 telemetry:
    - class path option and selected counts;
    - crest/branch class path option counts;
    - class attempted/found path counts;
    - crest/branch/class promoted spine cell counts;
    - class-path profile rejection count.
  - Added P144 fields to P107 detailed metrics and compact summary.
- Tests:
  - Extended
    `test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics` to assert P144
    telemetry.
  - Focused validation:
    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p144 or p143 or p142 or p141 or p140 or p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics'`
    -> `3 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full validation:
    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `152 passed`.
  - Array-render smoke:
    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell P1145-enabled probes:
  - Commands used `--set-global terrain.enable_p1145_whole_mask_spine_planner=1`.
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p144_class_component_path_planning_2500_seed909_20260704`
    - `out_p144_class_component_path_planning_2500_seed1111_20260704`
  - seed `909`:
    - P143 class repair needed: `true`;
    - P144 class path options: `0`;
    - reject reason: `no_class_path_options`;
    - class score remains `0.450 -> 0.450`;
    - class small-area fraction remains `1.000 -> 1.000`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P143 class repair needed: `true`;
    - P144 class path options: `0`;
    - reject reason: `no_class_path_options`;
    - class score remains `0.450 -> 0.450`;
    - class small-area fraction remains `1.000 -> 1.000`.
- Array attribution:
  - Reconstructed the 2500-cell Fibonacci adjacency from the P144 probe arrays.
  - seed `909`:
    - crest hierarchy components: `11`; crest spine components: `12`;
      only `1` crest hierarchy component contains multiple crest-spine
      components.
    - branch hierarchy components: `8`; branch spine components: `8`; no
      branch hierarchy component contains multiple branch-spine components.
  - seed `1111`:
    - crest hierarchy components: `8`; crest spine components: `10`; only `1`
      crest hierarchy component contains multiple crest-spine components.
    - branch hierarchy components: `9`; branch spine components: `9`; no
      branch hierarchy component contains multiple branch-spine components.
- Current conclusion:
  - P144 adds the missing class-component path capability, but the bad
    seed909/1111 cases still cannot be repaired at the spine-path level.
  - The dominant residual is now upstream class hierarchy fragmentation:
    many crest/branch hierarchy components are themselves only one to eight
    cells, and their spine is already one component inside each tiny hierarchy
    island.
  - Next step P145 should operate one level above spine:
    consolidate or suppress tiny crest/branch hierarchy components before spine
    planning, using parent-orogen continuity, boundary support, elevation rank,
    and P128 polyline readiness as gates.  This is the correct layer for the
    remaining class-linework fragmentation.

#### 2026-07-04 P145 class hierarchy component consolidation

- Motivation:
  - P144 proved that the remaining class-linework failure cannot be fixed by
    adding paths inside existing crest/branch hierarchy islands: the islands
    themselves are often only one to eight cells.
  - P145 therefore acts one layer upstream of the spine planner, before
    P111637a/P132/P1145, and tries to consolidate tiny crest/branch hierarchy
    components under strict class-aware guards.
- Implementation:
  - Added `consolidate_class_hierarchy_components(...)` inside the parent
    orogen hierarchy pass, after P111636 polar/edge cleanup and before terminal
    spine promotion/planning.
  - The first conservative attempt, pure tiny-component demotion, was kept
    guarded but proved insufficient: deleting isolated class fragments does not
    make the remaining all-small spine components less fragmented.
  - The accepted P145 behavior is support-band bridge consolidation:
    - detect class-bad cases where combined linework is high but class-aware
      score lags and class small-area fraction is high;
    - bridge same-class crest/branch spine components through a short
      parent-orogen support band (`parent_near`, foreland, shoulder halo, and
      highland apron), excluding the opposite peak class;
    - promote accepted support cells into the matching hierarchy and spine
      class, then clear those promoted cells from halo/apron/fringe masks;
    - fall back to tiny-component demotion only when it improves class metrics.
  - Acceptance remains guarded by:
    - combined linework score floor;
    - branch attachment non-regression;
    - class-aware score non-regression;
    - class small-area fraction non-increase.
  - Exposed P145 telemetry:
    - accepted flag;
    - added crest/branch cell counts and area fractions;
    - bridge path count;
    - removed crest/branch component and cell counts;
    - class score and class/crest/branch small-area fractions before/after.
  - Added P145 fields to P107 detailed metrics and compact summary.
- Tests:
  - Extended
    `test_p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics` to assert P145
    telemetry.
  - Focused validation:
    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p145 or p144 or p143 or p142 or p141 or p140 or p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics'`
    -> `3 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full validation:
    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    -> `152 passed`.
  - Array-render smoke:
    `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1 NUMEXPR_NUM_THREADS=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`
    -> `1 passed`.
- 2500-cell P1145-enabled probes:
  - Commands used `--set-global terrain.enable_p1145_whole_mask_spine_planner=1`.
  - Final support-band bridge outputs:
    - `out_p145_support_band_bridge_consolidation_2500_seed909_20260704`
    - `out_p145_support_band_bridge_consolidation_2500_seed1111_20260704`
  - seed `909`:
    - P145 accepted: `true`;
    - bridge paths: `2`;
    - added crest cells: `5`, added branch cells: `5`;
    - class score improved `0.633 -> 0.754`;
    - class small-area fraction improved `0.667 -> 0.447`;
    - final P142 class score: `0.769`;
    - final P142 class small-area fraction: `0.421`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P145 accepted: `true`;
    - bridge paths: `2`;
    - added crest cells: `0`, added branch cells: `13`;
    - class score improved `0.450 -> 0.868`;
    - class small-area fraction improved `1.000 -> 0.239`;
    - final P142 class score: `0.884`;
    - final P142 class small-area fraction: `0.212`.
- Current conclusion:
  - P145 fixes the immediate upstream class-fragmentation blind spot with a
    small, guarded support-band bridge instead of broad deletion or arbitrary
    growth.
  - P1145 can still end as `no_class_path_options` after P145 because the
    remaining terminal planner is no longer the first-order repair mechanism in
    these probes.  The important regression gate is now the P142/P145
    class-aware profile, which improves materially.
  - Next likely phase:
    - reassess rendered morphology with P145 enabled;
    - if the map still shows fragmented ridge pixels, move from raster support
      bridges to parent-orogen polyline/province objects so crest/branch
      systems are initialized as continuous geographic objects rather than
      repaired after rasterization.

#### 2026-07-04 P145 visual reassessment and P146 handoff

- Rendered evidence:
  - Rendered P107 terminal arrays without re-running the worlds:
    - `out_p145_support_band_bridge_consolidation_2500_seed909_20260704/rendered_arrays_1440`
    - `out_p145_support_band_bridge_consolidation_2500_seed1111_20260704/rendered_arrays_1440`
  - Assets include:
    - `elevation.png`
    - `orogenic_parent_hierarchy.png`
    - `orogenic_hierarchy_spines.png`
    - `orogenic_belt_morphology_overlay.png`
    - `p107_array_contact_sheet.png`
- Visual read:
  - P145 materially improves the worst class fragmentation:
    - seed `909` now has a recognizable curved orogenic belt in the western
      and southern continental margins;
    - seed `1111` shows a clearer main crest plus attached branch-spine
      organization.
  - The remaining issues are not primarily "missing one bridge path":
    - crest/branch lines still read as rasterized bead strings at 2500 cells;
    - foreland slope and shoulder halo positions remain locally irregular;
    - polar/edge highland classifications still over-partition some bands;
    - boundary and object panels remain made of many short raster fragments.
- Array attribution after P145:
  - seed `909`:
    - crest hierarchy: `57` cells, `9` components, small-area fraction `0.387`;
    - branch hierarchy: `49` cells, `6` components, small-area fraction `0.062`;
    - crest spine: `48` cells, `9` components, small-area fraction `0.438`;
    - branch spine: `34` cells, `7` components, small-area fraction `0.677`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - crest hierarchy: `70` cells, `6` components, small-area fraction `0.186`;
    - branch hierarchy: `29` cells, `5` components, small-area fraction `0.241`;
    - crest spine: `47` cells, `8` components, small-area fraction `0.489`;
    - branch spine: `22` cells, `7` components, small-area fraction `0.591`.
- Conclusion:
  - P145 is a valid stabilization step, not the final mountain-system solution.
  - Continuing to patch individual raster cells would preserve the wrong
    abstraction boundary.  The next phase should initialize parent-orogen belts
    as continuous object/corridor systems before hierarchy/spine rasterization.

#### P146 planned: parent-orogen corridor object initialization

- Goal:
  - Convert parent-orogen input from scattered raster support into explicit
    geographic corridor objects that produce continuous crest/branch/foreland
    structure by construction.
- Design:
  - Derive a parent-orogen corridor polyline from:
    - convergent/subduction/collision/suture parent boundary objects;
    - existing P128/P129 polyline readiness where available;
    - terrain elevation rank and protected high-relief anchors.
  - For each corridor object, build a local cross-section:
    - trunk/main crest along the parent axis;
    - branch ranges offset from the main crest, attached at controlled
      junction intervals;
    - foreland slope on the lower-relief side;
    - shoulder halo and highland apron outside the peak hierarchy.
  - Rasterize from the object model into hierarchy/spine masks with stable
    budgets and guardrails:
    - preserve land mask;
    - no halo/apron overlap with crest/branch/foreland;
    - branch spine must attach to crest spine;
    - class small-area fractions should improve or stay stable;
    - P128 polyline readiness should not regress.
- Microbenchmarks:
  - Synthetic single curved collision belt:
    - expected one dominant crest component;
    - branch attachment fraction near `1.0`;
    - foreland located on the intended side of the axis.
  - Synthetic two-belt continent:
    - two separate parent corridors must remain separate;
    - no accidental bridge across unrelated basins.
  - Polar/edge belt case:
    - corridor may wrap spherical edges correctly;
    - no artificial edge fragmentation.
  - Generated seed probes:
    - `909`, `1111`, and one higher-resolution seed at `8000` cells.
- Acceptance targets:
  - Reduce branch-spine small-area fraction below `0.50` on the known bad
    2500-cell seeds without reducing class-aware score.
  - Keep P107 full audit tests passing.
  - Rendered morphology overlay should show main crest, branch ranges,
    foreland, halo, and apron as organized belt zones rather than scattered
    post-hoc patches.

#### 2026-07-04 P146 implementation audit: guarded scaffold, not promotion

- Implemented:
  - `terrain.parent_orogen_corridors` object emission with
    `trunk_cells`, `branch_cells`, and `foreland_cells`.
  - Candidate, retained, pruned, and rejection telemetry:
    - `terrain.last_p146_candidate_corridor_component_count`;
    - `terrain.last_p146_candidate_corridor_cell_count`;
    - `terrain.last_p146_retained_corridor_component_count`;
    - `terrain.last_p146_retained_corridor_cell_count`;
    - `terrain.last_p146_pruned_corridor_component_count`;
    - `terrain.last_p146_trunk_parent_overlap_fraction`;
    - `terrain.last_p146_reject_code`.
  - Dominant-component pruning for overfragmented corridor candidates.
  - Foreland candidate tightening so the P146 foreland must attach to the
    trunk/branch peak front rather than floating as a detached fringe.
  - A trunk-parent overlap gate.  A generated-world P146 corridor is accepted
    only when the retained trunk overlaps enough of the parent boundary support
    to plausibly represent a coherent parent orogen.  Current default minimum:
    `0.15`.
- Tests:
  - `pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -k 'p146 or ...' -q`:
    `4 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - `pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_array_render.py -q`:
    `154 passed`.
- Failed promotion attempt:
  - Unrestricted/pruned P146 candidates were tried on generated seeds `909`
    and `1111`.
  - They produced object-like corridors, but downstream linework regressed
    badly:
    - seed `909`: P142 class-aware score fell to `0.387`, class small-area
      fraction rose to `0.635`;
    - seed `1111`: P142 class-aware score fell to `0.327`, class small-area
      fraction rose to `0.792`.
  - Cause: the current parent mask is an aggregate of many unrelated
    convergent/subduction parent inputs.  Selecting a few dominant raster
    components from that aggregate does not recover real orogenic corridor
    identity.
- Coverage-gated generated probes:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p146_parent_orogen_corridor_coveragegate_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p146_parent_orogen_corridor_coveragegate_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - seed `909`:
    - P146 accepted: `false`;
    - candidate components/cells: `8` / `177`;
    - retained components/cells after pruning: `6` / `170`;
    - trunk-parent overlap fraction: `0.041`;
    - reject code: `6`;
    - P145 accepted: `true`, class score `0.633 -> 0.754`,
      small-area fraction `0.667 -> 0.447`;
    - P142 class-aware score `0.769`, small-area fraction `0.421`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P146 accepted: `false`;
    - candidate components/cells: `5` / `177`;
    - retained components/cells after pruning: `5` / `177`;
    - trunk-parent overlap fraction: `0.044`;
    - reject code: `6`;
    - P145 accepted: `true`, class score `0.450 -> 0.868`,
      small-area fraction `1.000 -> 0.239`;
    - P142 class-aware score `0.884`, small-area fraction `0.212`.
- Decision:
  - P146 is now a safe scaffold and diagnostic layer, not a promoted
    generated-world modifier.
  - The gate correctly blocks low-coverage aggregate corridors and preserves
    the P145/P142 baseline.
  - The acceptance target is not met by P146 alone; the next step must change
    the upstream abstraction, not tune the pruning threshold.

#### P147 planned: boundary-object corridor grouping before raster hierarchy

- Problem:
  - Current P146 builds from a global parent mask.  That mask mixes separate
    arcs, sutures, collision belts, and subduction parents before corridor
    identity is known.
  - The low trunk-parent overlap fractions on generated seeds prove that the
    candidate corridors are only small samples of a much larger unrelated
    parent aggregate.
- Design direction:
  - Group parent inputs by existing `tectonics.boundary_objects` and
    `tectonics.boundary_polylines` before rasterization.
  - Build one corridor candidate per boundary object/group, not one candidate
    from the global parent mask.
  - Merge only adjacent/same-process groups that share plate-pair lineage,
    compatible boundary kind, and continuous spherical geometry.
  - Apply the P146 cross-section builder per group, then admit only corridors
    whose local trunk-parent overlap passes the coverage gate.
- Microbenchmarks:
  - Two unrelated collision belts on the same continent must produce two
    separate corridors.
  - A long arc split by transform offsets must remain one corridor with
    controlled gaps, not many bead-like fragments.
  - A global generated seed must show higher per-corridor parent coverage than
    the current aggregate P146 attempts.
- Promotion target:
  - Improve seed `909` beyond the P145/P142 baseline without reducing seed
    `1111`.
  - Keep branch-spine small-area fraction below `0.50` and avoid class-aware
    score regression.

#### 2026-07-04 P147 implementation audit: grouped scaffold, default off

- Implemented:
  - Terrain-side P147 group collector:
    - prefers `tectonics.boundary_polylines` for
      `convergent_parent_polyline` / `subduction_parent_polyline`;
    - falls back to `tectonics.boundary_objects` when ordered parent polylines
      are unavailable;
    - carries source group id, parent boundary kind, and parent plate ids into
      emitted corridor objects.
  - Per-group corridor construction:
    - calls the P146 trunk/branch/foreland builder with the local parent line;
    - prunes each group independently;
    - evaluates local trunk-parent overlap before admitting a group.
  - P147 telemetry in P107:
    - group counts by source type;
    - attempted/accepted/rejected/low-coverage group counts;
    - candidate and retained corridor component/cell counts;
    - trunk-parent overlap mean/min;
    - side-band to trunk ratio;
    - aggregate guard rejection flag;
    - P147 corridor object/cell counts.
  - Microbenchmark coverage:
    - single curved parent belt still initializes corridor zones when P147 is
      explicitly enabled;
    - two unrelated parent belts remain separate corridor objects with separate
      `source_parent_group_id` values.
- Tests:
  - Focused P146/P147/P11168 regression:
    `5 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full P107 + array render tests:
    `155 passed`.
- Explicit P147-on generated probes:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p147_boundary_group_corridor_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p147_boundary_group_corridor_2500_seed1111_20260704`;
    - guarded retry outputs:
      `out_p147_boundary_group_corridor_guarded_2500_seed909_20260704`,
      `out_p147_boundary_group_corridor_guarded_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - Initial P147-on behavior:
    - seed `909` improved strongly in one run:
      P142 class-aware score `0.865`, small-area fraction `0.245`;
    - seed `1111` regressed strongly:
      P142 class-aware score `0.386`, small-area fraction `0.657`.
  - After stricter per-group coverage and side/trunk aggregate guards:
    - seed `909` still regressed:
      P142 class-aware score `0.431`, small-area fraction `0.593`;
    - seed `1111` improved over the first P147-on attempt but remained below
      the P145/P142 baseline:
      P142 class-aware score `0.705`, small-area fraction `0.467`.
- Decision:
  - P147 grouping is implemented and useful as a diagnostic/microbenchmark
    scaffold, but it is not promoted by default.
  - Default `terrain.enable_p147_boundary_group_parent_orogen_corridors` is
    `0.0`.
  - Default generated-world safety probes with P147 off:
    - `out_p147_default_off_safety_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p147_default_off_safety_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - Default path results:
    - seed `909`: P147 accepted `false`, P146 rejected by coverage
      (`reject_code=6`), P142 class-aware score `0.769`, small-area fraction
      `0.421`;
    - seed `1111`: P147 accepted `false`, P146 rejected by coverage
      (`reject_code=6`), P142 class-aware score `0.884`, small-area fraction
      `0.212`.
- Next required work:
  - P148 should move beyond "one polyline object equals one corridor" and add
    a promotion/evaluation layer before terrain mutation:
    - classify parent groups by local boundary process and continental side;
    - identify trunk-dominant groups before side-band rasterization;
    - reject or defer groups that would suppress P145 consolidation;
    - ideally evaluate a trial hierarchy score before committing P147 masks.

#### 2026-07-04 P148 implementation audit: P147 trial promotion guard

- Implemented:
  - `terrain.enable_p148_p147_trial_promotion_guard`, default `1.0`.
  - The guard only matters when P147 is explicitly enabled; default generated
    worlds still keep P147 off.
  - Before P147 masks are committed, terrain now builds:
    - a baseline initial crest/branch hierarchy without P147 corridors;
    - a trial initial crest/branch hierarchy with P147 trunk/branch corridors.
  - The trial must pass:
    - class-score safety against baseline;
    - class-small-area non-regression;
    - strict trial small-area target, default `0.30`;
    - crest+branch component-count safety.
  - If the guard rejects, P147 telemetry remains visible but P147 masks are not
    injected into the terrain hierarchy.  The run falls back to the stable
    P145/P142 path.
- Telemetry added to P107:
  - `p148_p147_trial_guard_enabled`;
  - `p148_p147_trial_guard_accepted`;
  - `p148_p147_trial_guard_rejected`;
  - `p148_p147_trial_reject_code`;
  - baseline/trial class scores;
  - baseline/trial class-small fractions;
  - baseline/trial crest and branch component counts;
  - trial added corridor cell count.
- Tests:
  - Focused P146/P147/P148/P11168 regression:
    `6 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full P107 + array render tests:
    `156 passed`.
- P147-on + P148-guard generated probes:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p148_p147_trial_guard_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p148_p147_trial_guard_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - seed `909`:
    - P147 groups attempted `12`, locally accepted `4`;
    - P148 rejected, reject code `1` (class score unsafe);
    - baseline/trial class score `0.244 -> 0.227`;
    - baseline/trial class-small fraction `1.000 -> 0.375`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.769`, small-area fraction `0.421`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P147 groups attempted `12`, locally accepted `3`;
    - P148 rejected, reject code `3` (trial small-area target not met);
    - baseline/trial class score `0.311 -> 0.438`;
    - baseline/trial class-small fraction `1.000 -> 0.624`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.884`, small-area fraction `0.212`.
- Decision:
  - P148 is promoted as a safety gate for any explicit P147 experiments.
  - P147 remains default-off, but explicit P147 runs no longer silently degrade
    the known bad seeds because P148 rejects unsafe candidates before terrain
    mutation.
- Next required work:
  - P149 should improve candidate quality rather than relaxing the guard:
    - classify parent groups by collision/subduction/suture context;
    - build trunk-only trial first, then add side bands only after trunk passes;
    - evaluate foreland/branch bands separately from the main crest corridor;
    - add generated-seed acceptance targets that require at least one seed to
      improve without reducing the other known bad seed.

### 2026-07-04 P149 staged parent corridor promotion audit

- Implementation:
  - Added `terrain.enable_p149_p147_staged_promotion` as a guarded scaffold
    behind explicit P147 experiments.
  - When P148 rejects a full P147 trial, P149 now tests a trunk-only candidate
    first:
    - trunk candidate must not reduce class score beyond tolerance;
    - trunk candidate must not increase class-small fraction versus baseline;
    - trunk candidate must stay below
      `terrain.p149_trunk_trial_max_class_small_area_fraction`;
    - trunk candidate must not inflate crest/branch component count.
  - Only after trunk acceptance does P149 attempt side bands:
    - branch/foreland side bands must improve or preserve the trunk-only class
      score;
    - side-band class-small fraction must not increase and must stay below
      `terrain.p149_side_trial_max_class_small_area_fraction`.
  - If a staged candidate is accepted, committed corridor masks and
    `terrain.parent_orogen_corridors` objects are rebuilt from only the accepted
    staged masks using schema
    `aevum.p149_staged_parent_orogen_corridor.v1`.
- Telemetry added to P107:
  - staged/trunk/side enable, acceptance, rejection flags;
  - P149 reject code;
  - trunk-only and side-band class scores and class-small fractions;
  - committed trunk/branch/foreland cell counts.
- Tests:
  - Focused P146/P147/P148/P149/P11168 regression:
    `7 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full P107 + array render tests:
    `157 passed`.
- P147-on generated probes:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p149_staged_promotion_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p149_staged_promotion_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - seed `909`:
    - P147 groups attempted `12`, locally accepted `6`;
    - P148 rejected, reject code `1` (full trial class score unsafe);
    - baseline/full-trial class score `0.239 -> 0.165`;
    - baseline/full-trial class-small fraction `1.000 -> 0.556`;
    - P149 trunk-only rejected, reject code `1`;
    - trunk-only score `0.206`, trunk-small fraction `1.000`;
    - committed trunk/branch/foreland cells `0/0/0`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.754`, small-area fraction `0.448`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P147 groups attempted `12`, locally accepted `3`;
    - P148 rejected, reject code `3` (full trial small-area target unsafe);
    - baseline/full-trial class score `0.268 -> 0.369`;
    - baseline/full-trial class-small fraction `1.000 -> 0.639`;
    - P149 trunk-only rejected, reject code `2`;
    - trunk-only score `0.267`, trunk-small fraction `1.000`;
    - committed trunk/branch/foreland cells `0/0/0`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.888`, small-area fraction `0.204`.
- Decision:
  - P149 is safe as a default-on scaffold because P147 is still default-off and
    P149 does not relax P148's safety criteria.
  - P149 is not yet a production quality improvement for generated worlds:
    trunk-only candidates from P147 remain too fragmented in the known probes,
    so staged promotion cannot rescue them.
  - Keep P147 default-off and keep P148/P149 as explicit-experiment guards.
- Next required work:
  - P150 should move earlier in the pipeline and repair P147 candidate quality:
    - score each boundary group before accumulation;
    - reject groups whose trunk-only profile has class-small fraction `1.0`;
    - connect or merge neighboring accepted parent groups before trial scoring;
    - build trunk candidates from parent-line geometry and support fields, not
      from raw dilated group fragments alone;
    - require generated-seed proof that at least one known bad seed commits a
      nonzero trunk while preserving the other seed's final linework metrics.

### 2026-07-04 P150 group trunk quality repair audit

- Implementation:
  - Added P150 group-local trunk quality repair behind explicit P147
    experiments:
    - `terrain.enable_p150_group_trunk_quality_repair` defaults on only within
      the already explicit P147 path;
    - fragmented group trunks are profiled before accumulation;
    - trunks with class-small fraction `1.0` are either repaired by parent-line
      support-band bridging or rejected before P148/P149 trial scoring;
    - P107 now records P150 trial count, bridge acceptance, small-fragment
      rejection count, bridge cells/paths, and group trunk score/small metrics.
  - Added aggregate repair telemetry:
    - accepted P147 group trunks are profiled after accumulation;
    - aggregate trunk bridge acceptance and before/after score/small metrics are
      recorded.
  - Added experimental component-preserving cap:
    - `terrain.enable_p150_component_preserving_cap`;
    - default is **off** because generated probes found a serious regression;
    - the helper is retained for later guarded P151 work.
- Tests:
  - Focused P146/P147/P148/P149/P150/P11168 regression:
    `8 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full P107 + array render tests:
    `158 passed`.
- Default P150 generated probes, component-preserving cap off:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p150_default_capoff_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p150_default_capoff_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - seed `909`:
    - P150 enabled, component cap disabled;
    - group trials `12`, bridge accepted `0`, small-fragment rejected `1`;
    - aggregate trunk-small before/after `0.058 -> 0.058`;
    - P148 still rejected, code `1`;
    - P149 trunk still rejected, code `1`, trunk-small `1.000`;
    - committed trunk/branch/foreland cells `0/0/0`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.754`, small-area fraction `0.448`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P150 enabled, component cap disabled;
    - group trials `12`, bridge accepted `0`, small-fragment rejected `0`;
    - aggregate trunk-small before/after `0.401 -> 0.401`;
    - P148 still rejected, code `3`;
    - P149 trunk still rejected, code `2`, trunk-small `1.000`;
    - committed trunk/branch/foreland cells `0/0/0`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.888`, small-area fraction `0.204`.
- Experimental component-preserving cap probe:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p150_component_cap_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p150_component_cap_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - seed `909` improved:
    - P148 accepted;
    - P147/P149 committed trunk/branch/foreland cells `124/93/29`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.785`, small-area fraction `0.288`.
  - seed `1111` regressed badly:
    - P148 still rejected;
    - P149 still committed `0` cells;
    - final P142 class-aware score fell to `0.450`;
    - final P142 class-small fraction rose to `1.000`.
- Decision:
  - P150 group-local repair is safe as telemetry and guard scaffolding, but it
    does not yet improve the known generated seeds.
  - The real bottleneck is now sharper:
    - P147 aggregate trunks are not always bad;
    - the existing budget cap can turn a usable corridor trunk into scattered
      high-score cells;
    - replacing the cap naively can improve one seed while severely degrading
      another.
  - Keep `terrain.enable_p150_component_preserving_cap` default-off.
  - Keep P147 default-off.
- Next required work:
  - P151 should implement a guarded object-aware cap with terminal rollback:
    - compare old cap, component-preserving cap, and corridor-skeleton cap as
      separate candidates;
    - run candidate selection against both P148 local trial metrics and final
      P142/P145 linework metrics;
    - reject candidates that improve P147 admission but degrade terminal
      branch/crest class-small fractions;
    - require generated proof that seed `909` can keep the P147 trunk commit
      improvement while seed `1111` does not regress.

### 2026-07-04 P151 guarded object-aware cap audit

- Implementation:
  - Added `terrain.enable_p151_guarded_object_aware_cap`, default-on inside
    explicit P147 experiments.
  - P151 evaluates the old score-ranked cap and the component-preserving cap as
    separate trial candidates:
    - the component candidate must satisfy the existing P148 score, small-area,
      target-small, and component-count guards;
    - it must also improve class-small fraction versus the old cap without
      reducing class score beyond tolerance;
    - otherwise P151 keeps the old cap and records a reject code.
  - P151 then applies a final proxy guard before mutating the real crest/branch
    masks:
    - final component cap must preserve or improve final cap-stage class score;
    - final class-small fraction must not worsen and must stay under
      `terrain.p151_final_max_class_small_area_fraction`;
    - otherwise the final cap is rolled back to the old cap.
  - `terrain.enable_p150_component_preserving_cap` remains a force-on
    experiment switch and still defaults off.
- Telemetry added to P107:
  - P151 enabled flag;
  - trial component evaluated/accepted/rejected/selected flags;
  - trial old/component class score and class-small fraction;
  - trial reject code;
  - final component evaluated/accepted/rejected flags;
  - final old/component class score and class-small fraction;
  - final reject code.
- Tests:
  - Focused P146/P147/P148/P149/P150/P151/P11168 regression:
    `8 passed, 149 deselected`.
  - Full P107 + array render tests:
    `158 passed`.
- Generated probes:
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p151_guarded_cap_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - `out_p151_guarded_cap_2500_seed1111_20260704`.
  - seed `909`:
    - P151 trial evaluated and accepted the component cap;
    - trial old/component score `0.188 -> 0.276`;
    - trial old/component class-small fraction `0.210 -> 0.170`;
    - P151 final proxy accepted the component cap;
    - final old/component score `0.246 -> 0.417`;
    - final old/component class-small fraction `0.286 -> 0.286`;
    - P148 accepted;
    - P147/P149 committed trunk/branch/foreland cells `119/87/25`;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.828`, class-small fraction `0.313`.
  - seed `1111`:
    - P151 trial evaluated but rejected the component cap;
    - reject code `3` (target-small unsafe);
    - trial old/component score `0.369 -> 0.405`;
    - trial old/component class-small fraction `0.639 -> 0.639`;
    - P148 remained rejected, code `3`;
    - P147/P149 committed `0` cells;
    - final P142 class-aware score `0.888`, class-small fraction `0.204`.
- Decision:
  - P151 fixes the P150 regression:
    - it keeps the useful seed `909` corridor commit path;
    - it blocks the seed `1111` component-cap regression.
  - Keep P151 default-on for explicit P147 experiments.
  - Keep P147 default-off until broader multi-seed and image review confirms
    corridor commits do not overgrow land or create artificial mountain carpets.
- Next required work:
  - P152 should broaden validation from two known seeds to a small generated
    ladder:
    - at least seeds `909`, `1111`, `2024`, `24000`, and one random held-out
      seed at `2500` cells;
    - compare land fraction, P142/P145 linework, P147 commit cells, and
      generated elevation/morphology images;
    - promote P147 only if added parent corridors improve continuous mountain
      systems without increasing terminal small-area fractions or land overgrowth.

    - P147 accepted groups `1/12`;

    - P149 committed `0/0/0`;
    - P142 score/small `0.450/1.000`;

  - seed `424242`:
    - land `0.283`;
    - P147 accepted groups `3/12`;

      `max_fraction_width_gt2=0.119`.

  - P151 protects the useful seed `909` corridor path and blocks the known
    seed `1111` component-cap regression.

    - several seeds have weak or rejected P148/P149 admission;
    - high-mountain fragmentation can remain high even when semantic spine
      linework is numerically clean;
    - seed `909` still shows a locally wide boundary/mountain belt;
    - seed `2024` and `424242` show smoother planforms but still rely on
      coarse 2500-cell expression.
- Decision:
  - Keep P151 default-on inside explicit P147 experiments.
  - Keep P147 default-off.
  - Treat final high-elevation coherence as the next bottleneck:
    - the terrain surface can still contain fragmented `3000m+` flecks after
      the semantic orogenic hierarchy has been cleaned;
    - P142/P145 alone are no longer strong enough promotion gates because they
      evaluate semantic masks more than final high-relief expression.

### 2026-07-04 P153 terminal high-fleck cleanup experiment

- Implementation:
  - Added `terrain.enable_p153_terminal_high_fleck_cleanup`.
  - The pass is inside terminal P111 orogen-belt repair and only evaluates when
    a spine-guided hierarchy exists.
  - It identifies small `3000m+` continental high components that are:
    - far from `orogenic_hierarchy_spine`;
    - weakly connected to the spine neighborhood;
    - non-elongated;
    - below the extreme-peak preservation cutoff.
  - Selected flecks are softened below `3000m` while preserving the land mask.
  - Guardrails require:
    - no land/ocean change;
    - no high-mountain fragmentation worsening;
    - no excessive reduction of total high-mountain area.
- Telemetry:
  - Added P153 fields under `high_mountain_coherence`:
    - accepted flag;
    - candidate/softened cell and area counts;
    - high-component count before/after;
    - top-three high-component share before/after;
    - fragmentation index before/after;
    - guard-reverted flag.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p153_terminal_high_fleck_cleanup_softens_offaxis_spine_noise`.
  - Focused regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p153 or p1113 or p111634 or high_mountain_coherence or terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays'`
    returned `7 passed, 151 deselected`.
- Generated A/B:
  - P153-on:
    - outputs:
      `out_p153_high_fleck_2500_seed1111_20260704`,
      `out_p153_high_fleck_2500_seed2024_20260704`,
      `out_p153_high_fleck_2500_seed24000_20260704`,
      `out_p153_high_fleck_2500_seed424242_20260704`,
      `out_p153_high_fleck_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - seed `1111` high-mountain fragmentation improved
      `0.680 -> 0.145`;
    - seed `24000` high-mountain fragmentation improved
      `0.283 -> 0.074`;
    - seed `909` final audit fragmentation worsened despite local P153
      candidate improvement, showing the pass is not yet safe as a default.
  - P153-off seed `1111` control:
    - output `out_p153_off_ab_2500_seed1111_20260704`;
    - returned to the P152 baseline:
      high-mountain fragmentation `0.680`, P142 score/small `0.888/0.204`,
      P149 trunk commit `0`.
- Decision:
  - Keep P153 implemented as a microbenchmark and experiment hook.
  - Keep `terrain.enable_p153_terminal_high_fleck_cleanup` default-off.
  - Do not use P153 as a promotion gate for P147 yet.
- Next required work:
  - P154 should first repair the default earthlike planform regression exposed
    by `tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression`:
    - the final `terrain.continental_detail == 0` on land issue has a terminal
      fallback, but the same test now reaches the next failure:
      `largest_component_area_fraction_of_mask=0.974`, above the `0.75`
      threshold;
    - this means the modern default path can still produce an almost single
      connected exposed landmass even when local morphology looks improved;
    - use the final exposed-land component metric as the next promotion gate
      before more high-relief tuning.
  - P155 should then attack the final high-relief coherence problem with a
    safer design:
    - separate semantic-linework promotion metrics from final elevation metrics;
    - compute an explicit terminal high-relief A/B score before mutating the
      surface;
    - prefer lowering unsupported off-axis flecks only when it cannot change
      P147/P149 admission telemetry;
    - add a generated A/B gate for seeds `1111`, `909`, and `24000` before any
      default-on promotion.

### 2026-07-04 terminal land-detail consistency repair

- Finding:
  - Full-suite exploration exposed that the default `earthlike` reasonableness
    test failed before it could reach planform checks because final land cells
    could still carry `terrain.continental_detail == 0`.
  - Root cause:
    - late seaway, payback, and ocean/land polishing can change the final land
      mask after the earlier continental-detail classification;
    - the final exported `terrain.continental_detail` array was not repaired
      against the final `surface_new >= sea_level` mask.
- Implementation:
  - Added a terminal fallback immediately before final terrain fields are
    exported:
    - final land with detail `0` gets a valid land detail;
    - continental-crust land defaults to `CONT_DETAIL_PLATFORM`;
    - oceanic-crust exposed land defaults to `CONT_DETAIL_ARC_MICROCONTINENT`;
    - matching zero `terrain.continental_detail_region_code` values are repaired
      consistently.
  - Added telemetry:
    - `terrain.last_final_land_detail_repair_cell_count`.
- Validation:
  - `tests/test_p107_audit.py`: `158 passed`.
  - `tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression` now
    passes the land-detail assertion and exposes the next planform failure:
    largest exposed-land component share `0.974`, threshold `<0.75`.
- Decision:
  - Keep the terminal detail fallback; it is a consistency repair for exported
    final fields.
  - Do not treat the default earthlike path as release-ready until P154 fixes
    the largest-landmass/closed-supercontinent regression.

### 2026-07-04 P154 final planform gate

- Finding:
  - P110A/P111 modern seaway passes could split the default `earthlike`
    terminal landmass earlier in the terrain pipeline, but later terminal
    ocean/bathymetry/land polish could re-expose or refill enough weak
    corridors that the exported surface returned to an almost monolithic
    landmass.
  - Reproduced on default `earthlike`, `2500` cells, `4500 Myr`:
    - final exposed-land largest component before the P154 fix:
      `0.974`;
    - component count `11`;
    - land fraction `0.295`;
    - `tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression`
      failed the `<0.75` largest-component gate.
- Implementation:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p154_final_planform_gate(...)`.
  - The gate runs only in the terminal modern earthlike window and is
    default-on through `terrain.enable_p154_final_planform_gate`.
  - It re-applies the existing process-backed
    `_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish(...)` after terminal ocean polish, on
    the actual export surface.
  - Acceptance guardrails require:
    - largest exposed-land component falls below the P154 ceiling;
    - largest component improves by at least `0.08`;
    - final land fraction remains inside an Earth-like envelope;
    - component count remains bounded;
    - at least two major land components remain;
    - opened land area stays below the destructive-cut ceiling.
  - If the gate rejects a candidate, it restores
    `terrain.p1114_modern_endpoint_seaways` so object provenance stays
    consistent with the final surface.
  - After an accepted gate, terminal ocean semantic context is rebuilt so
    bathymetry objects, margin fields, and final trench provinces match the
    changed surface.
- Telemetry:
  - Added P154 fields under P107
    `p110a_modern_planform.seaway_cut_effectiveness`:
    - attempted/applied/reverted flags;
    - largest share before/after;
    - component and major-component count before/after;
    - land fraction before/after;
    - opened area fraction;
    - reject code.
- Validation:
  - `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics`
    and
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110a_final_modern_seaway_polish_default_allows_six_passes`:
    `2 passed`.
  - `tests/test_p107_audit.py`: `158 passed`.
  - `tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression`:
    `1 passed in 135.22s`.
  - Default `earthlike`, `2500` cells, `4500 Myr` diagnostic after P154:
    - land fraction `0.270`;
    - largest exposed-land component `0.622`;
    - component count `16`;
    - narrow necks per 1000 mask cells `23.70`;
    - P154 opened area fraction `0.036`;
    - P154 applied and did not revert.
- Decision:
  - Keep P154 default-on as a terminal consistency gate.
  - Treat it as a hard-regression repair, not final planform quality closure.
  - Residual warnings remain:
    - largest component is still in the soft-warning band;
    - major land component count can remain low for the selected archetype;
    - supercontinent-like diagnostics can still warn even when hard gates pass.
- Next required work:
  - P155 should return to final high-relief coherence only after the planform
    hard gate is stable.
  - P156 should add a separate modern-planform quality pass or microbenchmark
    for soft warnings:
    - target largest exposed-land share closer to the preferred `0.54-0.60`
      band when process corridors allow it;
    - improve major-component balance without cutting arbitrary canals;
    - preserve the new terminal object/context rebuild discipline.

### 2026-07-04 P155 terminal high-relief consistency gate

- Finding:
  - P153 proved that local off-axis high-fleck cleanup can improve selected
    seeds, but it is not safe as a default because local candidate success can
    still worsen final audit metrics on some worlds.
  - The root design problem is that semantic orogenic linework and final
    `3000m+` high-relief expression were evaluated by different gates:
    - P142/P145/P147/P149 mostly judge hierarchy/spine linework;
    - final visible terrain quality depends on the exported `surface_new`;
    - late planform repair can change the final surface after earlier high
      relief repair has already run.
- Implementation:
  - Added `TerrainModule._p155_terminal_high_relief_consistency_gate(...)`.
  - The gate runs after P154 and before P128 axis polyline export, so it uses
    the final modern planform and does not affect P147/P149 admission.
  - It computes a before/after profile for:
    - high-mountain component count;
    - spine `3000m+` component count and coverage;
    - top-three high-mountain component share;
    - high-mountain fragmentation index;
    - parent/spine overlap;
    - high-relief area fraction;
    - land p90/p98 hypsometry.
  - Candidate actions are conservative:
    - raise only spine cells that are true high-saddle candidates between
      existing `3000m+` high cells;
    - lower only small, non-elongated, off-spine continental high flecks below
      the `3000m` threshold;
    - preserve the land/ocean mask.
  - Acceptance requires global A/B improvement and rejects candidates that:
    - increase high-mountain component count;
    - increase spine `3000m+` component count;
    - reduce parent overlap or top-three share beyond tolerance;
    - violate land p90/p98 or high-relief area guardrails.
  - On guard rejection, after telemetry is restored to the before/final values
    so audit reports remain consistent with the exported surface.
- Telemetry:
  - Added P155 fields under P107 `high_mountain_coherence`:
    - accepted/reverted/land-mask flags;
    - candidate, raised, and softened cell/area counts;
    - before/after high component count;
    - before/after spine `3000m+` component count and coverage;
    - before/after fragmentation, parent overlap, high area, p90/p98;
    - reject code.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p155_terminal_high_relief_gate_accepts_global_ab_improvement`.
  - Extended
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics`
    to assert P155 audit fields.
- Validation:
  - Focused:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p155_terminal_high_relief_gate_accepts_global_ab_improvement tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics -q`
    returned `2 passed`.
  - Full P107 audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    returned `159 passed`.
  - Default engine regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    returned `1 passed in 134.83s`.
  - Default `earthlike`, `2500` cells, `4500 Myr` diagnostic after the stricter
    guard:
    - land fraction `0.270`;
    - largest land component `0.622`;
    - P155 candidate cells `2`;
    - P155 rejected and reverted, reject code `6`;
    - high component count remained `4 -> 4`;
    - spine `3000m+` component count remained `1 -> 1`;
    - high fragmentation remained `0.750 -> 0.750`;
    - exported surface stayed consistent with telemetry.
- Decision:
  - Keep P155 default-on because the gate is non-destructive and rejects unsafe
    candidates.
  - Treat P155 as the new safety harness for terminal high-relief edits, not as
    final high-mountain quality closure.
  - P153 remains default-off; future cleanup should be expressed through P155
    or a stricter successor gate.
- Next required work:
  - P156 should improve candidate generation under the P155 guard:
    - find multi-cell high-saddle paths rather than single adjacent cells;
    - favor paths that reduce both high component count and spine component
      count;
    - add seed-level microbenchmarks for `1111`, `909`, and `24000` before
      accepting default surface changes.
  - P157 should add an image/audit review focused on final `3000m+` belts,
    separating true ridge segmentation from acceptable plateau/highland
    patchiness.

### 2026-07-04 P156 multi-cell high-saddle path candidates

- Finding:
  - P155 can safely reject harmful candidates, but its first candidate
    generator was mostly cell-local:
    - it could raise one-cell saddles adjacent to existing `3000m+` high cells;
    - it could not connect two high spine segments separated by a short
      multi-cell `2400-3000m` saddle;
    - the default `earthlike` diagnostic showed the guard rejecting a candidate
      that improved broad fragmentation but increased spine `3000m+` component
      count, which is the wrong tradeoff for continuous mountain belts.
- Implementation:
  - Added a P156 path-candidate stage inside
    `_p155_terminal_high_relief_consistency_gate(...)`.
  - It operates on the spherical grid:
    - finds existing spine `3000m+` components;
    - chooses representative high cells per component;
    - searches deterministic ranked paths through eligible branch/crest spine
      saddle cells;
    - selects whole multi-cell paths instead of isolated pixels.
  - Path cells are eligible only when they are already semantic spine and near
    high-relief expression:
    - crest spine saddle threshold: `>=2400m`;
    - branch spine saddle threshold: `>=2300m`;
    - path length bounded to avoid arbitrary long ridge painting.
  - P155 still owns the final A/B acceptance:
    - accepted candidates may not increase total high-mountain component count;
    - accepted candidates may not increase spine `3000m+` component count;
    - land/ocean and hypsometry guardrails remain unchanged.
- Telemetry:
  - Added P156 fields under P107 `high_mountain_coherence`:
    - `p156_path_candidate_count`;
    - `p156_path_candidate_cell_count`;
    - `p156_selected_path_count`;
    - `p156_selected_path_cell_count`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p156_high_saddle_path_connects_multi_cell_spine_gap`.
  - Extended the P107 terminal audit smoke test to assert P156 telemetry.
- Validation:
  - Focused:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p156_high_saddle_path_connects_multi_cell_spine_gap tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p155_terminal_high_relief_gate_accepts_global_ab_improvement tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics -q`
    returned `3 passed`.
  - Full P107 audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    returned `160 passed`.
  - Default engine regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    returned `1 passed in 144.89s`.
  - Default `earthlike`, `2500` cells, `4500 Myr` diagnostic:
    - land fraction `0.270`;
    - largest land component `0.622`;
    - P155 remained rejected/reverted, reject code `6`;
    - high component count remained `4 -> 4`;
    - spine `3000m+` component count remained `1 -> 1`;
    - P156 path candidates `0`, selected paths `0`.
- Decision:
  - Keep P156 enabled inside the P155 gate.
  - The path logic is correct for the multi-cell saddle fixture, but it does
    not yet improve the default generated world because that world has only
    one spine `3000m+` component. There are no two high spine endpoints for
    P156 to connect.
  - Do not loosen P155 acceptance to make the default candidate pass; that
    would reintroduce the fragmented-spine failure P155 was designed to block.
- Next required work:
  - P157 should improve the upstream terminal high-relief seed/coverage stage:
    - create enough process-backed `3000m+` high cells along existing
      crest/branch spine for path candidates to have real endpoints;
    - keep the high-area and p90/p98 guardrails from P155;
    - validate with seeds `1111`, `909`, and `24000` before allowing default
      surface changes.
  - P158 should add a small image/audit sheet focused on final `3000m+`
    mountain belts after P157, because the numeric gate alone cannot judge
    whether a ridge visually reads as a coherent mountain system.

### 2026-07-04 P157 process-backed spine seed/coverage paths

- Finding:
  - P156 successfully connects two existing spine `3000m+` high segments, but
    the default `earthlike` world often has only one spine `3000m+` component.
  - With only one high segment, there are no P156 endpoints to connect; the
    bottleneck is upstream high-relief coverage along process-backed spine,
    not pathfinding.
- Implementation:
  - Added a P157 seed-path stage inside
    `_p155_terminal_high_relief_consistency_gate(...)`.
  - P157 finds one existing spine `3000m+` source component and searches for a
    short path to nearby high-potential semantic spine cells:
    - branch spine seed threshold: `>=2450m`;
    - crest spine seed threshold: `>=2550m`;
    - path saddle threshold: branch `>=2280m`, crest `>=2380m`;
    - path search uses the same spherical ranked shortest-path machinery as
      P156.
  - Target representative selection now prefers the distal/high-potential end
    of a saddle component rather than the cell immediately adjacent to an
    existing high segment.
  - If a P156/P157 path is selected, P155 does not append unrelated local
    one-cell raise candidates. This keeps the submitted candidate as a coherent
    path instead of mixing path repair with scattered local edits.
  - P155 acceptance now treats a significant spine `3000m+` coverage increase
    as a valid improvement only when:
    - high component count does not increase;
    - spine `3000m+` component count does not increase;
    - parent overlap, top-three share, high-area, p90/p98, and land mask guards
      still pass.
- Telemetry:
  - Added P157 fields under P107 `high_mountain_coherence`:
    - `p157_seed_path_candidate_count`;
    - `p157_seed_path_candidate_cell_count`;
    - `p157_selected_seed_path_count`;
    - `p157_selected_seed_path_cell_count`.
- Tests:
  - Added
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p157_seed_path_extends_process_backed_spine_high_coverage`.
  - Extended the P107 terminal audit smoke test to assert P157 telemetry.
- Validation:
  - Focused:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p157_seed_path_extends_process_backed_spine_high_coverage tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p156_high_saddle_path_connects_multi_cell_spine_gap tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p155_terminal_high_relief_gate_accepts_global_ab_improvement tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p107_terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays_and_required_metrics -q`
    returned `4 passed`.
  - Full P107 audit:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    returned `161 passed`.
  - Default engine regression:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_earthlike_map_reasonableness_regression -q`
    returned `1 passed in 147.30s`.
  - Default `earthlike`, `2500` cells, `4500 Myr` diagnostic:
    - land fraction `0.270`;
    - largest land component `0.622`;
    - P155/P157 accepted;
    - P157 seed path candidates `2`, selected paths `1`, selected cells `3`;
    - raised cells `3`, softened cells `1`;
    - high component count `4 -> 3`;
    - spine `3000m+` component count `1 -> 1`;
    - spine `3000m+` coverage `0.013 -> 0.052`;
    - high fragmentation `0.750 -> 0.500`;
    - high area fraction `0.0016 -> 0.0024`;
    - P154 planform remained stable.
- Decision:
  - Keep P157 enabled through the P155 gate.
  - Treat this as the first default-path terminal high-relief improvement that
    passes the stricter component-count and hypsometry guards.
  - Do not tune further from the single default seed alone.
- Next required work:
  - P158 should run a multi-seed high-relief microbenchmark for seeds `1111`,
    `909`, `24000`, plus the default seed:
    - collect P155/P156/P157 telemetry;
    - check high component count, spine coverage, fragmentation, p90/p98, and
      land fraction;
    - generate a focused image sheet for final `3000m+` mountain belts.
  - P159 should decide whether P157 needs seed-specific guard tightening or can
    remain as-is before broader planform/terrain visual promotion.

### 2026-07-04 P158 process-level multi-seed high-relief audit

- Scope:
  - Ran a process-level multi-seed P107 audit at `2500` cells / `36` plates /
    `4500 Myr`, with one BLAS/OMP thread per process.
  - Seeds checked:
    - required carry-over seeds `42`, `909`, `1111`, `24000`;
    - extra comparison seed `707`.
  - Outputs:
    - `out_p158_high_relief_multiseed_2500_20260704/p158_high_relief_multiseed_summary.json`;
    - `out_p158_high_relief_multiseed_2500_20260704/p158_high_relief_multiseed_summary.md`;
    - `out_p158_high_relief_multiseed_2500_20260704/p158_multiseed_high_relief_contact_sheet.png`;
    - per-seed P107 terminal metrics/arrays and 23 rendered PNG assets under
      `out_p158_high_relief_multiseed_2500_20260704/seed_*`.
  - After P159, the P158 summary is sourced from full per-seed
    `p107_terminal_metrics.json`, because the pre-P159 compact
    `p107_audit_summary.json` omitted several P155 spine/high-area fields.
- Metrics:

  | seed | land | largest land | P155 accepted | high comps before->after | spine3000 comps before->after | spine3000 coverage before->after | frag before->after | P156 paths/cells | P157 paths/cells | linework | run s |
  |---:|---:|---:|:---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
  | 42 | 27.44% | 73.61% | yes | 7->5 | 3->3 | 15.76%->17.19% | 0.385->0.125 | 0/0 | 0/0 | 1.000 | 158.2 |
  | 707 | 29.49% | 64.99% | no | 8->8 | 2->2 | 29.23%->29.23% | 0.254->0.254 | 0/0 | 0/0 | 1.000 | 152.7 |
  | 909 | 27.44% | 43.58% | yes | 6->3 | 2->2 | 26.78%->29.23% | 0.153->0.000 | 0/0 | 1/2 | 1.000 | 162.3 |
  | 1111 | 28.40% | 39.30% | yes | 10->6 | 5->3 | 40.58%->50.73% | 0.583->0.289 | 2/5 | 3/7 | 1.000 | 162.8 |
  | 24000 | 27.72% | 59.16% | yes | 5->4 | 3->3 | 34.34%->43.29% | 0.327->0.147 | 0/0 | 2/6 | 0.968 | 154.2 |

- Aggregate:
  - P155 accepted `4/5`; rejected/reverted `1/5`.
  - High-mountain fragmentation did not worsen in `5/5` seeds.
  - Spine `3000m+` component count did not increase in `5/5` seeds.
  - Spine `3000m+` coverage did not regress in `5/5` seeds.
  - Spine `3000m+` coverage increased in `4/5` seeds.
  - Spine `3000m+` component count decreased in `1/5` seeds.
  - Mean process runtime was `158.0s`; max runtime was `162.9s`.
  - Mean land fraction was `28.10%`; mean high-mountain area fraction was
    `2.10%`.
- Interpretation:
  - P155/P156/P157 is safe enough to keep default-on:
    - unsafe candidates are rejected and reverted;
    - accepted candidates reduce or preserve fragmentation;
    - no seed increased the terminal `3000m+` spine component count.
  - P157 is not yet a final mountain-quality closure:
    - visual review still shows polar/edge overclassification and coarse
      raster-width mountain belts;
    - linework scores can pass while the rendered highland belts still read
      too broad or grid-like in places;
  - P158 exposed a reporting bug rather than a terrain-expression zero:
    - full `p107_terminal_metrics.json` carried nonzero spine `3000m+`
      counts/coverage;
    - compact `p107_audit_summary.json` omitted those fields, which caused
      downstream summaries to default to zero;
    - P159 repairs that audit surface.
  - Planform is not the P158 target, but the audit still records relevant
    context:
    - seed `909` and seed `1111` have healthier modern multipolar land
      layouts;
    - seeds `42` and `707` retain supercontinent-like warnings, so later
      terrain-quality promotion should not rely on them alone.
- Decision:
  - Keep P157 enabled through the P155 safety gate.
  - Do not claim high-relief/mountain closure yet.
  - Treat P158 as evidence that the current gate is non-destructive across
    seeds, while also exposing an audit-surface blind spot in compact P107
    summaries.
- Next required work:
  - P159 should repair the P107 compact summary so P155 spine/high-area guard
    fields remain visible in `p107_audit_summary.json`.
  - P160 should target the remaining visual defects from the contact sheet:
    - polar/edge orogen overclassification;
    - coarse raster-width mountain belts;
    - over-broad high-latitude colored bands that read as map-edge artifacts.
  - P161 should rerun the same process-level multi-seed audit at `8000` cells
    before any broader terrain/planform promotion.

### 2026-07-04 P159 compact P107 high-relief telemetry repair

- Finding:
  - The P158 terrain itself had nonzero spine `3000m+` metrics in full terminal
    audit files:
    - seed `42`: spine components `3 -> 3`, coverage `15.76% -> 17.19%`;
    - seed `1111`: spine components `5 -> 3`, coverage `40.58% -> 50.73%`;
    - seed `24000`: spine components `3 -> 3`, coverage `34.34% -> 43.29%`.
  - The compact `p107_audit_summary.json` omitted these P155 fields, so the
    initial P158 table defaulted missing keys to zero.
  - This was an observability bug, not proof that terminal high-relief
    expression was absent.
- Implementation:
  - Extended P107 full `high_mountain_coherence` export to include P155 p90/p98
    land-relief guard telemetry.
  - Extended compact `high_mountain_coherence` to include the missing P155
    safety and guard fields:

- Next required work:
