    - polar/edge orogen overclassification;
    - coarse raster-width mountain belts;
    - high-latitude edge bands that read as artifacts.
  - P161 should rerun the process-level seed audit at `8000` cells after P160.

### 2026-07-04 P160 polar/edge spine overclassification thinning

- Finding:
  - The earlier P111636 repair only reclassified residual polar/edge peak
    hierarchy cells that were not part of `crest_spine` / `branch_spine`.
  - The remaining high-latitude and map-edge visual artifacts therefore came
    mostly from final spine semantics: red/orange spine bands could survive
    P111636, then be carried through later P111637a/P132/P1145 spine promotion.
  - A first conservative P160 draft protected the whole parent-core dilation
    band, which made it too weak at low and medium resolutions: in a `2500`
    seed-707 smoke audit every polar spine cell was inside that dilated core,
    so no candidate was available.
- Implementation:
  - Added final-stage `terrain.enable_p160_polar_edge_spine_thinning`, placed
    after P111637a/P132/P1145 and immediately before hierarchy/spine fields are
    written.
  - P160 now targets low-relief or locally over-wide polar/map-edge spine cells
    while protecting the true parent axis and `3300m+` extreme spine cells.
  - Candidate demotion is selected with a small area budget and a deterministic
    rank, then each cell is accepted only if the original spine component
    remains connected and keeps a protected parent/extreme axis.
  - Final guards reject the whole pass if it:
    - demotes any `3300m+` extreme cell;
    - increases total spine component count;
    - increases short spine component count;
    - reduces branch attachment;
    - drops linework score below `92%` of the previous value;
    - creates spine cells outside their crest/branch hierarchy.
  - P160 only thins spine semantics; it does not alter sea/land, plate topology,
    terrain elevation, crest/branch/foreland hierarchy, or fringe halo/apron
    classes.
- Audit surface:
  - At P160 time, `p111636_polar_edge_orogen_overclassification` advanced to
    `aevum.p111636_polar_edge_orogen_overclassification.v2`.
  - Full and compact P107 summaries now include P160 candidate/demoted counts,
    polar/edge demotion split, extreme-demotion count, polar spine area
    before/after, edge spine component count before/after, total/short spine
    component counts before/after, branch attachment, linework score, and
    non-expansion/no-extreme guard booleans.
- Validation:
  - Focused high-latitude fixture:
    - P160 accepted;
    - candidate cells `43`, demoted cells `14`;
    - polar spine area `1.103% -> 0.823%` of world area;
    - spine components `1 -> 1`;
    - short components `0 -> 0`;
    - linework score `1.0 -> 1.0`;
    - extreme demotion `0`.
  - Actual `2500` seed-707 fast-preview audit after the refined guard:
    - output:
      `out_p160_polar_edge_spine_thinning_2500_seed707_v2_20260704`;
    - P160 accepted;
    - candidate cells `8`, demoted cells `4`;
    - polar demoted cells `4`, edge demoted cells `0`;
    - extreme demotion `0`;
    - polar spine area `0.965% -> 0.804%` of world area;
    - spine components `2 -> 2`;
    - short components `0 -> 0`;
    - linework score `1.0 -> 1.0`.
  - Smoke audit before the refined guard is retained at
    `out_p160_polar_edge_spine_thinning_2500_seed707_20260704` as evidence
    that the original parent-core protection was too conservative.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111636_polar_edge_repair_reclassifies_residual_polar_peak_width tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p111636_audit_reports_polar_edge_overclassification_metrics -q`
    returned `2 passed`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111635 or p111636 or p111637a or p132 or p1145 or polar_edge'`
    returned `6 passed, 155 deselected`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    returned `161 passed`.
  - `git diff --check` passed.
- Decision:
  - Keep P160 default-on because it is final-stage, non-topological, and
    strongly guarded.
  - Treat P160 as a semantic thinning pass for visible polar/edge spine bands,
    not as a full solution for all coarse raster mountain-belt problems.
- Next required work:
  - P161 should run process-level parallel `8000`-cell multi-seed audits, up to
    five processes/cores, to verify P160 across seeds and identify the next
    dominant defect:
    - remaining polar/edge overclassification after P160;
    - coarse raster-width mountain belts outside polar/edge bands;
    - whether final rendered objects still show high-latitude color bands;
    - whether P160 ever becomes too active and needs a seed-level cap.

### 2026-07-04 P161 process-level 8000-cell P160 multi-seed audit

- Scope:
  - Ran five independent `8000`-cell P107 fast-preview audits with one process
    per seed and single-threaded BLAS/OpenMP:
    - seeds: `42`, `707`, `909`, `1111`, `24000`;
    - plates: `36`;
    - output root:
      `out_p161_p160_multiseed_8000_v2_20260704`.
  - Rendered array-derived visual QA assets for every seed with
    `p107-render-arrays --width 1600 --height 800`.
  - Built:
    - `p161_p160_multiseed_8000_summary.json`;
    - `p161_p160_multiseed_8000_summary.md`;
    - `p161_multiseed_visual_contact_sheet.png`.
- First audit result and correction:
  - The first 8000-cell P161 run
    `out_p161_p160_multiseed_8000_20260704` exposed a P160 guard gap:
    - seeds `707`, `909`, and `24000` accepted P160 but had
      `p160_nonexpansive_polar_edge_spine = false`;
    - the total spine component count stayed stable, but the edge-band subset
      component count increased by one.
  - P160 was repaired before final P161 acceptance:
    - per-cell demotion now refuses candidates that would increase the
      `abs(lon) >= 172` edge-band spine component count;
    - the final P160 guard also rejects any pass with edge-band component
      expansion.
- Final P161 v2 metrics:
  - P160 accepted in `4/5` seeds:
    - seed `1111` had no P160 candidate and remained unchanged.
  - P160 non-expansive polar/edge spine: `5/5` seeds.
  - Extreme spine demotion: `0/5` seeds.
  - Guard reverts: `0/5` seeds.
  - Per-seed P160 activity:
    - seed `42`: candidates `9`, demoted `5`, edge components `1 -> 1`;
    - seed `707`: candidates `30`, demoted `8`, edge components `2 -> 2`;
    - seed `909`: candidates `26`, demoted `14`, edge components `2 -> 2`;
    - seed `1111`: candidates `0`, demoted `0`, edge components `0 -> 0`;
    - seed `24000`: candidates `31`, demoted `11`, edge components `5 -> 5`.
  - Polar spine area changes:
    - seed `707`: `0.613% -> 0.550%`;
    - seed `909`: `0.062% -> 0.050%`;
    - seed `24000`: `0.801% -> 0.664%`;
    - seed `42` had no polar spine; seed `1111` unchanged.
- Visual QA observations:
  - P160 is no longer the dominant visible failure mode:
    - it removes small polar/edge spine fringes without breaking edge-band
      linework;
    - it does not visibly damage parent-supported mountain axes.
  - Remaining defects visible in `p161_multiseed_visual_contact_sheet.png`:
    - seed `909` has severe high-mountain fragmentation:
      high-mountain fragmentation index `0.954`, high components `28`, largest
      high component share `0.098`;
    - seed `42` still shows many red/orange crest flecks inside broad mountain
      regions;
    - seeds `707`, `1111`, and `24000` still show long southern high-latitude
      red/orange belts, although these are no longer edge-fragmentation
      regressions from P160;
    - several maps still read as coarse raster-width mountain belts rather than
      smooth ridge/branch/apron systems.
  - Planform at this stage is not the main blocker:
    - P110A largest land component shares in the rendered summaries are
      `0.363` to `0.514`;
    - only seed `1111` has renderer P110A provenance warnings.
- Performance evidence:
  - Process-level parallel wall-clock was about one 8000-cell run:
    individual runtimes were approximately `498-533s`.
  - Per-seed terrain time remained the largest cost:
    about `341-374s`, dominated by `semantic_object_build`,
    `terminal_bathymetry_polish`, and `province_surface_response`.
  - This confirms that process-level multi-seed parallelism is useful for
    audits, but does not remove the need for later terrain semantic-object
    optimization.
- Validation:
  - After the P160 edge guard repair:
    `.venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
  - Focused:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111636 or p160 or polar_edge'`
    returned `2 passed, 159 deselected`.
- Decision:
  - Keep P160 default-on with the edge-band component guard.
  - Treat P161 as passing the specific P160 multi-seed stability gate, but not
    as terrain-quality closure.
- Next required work:
  - P162 should target the new dominant defects:
    - high-mountain/crest fleck fragmentation, especially seed `909`;
    - overly broad or overly continuous southern high-latitude red/orange belts;
    - coarse raster-width mountain belts where main crest, branch range,
      foreland slope, and apron are still not sufficiently organized.

### 2026-07-04 P162 terminal high-mountain fragment cleanup

- Scope:
  - Added a guarded terminal terrain pass
    `terrain.enable_p162_terminal_high_mountain_fragment_cleanup`, default-on.
  - The pass runs after P155 and before final object consistency passes.
  - It addresses the P161 seed-`909` failure mode where semantic parent/spine
    linework is mostly present but `3000m+` elevation still contains many tiny
    detached red/orange high-mountain flecks.
- Design:
  - P162 only activates when the final high-mountain field is clearly
    overfragmented:
    - high-mountain component count at least `10`;
    - high-mountain fragmentation index at least `0.50`;
    - nonzero high-mountain area.
  - Candidate cells are restricted to small high-mountain components that are:
    - not among the three largest high-mountain components;
    - no larger than eight cells;
    - locally compact rather than elongated belt axes;
    - below the protected extreme-relief threshold;
    - not strongly supported by nearby spine or parent hierarchy.
  - Accepted candidate cells are lowered just below the `3000m` high-mountain
    threshold while preserving land/ocean classification.
- Guards:
  - Reject if sea/land mask changes.
  - Reject if any extreme high cell is softened.
  - Reject unless high-mountain components decrease by at least two.
  - Reject if small high-mountain component count increases.
  - Reject if spine-supported `3000m+` component count increases.
  - Reject unless high-mountain fragmentation improves materially.
  - Reject if the top-three high-mountain share worsens.
  - Reject if high-mountain area drops below the bounded area floor.
  - Reject if land `p90` or `p98` relief increases.
- Audit surface:
  - P107 high-mountain coherence now reports:
    - P162 accepted/reverted state;
    - candidate and softened cell counts and area fractions;
    - high component count before/after;
    - small high component count before/after;
    - spine `3000m+` component count before/after;
    - top-three high share before/after;
    - fragmentation index before/after;
    - high area before/after;
    - land `p90`/`p98` relief before/after;
    - mean/max lowering, extreme softened cells, and reject code.
- Validation:
  - Synthetic direct fixture:
    - builds a main high-mountain component plus ten tiny flecks;
    - P162 accepts;
    - flecks are lowered below `3000m`;
    - main high cells remain above `3000m`;
    - high component count and fragmentation decline;
    - land/ocean mask and extreme cells are preserved.
  - `2500` seed-`909` audit:
    - output:
      `out_p162_high_fragment_cleanup_2500_seed909_20260704`;
    - P162 intentionally did not trigger because the seed was below the
      severe-fragmentation threshold:
      fragmentation `0.421`, reject code `4`.
  - `8000` seed-`909` audit:
    - output:
      `out_p162_high_fragment_cleanup_8000_seed909_20260704`;
    - runtime `469s`;
    - P162 accepted without guard revert;
    - high-mountain components `28 -> 18`;
    - small high-mountain components `22 -> 12`;
    - spine `3000m+` components `15 -> 13`;
    - top-three high-mountain share `0.287 -> 0.404`;
    - high-mountain fragmentation index `0.954 -> 0.734`;
    - high-mountain world area `1.912% -> 1.362%`;
    - land `p90` relief unchanged at `2679m`;
    - land `p98` relief changed `3199m -> 3185m`;
    - extreme softened cells `0`.
  - Rendered QA assets for the `8000` seed-`909` audit:
    - `out_p162_high_fragment_cleanup_8000_seed909_20260704/00_8000cells_36p/rendered_arrays_1600`.
- Tests:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py`
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p162 or p155_terminal_high_relief_gate_accepts_global_ab_improvement or terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays'`
    returned `3 passed, 159 deselected`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p162 or high_mountain_coherence or terminal_audit_writes_raw_arrays'`
    returned `4 passed, 158 deselected`.
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    returned `162 passed`.
  - `git diff --check` passed for the touched source, test, and plan files.
- Decision:
  - Keep P162 default-on because it is terminal, narrow, deterministic, and
    strongly guarded.
  - Treat P162 as a targeted cleanup for unsupported high-mountain flecks, not
    as the final solution for mountain-belt geometry.
  - P162 improves the worst 8000-cell seed-`909` high-mountain metrics, but the
    rendered map still shows that upstream orogen hierarchy/spine geometry is
    locally fragmented.  A future pass must improve the line-object source
    itself rather than only cleaning terminal elevation flecks.
- Next required work:
  - Run a five-seed `8000`-cell process-level P162 regression audit to verify:
    - no degradation of already acceptable seeds;
    - high-mountain fragmentation non-worsening across seeds;
    - no loss of true active or inherited orogen axes.
  - Then target the remaining geometry problem upstream:
    - smoother continuous spine polylines;
    - less raster-like branch/crest class breakup;
    - more regular foreland slope and apron placement;
    - bounded southern high-latitude belt thickness.

### 2026-07-04 P163 five-seed P162 regression audit

- Scope:
  - Ran the required process-level `8000`-cell P162 regression audit using five
    independent single-threaded processes.
  - Seeds: `42`, `707`, `909`, `1111`, `24000`.
  - Plates: `36`.
  - Output root:
    `out_p163_p162_multiseed_8000_20260704`.
  - Rendered all five runs with
    `p107-render-arrays --width 1600 --height 800`.
  - Built:
    - `p163_p162_multiseed_8000_summary.json`;
    - `p163_p162_multiseed_8000_summary.md`;
    - `p163_p162_multiseed_visual_contact_sheet.png`.
- Runtime:
  - Individual run times were approximately:
    - seed `42`: `536s`;
    - seed `707`: `546s`;
    - seed `909`: `530s`;
    - seed `1111`: `554s`;
    - seed `24000`: `520s`.
  - Terrain remained the dominant cost at roughly `352-385s` per run.
  - The profile confirms the previous performance conclusion:
    process-level parallelism works for seed sweeps, while terrain
    `semantic_object_build`, `terminal_bathymetry_polish`, and
    `province_surface_response` remain the main single-run bottlenecks.
- Regression result:
  - P162 accepted in `1/5` seeds:
    - only seed `909` triggered the severe-fragmentation cleanup.
  - P162 guard reverts: `0/5`.
  - P162 land-mask failures: `0/5`.
  - P162 extreme softened seeds: `0/5`.
  - High-mountain fragmentation regressions versus P161: `0/5`.
  - P160 remained non-expansive for polar/edge spine components in `5/5`
    seeds.
- Per-seed high-mountain comparison versus P161:
  - seed `42`: components `13 -> 13`, fragmentation `0.348 -> 0.348`;
  - seed `707`: components `7 -> 7`, fragmentation `0.195 -> 0.195`;
  - seed `909`: components `28 -> 18`, fragmentation `0.954 -> 0.734`;
  - seed `1111`: components `11 -> 11`, fragmentation `0.321 -> 0.321`;
  - seed `24000`: components `13 -> 13`, fragmentation `0.404 -> 0.404`.
  - Mean high-mountain fragmentation changed by `-0.044` versus P161.
- Planform status:
  - Renderer P110A warnings remain limited to seed `1111`:
    `p110b_lineage_support_incomplete`,
    `p110b_landform_provenance_incomplete`.
  - This is unchanged from P161 and is not caused by P162.
- Visual QA:
  - Contact sheet:
    `out_p163_p162_multiseed_8000_20260704/p163_p162_multiseed_visual_contact_sheet.png`.
  - Seed `909` no longer has the worst pre-P162 red/orange fleck density, but
    it still carries visibly broken local crest/spine geometry.
  - Across all five seeds, the dominant remaining artifacts are upstream, not
    terminal P162 cleanup:
    - spine/branch overlays remain jagged and raster-like;
    - orogenic classes sometimes break into short local line segments;
    - southern high-latitude belts remain too wide and band-like in seeds
      `707`, `1111`, and `24000`;
    - foreland/apron organization remains sparse or irregular compared with
      the desired main-ridge, branch-range, slope, and apron hierarchy.
- Decision:
  - Keep P162 default-on.
  - Treat P163 as passing the five-seed P162 regression gate.
  - Do not broaden P162: it is doing the intended narrow terminal cleanup.
    The next quality improvement should operate upstream on orogenic spine and
    class line-object generation.
- Next required work:
  - P164 should target upstream orogenic geometry rather than elevation flecks:
    - convert remaining local jagged spine/class fragments into smoother
      parent-supported line objects;
    - improve crest/branch segmentation before terminal elevation response;
    - enforce better main crest, branch range, foreland slope, and apron
      ordering;
    - cap high-latitude belt width without deleting true inherited or active
      orogens.

### 2026-07-04 P164 terminal orogenic axis skeleton simplification

- Scope:
  - Added a terminal, default-on orogenic spine cleanup pass before final
    orogen morphology/elevation consumers.
  - The pass operates only on `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`; it does not
    directly alter surface elevation, sea/land, crust type, plate ids, or the
    parent orogen hierarchy.
  - It targets the remaining raster-like spine defects left after P163:
    excessive local junctions, high-degree knots, and off-axis crest/branch
    cells that make mountain belts read as blocky patches instead of line
    objects.
- Implementation:
  - New helper in `aevum/modules/terrain.py`:
    `_p164_terminal_orogenic_axis_skeleton_simplification`.
  - For each crest/branch spine component, P164 keeps an approximate diameter
    path as the protected axis and demotes only same-class off-axis spine
    cells that are locally over-connected.
  - Rebuilds terminal spine-derived objects after acceptance so downstream
    rendered objects and P107 audit fields describe the cleaned source.
  - Added P164 telemetry to `aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py`.
- Guard:
  - Rejects if component count, short-component count, endpoint count, or
    branch attachment worsens.
  - Rejects if linework/axis scores fall below the allowed tolerance.
  - Rejects if junction/high-degree fractions increase.
  - Rejects if too much spine is removed or if any protected `3300m+` extreme
    spine cell is demoted.
  - Rejects if remaining spine escapes the parent orogen hierarchy.
- Tests:
  - Added a focused synthetic unit test that builds a deliberately over-wide
    crest spine and verifies that P164 removes side cells while preserving the
    protected main axis.
  - Extended the P107 audit telemetry test to cover all P164 summary fields.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p164 or p111635_audit_reports_orogenic_spine_linework_metrics'`
    returned `2 passed, 161 deselected`.
- Validation, seed `909`, `2500` cells:
  - Output root:
    `out_p164_axis_skeleton_2500_seed909_20260704`.
  - Runtime `162s`; P164 stage time `0.026s`.
  - P164 accepted without guard revert.
  - Candidate cells `11`; demoted cells `3`.
  - Components `4 -> 4`; short components remained `0 -> 0`.
  - Junctions `31 -> 26`; high-degree cells `6 -> 4`.
  - Axis score `0.756 -> 0.772`; reject code `0`.
- Validation, seed `909`, `8000` cells:
  - Output root:
    `out_p164_axis_skeleton_8000_seed909_20260704`.
  - Runtime `501s`; terrain time `345s`; P164 stage time remained negligible.
  - P164 accepted without guard revert.
  - Candidate cells `45`; off-axis candidates `48`; demoted cells `15`.
  - Spine cells `270 -> 255`.
  - Components `4 -> 4`; short components `0 -> 0`; endpoints `21 -> 21`.
  - Junctions `126 -> 94`; high-degree cells `33 -> 19`.
  - Junction fraction `0.467 -> 0.369`; high-degree fraction
    `0.122 -> 0.075`.
  - Branch attachment remained `1.0 -> 1.0`.
  - Axis score improved `0.798 -> 0.844`; linework score remained within the
    guard tolerance `0.9889 -> 0.9882`.
  - Protected extreme demotions `0`; reject code `0`.
  - P162 high-mountain cleanup metrics remained effectively unchanged from the
    P163 baseline for the same seed:
    high-mountain components `28 -> 18`, fragmentation `0.954 -> 0.734`.
- Visual QA:
  - Rendered 8000-cell QA assets:
    `out_p164_axis_skeleton_8000_seed909_20260704/00_8000cells_36p/rendered_arrays_1600`.
  - Contact sheet:
    `out_p164_axis_skeleton_8000_seed909_20260704/00_8000cells_36p/rendered_arrays_1600/p107_array_contact_sheet.png`.
  - `orogenic_hierarchy_spine_overlay.png` shows cleaner, thinner spine
    sources with fewer local knots.
  - No obvious sea/land, bathymetry, crust-age, or elevation regression was
    introduced by P164.
  - Remaining visible defects are upstream/semantic:
    - left-side continental branch ranges are still locally fragmented;
    - high-latitude/southern edge belts are still too wide and over-classified;
    - foreland slope and apron placement is still sparse and irregular.
- Decision:
  - Keep P164 default-on.
  - Treat P164 as a successful narrow spine-source cleanup, not as a complete
    orogen-generation rewrite.
  - Do not broaden P164 into high-latitude pruning or foreland/apron synthesis;
    those require separate guarded passes.
- Next required work:
  - P165 should address high-latitude and map-edge overclassification:
    preserve real inherited/active orogens but reduce broad polar-edge belts
    that do not have strong boundary, elevation, or parent-hierarchy support.
  - P166 should then improve foreland slope and apron geometry so mountain
    belts have clearer main crest, branch range, slope, and basin/apron
    ordering.
  - P167 should revisit branch-range organization inside large continents so
    internal mountain systems stop reading as scattered local fragments.

### 2026-07-04 P165 terminal polar/edge orogenic semantic compaction

- Scope:
  - Added a terminal, default-on semantic compaction pass after P164 and before
    terminal relief repair.
  - P165 is deliberately conservative: it does not move sea/land, plate ids,
    surface elevation, crust type, or `terrain.orogenic_hierarchy_spine`.
  - It targets only weak high-latitude/map-edge orogenic semantics that are
    away from the protected spine axis:
    - unsupported peak cells become lower-order halo/apron semantics;
    - unsupported fringe cells are cleared from hierarchy/halo/apron.
- Implementation:
  - New helper in `aevum/modules/terrain.py`:
    `_p165_terminal_polar_edge_orogenic_semantic_compaction`.
  - New default flag:
    `terrain.enable_p165_terminal_polar_edge_orogenic_semantic_compaction`.
  - Updated terminal inventory fields on acceptance:
    - `terrain.orogenic_parent_hierarchy`;
    - `terrain.orogenic_shoulder_halo`;
    - `terrain.orogenic_highland_apron`.
  - Added P165 telemetry to
    `p111636_polar_edge_orogen_overclassification`; schema advanced to
    `aevum.p111636_polar_edge_orogen_overclassification.v3`.
- Guard:
  - Rejects the whole pass if protected spine hierarchy would be lost.
  - Rejects if any `3300m+` protected extreme cell is reclassified.
  - Rejects if total semantic area expands, polar/edge semantic area expands,
    or polar peak area expands.
  - Rejects if global or edge peak component count grows beyond the allowed
    tolerance.
  - Rejects if peak hierarchy cells overlap halo/apron after the operation.
- Tests:
  - Extended the P111636 audit test with P165 telemetry and v3 schema checks.
  - Added a synthetic P165 fixture that builds a broad polar peak band, a
    protected `3600m` spine axis, and detached halo/apron cells; the test
    verifies that P165 prunes weak semantics while preserving the protected
    axis.
  - Focused command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p165 or p111636_audit_reports_polar_edge_overclassification_metrics'`
    returned `2 passed, 162 deselected`.
  - Broader command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q -k 'p111636 or p165'`
    returned `3 passed, 161 deselected`.
  - Full command:
    `.venv/bin/python -m compileall -q aevum/modules/terrain.py aevum/diagnostics/p107_audit.py tests/test_p107_audit.py && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py -q`
    returned `164 passed`.
  - `git diff --check` passed for the touched source, test, diagnostic, and
    plan files.
- Validation, seed `909`, `2500` cells:
  - Output root:
    `out_p165_polar_edge_semantic_compaction_2500_seed909_20260704`.
  - Runtime `159s`; terrain time `102s`.
  - P165 accepted without guard revert.
  - Candidate cells `10`; all were weak fringe clears.
  - Polar semantic area `0.0253 -> 0.0213`.
  - Edge semantic area unchanged `0.00479 -> 0.00479`.
  - Polar peak area unchanged `0.00724 -> 0.00724`.
  - Peak components `4 -> 4`; edge peak components `2 -> 2`.
  - Protected spine changes `0`; protected extreme reclassifications `0`.
  - P164 and P162 remained stable in the same run.
- Validation, seed `909`, `8000` cells:
  - Output root:
    `out_p165_polar_edge_semantic_compaction_8000_seed909_20260704`.
  - Runtime `487s`; terrain time `335s`.
  - P165 did not trigger: candidate cells `0`.
  - Existing P160/P164 remained active and stable:
    - P160 demoted `14` polar/edge spine cells;
    - polar spine world area `0.000625 -> 0.000500`;
    - P164 demoted `15` off-axis spine cells;
    - P164 junctions `126 -> 94`;
    - P164 axis score `0.798 -> 0.844`.
  - P162 metrics remained aligned with the P163 baseline:
    high-mountain components `28 -> 18`, fragmentation `0.954 -> 0.734`.
- Visual QA:
  - Rendered 2500-cell QA assets:
    `out_p165_polar_edge_semantic_compaction_2500_seed909_20260704/00_2500cells_36p/rendered_arrays_1200`.
  - Rendered 8000-cell QA assets:
    `out_p165_polar_edge_semantic_compaction_8000_seed909_20260704/00_8000cells_36p/rendered_arrays_1600`.
  - The 2500-cell map confirms P165 can remove weak polar fringe semantics.
  - The 8000-cell map confirms the dominant remaining artifact is not weak
    halo/apron fringe; it is spine/peak-axis organization and branch-range
    placement.  The most visible mountain defects are still:
    - left-side continental branch ranges with local fragmentation;
    - long near-edge spine/peak bands where the axis itself needs better
      eligibility and smoothing;
    - sparse/irregular foreland slope and apron ordering.
- Decision:
  - Keep P165 default-on because it is narrow, deterministic, guarded, and
    non-destructive.
  - Do not broaden P165 to delete spine axes.  The 8000 validation shows that
    the remaining high-latitude/edge visual problem is not mostly unsupported
    fringe; deleting it here would conflate two different failure modes.
- Next required work:
  - P166 should split into two concrete tasks before foreland/apron synthesis:
    - P166a: polar/map-edge spine-axis eligibility audit and guarded source
      repair, using boundary support, parent axis support, and high-relief
      continuity to decide which near-edge spine cells are real;
    - P166b: branch-range organization inside large continental belts so
      local branches group into coherent ridge systems instead of scattered
      fragments.
  - P167 can then return to foreland slope and highland apron ordering once
    the spine/branch source is cleaner.

#### 2026-07-08 P166a closed-ocean constraint rollback and process islands

- Problem:
  - Recent 8000-cell worlds still showed narrow cracks through continental
    interiors.
  - Large ocean basins were visually too empty compared with Earth, where
    island arcs, hotspot chains, microcontinents, and oceanic plateaus create
    frequent island/seamount punctuation.
- Diagnosis:
  - `_connect_large_closed_oceans(...)` and
    `_p111617_late_closed_ocean_repair(...)` treated isolated/closed ocean
    basins as a topology defect and could cut narrow seaways through land to
    connect them.
  - `_p110b_internal_domain_seaway_polish(...)` could convert internal
    geographic-domain boundaries into water corridors by default.
  - Terminal ocean cleanup preserved explicit object-backed shoals only when
    `terrain.enable_p1115_object_backed_shoal_preservation` was manually set,
    and no terminal pass promoted sparse process-backed ocean highs into
    visible islands.
- Implementation:
  - Made closed-ocean seaway repair opt-in:
    `terrain.enable_closed_ocean_seaway_repair=1` is now required for
    `_connect_large_closed_oceans(...)`, and
    `terrain.enable_p111617_late_closed_ocean_repair` now defaults to `0`.
  - Made P110B internal-domain seaway cutting opt-in behind
    `terrain.enable_p110b_internal_domain_seaway_opening`; the default still
    records and expresses internal domain boundaries as lowland/suture belts.
  - Changed object-backed shoal preservation to default-on in
    `_deepen_modern_earthlike_open_ocean_shoals(...)`.
  - Added `_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion(...)`, a terminal,
    budget-limited island promotion pass.  It only promotes sparse offshore
    cells backed by `island_arc`, `seamount_chain`, `hotspot_track`,
    `oceanic_plateau`, `large_igneous_province`, or `microcontinent` objects;
    it does not promote unsupported open-ocean shoal noise.
- Tests:
  - Added default-off coverage for closed-ocean repair.
  - Added P110B coverage showing default boundary-only behavior and explicit
    opt-in seaway opening.
  - Added process-island coverage proving unsupported shallow ocean remains
    submerged while seamount-chain-backed highs can emerge.
  - Focused commands passed:
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_is_opt_in_by_default tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_opens_seaway_to_main_ocean tests/test_engine.py::test_late_closed_ocean_repair_reports_improvement_and_preserves_corridors -q`
    returned `3 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110b_internal_domain_seaway_polish_opens_protected_corridor tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110b_internal_domain_polish_defaults_to_boundary_not_seaway tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion_uses_object_backed_ocean_highs -q`
    returned `5 passed`.
    `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_selected_snapshot_refinement.py -q`
    returned `18 passed`.
- Expected visual effect:
  - Existing generated PNGs remain old outputs until regenerated.
  - New worlds should tolerate some closed/semiclosed seas instead of forcing
    them open through continental interiors.
  - Open ocean should gain sparse, process-backed islands without resurrecting
    broad unsupported shallow patches.

#### 2026-07-08 P166b process-backed ocean detail tuning

- Problem:
  - The P166a rollback removed forced continental cracks and restored sparse
    object-backed islands, but 8000-cell oceans still read too empty compared
    with Earth-like island arcs, hotspot chains, microcontinents, and shallow
    shelves around emergent ocean highs.
- Implementation:
  - Retuned `_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion(...)`:
    - increased the default process-island budget from `0.0035` to `0.0065`
      of world area, still bounded by an Earth-like land-fraction ceiling;
    - relaxed process-object depth thresholds for `island_arc`,
      `seamount_chain`, `hotspot_track`, `oceanic_plateau`,
      `large_igneous_province`, and `microcontinent`;
    - made object-axis spacing denser for chains and plateaus;
    - added a process-backed shallow-shoulder/shelf halo around selected
      emergent islands, restricted to the same ocean object support and kept
      below sea level.
  - Added telemetry:
    `terrain.last_p173_terminal_process_island_shelf_area_fraction` and
    `terrain.last_p173_terminal_process_island_shelf_cells`.
- Validation:
  - Focused tests passed with Homebrew expat on the dynamic library path:
    `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/expat/lib .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion_uses_object_backed_ocean_highs -q`
    returned `1 passed`.
    `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/expat/lib .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_is_opt_in_by_default tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110b_internal_domain_polish_defaults_to_boundary_not_seaway -q`
    returned `4 passed`.
  - `compileall` passed for `aevum/modules/terrain.py` and
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py`.
- Preview:
  - Applied the new pass as a post-process preview to the five P166a 8000-cell
    outputs without rerunning the full engine.
  - Added island cells per world: `14-19`.
  - Added process-backed shallow shelf cells per world: `30-57`.
  - Preview sheet:
    `out_p166a_process_islands_8000_5worlds_20260708/ocean_detail_preview_after_tuning/five_worlds_ocean_detail_preview_sheet.png`.
- Decision:
  - Keep the tuning process-backed rather than random.
  - The next full 8000 run should confirm that the richer island/shelf signal
    survives the complete terrain pipeline.

#### 2026-07-08 P166c dense process archipelago preview

- Problem:
  - P166b improved oceans, but visual QA still read as too sparse at 8000
    cells.
  - At this resolution, a single grid cell is already a very large island or
    small archipelago, so continuing to increase true land cells risks creating
    oversized oceanic landmasses.
- Implementation:
  - Retuned `_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion(...)` more aggressively:
    - default island budget increased to `0.012` of world area, with an
      opt-in ceiling up to `0.025`;
    - land-fraction ceiling offset increased to `0.080`;
    - depth thresholds widened for island arcs, seamount chains, hotspot
      tracks, oceanic plateaus, LIPs, and microcontinents;
    - candidate selection now includes both sparse object axes and secondary
      object-backed cells near those axes;
    - selected islands can form small adjacent archipelago clusters, but cells
      with two selected neighbors are rejected to avoid turning whole plateaus
      into continents;
    - shallow shoulders now use a two-cell halo around selected islands and
      support-object shoulders;
    - added a second, deeper `reef`/outer-shoal halo, still restricted to
      process-object support and kept below sea level.
  - Added telemetry:
    `terrain.last_p173_terminal_process_island_reef_area_fraction` and
    `terrain.last_p173_terminal_process_island_reef_cells`.
- Validation:
  - Focused tests passed:
    `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/expat/lib PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion_uses_object_backed_ocean_highs -q`
    returned `1 passed`.
    `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/expat/lib PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_is_opt_in_by_default tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110b_internal_domain_polish_defaults_to_boundary_not_seaway -q`
    returned `4 passed`.
  - `compileall` passed for `aevum/modules/terrain.py` and
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py`.
- Preview:
  - Re-applied the retuned pass as a post-process preview to the five P166a
    8000-cell worlds.
  - Added island cells per world: `41-69`.
  - Added shallow shelf cells per world: `185-385`.
  - Added reef/outer-shoal cells per world: `39-77`.
  - Preview sheet:
    `out_p166a_process_islands_8000_5worlds_20260708/ocean_detail_preview_after_tuning_v2/five_worlds_ocean_detail_preview_sheet.png`.
- Decision:
  - This is close to the sensible true-cell island limit at 8000 cells.
  - If visual density still needs to increase, prefer a renderer/compiler
    subgrid-island overlay rather than turning more full cells into land.

#### 2026-07-08 P166d balanced process archipelago default

- Problem:
  - Visual QA on the P166c preview found the ocean detail correction
    over-shot: island/reef/shoal signal became too dense for an 8000-cell
    physical grid.
  - At this resolution, dense true-cell island promotion reads as many large
    archipelago cells rather than realistic small islands.
- Implementation:
  - Retuned `_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion(...)` back to a balanced
    default:
    - default island budget lowered from `0.012` to `0.008` of world area;
    - land-fraction ceiling offset lowered from `0.080` to `0.065`;
    - process depth thresholds tightened between P166b and P166c;
    - secondary object-backed cells now require shallower support and stronger
      local object continuity;
    - adjacent island clusters are only allowed for high-score cells, reducing
      patchy archipelago overgrowth;
    - shelf halo reduced to one pass, with a shallower maximum adjustment;
    - reef/outer-shoal halo remains available but is kept as a lower-relief,
      process-backed bathymetry signal rather than a land-promotion driver.
  - Kept telemetry for added islands, shelf cells, and reef cells so future
    seed sweeps can tune density quantitatively.
- Validation:
  - Focused regression passed:
    `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/expat/lib PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p173_terminal_process_island_promotion_uses_object_backed_ocean_highs -q`
    returned `1 passed`.
  - Related guard tests passed:
    `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/expat/lib PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p1115_open_ocean_shoal_deepen_preserves_object_backed_relief tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p11153_object_backed_shoal_preservation_does_not_keep_halo tests/test_engine.py::test_large_closed_ocean_repair_is_opt_in_by_default tests/test_p107_audit.py::test_p110b_internal_domain_polish_defaults_to_boundary_not_seaway -q`
    returned `4 passed`.
  - `compileall` passed for `aevum/modules/terrain.py` and
    `tests/test_p107_audit.py`.
- Preview:
  - Re-applied the balanced pass as a post-process preview to the five P166a
    8000-cell worlds.
  - Added island cells per world: `19-28`.
  - Added process-backed shallow shelf cells per world: `101-152`.
  - Added reef/outer-shoal cells per world: `64-124`.
  - Preview sheet:
    `out_p166a_process_islands_8000_5worlds_20260708/ocean_detail_preview_after_tuning_v3_balanced/five_worlds_ocean_detail_preview_sheet.png`.
- Decision:
  - Adopt P166d as the current default. It restores visible oceanic
    archipelagos and shoals without turning every process object into a dense
    island field.
  - Further tiny-island richness should be handled at the selected-snapshot
    72000 refinement/compiler layer, where sub-cell islands, reefs, atolls, and
    shoals can be rendered without changing the coarse physical land/ocean mask.
